# Skyrim:Unmarked Places Solstheim

## Overview

[Overview](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places "Skyrim:Unmarked Places") | [Eastmarch](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Eastmarch "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Eastmarch") — [Falkreath](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Falkreath "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Falkreath") — [Haafingar](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Haafingar "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Haafingar") — [Hjaalmarch](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Hjaalmarch "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Hjaalmarch") — [The Pale](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_The_Pale "Skyrim:Unmarked Places The Pale") — [The Reach](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_The_Reach "Skyrim:Unmarked Places The Reach") — [The Rift](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_The_Rift "Skyrim:Unmarked Places The Rift") — [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Whiterun "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Whiterun") — [Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Winterhold "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Winterhold") — Solstheim

---

This section describes unmarked places on the island of [Solstheim](/wiki/Skyrim:Solstheim "Skyrim:Solstheim") added by the [Dragonborn](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonborn "Skyrim:Dragonborn") add-on. They usually aren't directly related to any quests (though several of them contain East Empire Company strongboxes, related to the [Pain in the Necklace](/wiki/Skyrim:Pain_in_the_Necklace "Skyrim:Pain in the Necklace") quest).

### Bloodskal Copse

[File:SR-place-Bloodskal Copse.jpg](/w/index.php?title=Special:Upload&wpDestFile=SR-place-Bloodskal_Copse.jpg "File:SR-place-Bloodskal Copse.jpg")

Bloodskal Copse

This is in a small natural harbor southwest of Bloodskal Barrow and northwest of Raven Rock. During the quest Deathbrand, a small Reaver rowboat is moored near this copse of trees. Defeat the Reavers here (who were discussing the treasure they'd dug up and were unwilling to open it) before prying the adept Ancient Chest open
with your lockpicks. This chest is not present unless the quest has begun.

### Desolate Hunter's Camp

Reaver's End

Southwest of [Hrothmund's Barrow](/wiki/Skyrim:Hrothmund%27s_Barrow "Skyrim:Hrothmund's Barrow"), north-northeast of Moesring Pass, you'll find a cindering fire at the center of a few crates, barrels, and sacks, with a dead reaver slumped next to it. Two Rieklings will ambush you when you approach. On the north side of the fire, an apprentice-locked strongbox sits atop a crate. In front of that crate is an expert-locked East Empire Company strongbox. A total of three helmets can be found here, including a bonemold helmet, a fur helmet, and a Skaal hat. On the south side of the fire, to the right of the dead reaver is a wooden chest. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=41009&y=72590&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Dragon Mound: Frozen Shoals

Frozen Shoals Dragon Mound

Frozen Shoals Dragon Mound

In the far northeastern corner of the map, north of Frossel and [Haknir's Shoal](/wiki/Skyrim:Haknir%27s_Shoal "Skyrim:Haknir's Shoal"), there are three islands which form a triangle. The northwest of these three islands is home to a [dragon burial mound](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Mounds "Skyrim:Dragon Mounds"). A [dragon](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon "Skyrim:Dragon") lies in wait here, along with several [horkers](/wiki/Skyrim:Horker "Skyrim:Horker"). Just northwest of the dragon mound is a novice-locked chest next to some skeletal remains. Scattered across the island are ten [clams](/wiki/Skyrim:Clam "Skyrim:Clam"), three [nirnroot](/wiki/Skyrim:Nirnroot "Skyrim:Nirnroot") plants, and two [pearl oysters](/wiki/Skyrim:Pearl_Oyster "Skyrim:Pearl Oyster"). [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=60487&y=100884&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Forgotten Spoils

Head northwest from Hrothmund's Barrow, looking for a sapphire Geode, and the summit of a long cascading waterfall that tumbles down to Castle Karstaag. On the rocky outcrops at the top of the waterfall is an adept chest to loot alongside a sword and a [steel plate](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Plate_Helmet "Skyrim:Steel Plate Helmet") (or leather) helmet (helmet is sometimes clipped into the rock).

### Giant Nirnroot Island

Giant Nirnroot Island

Giant Nirnroot

On a large island in the far northeastern corner of the map, north of [Frossel](/wiki/Skyrim:Frossel "Skyrim:Frossel"), there is a small collection of six giant [nirnroot](/wiki/Skyrim:Nirnroot "Skyrim:Nirnroot") plants, each of which yield three ingredients when harvested. Two [spriggans](/wiki/Skyrim:Spriggan "Skyrim:Spriggan") and a giant spriggan matron can be found here. On the east side of the island is a small boat containing an unlocked chest, an ingredient barrel, a knapsack, an apothecary's satchel, three sacks, a coin purse, and an East Empire Company strongbox. The boat can sometimes become invisible, leaving only the reflection of the lantern revealing its position.[*verification needed — Observed with PC*] A dead [Alchemist](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemist "Skyrim:Alchemist") can be found lying facedown next to a giant nirnroot plant. Scattered across the island are two regular nirnroot plants, eleven [pearl oysters](/wiki/Skyrim:Pearl_Oyster "Skyrim:Pearl Oyster"), and four spiky grass plants which yield [ashen grass pods](/wiki/Skyrim:Ashen_Grass_Pod "Skyrim:Ashen Grass Pod"). [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=75725&y=102808&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Horker Iceberg

Horker Stalhrim Island

Directly northeast of [Northshore Landing](/wiki/Skyrim:Northshore_Landing "Skyrim:Northshore Landing") is a small island guarded by two horkers. Two [stalhrim](/wiki/Skyrim:Stalhrim "Skyrim:Stalhrim") deposits are located on the island. Mining them, you can find a leveled enchanted [battleaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Battleaxe "Skyrim:Battleaxe") in the right one, and the lootable corpse of a [draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Draugr "Skyrim:Draugr") in the left one. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=28333&y=99959&zoom=16&legacy=true)

With the [Alternative Armors - Stalhrim Fur](/wiki/Skyrim:Alternative_Armors_-_Stalhrim_Fur "Skyrim:Alternative Armors - Stalhrim Fur") [Creation](/wiki/Skyrim:Creation_Club "Skyrim:Creation Club") installed, this island becomes involved with the quest [Ancient Ice](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Ice "Skyrim:Ancient Ice"), during which you will be sent here to face [Skjol the Tongueless](/wiki/Skyrim:Skjol_the_Tongueless "Skyrim:Skjol the Tongueless") in battle. After defeating him, you will be able to claim the full set of Stalhrim Fur armor found within one of the sarcophagi on the island.

### Lurker Ambush (Tidal Pool)

Lurker Ambush

West-northwest of [Haknir's Shoal](/wiki/Skyrim:Haknir%27s_Shoal "Skyrim:Haknir's Shoal"), you will find a small inlet of the larger bay that surrounds the shoal. When you approach it, a leveled [Lurker](/wiki/Skyrim:Lurker "Skyrim:Lurker") may rise up from the shallow waters and attack you. Three [nirnroot](/wiki/Skyrim:Nirnroot "Skyrim:Nirnroot") grow in and near the inlet, and two [orichalcum](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish "Skyrim:Orcish") deposits can be mined to the west and northwest. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=61748.2&y=85050&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Mudcrab Tidal Pools

This part of the shoreline is north of the Water Stone and south of Bristleback Cave. During Deathbrand, the water in one of the tidal pools recedes revealing an ancient chest which contains the Deathbrand Gauntlets.

Rieklings can attack the mudcrabs found here.

### Riekling Barrel Hoard

Riekling Ambush

From [Damphall Mine](/wiki/Skyrim:Damphall_Mine "Skyrim:Damphall Mine"), head north until you see a few patches of [snowberries](/wiki/Skyrim:Snowberries "Skyrim:Snowberries"). Turn left at the next rock and you'll see a wooden platform with many barrels, a chest, and a couple crates. An East Empire Company strongbox sits on the crate to the left. Beware, as Rieklings will [ambush](/wiki/Skyrim:Riekling_Barrel "Skyrim:Riekling Barrel") you when you approach, popping out of the barrels and the snow. The remaining barrels contain foodstuffs. Using the [Aura Whisper](/wiki/Skyrim:Aura_Whisper "Skyrim:Aura Whisper") shout will reveal the Rieklings' locations before they emerge; using this you can strike them before they burst from the snow. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=24683&y=61231&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Riekling Chest

Another random chest

Follow the shoreline southwest of [Northshore Landing](/wiki/Skyrim:Northshore_Landing "Skyrim:Northshore Landing") to find a novice-locked wooden chest covered in riekling painting and with a ring of [riekling spears](/wiki/Skyrim:Riekling_Spear "Skyrim:Riekling Spear") sticking from the sides of it against the cliff side. It is approximately an equal distance between the landing and [Bristleback Cave](/wiki/Skyrim:Bristleback_Cave "Skyrim:Bristleback Cave"), on the northwestern corner of [Solstheim](/wiki/Lore:Solstheim "Lore:Solstheim"). There are two [horkers](/wiki/Skyrim:Horker "Skyrim:Horker") on the shore in front of it. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=19550&y=89100&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Riekling Outpost Island

Riekling Island

On a small island in the far northeastern corner of the map, northwest of [Frossel](/wiki/Skyrim:Frossel "Skyrim:Frossel") and north of [Haknir's Shoal](/wiki/Skyrim:Haknir%27s_Shoal "Skyrim:Haknir's Shoal"), there is a small tribe of [Rieklings](/wiki/Skyrim:Riekling "Skyrim:Riekling"). There are two spiky grass plants which yield [ashen grass pods](/wiki/Skyrim:Ashen_Grass_Pod "Skyrim:Ashen Grass Pod"), five [pearl oysters](/wiki/Skyrim:Pearl_Oyster "Skyrim:Pearl Oyster"), and three [nirnroot](/wiki/Skyrim:Nirnroot "Skyrim:Nirnroot") plants on the shore of the island, with the Riekling camp at the center. On the east side of the camp is a small tent with an unlocked chest and two sacks inside and a knapsack just outside. Near the northwest Riekling structure is another unlocked chest, a few sacks, and a coin purse. Beside the chest is another small tent which contains a sack and a satchel. At the very top of the south Riekling structure is an East Empire Company strongbox, a knapsack, and a coin purse. Below the top structure among the rocks is a final chest. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=66899&y=92629&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Spiky Grass Island

A tiny island with two dying trees, four [spiky grass](/wiki/Skyrim:Spiky_Grass "Skyrim:Spiky Grass") and a nirnroot.

### The Ever-Anchored Ship

This ship and two rowboats on the shore can be found before the Water Stone is cleansed, with the crew being bound to it. You cannot board the ship, and it will disappear after freeing the crew.

### The Fisherman's Haul

Fishing Camp

Southwest of [Damphall Mine](/wiki/Skyrim:Damphall_Mine "Skyrim:Damphall Mine"), you'll find a small fishing camp with a [bristleback boar](/wiki/Skyrim:Bristleback "Skyrim:Bristleback"). The camp is mostly bare, consisting of a fishing rod, a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#0003452f "Skyrim:Boots"), one bottle each of [matze](/wiki/Skyrim:Matze "Skyrim:Matze") and [shein](/wiki/Skyrim:Shein "Skyrim:Shein"), one crate of dead [salmon](/wiki/Skyrim:Salmon_(dead) "Skyrim:Salmon (dead)") and one of [ash yams](/wiki/Skyrim:Ash_Yam "Skyrim:Ash Yam") and two [fish barrels](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Fish_Barrel "Skyrim:Containers"). You can also fish here with the fishing supplies. There is also an empty fishing boat nearby. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=21802&y=53072&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Waterfall and Grazing Netch

A herd of Netch can be found at the base of this waterfall that flows next to the Water Stone, along with a nirnroot, spiky grass and clams.

### Ashfallow Reaver Camp

Reaver Camp

Across the water west of [Ashfallow Citadel](/wiki/Skyrim:Ashfallow_Citadel "Skyrim:Ashfallow Citadel"), you'll find a small camp inhabited by two [reavers](/wiki/Skyrim:Reaver "Skyrim:Reaver"). The camp consists of a fire with two hanging pots—one of which contains a [red mountain flower](/wiki/Skyrim:Red_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Red Mountain Flower")—a chair, an apprentice-locked chest, and a sheltered bedroll. An East Empire Company strongbox can be found next to the bedroll. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=61902&y=40264&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Basalt Causeway

This is a tiny outcrop of basalt columns west of Tel Mithryn, and east of the mouth of the Harstrad River. During the quest Deathbrand, four or five Ash Spawn have slain the [two Treasure Hunters](/wiki/Skyrim:Treasure_Hunter_(dead) "Skyrim:Treasure Hunter (dead)") who uncovered the chest at this location. Pick the expert-leveled lock on the chest and claim the Deathbrand Boots.

### Bloodied Grove

Ash Spawn Ambush

Northwest of [Tel Mithryn](/wiki/Skyrim:Tel_Mithryn_(settlement) "Skyrim:Tel Mithryn (settlement)"), upstream from where [Revus Sarvani](/wiki/Skyrim:Revus_Sarvani "Skyrim:Revus Sarvani") tends to Dusty the [silt strider](/wiki/Skyrim:Silt_Strider "Skyrim:Silt Strider"), you will find a dead [reaver](/wiki/Skyrim:Reaver "Skyrim:Reaver") leaning up against a tree next to an unlocked chest, with three lootable skeletons scattered about. As you approach, three leveled [ash spawn](/wiki/Skyrim:Ash_Spawn "Skyrim:Ash Spawn") will emerge from the ground and attack. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=64316.6&y=33364.5&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Dragon Mound: Temple Foothills

Isinfier Dragon Mound

A [dragon burial mound](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Mounds "Skyrim:Dragon Mounds") can be found directly west of [Vahlok's Tomb](/wiki/Skyrim:Vahlok%27s_Tomb "Skyrim:Vahlok's Tomb"), atop some rocks. There is also a novice-locked chest just south of the mound, up the hill. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=61958&y=48899&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Driftwood Isle

This small tidal island can be found to the very west of [Bloodskal Barrow](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloodskal_Barrow "Skyrim:Bloodskal Barrow") and north of the Isle of Basalt. A lone tree can be found here alongside some driftwood and 3 samples of spiky grass.

### Dunmeri Camp

Dark Elf Camp

Far north of the [Sun Stone](/wiki/Skyrim:Sun_Stone "Skyrim:Sun Stone"), southwest of [Bujold's Retreat](/wiki/Skyrim:Bujold%27s_Retreat "Skyrim:Bujold's Retreat") (just east of [Vahlok's Tomb](/wiki/Skyrim:Vahlok%27s_Tomb "Skyrim:Vahlok's Tomb") if you have started [Lost Legacy](/wiki/Skyrim:Lost_Legacy "Skyrim:Lost Legacy")), you'll find a small camp where two [Dark Elves](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Elf_(dead) "Skyrim:Dark Elf (dead)") lie dead. The camp is mostly bare, consisting of a chest, a pot over a dry firepit, a barrel, and a knapsack, all of which are empty. The tent, however, contains an East Empire Company strongbox, as well as several bottles of [Nord mead](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord_Mead "Skyrim:Nord Mead"). You may encounter [Rieklings](/wiki/Skyrim:Riekling "Skyrim:Riekling") nearby. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=73573&y=49533&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Gold Miners' Floodgate

Usha's Camp

Follow the stream west of [Ashfallow Citadel](/wiki/Skyrim:Ashfallow_Citadel "Skyrim:Ashfallow Citadel") north to a barred gate. You can actually unbar the gate from the outside by aiming at the gap between the gates, but you won't be able to climb up onto the platforms once inside. It's tricky, but possible, to reach the lowermost platform by moving as far upstream as you can, facing the platform, and simultaneously pressing forward and jump. Instead of climbing the platforms inside, climb the mountain on the side and jump down to the wooden structure behind the gate. [Usha](/wiki/Skyrim:Usha "Skyrim:Usha") lies dead on a bedroll with a few empty skooma bottles strewn about his side. On the table next to him is a lantern, three bottles of skooma, a coin purse, and a [letter](/wiki/Skyrim:Letter_to_Usha "Skyrim:Letter to Usha") from his lover. On another table, you'll find six loose [gold ores](/wiki/Skyrim:Gold_Ore "Skyrim:Gold Ore"). To the right of this table is an unlocked chest with a pickaxe sitting on top, and an East Empire Company strongbox in front of it. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=65438&y=47265&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Hrodulf's Last Stand

Located on the southern tip of Solstheim, south of Hrodulf's House. [Hrodulf](/wiki/Skyrim:Hrodulf "Skyrim:Hrodulf") is a dead [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [farmer](/wiki/Skyrim:Farmer_(class) "Skyrim:Farmer (class)"), he was presumably killed by the [burnt spriggan](/wiki/Skyrim:Burnt_Spriggan "Skyrim:Burnt Spriggan") standing over his body.

Next to Hrodulf's body is an apprentice-locked strong box and a chest, both of which contain a decent amount of loot. There is an [amulet of Mara](/wiki/Skyrim:Amulet_of_Mara "Skyrim:Amulet of Mara") and a [gold diamond ring](/wiki/Skyrim:Gold_Diamond_Ring "Skyrim:Gold Diamond Ring") in the boat resting on one of the wharfs.

### Impact Crater

Two burnt spriggans are ready to fight if you tread on their ashen territory-an impact crater with a piece of molten rock from Red Mountain, still glowing. Two heart stone deposits can also be found here.

### Impact Crater (Tel Mithryn)

This impact crater has flame cloaked spiders and jumping flame spiders surrounding it, alongside burning trees. A heart stone deposit can also be found here.

### Isle of Basalt

A small island west of the [Earth Stone](/wiki/Skyrim:Earth_Stone "Skyrim:Earth Stone") that offers little but a great view of the southern parts of Solstheim. With the [Saints & Seducers](/wiki/Skyrim:Saints_%26_Seducers "Skyrim:Saints & Seducers") [Creation](/wiki/Skyrim:Creation_Club "Skyrim:Creation Club") installed, [green](/wiki/Skyrim:Green_Butterfly "Skyrim:Green Butterfly") and [purple butterflies](/wiki/Skyrim:Purple_Butterfly "Skyrim:Purple Butterfly") and [bliss bugs](/wiki/Skyrim:Bliss_Bug "Skyrim:Bliss Bug") can be found flying around the basalt columns during the day.

As part of the [Staff of Sheogorath](/wiki/Skyrim:Staff_of_Sheogorath "Skyrim:Staff of Sheogorath") Creation, you will be sent here during the quest [Put A Fork In It](/wiki/Skyrim:Put_A_Fork_In_It "Skyrim:Put A Fork In It") to kill a [bull netch](/wiki/Skyrim:Bull_Netch "Skyrim:Bull Netch").

### Lurker Ambush (Ashfallow Dunes)

As you complete the Dragonborn Main Quest, Lurkers begin to appear across Solstheim, usually close to the coast. Just southeast of Ashfallow Citadel, on the sand dunes north of the Sun Stone, is an area in which a Lurker can spawn.

### Lurker Ambush (Tel Mithryn Peninsula)

West of the Sun Stone, in a small bay immediately north of the small graveyard, two lurkers wait to arise from the sea. It can be difficult to trigger their emergence. A detect life spell will show them (in blue) below the surface of the water.

### Miner Immolation

Miner's End

Southeast of [Tel Mithryn](/wiki/Skyrim:Tel_Mithryn_(settlement) "Skyrim:Tel Mithryn (settlement)"), near the shore, you can find two levels of terrace. Two ash spawn will come out of the ground and attack you. You can find a [heart stone deposit](/wiki/Skyrim:Mining#Deposits "Skyrim:Mining") and a burnt corpse on each terrace. In addition, you can find a knapsack on the lower level, along with a pickaxe and a bottle of flin on the upper level. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=79025&y=21232&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Reavers' Folly

Ash Hopper Island

South of the [Earth Stone](/wiki/Skyrim:Earth_Stone "Skyrim:Earth Stone"), you'll find a small island with some crumbling ruins, guarded by three [ash hoppers](/wiki/Skyrim:Ash_Hopper "Skyrim:Ash Hopper"). The island features a small camp with two bloodied bed rolls (one of which can be used to sleep), a strongbox, a chest, an apothecary's satchel, a box with two bowls of [bone meal](/wiki/Skyrim:Bone_Meal "Skyrim:Bone Meal") and a pair of [bonemold gauntlets](/wiki/Skyrim:Bonemold_Gauntlets "Skyrim:Bonemold Gauntlets"), a food barrel, and two dead [reavers](/wiki/Skyrim:Reaver "Skyrim:Reaver"). There is a small boat on the southern side of the island, with a sack inside and a pickaxe on a crate nearby. Scattered throughout the island are three spiky grass plants which yield standard [grass pods](/wiki/Skyrim:Grass_Pod "Skyrim:Grass Pod"), as well as one [heart stone](/wiki/Skyrim:Heart_Stone "Skyrim:Heart Stone") deposit. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=27160&y=23006&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Spider Nest (Logs)

This spider nest is found northeast of Raven Rock, between two logs. Three albino spider eggs can be looted, creepcluster can also be found near the logs.

### Spider Nest (Kagrumez)

Spider Ambush

West-southwest of [Kagrumez](/wiki/Skyrim:Kagrumez "Skyrim:Kagrumez") you will find a broken tree stump with a burning log next to it. There are green, slimy pods stuck to the stump, the largest of which will burst open when you approach, releasing a [jumping flame spider](/wiki/Skyrim:Jumping_Flame_Spider_(creature) "Skyrim:Jumping Flame Spider (creature)"). There is a knapsack, an apothecary's satchel, and a [ruby geode](/wiki/Skyrim:Ruby_Geode "Skyrim:Ruby Geode") next to the stump. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=51931.2&y=48259.9&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Spriggan Grove

This grove contains two to three spriggans alongside a wild animal. Blue mountain flowers and scathecraw can be found in abundance here as well.

### The Miner's Lament

Miner Camp

Directly west of [Brodir Grove](/wiki/Skyrim:Brodir_Grove "Skyrim:Brodir Grove"), you can find a small camp where an unfortunate [Miner](/wiki/Skyrim:Miner "Skyrim:Miner") met his demise. He lies beneath a wagon and some loose rocks and four [gold](/wiki/Skyrim:Gold "Skyrim:Gold") ores. Next to his nearby tent is a crate holding a strongbox, an [emerald](/wiki/Skyrim:Emerald "Skyrim:Emerald"), an [apple pie](/wiki/Skyrim:Apple_Pie "Skyrim:Apple Pie"), and two bottles of [Nord mead](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord_Mead "Skyrim:Nord Mead"). Three more bottles of Nord mead are in an overturned bucket in front of the tent, along with a pair of boots. Just east of the miner's body are two emerald geodes and a gold ore vein behind them, and to the north and west of his body is a gold ore vein and two creep clusters which yield [ash creep clusters](/wiki/Skyrim:Ash_Creep_Cluster "Skyrim:Ash Creep Cluster"). [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=37533&y=44560&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Wilderness Shrine of Kynareth

Shrine of Kynareth

Travel to [Bloodskal Barrow](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloodskal_Barrow "Skyrim:Bloodskal Barrow"). Turn around and follow the coast south until you see a downed tree to your left. It will be near a large patch of grass, an [ash creep cluster](/wiki/Skyrim:Ash_Creep_Cluster "Skyrim:Ash Creep Cluster"), and three [scathecraw](/wiki/Skyrim:Scathecraw "Skyrim:Scathecraw"). There should be a small gap between the tree and the rock just to its right (west). The [Earth Stone](/wiki/Skyrim:Earth_Stone "Skyrim:Earth Stone") should be almost directly south from your position. If you look south into the rocky hills, you will see a trail marker cairn. Head toward the marker to find a second trail marker cairn; look a few feet east of that second cairn to find the shrine on top of a lantern. Beside the shrine are an [amulet of Kynareth](/wiki/Skyrim:Amulet_of_Kynareth "Skyrim:Amulet of Kynareth"), [flin](/wiki/Skyrim:Flin "Skyrim:Flin"), two [trama roots](/wiki/Skyrim:Trama_Root "Skyrim:Trama Root"), three [ash yams](/wiki/Skyrim:Ash_Yam "Skyrim:Ash Yam"), and an [albino spider pod](/wiki/Skyrim:Albino_Spider_Pod "Skyrim:Albino Spider Pod"). [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=23822&y=39380&zoom=16&legacy=true)

### Chest and Gold

Chest and coin purses

Following the path from [Bujold's Retreat](/wiki/Skyrim:Bujold%27s_Retreat "Skyrim:Bujold's Retreat") towards [Thirsk Mead Hall](/wiki/Skyrim:Thirsk_Mead_Hall "Skyrim:Thirsk Mead Hall"), soon after turning to the southwest, you pass four [scathecraw](/wiki/Skyrim:Scathecraw "Skyrim:Scathecraw") plants on your right. Turn to the north, passing three trees on your left and a tall stump on your right. Right after passing a bush, you should be able to see the unlocked chest and two large [coin purses](/wiki/Skyrim:Coin_Purse "Skyrim:Coin Purse") beside it, lying between rocks. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=79794&y=63375&zoom=17&legacy=true)

### Chest and Satchel

Chest and satchel

Follow a precipitous path east-southeast of [Bloodskal Barrow](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloodskal_Barrow "Skyrim:Bloodskal Barrow"), and you will find an unlocked large chest under two trees, plus a satchel at the base of one of the trees (see picture). Keep moving up to find an [emerald geode](/wiki/Skyrim:Mining#Emerald_Geode "Skyrim:Mining"), along with a gold ore vein. Another emerald geode is to the east. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&x=29593&y=45238&zoom=16&legacy=true)

# Skyrim:Unmarked Places

## Overview

|  |
| --- |
| **This page or section is incomplete. You can help by adding to it.** Needs official Prima Guide location names + new Creation Club locations, copyedit for wording, grammar and spelling For more information, see the [help files](/wiki/Help:Contents "Help:Contents"), the [style guide](/wiki/UESPWiki:Style_Guide "UESPWiki:Style Guide"), and this article's [talk page](https://en.uesp.net/wiki/Skyrim_talk:Unmarked_Places). |

Overview | [Eastmarch](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Eastmarch "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Eastmarch") — [Falkreath](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Falkreath "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Falkreath") — [Haafingar](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Haafingar "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Haafingar") — [Hjaalmarch](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Hjaalmarch "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Hjaalmarch") — [The Pale](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_The_Pale "Skyrim:Unmarked Places The Pale") — [The Reach](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_The_Reach "Skyrim:Unmarked Places The Reach") — [The Rift](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_The_Rift "Skyrim:Unmarked Places The Rift") — [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Whiterun "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Whiterun") — [Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Winterhold "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Winterhold") — [Solstheim](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Solstheim "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Solstheim")

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| 26 — [Eastmarch](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Eastmarch "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Eastmarch")  ## — [Falkreath](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Falkreath "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Falkreath")  11 — [Haafingar](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Haafingar "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Haafingar")  ## — [Hjallmarh](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Hjaalmarch "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Hjaalmarch")  ## — [The Pale](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_The_Pale "Skyrim:Unmarked Places The Pale") | ## — [The Reach](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_The_Reach "Skyrim:Unmarked Places The Reach")  ## — [The Rift](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_The_Rift "Skyrim:Unmarked Places The Rift")   32 — [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Whiterun "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Whiterun")   ## — [Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Winterhold "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Winterhold")  ## — [Solstheim](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Solstheim "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Solstheim") |

This overview article describes places of interest in Skyrim that are not marked on your world map upon discovery, are not represented by [icons on your compass](/wiki/Skyrim:Maps#Icons "Skyrim:Maps"), thus cannot be [fast traveled](/wiki/Skyrim:Transport#Fast_Travel "Skyrim:Transport") to, and in most cases are not directly related to any [quests](/wiki/Skyrim:Quests "Skyrim:Quests"). Some of the places on this list do not have official names or titles, in which case unofficial names have been used to describe the places.

Each location listed in the table here is discussed in detail, with images, on the unmarked locations pages for the regions linked in the menu above.

This sortable table features ALL unmarked locations in the game. Only exterior, unmarked [shrines](/wiki/Skyrim:Shrines "Skyrim:Shrines") are included, not those inside locations or at locations with map markers. The secondary location label is as listed in the [Prima Guide](/wiki/Books:The_Elder_Scrolls_V:_Skyrim:_Prima_Official_Game_Guide "Books:The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim: Prima Official Game Guide"), for example 8.D is A Peddler's Misfortune. Location types include Hostile (bandit/forsworn/hagraven/necromancer/etc), Creature, Camp (hunter/fisher/empty/etc), Caravan, Boat (afloat/wrecked), Shrine, House, Dragon Mound, Jail, Ruin/monument, just Loot, or Other place of interest.
TEMPORARY EXAMPLE TABLE :

|  |  | Closest Map Marker(s) | Region | Type | Spell Tome | Skill Book | Unique Item | Quest | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [A Peddler's Misfortune](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Falkreath#A_Peddler.27s_Misfortune "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Falkreath") [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-59152&y=-31703&zoom=17&legacy=true) | 8.D | [Fort Sungard](/wiki/Skyrim:Fort_Sungard "Skyrim:Fort Sungard"),  [Sunderstone Gorge](/wiki/Skyrim:Sunderstone_Gorge "Skyrim:Sunderstone Gorge") | [Falkreath](/wiki/Skyrim:Falkreath "Skyrim:Falkreath") | Caravan |  | ☑ |  |  | dead Khajiit toppled cart |
| [Alchemist's Camp: Evergreen Woods](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Falkreath#Alchemist.27s_Camp:_Evergreen_Woods "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Falkreath") [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-50719&y=-62713&zoom=17&legacy=true) | 8.K | [Bannermist Tower](/wiki/Skyrim:Bannermist_Tower "Skyrim:Bannermist Tower"),  [Evergreen Grove](/wiki/Skyrim:Evergreen_Grove "Skyrim:Evergreen Grove") | [Falkreath](/wiki/Skyrim:Falkreath "Skyrim:Falkreath") | Camp |  | ☑ |  |  | unoccupied alchemist camp by a small pond |
| [Bandit Camp: Helgen Cliffs](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Falkreath#Bandit_Camp:_Helgen_Cliffs "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Falkreath") [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=4240&y=-78883&zoom=17&legacy=true) | 8.AE | [Helgen](/wiki/Skyrim:Helgen "Skyrim:Helgen") | [Falkreath](/wiki/Skyrim:Falkreath "Skyrim:Falkreath") | Hostile |  | ☑ |  |  | although it initially appears abandoned, 3 bandits camp here |
| [Bandit Camp: Ilinalta Foothills](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Falkreath#Bandit_Camp:_Ilinalta_Foothills "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Falkreath") [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=3195&y=-66333&zoom=17&legacy=true) | 8.X | [Guardian Stones](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Guardian_Stones "Skyrim:The Guardian Stones") | [Falkreath](/wiki/Skyrim:Falkreath "Skyrim:Falkreath") | Hostile |  | ☑ |  |  | one of the 3 bandits carries *[Treasure Map I](/wiki/Skyrim:Treasure_Map_I "Skyrim:Treasure Map I")* |
| (... cont ...) |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

## Bandit Camps and Bandit Ambushes

- FALKREATH Bandit Camp: Ilinalta Foothills ----- On the road between [Helgen](/wiki/Skyrim:Helgen "Skyrim:Helgen") and [Riverwood](/wiki/Skyrim:Riverwood "Skyrim:Riverwood"), just south of the [Guardian Stones](/wiki/Skyrim:Guardian_Stones "Skyrim:Guardian Stones") and east of a Shrine of Talos, there is a camp inhabited by three leveled [bandits](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit "Skyrim:Bandit"). It contains a chest, [Treasure Map I](/wiki/Skyrim:Treasure_Map_I "Skyrim:Treasure Map I"), and a copy of the [One-handed](/wiki/Skyrim:One-handed "Skyrim:One-handed") skill book *[Night Falls on Sentinel](/wiki/Skyrim:Night_Falls_on_Sentinel "Skyrim:Night Falls on Sentinel")*. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=3760&y=-65659&zoom=6.288&legacy=true)
- THE PALE 2 mixed up (Dawnstar Frost Troll Den / Shoreline Bandit Camp) ----- Between [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar") and [Pale Imperial Camp](/wiki/Skyrim:Pale_Imperial_Camp "Skyrim:Pale Imperial Camp"), there is a camp inhabited by three leveled bandits. It has a chest, food barrels and a copy of the [Block](/wiki/Skyrim:Block "Skyrim:Block") skill book *[Death Blow of Abernanit](/wiki/Skyrim:Death_Blow_of_Abernanit "Skyrim:Death Blow of Abernanit")*. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=10657&y=99519&zoom=6.288&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH Khajiit Caravan Massacre ----- On the road between Helgen and [Haemar's Shame](/wiki/Skyrim:Haemar%27s_Shame "Skyrim:Haemar's Shame") is a wrecked Khajiit caravan, complete with two carts, seven dead Khajiit and two dead horses. In the furthest cart are some sacks and a chest, as well as a copy of the [Speech](/wiki/Skyrim:Speech "Skyrim:Speech") skill book, *[The Buying Game](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Buying_Game "Skyrim:The Buying Game")*. When you approach from Helgen, multiple bandits will ambush you, usually consisting of one or two melee fighters and two archers. If you head up the road over the hill and then turn around and come back, another three-four bandits will spawn around the wreckage and attack. Note that this ambush only spawns if you head directly there after exiting Helgen. If you went through Helgen Keep with Hadvar, you can go to Riverwood, recruit Faendal, and have him help you battle the bandits and collect the skill book for you, but only if you do not enter Alvor's house. If you went through Helgen Keep with Ralof, the bandits and caravan will disappear as soon as you enter Riverwood or another village or city. If you've already spawned the ambush previously, by the time the nearby Stormcloak camp spawns (which happens only after visiting a major city), the caravan remains will have been cleared up and all the bandits will respawn, sitting around the stump just off the road to the right. They will be non-hostile unless spoken to, or outright attacked. x=37220&y=-79480 When the [Falkreath Stormcloak Camp](/wiki/Skyrim:Falkreath_Stormcloak_Camp "Skyrim:Falkreath Stormcloak Camp") is enabled for the first time it permanently disables an unmarked place of a Khajiit Caravan Massacre south-southwest with a skill book The Buying Game.

## Caravan Remains and Shipwrecks

- HJAALMARCH Ambushed Caravan ----- On the side of the road southeast of Dragon Bridge is a caravan loaded with crates, rugs, and a knapsack. Next to it are the corpses of a horse, a Nord man, and a Redguard woman, the latter carrying the *[Merchant's Journal](/wiki/Skyrim:Merchant%27s_Journal "Skyrim:Merchant's Journal")*. [Falmer arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Falmer_Arrow "Skyrim:Falmer Arrow") stuck in the horse, other Falmer arrows around the location, and a [Falmer ear](/wiki/Skyrim:Falmer_Ear "Skyrim:Falmer Ear") suggest a [Falmer](/wiki/Skyrim:Falmer "Skyrim:Falmer") attack, possibly related to the nearby [Chillwind Depths](/wiki/Skyrim:Chillwind_Depths "Skyrim:Chillwind Depths"). [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-92152&y=67427&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH The Silvermoon: Lake Ilinalta ----- South of [The Lady Stone](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Lady_Stone "Skyrim:The Lady Stone") is a shipwreck beneath the waters of Lake Ilinalta. The shipwreck contains a chest and a leveled weapon. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-32600&y=-59576&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- WINTERHOLD ----- Northeast of the [Wreck of the Winter War](/wiki/Skyrim:Wreck_of_the_Winter_War "Skyrim:Wreck of the Winter War") are the sparse remains of ship. Among the oars and wood are three barrels. A short distance to the north is a birds' nest with a [pine thrush egg](/wiki/Skyrim:Pine_Thrush_Egg "Skyrim:Pine Thrush Egg"). [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=184352&y=74240&zoom=15&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH Burning Caravan: Evergreen Grove ----- East of [Knifepoint Ridge](/wiki/Skyrim:Knifepoint_Ridge "Skyrim:Knifepoint Ridge") and west of the Evergreen Woods dragon mound, there are remnants of a caravan loaded with crates, rugs, and a chest. Next to it are the corpses of a horse and two burned humanoids. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-62808&y=-70289&zoom=7.964&legacy=true)

## Creature Lairs

- FALKREATH ----- Southeast of [Evergreen Grove](/wiki/Skyrim:Evergreen_Grove "Skyrim:Evergreen Grove") is a bugling elk near a stony Nordic bust. A hostile animal often wanders into the area. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-46997&y=-70386&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- THE PALE Barnacle Boat ----- On the coast between [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar") and the [Wreck Of The Brinehammer](/wiki/Skyrim:Wreck_Of_The_Brinehammer "Skyrim:Wreck Of The Brinehammer"), there is an upturned skiff inhabited by two mudcrabs and containing a skeleton hand, a knapsack, a scimitar, two pieces of mudcrab chitin, and a copy of the [Alteration](/wiki/Skyrim:Alteration "Skyrim:Alteration") skill book *[Daughter of the Niben](/wiki/Skyrim:Daughter_of_the_Niben "Skyrim:Daughter of the Niben")*. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=13463&y=108099&zoom=6.288&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH Bear Cave: Halldir's Cairn ----- One or two bears lair WNW of Halldir's Cairn under a rocky overhang. The corpses of two hunters and an elk are found on the site, and beehives can be found in the trees nearby.
- FALKREATH Sunken Barrow: Lake Ilinalta ----- Underwater in the east end of Lake Ilinalta, southwest of Anise's Cabin, there is a sunken ruin where two slaughterfish reside. The ruins also contain a leveled mace, two soul gems in a stone bowl, and an unlocked chest under the stairs.

## Houses

- HJAALMARCH ----- Northeast of [Ustengrav](/wiki/Skyrim:Ustengrav "Skyrim:Ustengrav") and north of [Hjaalmarch Stormcloak Camp](/wiki/Skyrim:Hjaalmarch_Stormcloak_Camp "Skyrim:Hjaalmarch Stormcloak Camp"), there is a ruined, burning shack. Fire continuously burns the previous occupant and remaining furniture. Before dragons are activated, this shack is home to a single bandit. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-13853&y=92090&zoom=6.288&legacy=true)
- THE RIFT Burning Farmhouse ----- In a valley south of [Forelhost](/wiki/Skyrim:Forelhost "Skyrim:Forelhost") there is another burning shack where fire continuously burns the previous occupant and remaining furniture. A scroll of [Conjure Flame Atronach](/wiki/Skyrim:Conjure_Flame_Atronach "Skyrim:Conjure Flame Atronach") can be found here, indicating a possible cause of the fire. Some potatoes and leeks may be harvested from the untouched garden. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=189636&y=-122036&zoom=6.288&legacy=true)
- THE PALE ----- With [Fishing](/wiki/Skyrim:Fishing "Skyrim:Fishing") installed, there is a hut on the coast west of [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar") which contains a bed, various furniture, fishing supplies, and other supplies. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=12462&y=109512&zoom=8&legacy=true)

## Hunter's Camps

- FALKREATH Hunter's Camp: The Guardian Stones ----- There is a downhill path west of the [Guardian Stones](/wiki/Skyrim:Guardian_Stones "Skyrim:Guardian Stones") which leads to a fisherman's camp. With [Fishing](/wiki/Skyrim:Fishing "Skyrim:Fishing") installed, this site becomes a [fishing spot](/wiki/Skyrim:Fishing_(activity) "Skyrim:Fishing (activity)"). [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-2900&y=-61566&zoom=7.801&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH Fisherman's Island: Lake Ilinalta ----- On a small island in the eastern part of [Lake Ilinalta](/wiki/Skyrim:Lake_Ilinalta "Skyrim:Lake Ilinalta"), there is another fisherman's camp. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-17343&y=-60775&zoom=7.801&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH Fisherman's Camp: Lake Ilinalta ----- On the northwestern part of Lake Ilinalta, there is another camp belonging to a fisherman. This camp contains a copy of the One-handed skill book *[Fire and Darkness](/wiki/Skyrim:Fire_and_Darkness "Skyrim:Fire and Darkness")*. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-42436&y=-47165&zoom=7.731&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH Hunter's Camp: Sunderstone Gorge ----- West of [Sunderstone Gorge](/wiki/Skyrim:Sunderstone_Gorge "Skyrim:Sunderstone Gorge") lies a hunter's camp. It consists of a chest, a sack, a small tent with a bedroll, and a hunter's bow with 12 iron arrows. One hunter lives here. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-63074&y=-40210&zoom=8&legacy=true)
- THE RIFT Hunters' Camp: Autumnshade Hills ----- Southeast of [Autumnshade Clearing](/wiki/Skyrim:Autumnshade_Clearing "Skyrim:Autumnshade Clearing") is a camp occupied by a pair of hunters. One of their tents contains a copy of the [Archery](/wiki/Skyrim:Archery "Skyrim:Archery") skill book *[The Gold Ribbon of Merit](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Gold_Ribbon_of_Merit "Skyrim:The Gold Ribbon of Merit")*. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=158381&y=-83993&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- WINTERHOLD Hunter's Overlook: Fellhammer Wastes ----- Due south of [The White Hall](/wiki/Skyrim:The_White_Hall "Skyrim:The White Hall") in Dawnstar and southeast of the [Shrine of Dibella](/wiki/Skyrim:Shrines#Watching_Dawnstar "Skyrim:Shrines") is a small camp occupied by two hunters. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=35330&y=83811&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- HJAALMARCH Single Hunter Camp ----- 290 ft N of [North Cold Rock Pass](/wiki/Skyrim:North_Cold_Rock_Pass "Skyrim:North Cold Rock Pass"), is an unmarked hunter encampment, occupied by a single hunter. Under the tent is a copy of the [Archery](/wiki/Skyrim:Archery "Skyrim:Archery") skill book *The Gold Ribbon of Merit*. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-47237&y=43306&zoom=17&legacy=true)

## Warlock Altars

- FALKREATH Sacrificial Altar: Evergreen Woods ----- Halfway between [Cracked Tusk Keep](/wiki/Skyrim:Cracked_Tusk_Keep "Skyrim:Cracked Tusk Keep") and [Evergreen Grove](/wiki/Skyrim:Evergreen_Grove "Skyrim:Evergreen Grove") is a Nordic altar occupied by a [necromancer](/wiki/Skyrim:Necromancer "Skyrim:Necromancer"). The altar holds two [poisons](/wiki/Skyrim:Poison "Skyrim:Poison") and a copy of the [Conjuration](/wiki/Skyrim:Conjuration "Skyrim:Conjuration") [skill book](/wiki/Skyrim:Skill_Books "Skyrim:Skill Books") *[2920, Frostfall, v10](/wiki/Skyrim:2920,_Frostfall,_v10 "Skyrim:2920, Frostfall, v10")*.
- FALKREATH The Conjurer's Altar: Lake Ilinalta ----- North of [Lakeview Manor](/wiki/Skyrim:Lakeview_Manor "Skyrim:Lakeview Manor"), there is a Nordic altar occupied by a necromancer. This site contains embalming tools, a sword, a burial urn, and the Conjuration skill book *[2920, Hearth Fire, v9](/wiki/Skyrim:2920,_Hearth_Fire,_v9 "Skyrim:2920, Hearth Fire, v9")*. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-7964&y=-66405&zoom=7.512&legacy=true)

## Smaller Places of Interest

- FALKREATH ----- South of [South Brittleshin Pass](/wiki/Skyrim:South_Brittleshin_Pass "Skyrim:South Brittleshin Pass") is an empty dock. With the [Fishing](/wiki/Skyrim:Fishing "Skyrim:Fishing") creation, some [fishing supplies](/wiki/Skyrim:Fishing_Supplies "Skyrim:Fishing Supplies") can be found here. Fish from [Temperate Lakes](/wiki/Skyrim:Fishing_(activity)#Biomes "Skyrim:Fishing (activity)") can be caught here. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-18816&y=-54387&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- THE RIFT ----- Northwest of [Fallowstone Cave](/wiki/Skyrim:Fallowstone_Cave "Skyrim:Fallowstone Cave"), partway up the mountain slope, there is an unmarked clearing with a [silver ore](/wiki/Skyrim:Silver_Ore "Skyrim:Silver Ore") vein and an [orichalcum ore](/wiki/Skyrim:Orichalcum_Ore "Skyrim:Orichalcum Ore") vein. A copy of the [Illusion](/wiki/Skyrim:Illusion "Skyrim:Illusion") skill book *[Incident at Necrom](/wiki/Skyrim:Incident_at_Necrom "Skyrim:Incident at Necrom")* is well-hidden at the base of one of the trees on that slope. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=189522&y=-79771&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH ----- West-southwest of [Knifepoint Ridge](/wiki/Skyrim:Knifepoint_Ridge "Skyrim:Knifepoint Ridge"), if you climb the ridge to the top, you'll find a skeleton with a blood-spattered knapsack and leveled bow lying next to it. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-73485&y=-69980&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- THE REACH ----- Between [Reachwater Rock](/wiki/Skyrim:Reachwater_Rock "Skyrim:Reachwater Rock") and [Kolskeggr Mine](/wiki/Skyrim:Kolskeggr_Mine "Skyrim:Kolskeggr Mine") there's an abandoned rowboat at a bend in the river. In the boat are some bottles, an apothecary's satchel, an adept-locked strongbox, and one each of the following gems: an emerald, a garnet, a flawless garnet, a ruby, a flawless ruby, and a flawless diamond. In the river next to the boat is a chest and two mead barrels. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-145818&y=4291&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- THE REACH Brush Strongbox: Riverside ----- Southeast of [Karthwasten](/wiki/Skyrim:Karthwasten "Skyrim:Karthwasten"), at a point roughly between [Four Skull Lookout](/wiki/Skyrim:Four_Skull_Lookout "Skyrim:Four Skull Lookout") and [Sundered Towers](/wiki/Skyrim:Sundered_Towers "Skyrim:Sundered Towers"), there is a locked strongbox with a leveled dagger leaned up against it hidden in the center of a pile of driftwood. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-128320&y=31744&zoom=17&legacy=true)

A tattered flag and an unfortunate soul

- THE REACH The Bloodied Bandit ----- In the mountains almost due east of [Ragnvald](/wiki/Skyrim:Ragnvald "Skyrim:Ragnvald"), south of [Bthardamz](/wiki/Skyrim:Bthardamz "Skyrim:Bthardamz"), a dead [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") bandit can be found draped over a novice-locked chest containing minor loot. A random one-handed weapon is beside it, and another one is carried by the Orc. Two dead [wolves](/wiki/Skyrim:Wolf "Skyrim:Wolf") next to him hint at his cause of death. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-166352&y=30998&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH / THE RIFT ----- Between [Haemar's Shame](/wiki/Skyrim:Haemar%27s_Shame "Skyrim:Haemar's Shame") and [Alchemist's Shack](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemist%27s_Shack "Skyrim:Alchemist's Shack"), off the mountain pass between [Helgen](/wiki/Skyrim:Helgen "Skyrim:Helgen") and the western lands of [the Rift](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Rift "Skyrim:The Rift"), a pile of stones marks a steep, narrow path heading east from the road up through a small notch in the rock walls of the pass. A [quicksilver](/wiki/Skyrim:Quicksilver "Skyrim:Quicksilver") ore deposit and a copy of the Alteration skill book *[Daughter of the Niben](/wiki/Skyrim:Daughter_of_the_Niben "Skyrim:Daughter of the Niben")* can be found at the end of this path. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=63028.8&y=-80338.8&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH ----- West-northwest of Haemar's Shame and east of [Falkreath Stormcloak Camp](/wiki/Skyrim:Falkreath_Stormcloak_Camp "Skyrim:Falkreath Stormcloak Camp") is an overhanging rock face. Underneath lies a skeleton with a coin purse, along with a few animal bones. There are two [snowberry](/wiki/Skyrim:Snowberries "Skyrim:Snowberries") bushes here as well. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=43901&y=-74187&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- THE REACH ----- East of [Bruca's Leap Redoubt](/wiki/Skyrim:Bruca%27s_Leap_Redoubt "Skyrim:Bruca's Leap Redoubt") and southwest of [Dragon Bridge Overlook](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Bridge_Overlook "Skyrim:Dragon Bridge Overlook") at the bottom of the middle waterfall is an apprentice-locked [dungeon boss chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Chest "Skyrim:Containers"). It's near a fallen tree crossing the river. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-118397&y=64158&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- THE RIFT ----- Southeast of [Angarvunde](/wiki/Skyrim:Angarvunde "Skyrim:Angarvunde") and northwest of [Avanchnzel](/wiki/Skyrim:Avanchnzel "Skyrim:Avanchnzel") is a lean-to sheltering an apprentice-locked strongbox, two lockpicks, and two leveled potions (healing and invisibility). Two canis roots grow next to it. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=116739&y=-102370&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- HJAALMARCH ----- Northwest of [Orotheim](/wiki/Skyrim:Orotheim "Skyrim:Orotheim") on the river shore in a tiny alcove is a lootable skeleton next to a novice-locked common chest. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=-70629&y=38086&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH ----- Southwest, next to [Orphan Rock](/wiki/Skyrim:Orphan_Rock "Skyrim:Orphan Rock") is a lootable skeleton, helm, and coin purse in a hollow fallen tree. Mora tapinella grows on the tree. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=35387&y=-71170&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- WINTERHOLD ----- On an island between the [Wreck of The Pride of Tel Vos](/wiki/Skyrim:Wreck_of_The_Pride_of_Tel_Vos "Skyrim:Wreck of The Pride of Tel Vos") and [Bleakcoast Cave](/wiki/Skyrim:Bleakcoast_Cave "Skyrim:Bleakcoast Cave") is a helmet inside the trunk of a hollow fallen tree along with the Heavy Armor skill book *[Chimarvamidium](/wiki/Skyrim:Chimarvamidium "Skyrim:Chimarvamidium")*. [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=142307&y=100264&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- THE RIFT ----- Northeast of [Ivarstead](/wiki/Skyrim:Ivarstead "Skyrim:Ivarstead") and southwest of [Darkwater Crossing](/wiki/Skyrim:Darkwater_Crossing "Skyrim:Darkwater Crossing"), north of Nilheim, on the southern shore of the river is a dungeon chest. That section of the river has a keyhole shape and is at the base of a waterfall. Skill book *[Aevar Stone-Singer](/wiki/Skyrim:Aevar_Stone-Singer "Skyrim:Aevar Stone-Singer")* is almost due north of and very close to this location (as described above). [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?x=100975&y=-51434&zoom=17&legacy=true)
- FALKREATH ----- Southeast from [South Skybound Watch](/wiki/Skyrim:South_Skybound_Watch "Skyrim:South Skybound Watch"), at the base of a large tree surrounded by boulders and rocks is a skull with a [red mountain flower](/wiki/Skyrim:Red_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Red Mountain Flower") in its mouth, perched atop an apothecary's satchel.

# Skyrim:Unseen Visions

## Overview

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [SR-qico-Dawnguard Dawnguard.png](/wiki/File:SR-qico-Dawnguard_Dawnguard.png) | **Perform a ceremony to read the Elder Scroll.** |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Quest Giver: | [Dexion](/wiki/Skyrim:Dexion_Evicus "Skyrim:Dexion Evicus") |
| Location(s): | [Ancestor Glade](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancestor_Glade "Skyrim:Ancestor Glade") |
| Reward: | None |
| ID: | DLC1VQ06 |
| --- | |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | **← Previous** [Seeking Disclosure](/wiki/Skyrim:Seeking_Disclosure "Skyrim:Seeking Disclosure") | **Next →** [Touching the Sky](/wiki/Skyrim:Touching_the_Sky "Skyrim:Touching the Sky") | | |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | ← Previous | [Seeking Disclosure](/wiki/Skyrim:Seeking_Disclosure "Skyrim:Seeking Disclosure") | | → Next | [Touching the Sky](/wiki/Skyrim:Touching_the_Sky "Skyrim:Touching the Sky") | | |

A Moth Priest's knife

## Quick Walkthrough

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [Attention.svg](/wiki/File:Attention.svg) | Make a save or rely on the most recent auto-save before entering the glade as a series of scripts need to be triggered, but can easily fail. Leaving and re-entering the glade may correct some problems, but script failures can cascade, so redoing the glade from the last save is preferable. See [bugs](#Bugs) for more information on possible issues. |

1. Find a Moth Priest's [draw knife](/wiki/Skyrim:Draw_Knife "Skyrim:Draw Knife").
2. Gather [bark](/wiki/Skyrim:Canticle_Bark "Skyrim:Canticle Bark") from a Canticle Tree.
3. Attract [Ancestor Moth](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancestor_Moth "Skyrim:Ancestor Moth") Swarms.
4. Read the Elder Scroll.
5. Speak to [Serana](/wiki/Skyrim:Serana "Skyrim:Serana").

## Detailed Walkthrough

A Canticle Tree

Ancestor Moths surrounding the player

### Finding Ancestor Glade

As instructed by [Dexion Evicus](/wiki/Skyrim:Dexion_Evicus "Skyrim:Dexion Evicus"), your first task is to acquire a Moth Priest's [draw knife](/wiki/Skyrim:Draw_Knife "Skyrim:Draw Knife") from the [Ancestor Glade](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancestor_Glade "Skyrim:Ancestor Glade") northeast of [Falkreath](/wiki/Skyrim:Falkreath "Skyrim:Falkreath").

Take the eastern road out of Falkreath and at the first four-way intersection you come across, head northeast. Soon after negotiating the [bandit bridge](/wiki/Skyrim:Unmarked_Places_Falkreath#Bandit_Bridge:_Pinewatch "Skyrim:Unmarked Places Falkreath") guarding the approach to [Pinewatch](/wiki/Skyrim:Pinewatch "Skyrim:Pinewatch"), turn south onto a narrow mountain trail. After climbing for a while you will come across a bandit camp; from here press on to the southwest and continue climbing high into the mountain. The entrance to the cave is marked by a series of cairns.

Reading the Elder Scrolls

The vision

### Ancestor Glade

If you told [Serana](/wiki/Skyrim:Serana "Skyrim:Serana") to leave you, she will be back with you once you enter the cave. Head deeper into the cave, up a ledge and across a fallen tree trunk until you emerge into a large hidden clearing. Having been initially unimpressed by the glade, Serana will now gasp in astonishment at the sight before you. Descend down the path towards the stream bisecting the carvern; in particular, make for the glowing tree on the eastern side, just by some rapids. Here you will find a ring-shaped stone with a draw knife lodged inside it.

### Using the Draw Knife

Activate the stone to acquire the draw knife (note that from this point a gate bars your exit from the cavern) and use it on the canticle tree just to the north. Once you have obtained some bark, you will need to attract seven Ancestor Moth swarms. There are swarms moving all across the Glade, which you can attract by standing next to them for some time. As soon as you have seven swarms following you, a shaft of unnatural light will illuminate the stone knife holder. Return there and read the [Elder Scroll (Blood)](/wiki/Skyrim:Elder_Scroll_(Blood) "Skyrim:Elder Scroll (Blood)"), which will actually cause you to read all three scrolls.

### Reading the Elder Scrolls

After reading the Scrolls, you have a vision of a glowing map with lines for rivers and with two notable features glowing red: in the lower left the ram of Markarth and in the upper right the wolf of Solitude. The location of Auriel's Bow is shown by the small white symbol, similar to the Elder glyph , near the head of the river in the top-left quarter of the map.

### Talk To Serana

Once the vision fades, Serana will immediately come over and ask if everything is alright. She will ask about [Auriel's Bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Auriel%27s_Bow "Skyrim:Auriel's Bow")'s location and you will be able to point her to [Darkfall Cave](/wiki/Skyrim:Darkfall_Cave "Skyrim:Darkfall Cave") thanks to your vision, thus completing the quest. As you leave the cavern, you will be ambushed by Dawnguard Soldiers or Vampires, depending on which faction you have sided with.

## Notes

- Serana will automatically become a [follower](/wiki/Skyrim:Followers "Skyrim:Followers") at the start of this quest. Your other follower is automatically dismissed upon entering Ancestor Glade.
  - If you had a second follower due to the soul cairn loophole it may be possible to keep the second follower by telling them to 'wait here' while outside Ancestor Glade. You must do this before entering otherwise the second follower will be dismissed. Once the quest is completed, exit Ancestor Glade and your second follower will still be outside. Simply tell your second follower to follow you again. This does not always work.
  - Another way to keep your second follower is to sprint past them or make sure they take a long route getting to Ancestor Glade. As soon as you enter whirlwind sprint through the entrance into the clearing. The point where your follower is required to part is scripted around half way down the entrance to the glade and by using the shout it can be avoided altogether. It may take a few tries, so save the game before attempting. If you succeed here the second slot will be permanent unless you dismiss Serana. Even if you can't get it to work there is another opportunity during Touching the Sky.
- None of the Elder Scrolls can be removed from your inventory.
  - When on the [Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard_(faction) "Skyrim:Dawnguard (faction)")'s side, it is possible to sell two Elder Scrolls to [Dexion Evicus](/wiki/Skyrim:Dexion_Evicus "Skyrim:Dexion Evicus").
  - The [Elder Scroll (Dragon)](/wiki/Skyrim:Elder_Scroll_(Dragon) "Skyrim:Elder Scroll (Dragon)") can be sold to [Urag gro-Shub](/wiki/Skyrim:Urag_gro-Shub "Skyrim:Urag gro-Shub"), but only if you did not buy it back from him.
- If you transform into different form (Vampire, Werewolf) while gathering moths, all existing moths will disappear around you, but will not interfere with the ongoing quest count.

## Bugs

- Serana may not have entered the glade with you and is shut out behind the barred gate.
  - To fix this, you can wait a couple of hours and she will appear next to you or you can reload and make sure she follows you into the cave (possibly needing to use Unrelenting Force) or while looking at the closed bars, move to the left and glitch outside of the level by walking into the wall, then jumping over to the area on the other side of the barred doorway and go back inside the level. Alternatively, you can try to give move commands to Serana by holding down the interact button while facing her until you can command her.
- After obtaining the Draw Knife, you may not be able to harvest the bark from the Canticle Trees.
- You may be able to harvest from the canticle trees, but the objective for attracting moths will never appear.
  -  Use the console command `SetStage DLC1VQ06 30` to set the objective to gathering moths.
- The barred gate may not shut.
- You may not be able to read the scroll.
  -  Add a second copy of the Elder Scroll to your inventory with `player.additem xx0118F9 1`. You end up with two copies, but should be able to read the extra scroll.
- The post scroll reading attack may not occur.
- The gate may not reopen. This can occur if you fail to speak with Serana before approaching the gate.
  - If Serana is in combat mode, kill all the enemies on the far side of the gate (which can be a challenge, since they often move out of range), then speak with her and the gate will open.
  -  Alternatively, select Serana in the console, move to the far side of the cavern from the gate, then use `moveto player` followed by `stopcombat`. This should allow you to speak to Serana and the quest will proceed normally after that.
  -  The gate can be removed by opening the console, selecting the gate with the mouse, then using the `disable` command. This will allow the enemies in if they're trapped on the other side, and/or allow you to escape.
- The vampire or Dawnguard attackers who get enabled are never disabled again after the quest is over. This leads to some of them respawning back in Ancestor Glade later when they aren't supposed to.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Legendary Edition Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Legendary_Edition_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Legendary Edition Patch"), version 3.0.3, fixes this bug.
- Serana may not engage in dialogue after the battle.

## Quest Stages

| Unseen Visions (DLC1VQ06) | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Stage | Finishes Quest | Journal Entry |
| 10 |  | After discovering that Dexion has become blind, I've had to secure alternate means to decipher the Elder Scrolls and locate Auriel's Bow. It's been suggested that I might be able to read the Elder Scrolls myself if I use an ancient technique performed by the original Moth Priests. I'm now on my way to a place known as the Ancestor Glade to discover the origin of the mysterious ritual. *Objective 10:* Find a Moth Priest's knife |
| 20 |  | *Objective 20:* Gather bark from a Canticle Tree |
| 30 |  | *Objective 30:* Attract Ancestor Moth Swarms (<Global=DLC1VQ06MothsFound>/7) |
| 50 |  | I've used the unusual knife to collect the bark from the Canticle Trees dotting the Ancestor Glade, which has in turn drawn Ancestor Moths to me. I've felt a surge of energy run through my body and I feel drawn towards the large column of light in the center of the Glade. *Objective 40:* Enter the column of light and read the Elder Scroll (Blood) |
| 60 |  | I'm now standing in a column of light which appears to stretch into the sky at the center of the Ancestor Glade. According to Dexion, I've completed the Ritual of the Ancestor Moth and can now read the Elder Scrolls. I'll need to begin with the Elder Scroll I obtained from the Soul Cairn in order to decipher the location of Auriel's Bow. |
| 70 |  | *Objective 60:* Speak to Serana |
| 200 | ☑ | After entering the column of light in the Ancestor Glade, I was able to read the Elder Scrolls. I had an incredible vision that pinpointed a cave entrance far away on the western edge of Skyrim. This appears to be the location of Auriel's Bow. If I'm to stop Harkon from completing the Tyranny of the Sun, I should hasten there immediately. |
| ☑ | After entering the column of light in the Ancestor Glade, I was able to read the Elder Scrolls. I had an incredible vision that pinpointed a cave entrance far away on the western edge of Skyrim. This appears to be the location of Auriel's Bow. If I'm to complete the Tyranny of the Sun before Harkon, I should hasten there immediately. |

- The following empty quest stages were omitted from the table: 0, 40, 61, 62, 63, 64, 65.

Notes

- Any text displayed in angle brackets (e.g., `<Alias=LocationHold>`) is dynamically set by the Radiant Quest system, and will be filled in with the appropriate word(s) when seen in game.
- Not all Journal Entries may appear in your journal; which entries appear and which entries do not depends on the manner in which the quest is done.
- Stages are not always in order of progress. This is usually the case with quests that have multiple possible outcomes or quests where certain tasks may be done in any order. Some stages may therefore repeat objectives seen in other stages.
- If an entry is marked as "Finishes Quest" it means the quest disappears from the Active Quest list, but you may still receive new entries for that quest.
- On the PC, it is possible to use the [console](/wiki/Skyrim:Console "Skyrim:Console") to advance through the quest by entering `setstage DLC1VQ06 stage`, where `stage` is the number of the stage you wish to complete. It is not possible to un-complete (i.e. go back) quest stages, but it is possible to clear all stages of the quest using `resetquest DLC1VQ06`.

# Skyrim:Unused NPCs

## Overview

This page lists all **unused NPCs** in Skyrim. None of these NPCs are present in the game; only those in [test cells](/wiki/Skyrim:Test_Cells "Skyrim:Test Cells") can be encountered without spawning them via console commands. Their descriptions on this page treat them as they would be if they were implemented in the game. Some of the locations, [quests](/wiki/Skyrim:Unfinished_Quests "Skyrim:Unfinished Quests"), and buildings mentioned in their descriptions do not exist either, yet are treated on this page as if they do. For information on unused content in general, please see the [related page](/wiki/Skyrim:Unused_Content "Skyrim:Unused Content").

### Affable Gent

The Affable Gent

The **Affable Gent**, a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H"), is a testing NPC who exists in test cell `WarehouseAmbushes`. He sleeps when there is nobody around, and will ambush you. He wears [noble clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Noble_Clothes "Skyrim:Noble Clothes") and [fur-lined boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Fur-lined_Boots "Skyrim:Fur-lined Boots"), and carries a [honed ancient Nord battle axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Honed_Ancient_Nord_Battle_Axe "Skyrim:Honed Ancient Nord Battle Axe").

Argi Farseer

**Argi Farseer** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [mystic](/wiki/Skyrim:Mystic "Skyrim:Mystic"). She is [Idgrod Ravencrone](/wiki/Skyrim:Idgrod_Ravencrone "Skyrim:Idgrod Ravencrone")'s sister, as indicated by a relationship marker. Argi wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#0006c1d9 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a5 "Skyrim:Clothing"). She carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger") and a [key](/wiki/Skyrim:Key_to_Argi%27s_Hut "Skyrim:Key to Argi's Hut") to her hut located in [Stonehills](/wiki/Skyrim:Stonehills "Skyrim:Stonehills").

She sleeps at 11pm, waking up at 10am. There is under a 20% chance that she will interrupt her sleep at 3am for a three-hour stroll around Stonehills. She spends the rest of her time in her house.

### Benkum

This article is about the unused NPC from the base game. For the NPC added by Dragonborn, see [Benkum](/wiki/Skyrim:Benkum "Skyrim:Benkum").

Benkum

**Benkum** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who manages the wagers for [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm")'s arena. He is [Angrenor](/wiki/Skyrim:Angrenor_Once-Honored "Skyrim:Angrenor Once-Honored")'s friend and [Huki](#Huki_Seven-Swords) is his boss. His schedule keeps him in the Bloodworks all the time, never sleeping or moving from his spot. He wears [scaled armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Armor "Skyrim:Scaled Armor") and [scaled boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Boots "Skyrim:Scaled Boots"), and carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

**Dialogue:**

:   *"Hello, hello, hello. Interested in fighting in the Pit? The rules are strict, but if I don't see it, it didn't happen, know what I mean?"*
:   *"It's very important to Huki that all the battles are fair. What's more important to me is that my pay is fair."*
:   *"Prove your valor in the Pit! If you haven't got any valor, prove your pocketbook!"*

| [Faction(s)](/wiki/Skyrim:Factions "Skyrim:Factions") | [CrimeFactionEastmarch](/wiki/Skyrim:CrimeFactionEastmarch "Skyrim:CrimeFactionEastmarch"); [FavorJobsDrunksFaction](/wiki/Skyrim:FavorJobsDrunksFaction "Skyrim:FavorJobsDrunksFaction"); [TownWindhelmFaction](/wiki/Skyrim:TownWindhelmFaction "Skyrim:TownWindhelmFaction"); [Windhelm Pit](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm_Pit "Skyrim:Windhelm Pit") (Pit Lord) | | |

Brond

**Brond** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") used in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm")'s arena. He wears [hide armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Armor "Skyrim:Hide Armor") and [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and carries a [dragonplate shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonplate_Shield "Skyrim:Dragonplate Shield"), [steel greatsword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Greatsword "Skyrim:Steel Greatsword"), [Imperial bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Bow "Skyrim:Imperial Bow"), and 30 [iron arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Arrow "Skyrim:Iron Arrow").

He is [Huki](#Huki_Seven-Swords)'s friend, [Edorfin](#Edorfin)'s rival, and [Liesl](#Liesl)'s foe. He sleeps for seven hours starting at 10pm, and spends the rest of his time in the Bloodworks.

**Dialogue:**

:   *"I've been a lot of things in my life. Pit fighter's the one I'm best at."*
:   *"We should get a dragon for the Pit. I'd love to fight one."*
:   *"I've been fighting in the Pit for years. That should tell you something."*
:   *"There's two kinds of Pit fighters -- good ones and dead ones."*
:   *"Ulfric once tried to get me to fight for him, but marching bores me."*
:   *"Steel. Barehanded. Armor. Naked. It's all fighting. I can kill you with a weapon or by myself. No difference."*

Captain Metilius

**Captain Metilius** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [Imperial Soldier](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Soldier_(class) "Skyrim:Imperial Soldier (class)"). He wears an [Imperial soldier outfit](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear#Imperial_Soldier_Armor "Skyrim:Specialty Gear") without a helmet, and carries an [Imperial bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Bow "Skyrim:Imperial Bow"), 10 [steel arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Arrow "Skyrim:Steel Arrow"), and a [steel greatsword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Greatsword "Skyrim:Steel Greatsword").

If both [Bleak Falls Barrow](/wiki/Skyrim:Bleak_Falls_Barrow_(quest) "Skyrim:Bleak Falls Barrow (quest)") and [The Jagged Crown](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Jagged_Crown "Skyrim:The Jagged Crown") (either faction) are in your quest log, he stands near the war room in [Castle Dour](/wiki/Skyrim:Castle_Dour "Skyrim:Castle Dour"). He sleeps for eight hours at midnight, and wanders around Castle Dour from 8am to 8pm. The rest of the time he wanders near or around the castle.

Captain Metilius was meant to aid you in an earlier version of the quest [Dragon Rising](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Rising "Skyrim:Dragon Rising"). In this quest's Legion variation (as there was a Stormcloak equivalent as well) [General Tullius](/wiki/Skyrim:General_Tullius "Skyrim:General Tullius") would've sent you alongside Metilius and a squad of generic friendly soldiers to investigate reports of a dragon at [Fort Hraggstad](/wiki/Skyrim:Fort_Hraggstad "Skyrim:Fort Hraggstad"). Once there, you would find the fort under active attack by the dragon, and Metilius and the soldiers would help you defeat it, similar to [Irileth](/wiki/Skyrim:Irileth "Skyrim:Irileth") and the Whiterun guards in the final version of the quest. It seems that after this quest, he would not get sent out with you any longer, as there are no other relevant leftovers pointing to this.

Castor the Puzzlemaker's Corpse

**Castor the Puzzlemaker's Corpse** is a Dark Elf bandit added by Dragonborn who would appear in [Damphall Mine](/wiki/Skyrim:Damphall_Mine "Skyrim:Damphall Mine") along with a dead Bandit Highwayman. He wears leveled bandit boots and cuirass, and carries a steel dagger, some random loot, and sometimes a small amount of gold. He knows Fast Healing, Magicka Recovery, Stoneflesh, a ward spell, and either a fire, ice, or lightning spell. Despite his name, he's not actually set up to spawn dead.

College Guard

The **College Guard** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [sorcerer](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatSorcerer "Skyrim:CombatSorcerer"). He wears a set of [master robes of Destruction](/wiki/Skyrim:Master_Robes_of_Destruction "Skyrim:Master Robes of Destruction") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#0006b46c "Skyrim:Clothing"). He uses [guard dialogue](/wiki/Skyrim:Guard_Dialogue "Skyrim:Guard Dialogue").

Cow Hand

**Cow Hand** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H"). He and a painted [cow](/wiki/Skyrim:Cow "Skyrim:Cow") are used in the unimplemented quest `CWMission08`, which involves [Giants](/wiki/Skyrim:Giant "Skyrim:Giant") and the [Civil War](/wiki/Skyrim:Civil_War "Skyrim:Civil War"). He wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a6 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a [hat](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209aa "Skyrim:Clothing") and a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a5 "Skyrim:Clothing").

Dawnguard Hunter

DomnaMagia

**DomnaMagia** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") that was meant to be killed by you in a removed vampire-related quest, internally known as `V02`, the second of three such removed quests. She is placed in front of [Solitude](/wiki/Skyrim:Solitude "Skyrim:Solitude")'s [Hall of the Dead](/wiki/Skyrim:Hall_of_the_Dead_(Solitude) "Skyrim:Hall of the Dead (Solitude)"), but her ref is never enabled. She wears necromancer boots and leveled vampire attire. [Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard") changes her inventory, giving her [vampire armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Armor "Skyrim:Vampire Armor").

Do Not Delete Me - needed for export to work

**Do Not Delete Me - needed for export to work** is a naked Nord added by [Dragonborn](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonborn "Skyrim:Dragonborn") who is not located anywhere. Visually, she appears identical to [Frea](/wiki/Skyrim:Frea "Skyrim:Frea"), and might have served as a "face holder" NPC, an NPC on which developers test creating a face before porting it to the final NPC. Her name seems to imply this.

This **Dremora Messenger** would have played some kind of role in the [College of Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:College_of_Winterhold_(faction) "Skyrim:College of Winterhold (faction)") quest [Containment](/wiki/Skyrim:Containment "Skyrim:Containment"), based on his Editor ID of `MG05Messenger`. His inventory is empty and he has no voice type set, so his message is currently unknown. He is one of the few Dremora NPCs with a uniquely designed face, and he wears the very rare [mourners's clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Mourner%27s_Clothes "Skyrim:Mourner's Clothes") with [accompanying hat](/wiki/Skyrim:Mourner%27s_Hat "Skyrim:Mourner's Hat"). The Mages Guild was previously seen using Dremora messengers in *[Oblivion](/wiki/Oblivion:Misdirection "Oblivion:Misdirection")*.

The Drunk Cultist

This **Drunk Cultist** is a [Breton](/wiki/Skyrim:Breton "Skyrim:Breton") mystic who was to be used in the quest [A Night To Remember](/wiki/Skyrim:A_Night_To_Remember "Skyrim:A Night To Remember"). She is placed in [Morvunskar](/wiki/Skyrim:Morvunskar "Skyrim:Morvunskar") at the base of the steps before the portal to Sanguine's realm, but her ref is never enabled. She sleeps for eight hours at midnight, wandering around Morvunskar the rest of the time. She wears warlock boots and leveled warlock robes.

Edorfin

**Edorfin** is a [Wood Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Elf "Skyrim:Wood Elf") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)") used in [Windhelm's](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm") arena. He wears [studded armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Studded_Armor "Skyrim:Studded Armor") and [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and carries a [steel sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Sword "Skyrim:Steel Sword"), [hunting bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Hunting_Bow "Skyrim:Hunting Bow"), and 200 [steel arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Arrow "Skyrim:Steel Arrow").

He is rivals with both [Brond](#Brond) and [Liesl](#Liesl). He sleeps for 8 hours a day starting at noon, and the rest of the time he attacks a ranged target.

**Dialogue:**

:   *"What I lack in strength, I make up for with tenacity."*

| [Faction(s)](/wiki/Skyrim:Factions "Skyrim:Factions") | [DraugrFaction](/wiki/Skyrim:DraugrFaction "Skyrim:DraugrFaction") | | |

Enthralled Wizard

This **Enthralled Wizard** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [bandit wizard](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit_Wizard "Skyrim:Bandit Wizard"). He wears [black mage robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Black_Mage_Robes "Skyrim:Black Mage Robes") and warlock [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots "Skyrim:Boots"). He carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"), and may drop a [scroll](/wiki/Skyrim:Scrolls "Skyrim:Scrolls") and [potions](/wiki/Skyrim:Potions "Skyrim:Potions") when killed.

**Note**: Two separate enthralled wizards are actually encountered in the game, during [The Staff of Magnus](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Staff_of_Magnus "Skyrim:The Staff of Magnus"). Information on these two NPCs can be found on [this page](/wiki/Skyrim:Enthralled_Wizard "Skyrim:Enthralled Wizard").

Escaped Prisoner

The **Escaped Prisoner** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)"). He wears a [roughspun tunic](/wiki/Skyrim:Roughspun_Tunic "Skyrim:Roughspun Tunic") and [footwraps](/wiki/Skyrim:Footwraps "Skyrim:Footwraps"). He was to be used in a [world interaction](/wiki/Skyrim:World_Interactions "Skyrim:World Interactions"). He has no inventory.

### Eyldi the Bear

|  |
| --- |
| **This article could benefit from an image**. See [Help:Images](/wiki/Help:Images "Help:Images") for information on how to upload images. Please remove this template from the page when finished. |

| Eyldi the Bear | | | |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Race | [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") | Gender | Female |
| Level |  | Class | [(?)](/wiki/Category:Pages_Missing_Data "Category:Pages Missing Data") |
| [RefID](/wiki/Skyrim:NPCs#Console_IDs "Skyrim:NPCs") | N/A | [BaseID](/wiki/Skyrim:NPCs#Console_IDs "Skyrim:NPCs") | N/A |
| Other Information | | | |
| Voice Type | [FemaleNord](/wiki/Category:Skyrim-Voice-FemaleNord "Category:Skyrim-Voice-FemaleNord") | | |

**Eyldi the Bear** is a retired [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") hero. She would have been the owner of [Bear's Cave Mill](http://content3.uesp.net/oblivion/cs/cslist.php?game=sr&formid=0x00018c8e%7C). While no NPC data exists for her, the voice files for her greetings remain in the game, possibly due to a mix-up which has them marked for [Half-Moon Mill](/wiki/Skyrim:Half-Moon_Mill "Skyrim:Half-Moon Mill"). In addition, [Jorn](/wiki/Skyrim:Jorn "Skyrim:Jorn") of the [Bards College](/wiki/Skyrim:Bards_College "Skyrim:Bards College") still mentions her, and unused lines for [Hold Guards](/wiki/Skyrim:Guards "Skyrim:Guards") also make mention of her.

:   *"Come in, come in! Welcome to the Bear's Cave!"*
:   *"Company's a rare delight. Stay a spell."*
:   *"I trust the roads are safe? I take good care of my land."*
:   *"Do the bards still sing of my adventures?"*
:   *"Time was, the name Eyldi the Bear could make brigands flee for miles around."*
:   *"The world has changed since I was young. Let there be new heroes. I’ve earned a rest."*

Due to her heroic feats, Stormcloak-aligned guards would have been all too familiar with Eyldi and could have said:

:   *"I grew up hearing songs about Eyldi the Bear! She wrestled a storm out of the sky!"*

Imperial-aligned guards did not see Eyldi in the same light, however:

:   *"Just my luck, stuck in the sticks with this crazy old woman."*

Fellow Prisoner

The **Fellow Prisoner** is a random bandit who fights in [Windhelm's](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm") arena. He or she carries an [iron sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Sword "Skyrim:Iron Sword"), an [iron shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Shield "Skyrim:Iron Shield"), and a lockpick.

Grushnag

**Grushnag** is an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") warrior who travels with a [Khajiit caravan](/wiki/Skyrim:Khajiit_Traders "Skyrim:Khajiit Traders"). He wanders around the caravan when stopped. He wears [scaled horn armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Horn_Armor "Skyrim:Scaled Horn Armor"), [scaled boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Boots "Skyrim:Scaled Boots"), and [scaled bracers](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Bracers "Skyrim:Scaled Bracers").

Two [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") **Headsmen** are used in the unimplemented quest `WEDL11`, a [world interaction](/wiki/Skyrim:World_Interactions "Skyrim:World Interactions"). They both wear executioner outfits and carry a [headsman's axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Headsman%27s_Axe "Skyrim:Headsman's Axe").

Henrik

**Henrik** is a dead [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)"). He wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a6 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a5 "Skyrim:Clothing"). He has no voicetype assigned, and carries nothing. He was meant to appear in [The Wolf Queen Awakened](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Wolf_Queen_Awakened "Skyrim:The Wolf Queen Awakened") if his Editor ID of `MS06Victim` is anything to go by.

Herebane Sorenshield

**Herebane Sorenshield** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") citizen. He wears an outfit of [Imperial armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear#Imperial_Soldier_Armor "Skyrim:Specialty Gear") with a full-faced helmet and carries a leveled amount of gold. He patrols a small area around him, but does not converse with anyone.

There are two items present in test cell `QASmoke` that appear to be named after him: an elven shield named [Herebane's Fortress](/wiki/Skyrim:Herebane%27s_Fortress "Skyrim:Herebane's Fortress") that resists frost damage and an elven sword named [Herebane's Courage](/wiki/Skyrim:Herebane%27s_Courage "Skyrim:Herebane's Courage") that does fire damage.

Hrolmir

**Hrolmir** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") involved in the quest [The Pursuit](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Pursuit "Skyrim:The Pursuit"). Besides his name and Editor ID, he has no other unique traits.
