Escaped Prisoner

The **Escaped Prisoner** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)"). He wears a [roughspun tunic](/wiki/Skyrim:Roughspun_Tunic "Skyrim:Roughspun Tunic") and [footwraps](/wiki/Skyrim:Footwraps "Skyrim:Footwraps"). He was to be used in a [world interaction](/wiki/Skyrim:World_Interactions "Skyrim:World Interactions"). He has no inventory.

### Eyldi the Bear

|  |
| --- |
| **This article could benefit from an image**. See [Help:Images](/wiki/Help:Images "Help:Images") for information on how to upload images. Please remove this template from the page when finished. |

| Eyldi the Bear | | | |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Race | [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") | Gender | Female |
| Level |  | Class | [(?)](/wiki/Category:Pages_Missing_Data "Category:Pages Missing Data") |
| [RefID](/wiki/Skyrim:NPCs#Console_IDs "Skyrim:NPCs") | N/A | [BaseID](/wiki/Skyrim:NPCs#Console_IDs "Skyrim:NPCs") | N/A |
| Other Information | | | |
| Voice Type | [FemaleNord](/wiki/Category:Skyrim-Voice-FemaleNord "Category:Skyrim-Voice-FemaleNord") | | |

**Eyldi the Bear** is a retired [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") hero. She would have been the owner of [Bear's Cave Mill](http://content3.uesp.net/oblivion/cs/cslist.php?game=sr&formid=0x00018c8e%7C). While no NPC data exists for her, the voice files for her greetings remain in the game, possibly due to a mix-up which has them marked for [Half-Moon Mill](/wiki/Skyrim:Half-Moon_Mill "Skyrim:Half-Moon Mill"). In addition, [Jorn](/wiki/Skyrim:Jorn "Skyrim:Jorn") of the [Bards College](/wiki/Skyrim:Bards_College "Skyrim:Bards College") still mentions her, and unused lines for [Hold Guards](/wiki/Skyrim:Guards "Skyrim:Guards") also make mention of her.

:   *"Come in, come in! Welcome to the Bear's Cave!"*
:   *"Company's a rare delight. Stay a spell."*
:   *"I trust the roads are safe? I take good care of my land."*
:   *"Do the bards still sing of my adventures?"*
:   *"Time was, the name Eyldi the Bear could make brigands flee for miles around."*
:   *"The world has changed since I was young. Let there be new heroes. I’ve earned a rest."*

Due to her heroic feats, Stormcloak-aligned guards would have been all too familiar with Eyldi and could have said:

:   *"I grew up hearing songs about Eyldi the Bear! She wrestled a storm out of the sky!"*

Imperial-aligned guards did not see Eyldi in the same light, however:

:   *"Just my luck, stuck in the sticks with this crazy old woman."*

Fellow Prisoner

The **Fellow Prisoner** is a random bandit who fights in [Windhelm's](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm") arena. He or she carries an [iron sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Sword "Skyrim:Iron Sword"), an [iron shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Shield "Skyrim:Iron Shield"), and a lockpick.

Grushnag

**Grushnag** is an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") warrior who travels with a [Khajiit caravan](/wiki/Skyrim:Khajiit_Traders "Skyrim:Khajiit Traders"). He wanders around the caravan when stopped. He wears [scaled horn armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Horn_Armor "Skyrim:Scaled Horn Armor"), [scaled boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Boots "Skyrim:Scaled Boots"), and [scaled bracers](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Bracers "Skyrim:Scaled Bracers").

Henrik

**Henrik** is a dead [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)"). He wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a6 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a5 "Skyrim:Clothing"). He has no voicetype assigned, and carries nothing. He was meant to appear in [The Wolf Queen Awakened](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Wolf_Queen_Awakened "Skyrim:The Wolf Queen Awakened") if his Editor ID of `MS06Victim` is anything to go by.

Herebane Sorenshield

**Herebane Sorenshield** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") citizen. He wears an outfit of [Imperial armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear#Imperial_Soldier_Armor "Skyrim:Specialty Gear") with a full-faced helmet and carries a leveled amount of gold. He patrols a small area around him, but does not converse with anyone.

There are two items present in test cell `QASmoke` that appear to be named after him: an elven shield named [Herebane's Fortress](/wiki/Skyrim:Herebane%27s_Fortress "Skyrim:Herebane's Fortress") that resists frost damage and an elven sword named [Herebane's Courage](/wiki/Skyrim:Herebane%27s_Courage "Skyrim:Herebane's Courage") that does fire damage.

Huki Seven-Swords

**Huki Seven-Swords** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who works as jailkeeper in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm")'s arena. She is [Brond](#Brond) and [Brunwulf](/wiki/Skyrim:Brunwulf_Free-Winter "Skyrim:Brunwulf Free-Winter")'s friend, and [Benkum](#Benkum) is her employee. She stands in the Bloodworks all day. She wears [studded armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Studded_Armor "Skyrim:Studded Armor") and [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and carries an [iron greatsword](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Greatsword "Skyrim:Iron Greatsword").

**Dialogue:**

:   *"Looking for something?"*
:   *"Real Nords like to prove their mettle in the pit."*
:   *"Just between you and me, I wouldn't mind if the Empire just up and left for good."*
:   *"I'd love get one of those Imperial weasels under my boot..."*
:   *"All right, meat, it's time for you to earn your freedom."*

:   *"What is this, you think you're clever? If you want to fight your way out of here, you have to do it in the Pit. Gotta give the crowd a show. Luckily there's no shortage of Pit bait around here. Now get in there and earn your freedom."*
:   **What do I have to do?**
:   *"We don't just let people rot in cells until they've served their time. Here, you fight. If you win, you go free. If you lose, well, you'll know the freedom of Sovngarde if you're lucky."*
:   **I'm ready to go.**
:   *"All right. Head to the pit when you think you're ready."*
:   **I want to fight [Alain Dufont](/wiki/Skyrim:Alain_Dufont "Skyrim:Alain Dufont").** (Only if you have reached stage 20 and have not yet reached stage 40 of quest [Mourning Never Comes](/wiki/Skyrim:Mourning_Never_Comes "Skyrim:Mourning Never Comes").)
:   *"Well aren't you in luck. He's the next piece of gutter scum on the docket. Between you and me, I hope you cleave his arrogant head open."*

Imperial Officer

The **Imperial Officer** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") soldier. He wears an [Imperial soldier outfit](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear#Imperial_Soldier_Armor "Skyrim:Specialty Gear") without a helm, and carries an [Imperial light shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Light_Shield "Skyrim:Imperial Light Shield"), [Imperial sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Sword "Skyrim:Imperial Sword"), and [steel dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Dagger "Skyrim:Steel Dagger"). He was intended to be used for [Civil War](/wiki/Skyrim:Civil_War "Skyrim:Civil War") sieges. He patrols the area around him.

Imperial Wizard

The **Imperial Wizard** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") mage. He wears an [Imperial guard outfit](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear#Imperial_Soldier_Armor "Skyrim:Specialty Gear") (light armor), and carries a leveled warlock [dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Dagger "Skyrim:Dagger"). Possible spells include [Firebolt](/wiki/Skyrim:Firebolt "Skyrim:Firebolt"), [Healing](/wiki/Skyrim:Healing "Skyrim:Healing"), [Flames](/wiki/Skyrim:Flames "Skyrim:Flames"), [Oakflesh](/wiki/Skyrim:Oakflesh "Skyrim:Oakflesh"), and [Lesser Ward](/wiki/Skyrim:Lesser_Ward "Skyrim:Lesser Ward"), with the perks Regeneration and Augmented Flames. He was intended to be used for [Civil War](/wiki/Skyrim:Civil_War "Skyrim:Civil War") sieges.

Insane College Wizard

The **Insane College Wizard** is a [High Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:High_Elf "Skyrim:High Elf") mage used in the unimplemented quest [Rogue Wizard](/wiki/Skyrim:Unfinished_Quests#Rogue_Wizard "Skyrim:Unfinished Quests"). He has been wandering a hold of Skyrim, irritating the local Jarl, and the [College of Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:College_of_Winterhold_(faction) "Skyrim:College of Winterhold (faction)") has severed all ties with him but must save their reputation by killing him. He wears [expert robes of Destruction](/wiki/Skyrim:Expert_Robes_of_Destruction "Skyrim:Expert Robes of Destruction") with a pair of boots.

**Spells**:

- Fast Healing
- Fireball
- Firebolt
- Flame Cloak
- Flames
- Frostbite
- Frost Cloak
- Healing
- Ice Spike
- Ice Storm
- Lightning Bolt
- Sparks
- Greater Ward
- Lesser Ward
- Steadfast Ward

Jolf

**Jolf** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") citizen. He wears a leveled farmer's tunic.

Liesl

**Liesl** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who fights in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm")'s arena. She is [Edorfin](#Edorfin)'s rival and [Brond](#Brond)'s foe. She sleeps at midnight for seven hours, and then wanders around the Bloodworks for the rest of the time. She wears [scaled horn armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Horn_Armor "Skyrim:Scaled Horn Armor") and [scaled boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Boots "Skyrim:Scaled Boots"), and carries an [iron shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Shield "Skyrim:Iron Shield") and [iron battleaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Battleaxe "Skyrim:Iron Battleaxe").

**Dialogue:**

:   *"Stand away from me, or I'll crack you like an egg."*
:   *"What are you looking at? Scared of strong women?"*
:   *"You look mighty tasty. Better not stick around for supper."*

Makhel Abbas

**Makhel Abbas** is an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") bandit used in the nonexistent [Civil War](/wiki/Skyrim:Civil_War "Skyrim:Civil War") quest `CWMission11`. He hunts animals while the quest is active. He wears leveled bandit armor, with a 50% chance of having gauntlets or a helmet, and carries a leveled dagger, two-handed weapon, and gold.

### Marauder

**Marauders** are [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") bandits used in the E3 demo of Skyrim in the location Bleak Falls Tower.

The marauder archer wears ancient Nord [armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Armor "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Armor"), [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Boots "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Boots"), and [gauntlets](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Gauntlets "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Gauntlets"), and he carries an [Orcish bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Bow "Skyrim:Orcish Bow") with 11 [Orcish arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Arrow "Skyrim:Orcish Arrow"), along with two [steel daggers](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Dagger "Skyrim:Steel Dagger") and leveled gold, with a 25% chance to carry a lockpick. He has a few [Archery](/wiki/Skyrim:Archery "Skyrim:Archery") perks.

Mithorpa Nasyal

**Mithorpa Nasyal** is a [Dark Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Elf "Skyrim:Dark Elf") [farmer](/wiki/Skyrim:Farmer_(class) "Skyrim:Farmer (class)") at Barleydark Farm, a location near [Sleeping Tree Camp](/wiki/Skyrim:Sleeping_Tree_Camp "Skyrim:Sleeping Tree Camp") that also does not appear in the game. He is the only one in his faction.

He wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothes#000261c0 "Skyrim:Clothes") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#000261bd "Skyrim:Boots"). He carries his [house key](/wiki/Skyrim:Mithorpa%27s_House_Key "Skyrim:Mithorpa's House Key") and an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

When interacted with, he will at first say, *"Come to gawk at the Dark Elf?"* and then apologize, *"I mean no offense. I am but a humble farmer. Not a... a great wanderer, like you."* If you are a Dark Elf, he may also be found saying, *"Well look at us. Like two gray specks in the snow."*

Muril

**Muril** is a [High Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:High_Elf "Skyrim:High Elf") [elemental mage](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatMageElemental "Skyrim:CombatMageElemental"). Along with [Ancano](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancano "Skyrim:Ancano") and his underling [Estormo](/wiki/Skyrim:Estormo "Skyrim:Estormo"), Muril is part of a [Thalmor](/wiki/Skyrim:Thalmor "Skyrim:Thalmor") infiltration unit attempting to undermine the [College of Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:College_of_Winterhold_(faction) "Skyrim:College of Winterhold (faction)"). He is set to follow Estormo during [The Staff of Magnus](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Staff_of_Magnus "Skyrim:The Staff of Magnus").

Noble

**Noble** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") [citizen](/wiki/Skyrim:Citizen_(class) "Skyrim:Citizen (class)") who was to be involved in the same encounter as the [male Noble](/wiki/Skyrim:Noble "Skyrim:Noble"). A Nord

This **Nord** warrior was to be used in a [random encounter](/wiki/Skyrim:World_Interactions "Skyrim:World Interactions"), or was to be a template for NPCs used in such an encounter. .jpg)

A Pit Fan

**Pit Fans** are used in [Windhelm's](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm") arena. They exist in cell `WindhelmPitEntrance`. They ignore all combat. They are intended to cheer, but their AI packages are not actually set up to do so. They wear boots and clothes, with the outfits varying between fans. They have no voices. Presumably these Pit Fans were placeholder NPCs, and Windhelm citizens were meant to take up their spots if the Pit was finalized.

Player Friend

**Player Friend** is an unlucky [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") used in the quest [With Friends Like These...](/wiki/Skyrim:With_Friends_Like_These... "Skyrim:With Friends Like These..."). He is friendly with you. He wanders around [Candlehearth Hall](/wiki/Skyrim:Candlehearth_Hall "Skyrim:Candlehearth Hall"), acting as a normal NPC would. He wears and carries nothing.

His name hints at the original direction of the related quest. Pre-release reports for Skyrim claimed that the [Dark Brotherhood](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Brotherhood "Skyrim:Dark Brotherhood") would make you assassinate NPCs whom you had befriended, although this didn't make it into the final game.

Rogen

**Rogen** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") added by the [Dawnguard add-on](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard"). He wears a set of [vampire robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Robes "Skyrim:Vampire Robes") with matching [hood](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Hood "Skyrim:Vampire Hood"), [gloves](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Gloves "Skyrim:Vampire Gloves"), and [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Boots "Skyrim:Vampire Boots"). In combat, he relies on an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe").

Dark Elf Saarthal Miner

There are six **Saarthal Miners**, three of which are [Wood Elves](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Elf "Skyrim:Wood Elf") and three of which are [Dark Elves](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Elf "Skyrim:Dark Elf"). Each of them carries a [pickaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Pickaxe "Skyrim:Pickaxe"). They all wear [miner's clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Miner%27s_Clothes "Skyrim:Miner's Clothes") with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#00080699 "Skyrim:Boots"), and two of them also wear a pair of [gloves](/wiki/Skyrim:Gloves#000261c1 "Skyrim:Gloves"). They are present in [Saarthal](/wiki/Skyrim:Saarthal "Skyrim:Saarthal").

They all share the same dialogue:

:   *"I'm just doing what I'm told."*
:   *"Gods, it's cold. They'd better find something good in there to make this worthwhile."*
:   *"As long as the [College](/wiki/Skyrim:College_of_Winterhold_(faction) "Skyrim:College of Winterhold (faction)") keeps paying, I'll keep digging."*
:   *"For thousand-year old ruins, they're not much to look at."*
:   *"Can't imagine what the College expects to find in here."*

Spirit of the Ancient Traveler

**Spirit of the Ancient Traveler** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") bandit ghost in [Wayward Pass](/wiki/Skyrim:Wayward_Pass "Skyrim:Wayward Pass"). He wears Steel Plate Armor, Steel Plate Boots, and Steel Plate Gauntlets—versions that you cannot wear—and carries a [steel sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Sword "Skyrim:Steel Sword").

Steirod

**Steirod** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [lumberjack](/wiki/Skyrim:Lumberjack "Skyrim:Lumberjack") who lives together with his brother [Trilf](#Trilf) at Irontree Mill. He wears some farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261c0 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261bd "Skyrim:Clothing"). He carries a [key](/wiki/Skyrim:Irontree_Mill_House_Key "Skyrim:Irontree Mill House Key") to the Irontree Mill and wields an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

Much unlike his brother Trilf, he will not want anything to do with you and will quickly turn you away:

:   *"We got everything in hand here. You aren't needed 'round here."*

He will be a little nicer if you continue conversation, giving a simple description of what he and Trilif do for a living:

:   *"We chop trees, we mill the wood. What more would you want to know?"*

He will then elaborate on this:

:   *"It's a hard way to make a living, but we find ways to make ends meet."*

He will then shift back to the same attitude he started the conversation with:

:   *"We don't take kindly to people snooping around our business."*

If his cold comments have not driven you away yet, he will state one last comment that might scare you away:

:   *"These axes can chop a lot more than wood, you know."*

Guards protecting their mill will also comment on the two brothers, stating things such as:

:   *"I used to serve with Trilf and Steirod. Good guys, but don't get on their bad side, you know?"*
:   *"These boys do good work for the Empire!"*

They also would have told you about Steirod's strength by recalling:

:   *"I once saw Steirod tear a whole tree out the ground with his bare hands. Glad he's on our side!"*

Sulvar the Steady

**Sulvar the Steady** is an [elderly](/wiki/Skyrim:Elder "Skyrim:Elder") [mystic](/wiki/Skyrim:Mystic "Skyrim:Mystic") who watches the Wheel in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm"). He wears a [belted tunic](/wiki/Skyrim:Belted_Tunic "Skyrim:Belted Tunic"), some [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#0001be1b "Skyrim:Boots"), and a [hat](/wiki/Skyrim:Hat#000209aa "Skyrim:Hat"). He also carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). He sleeps at noon for four hours and at 2am for four hours. He spends the rest of his time in the Wheelhouse.

**Dialogue:**

:   *"My job? Well, I'm the Watcher of the Wheel! Which is... exactly what it sounds like. I watch the Wheel."*
:   *"Watching the wheel isn't as simple as you might think. Have to inspect all the way around the ice, make sure it's not melting."*
:   *"Don't get too many folks up here these days. Sometimes I think they've forgotten about me."*

Talib

**Talib** is a [Redguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Redguard "Skyrim:Redguard") [mage](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatMageElemental "Skyrim:CombatMageElemental") living in [Stonehills](/wiki/Skyrim:Stonehills "Skyrim:Stonehills"). He is rivals with [Jesper](/wiki/Skyrim:Jesper "Skyrim:Jesper"). [Gestur Rockbreaker](/wiki/Skyrim:Gestur_Rockbreaker "Skyrim:Gestur Rockbreaker") is his father. He sleeps for eight hours at noon, then wanders around outside for two hours starting at 9pm. He works in the mine from 8am to 6pm, then visits [Argi](#Argi_Farseer) until 9pm. From 11pm to 8am he can be found inside his house. He wears a [belted tunic](/wiki/Skyrim:Belted_Tunic "Skyrim:Belted Tunic"), some [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#0001be1b "Skyrim:Boots"). He also carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). Despite being a mage, he knows no spells.

**Dialogue:**

:   **Gestur:** *"How goes it, son?"*
:   **Talib:** *"I'm fine, father. Work is fine."*
:   **Gestur:** *"Good, good. It's honest work, son. No shame in that."*
:   **Talib:** *"Of course not. I never thought otherwise."*
:   **Gestur:** *"Right. Well, you let me know if you need anything."*

Tasius Tragus

**Tasius Tragus** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") soldier living in [Dragon Bridge](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Bridge "Skyrim:Dragon Bridge"). He works for [General Tullius](/wiki/Skyrim:General_Tullius "Skyrim:General Tullius"), and [Varnius Junius](/wiki/Skyrim:Varnius_Junius "Skyrim:Varnius Junius") works for him. [Faida](/wiki/Skyrim:Faida "Skyrim:Faida") is his girlfriend and [Julienne Lylvieve](/wiki/Skyrim:Julienne_Lylvieve "Skyrim:Julienne Lylvieve") is his other lover. He sleeps for eight hours at midnight and spends the rest of his time wandering around a small area. He wears [scaled armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Armor "Skyrim:Scaled Armor") and [scaled boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Boots "Skyrim:Scaled Boots"), and carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). Tasius originally had the role that [Commander Maro](/wiki/Skyrim:Commander_Maro "Skyrim:Commander Maro") has in the final game, as he was meant to be the local Legion head. Varnius Junius still mentions Tasius in the final game.

Terek

**Terek** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") citizen who illegally lives in [Breezehome](/wiki/Skyrim:Breezehome "Skyrim:Breezehome"). He sleeps for eight hours at midnight. He wears farmer clothes and carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). A burial urn for Terek is present in Whiterun's Hall of the Dead, and will activate in case he dies.

**Dialogue:**

:   *"Don't let the door slam into your backside on the way out."*
:   *"Now if ya don't mind, my house could do with a bit of dusting."*
:   *"You're in my home. That's right. This here place, Breezehome, it belongs to old Terek."*
:   *"Oho! And what do you want, hmm?"*
:   *"You can't kick me out of here. You want me gone, you're gonna have to kill me."*
:   *"Now I don't care about no deeds, or laws, or other nonsense like that. This house was empty, so I settled in. And now it's mine."*
:   *"I'm a generous man, to those who treat me right. Might be I'll even let you live here with me, if you mind your manners."*

TestJeffBCarryWaterBucket

**TestJeffBCarryWaterBucket** is a Nord warrior. He wears default Redguard clothing when awake and a Psijic Monk outfit when sleeping, but never sleeps. He also carries iron armor and gauntlets, and knows the [Phantom Form](/wiki/Skyrim:Phantom_Form "Skyrim:Phantom Form") shout. He does not exist in any cell and does not say anything special. He does, however, use CarryActorScript, and was probably used to test how NPCs carry the bucket item used in specific animations. It's very likely that finalizing the process never got completed, seeing as the animation is broken in the final game.

Thaer

**Thaer** is a [Wood Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Elf "Skyrim:Wood Elf") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)") and [carriage](/wiki/Skyrim:Carriage "Skyrim:Carriage") driver added by the Dawnguard add-on. He wears farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#0006c1da "Skyrim:Clothing") and a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261bd "Skyrim:Clothing"), and carries an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe"). He shares his dialogue with other carriage drivers. He might have been planned as replacement for the Nord [Thaer](/wiki/Skyrim:Thaer "Skyrim:Thaer").

Thjollod

**Thjollod** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") bandit who was meant to appear as a hunter in [Ill Met By Moonlight](/wiki/Skyrim:Ill_Met_By_Moonlight "Skyrim:Ill Met By Moonlight"). He patrols [Bloated Man's Grotto](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloated_Man%27s_Grotto "Skyrim:Bloated Man's Grotto") and follows you in between stages 75 and 80 of the related quest. He wears [iron armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Armor "Skyrim:Iron Armor"), [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and [hide bracers](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Bracers "Skyrim:Hide Bracers"), and carries a leveled bandit sword, a leveled bandit bow, and leveled gold (plus 25 extra gold).

| [Faction(s)](/wiki/Skyrim:Factions "Skyrim:Factions") | [CrimeFactionHaafingar](/wiki/Skyrim:CrimeFactionHaafingar "Skyrim:CrimeFactionHaafingar"); [FavorJobsChopWoodFaction](/wiki/Skyrim:FavorJobsChopWoodFaction "Skyrim:FavorJobsChopWoodFaction"); [JobLumberjackFaction](/wiki/Skyrim:JobLumberjackFaction "Skyrim:JobLumberjackFaction"); [TownIrontreeMillFaction](/wiki/Skyrim:TownIrontreeMillFaction "Skyrim:TownIrontreeMillFaction") | | |

Trilf

**Trilf** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [lumberjack](/wiki/Skyrim:Lumberjack "Skyrim:Lumberjack") who lives together with his brother [Steirod](#Steirod) at Irontree Mill, located between the [Thalmor Embassy](/wiki/Skyrim:Thalmor_Embassy "Skyrim:Thalmor Embassy") and [Ironback Hideout](/wiki/Skyrim:Ironback_Hideout "Skyrim:Ironback Hideout"). He wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a6 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a5 "Skyrim:Clothing"). He carries a [key](/wiki/Skyrim:Irontree_Mill_House_Key "Skyrim:Irontree Mill House Key") to the Irontree Mill and wields an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe").

Trilf greets you quite warmly when you arrive at the mill:

:   *"Hey, welcome to the mill!"*

He will then reveal to you that his brother and him are veterans:

:   *"My brother and me bought this place when we left the army. Boy, did we lose a bundle, but at least we're not fighting the war!"*

He will then elaborate on his days in the army:

:   *"We served our time in the [Legion](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Legion "Skyrim:Imperial Legion"). Now we're just trying to make it day to day.*"

He will continue on about the financial difficulties he and Steirod are facing:

:   *"We're making our money back, bit by bit. Every extra bit of work helps..."*

Trius

**Trius** is a Breton bandit wizard. A [note](/wiki/Skyrim:For_Shelly "Skyrim:For Shelly") by him to someone named Shelly can be found at his camp near [Pilgrim's Trench](/wiki/Skyrim:Pilgrim%27s_Trench "Skyrim:Pilgrim's Trench"). He is involved in the unfinished quest [Trius' Trinket](/wiki/Skyrim:Trius%27_Trinket "Skyrim:Trius' Trinket"), where you would have had to fetch a jewelry box for him in exchange for a new spell.

Uglarz

**Uglarz** is an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H"). She wears [ragged trousers](/wiki/Skyrim:Ragged_Trousers "Skyrim:Ragged Trousers") with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#00080699 "Skyrim:Boots"), and carries an [Orcish bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Bow "Skyrim:Orcish Bow") and [Orcish dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Dagger "Skyrim:Orcish Dagger"), along with a selection of [lower-class items and gold](/wiki/Skyrim:Leveled_Lists#LootCitizenPocketsPoor "Skyrim:Leveled Lists"). She is placed inside of Mauhulakh's Longhouse, but her ref is permanently disabled.

Uglarz appears to have been intended to be the missing wife of [Chief Mauhulakh](/wiki/Skyrim:Chief_Mauhulakh "Skyrim:Chief Mauhulakh") in an [unfinished quest](/wiki/Skyrim:Unfinished_Quests "Skyrim:Unfinished Quests") called `NarzulburFreeFormWifeQuest` which also wasn't implemented in the game.

At the start of the quest to find her, Chief Mauhulakh speaks of her: *"I think of Uglarz. An old friend. When we were little she ran to the woods to hunt, and never came back. If someone found her, she would make an excellent mate."*

Before she comes back to the stronghold, Uglarz has a few lines of unique dialogue:

:   *"I am Uglarz, huntress of all."*
:   *"It was my home. But I left. The woods felt more alive, at least for a time."*
:   *"Mauhulakh. I remember him. Little, scrawny, fellow."*
:   *"Ha! Now that I would like to see. I may go back there some day."*
:   *"Always thought that old mine had more to it. Just like Mauhulakh to smash his way to good fortune."*
:   *"I'm going back to [Narzulbur](/wiki/Skyrim:Narzulbur "Skyrim:Narzulbur"). Want to see how it's come."*

After Uglarz returns to Narzulbur, she has a few more lines of unique dialogue:

:   *"You were right about this place. And about Mauhulakh."*
:   *"This was worth coming back for."*
:   *"[Urog](/wiki/Skyrim:Urog "Skyrim:Urog") is a good learner. I'm glad I have the chance to teach her. Makes me not miss the woods as much."*
:   *"Urog and I still hunt a lot, so it's not like I'm cooped up in the longhouse."*

When Uglarz is in Narzulbur, Urog will comment on her hunting skills, saying:

:   *"Uglarz is a great huntress. Perhaps she can teach me something new."*
:   *"I never would have thought, but Uglarz is an even better hunter than [Yatul](/wiki/Skyrim:Yatul "Skyrim:Yatul") was. The things I'm learning now..."*

Urchin

The **Urchin** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who exists in `WICourierCell`. He stays in a set location, never moving. He may have been meant for a [Dark Brotherhood](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Brotherhood "Skyrim:Dark Brotherhood") quest, as his Editor ID is `DBCourierUrchin`. He wears [ragged robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Ragged_Robes "Skyrim:Ragged Robes") along with [ragged boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Ragged_Boots "Skyrim:Ragged Boots").

Viding

**Viding** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who was meant to appear as a hunter in [Ill Met By Moonlight](/wiki/Skyrim:Ill_Met_By_Moonlight "Skyrim:Ill Met By Moonlight"). He patrols [Bloated Man's Grotto](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloated_Man%27s_Grotto "Skyrim:Bloated Man's Grotto") and follows you in between stages 66 and 80 of the related quest. He wears farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261c0 "Skyrim:Clothing"), [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and [hide bracers](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Bracers "Skyrim:Hide Bracers"), and carries a leveled potion of Restore Magicka, leveled gold, and an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

**Spells**:

- Firebolt
- Flames
- Healing
- Oakflesh
- Lesser Ward

### Yisra

This article is about the unused NPC in the base game. For the burnt corpse in the base game, see [Yisra](/wiki/Skyrim:Yisra "Skyrim:Yisra").

An unused version of **Yisra**, the dead mage related to [The Missing Apprentices](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Missing_Apprentices "Skyrim:The Missing Apprentices"). She wears the standard court mage outfit consisting of [blue mage robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Blue_Mage_Robes "Skyrim:Blue Mage Robes") and a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#0006b46c "Skyrim:Clothing"). [Her necklace](/wiki/Skyrim:Yisra%27s_Necklace "Skyrim:Yisra's Necklace") and an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger") can also be found on her corpse. While in the final game, Yisra succumbed to a fire cloak, this version of her has the same frost corpse properties as [Rundi](/wiki/Skyrim:Rundi "Skyrim:Rundi") in the final game. Suggesting her death might have happened differently.

**Ysleif** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)") and the [ferryman](/wiki/Skyrim:Ferryman "Skyrim:Ferryman") for [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar"). He wears farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#0006c1da "Skyrim:Clothing"), a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261bd "Skyrim:Clothing"), and carries an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe").

In the [Creation Kit](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Creation_Kit "Skyrim Mod:Creation Kit"), his Editor ID is `ccKRTSSE001_FerrymanDawnstarBACKUP`, implying he was meant to be a backup in case [Harlaug](/wiki/Skyrim:Harlaug "Skyrim:Harlaug") was dead. However, Harlaug cannot be killed under normal conditions as he is essential and is also set to respawn.

## Behind-the-scenes NPCs

While these NPCs technically can't be encountered directly, they are in fact used by the game, usually in the background, for a variety of technical reasons.

Azura

Clavicus Vile

**Clavicus Vile** is never instantiated. He is used as speaker for one line of dialogue and mentioned in three aliases for the quest [A Daedra's Best Friend](/wiki/Skyrim:A_Daedra%27s_Best_Friend "Skyrim:A Daedra's Best Friend") as *"Additional Valid Voice Type For Export"*. He carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"), and wears red colored [child's clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Child%27s_Clothes "Skyrim:Child's Clothes") and a pair of [shoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000e80a8 "Skyrim:Clothing"). However, due to him being an adult NPC, the clothes don't appear at all, and while he appears to be wearing shoes, parts of his calves go invisible as a result.

Hircine

Various aspects of Hircine will be encountered as ghosts during [Ill Met By Moonlight](/wiki/Skyrim:Ill_Met_By_Moonlight "Skyrim:Ill Met By Moonlight"). He first appears in the quest after you kill a [white stag](/wiki/Skyrim:White_Stag "Skyrim:White Stag").

Hrothmund

**Hrothmund** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") placeholder NPC used for when his spirit speaks during [Retaking Thirsk](/wiki/Skyrim:Retaking_Thirsk "Skyrim:Retaking Thirsk"). His axe can be found lodged in an altar inside the [barrow](/wiki/Skyrim:Hrothmund%27s_Barrow "Skyrim:Hrothmund's Barrow") where he is buried. For more information on the historical person, see [Hrothmund the Red](/wiki/Lore:Hrothmund_the_Red "Lore:Hrothmund the Red").

Malacath

**Malacath** resides in [Largashbur Longhouse](/wiki/Skyrim:Largashbur_Longhouse "Skyrim:Largashbur Longhouse") and acts as a speaker during [The Cursed Tribe](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Cursed_Tribe "Skyrim:The Cursed Tribe"). He can't be seen without console use, because he is always disabled. His reference has an editor ID of `ShagrolRef`. Shagrol is the name of the previous leader of the [Largashbur](/wiki/Skyrim:Largashbur "Skyrim:Largashbur") tribe and during the quest [Shagrol's Warhammer](/wiki/Skyrim:Shagrol%27s_Warhammer "Skyrim:Shagrol's Warhammer") needs to be recovered.

Mehrunes Dagon

**Mehrunes Dagon** is a [Daedric Prince](/wiki/Lore:Daedric_Princes "Lore:Daedric Princes") who is encountered during the quest [Pieces of the Past](/wiki/Skyrim:Pieces_of_the_Past "Skyrim:Pieces of the Past"). As a reward for betraying [Silus Vesuius](/wiki/Skyrim:Silus_Vesuius "Skyrim:Silus Vesuius") at the [Shrine of Mehrunes Dagon](/wiki/Skyrim:Shrine_of_Mehrunes_Dagon "Skyrim:Shrine of Mehrunes Dagon") you will receive the Daedric artifact [Mehrunes' Razor](/wiki/Skyrim:Mehrunes%27_Razor "Skyrim:Mehrunes' Razor").

Mephala

**Mephala** is a [Daedric Prince](/wiki/Lore:Daedric_Princes "Lore:Daedric Princes") who has a presence behind a locked door in [Dragonsreach](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonsreach "Skyrim:Dragonsreach"). She is encountered during [The Whispering Door](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Whispering_Door "Skyrim:The Whispering Door"), in which she tasks you with unsealing a locked room in [Dragonsreach](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonsreach "Skyrim:Dragonsreach"). As a reward for obeying her, you are gifted the [Ebony Blade](/wiki/Skyrim:Ebony_Blade "Skyrim:Ebony Blade").

Meridia

**Meridia** is a [Daedric Prince](/wiki/Lore:Daedric_Princes "Lore:Daedric Princes"). If you happen to find [Meridia's Beacon](/wiki/Skyrim:Meridia%27s_Beacon "Skyrim:Meridia's Beacon") by accident or by asking at [Meridia's Shrine](/wiki/Skyrim:Statue_to_Meridia "Skyrim:Statue to Meridia"), then the quest [The Break of Dawn](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Break_of_Dawn "Skyrim:The Break of Dawn") is started.

Meridia seeks for her Temple to be restored and sends you to defeat [Malkoran](/wiki/Skyrim:Malkoran "Skyrim:Malkoran") in [Kilkreath Ruins](/wiki/Skyrim:Kilkreath_Ruins "Skyrim:Kilkreath Ruins").

Vaermina

**Vaermina** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") placed in the `ThievesGuildHoldingCell`. She is used to play Vaermina's lines during [Waking Nightmare](/wiki/Skyrim:Waking_Nightmare "Skyrim:Waking Nightmare").

Voice of Boethiah

The **Voice of Boethiah** is a [Dark Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Elf "Skyrim:Dark Elf") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)") that isn't placed anywhere, but is used as the speaker for Boethiah's lines in [Boethiah's Calling](/wiki/Skyrim:Boethiah%27s_Calling "Skyrim:Boethiah's Calling").

Voice of Namira

The **Voice of Namira** is a [Wood Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Elf "Skyrim:Wood Elf") [nightblade](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightblade "Skyrim:Nightblade") who exists in a cell to the east of the dining room in [Reachcliff Cave](/wiki/Skyrim:Reachcliff_Cave "Skyrim:Reachcliff Cave"). She can only be seen by using either the Aura Whisper shout or Detect Life spell. Although she cannot be killed, it is possible to hit her with the Chain Lightning spell if you are pressed against the dining room's eastern wall, thus causing you to be charged with an assault. She wears no clothes, carries no items and cannot otherwise be interacted with.

If you do manage to get to her cell, you can attack her like other NPCs, but not kill her. Doing so will result in a bounty for [The Reach](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Reach "Skyrim:The Reach"). It is possible to reach her via the [Whirlwind Sprint exploit](/wiki/Skyrim:Whirlwind_Sprint#Notes "Skyrim:Whirlwind Sprint") at the rightmost sarcophagus on the eastern wall of the dining hall in Reachcliff Cave.

# Skyrim:Unused NPCs

## Overview

This page lists all **unused NPCs** in Skyrim. None of these NPCs are present in the game; only those in [test cells](/wiki/Skyrim:Test_Cells "Skyrim:Test Cells") can be encountered without spawning them via console commands. Their descriptions on this page treat them as they would be if they were implemented in the game. Some of the locations, [quests](/wiki/Skyrim:Unfinished_Quests "Skyrim:Unfinished Quests"), and buildings mentioned in their descriptions do not exist either, yet are treated on this page as if they do. For information on unused content in general, please see the [related page](/wiki/Skyrim:Unused_Content "Skyrim:Unused Content").

The Affable Gent

The **Affable Gent**, a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H"), is a testing NPC who exists in test cell `WarehouseAmbushes`. He sleeps when there is nobody around, and will ambush you. He wears [noble clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Noble_Clothes "Skyrim:Noble Clothes") and [fur-lined boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Fur-lined_Boots "Skyrim:Fur-lined Boots"), and carries a [honed ancient Nord battle axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Honed_Ancient_Nord_Battle_Axe "Skyrim:Honed Ancient Nord Battle Axe").

Argi Farseer

**Argi Farseer** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [mystic](/wiki/Skyrim:Mystic "Skyrim:Mystic"). She is [Idgrod Ravencrone](/wiki/Skyrim:Idgrod_Ravencrone "Skyrim:Idgrod Ravencrone")'s sister, as indicated by a relationship marker. Argi wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#0006c1d9 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a5 "Skyrim:Clothing"). She carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger") and a [key](/wiki/Skyrim:Key_to_Argi%27s_Hut "Skyrim:Key to Argi's Hut") to her hut located in [Stonehills](/wiki/Skyrim:Stonehills "Skyrim:Stonehills").

She sleeps at 11pm, waking up at 10am. There is under a 20% chance that she will interrupt her sleep at 3am for a three-hour stroll around Stonehills. She spends the rest of her time in her house.

Benkum

**Benkum** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who manages the wagers for [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm")'s arena. He is [Angrenor](/wiki/Skyrim:Angrenor_Once-Honored "Skyrim:Angrenor Once-Honored")'s friend and [Huki](#Huki_Seven-Swords) is his boss. His schedule keeps him in the Bloodworks all the time, never sleeping or moving from his spot. He wears [scaled armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Armor "Skyrim:Scaled Armor") and [scaled boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Boots "Skyrim:Scaled Boots"), and carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

**Dialogue:**

:   *"Hello, hello, hello. Interested in fighting in the Pit? The rules are strict, but if I don't see it, it didn't happen, know what I mean?"*
:   *"It's very important to Huki that all the battles are fair. What's more important to me is that my pay is fair."*
:   *"Prove your valor in the Pit! If you haven't got any valor, prove your pocketbook!"*

Brond

**Brond** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") used in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm")'s arena. He wears [hide armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Armor "Skyrim:Hide Armor") and [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and carries a [dragonplate shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonplate_Shield "Skyrim:Dragonplate Shield"), [steel greatsword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Greatsword "Skyrim:Steel Greatsword"), [Imperial bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Bow "Skyrim:Imperial Bow"), and 30 [iron arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Arrow "Skyrim:Iron Arrow").

He is [Huki](#Huki_Seven-Swords)'s friend, [Edorfin](#Edorfin)'s rival, and [Liesl](#Liesl)'s foe. He sleeps for seven hours starting at 10pm, and spends the rest of his time in the Bloodworks.

**Dialogue:**

:   *"I've been a lot of things in my life. Pit fighter's the one I'm best at."*
:   *"We should get a dragon for the Pit. I'd love to fight one."*
:   *"I've been fighting in the Pit for years. That should tell you something."*
:   *"There's two kinds of Pit fighters -- good ones and dead ones."*
:   *"Ulfric once tried to get me to fight for him, but marching bores me."*
:   *"Steel. Barehanded. Armor. Naked. It's all fighting. I can kill you with a weapon or by myself. No difference."*

Captain Metilius

**Captain Metilius** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [Imperial Soldier](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Soldier_(class) "Skyrim:Imperial Soldier (class)"). He wears an [Imperial soldier outfit](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear#Imperial_Soldier_Armor "Skyrim:Specialty Gear") without a helmet, and carries an [Imperial bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Bow "Skyrim:Imperial Bow"), 10 [steel arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Arrow "Skyrim:Steel Arrow"), and a [steel greatsword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Greatsword "Skyrim:Steel Greatsword").

If both [Bleak Falls Barrow](/wiki/Skyrim:Bleak_Falls_Barrow_(quest) "Skyrim:Bleak Falls Barrow (quest)") and [The Jagged Crown](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Jagged_Crown "Skyrim:The Jagged Crown") (either faction) are in your quest log, he stands near the war room in [Castle Dour](/wiki/Skyrim:Castle_Dour "Skyrim:Castle Dour"). He sleeps for eight hours at midnight, and wanders around Castle Dour from 8am to 8pm. The rest of the time he wanders near or around the castle.

Captain Metilius was meant to aid you in an earlier version of the quest [Dragon Rising](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Rising "Skyrim:Dragon Rising"). In this quest's Legion variation (as there was a Stormcloak equivalent as well) [General Tullius](/wiki/Skyrim:General_Tullius "Skyrim:General Tullius") would've sent you alongside Metilius and a squad of generic friendly soldiers to investigate reports of a dragon at [Fort Hraggstad](/wiki/Skyrim:Fort_Hraggstad "Skyrim:Fort Hraggstad"). Once there, you would find the fort under active attack by the dragon, and Metilius and the soldiers would help you defeat it, similar to [Irileth](/wiki/Skyrim:Irileth "Skyrim:Irileth") and the Whiterun guards in the final version of the quest. It seems that after this quest, he would not get sent out with you any longer, as there are no other relevant leftovers pointing to this.

Castor the Puzzlemaker's Corpse

**Castor the Puzzlemaker's Corpse** is a Dark Elf bandit added by Dragonborn who would appear in [Damphall Mine](/wiki/Skyrim:Damphall_Mine "Skyrim:Damphall Mine") along with a dead Bandit Highwayman. He wears leveled bandit boots and cuirass, and carries a steel dagger, some random loot, and sometimes a small amount of gold. He knows Fast Healing, Magicka Recovery, Stoneflesh, a ward spell, and either a fire, ice, or lightning spell. Despite his name, he's not actually set up to spawn dead.

College Guard

The **College Guard** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [sorcerer](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatSorcerer "Skyrim:CombatSorcerer"). He wears a set of [master robes of Destruction](/wiki/Skyrim:Master_Robes_of_Destruction "Skyrim:Master Robes of Destruction") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#0006b46c "Skyrim:Clothing"). He uses [guard dialogue](/wiki/Skyrim:Guard_Dialogue "Skyrim:Guard Dialogue").

Cow Hand

**Cow Hand** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H"). He and a painted [cow](/wiki/Skyrim:Cow "Skyrim:Cow") are used in the unimplemented quest `CWMission08`, which involves [Giants](/wiki/Skyrim:Giant "Skyrim:Giant") and the [Civil War](/wiki/Skyrim:Civil_War "Skyrim:Civil War"). He wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a6 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a [hat](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209aa "Skyrim:Clothing") and a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a5 "Skyrim:Clothing").

Dawnguard Hunter

He wears a set of [Dawnguard heavy armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard_Heavy_Armor "Skyrim:Dawnguard Heavy Armor") with matching [gauntlets](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard_Heavy_Gauntlets "Skyrim:Dawnguard Heavy Gauntlets") and [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard_Heavy_Boots "Skyrim:Dawnguard Heavy Boots").

DomnaMagia

**DomnaMagia** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") that was meant to be killed by you in a removed vampire-related quest, internally known as `V02`, the second of three such removed quests. She is placed in front of [Solitude](/wiki/Skyrim:Solitude "Skyrim:Solitude")'s [Hall of the Dead](/wiki/Skyrim:Hall_of_the_Dead_(Solitude) "Skyrim:Hall of the Dead (Solitude)"), but her ref is never enabled. She wears necromancer boots and leveled vampire attire. [Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard") changes her inventory, giving her [vampire armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Armor "Skyrim:Vampire Armor").

Do Not Delete Me - needed for export to work

**Do Not Delete Me - needed for export to work** is a naked Nord added by [Dragonborn](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonborn "Skyrim:Dragonborn") who is not located anywhere. Visually, she appears identical to [Frea](/wiki/Skyrim:Frea "Skyrim:Frea"), and might have served as a "face holder" NPC, an NPC on which developers test creating a face before porting it to the final NPC. Her name seems to imply this.

This **Dremora Messenger** would have played some kind of role in the [College of Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:College_of_Winterhold_(faction) "Skyrim:College of Winterhold (faction)") quest [Containment](/wiki/Skyrim:Containment "Skyrim:Containment"), based on his Editor ID of `MG05Messenger`. His inventory is empty and he has no voice type set, so his message is currently unknown. He is one of the few Dremora NPCs with a uniquely designed face, and he wears the very rare [mourners's clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Mourner%27s_Clothes "Skyrim:Mourner's Clothes") with [accompanying hat](/wiki/Skyrim:Mourner%27s_Hat "Skyrim:Mourner's Hat"). The Mages Guild was previously seen using Dremora messengers in *[Oblivion](/wiki/Oblivion:Misdirection "Oblivion:Misdirection")*.

The Drunk Cultist

This **Drunk Cultist** is a [Breton](/wiki/Skyrim:Breton "Skyrim:Breton") mystic who was to be used in the quest [A Night To Remember](/wiki/Skyrim:A_Night_To_Remember "Skyrim:A Night To Remember"). She is placed in [Morvunskar](/wiki/Skyrim:Morvunskar "Skyrim:Morvunskar") at the base of the steps before the portal to Sanguine's realm, but her ref is never enabled. She sleeps for eight hours at midnight, wandering around Morvunskar the rest of the time. She wears warlock boots and leveled warlock robes.

Edorfin

**Edorfin** is a [Wood Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Elf "Skyrim:Wood Elf") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)") used in [Windhelm's](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm") arena. He wears [studded armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Studded_Armor "Skyrim:Studded Armor") and [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and carries a [steel sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Sword "Skyrim:Steel Sword"), [hunting bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Hunting_Bow "Skyrim:Hunting Bow"), and 200 [steel arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Arrow "Skyrim:Steel Arrow").

He is rivals with both [Brond](#Brond) and [Liesl](#Liesl). He sleeps for 8 hours a day starting at noon, and the rest of the time he attacks a ranged target.

**Dialogue:**

:   *"What I lack in strength, I make up for with tenacity."*

Enthralled Wizard

This **Enthralled Wizard** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [bandit wizard](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit_Wizard "Skyrim:Bandit Wizard"). He wears [black mage robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Black_Mage_Robes "Skyrim:Black Mage Robes") and warlock [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots "Skyrim:Boots"). He carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"), and may drop a [scroll](/wiki/Skyrim:Scrolls "Skyrim:Scrolls") and [potions](/wiki/Skyrim:Potions "Skyrim:Potions") when killed.

**Note**: Two separate enthralled wizards are actually encountered in the game, during [The Staff of Magnus](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Staff_of_Magnus "Skyrim:The Staff of Magnus"). Information on these two NPCs can be found on [this page](/wiki/Skyrim:Enthralled_Wizard "Skyrim:Enthralled Wizard").

Escaped Prisoner

The **Escaped Prisoner** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)"). He wears a [roughspun tunic](/wiki/Skyrim:Roughspun_Tunic "Skyrim:Roughspun Tunic") and [footwraps](/wiki/Skyrim:Footwraps "Skyrim:Footwraps"). He was to be used in a [world interaction](/wiki/Skyrim:World_Interactions "Skyrim:World Interactions"). He has no inventory.

### Eyldi the Bear

|  |
| --- |
| **This article could benefit from an image**. See [Help:Images](/wiki/Help:Images "Help:Images") for information on how to upload images. Please remove this template from the page when finished. |

| Eyldi the Bear | | | |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Race | [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") | Gender | Female |
| Level |  | Class | [(?)](/wiki/Category:Pages_Missing_Data "Category:Pages Missing Data") |
| [RefID](/wiki/Skyrim:NPCs#Console_IDs "Skyrim:NPCs") | N/A | [BaseID](/wiki/Skyrim:NPCs#Console_IDs "Skyrim:NPCs") | N/A |
| Other Information | | | |
| Voice Type | [FemaleNord](/wiki/Category:Skyrim-Voice-FemaleNord "Category:Skyrim-Voice-FemaleNord") | | |

**Eyldi the Bear** is a retired [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") hero. She would have been the owner of [Bear's Cave Mill](http://content3.uesp.net/oblivion/cs/cslist.php?game=sr&formid=0x00018c8e%7C). While no NPC data exists for her, the voice files for her greetings remain in the game, possibly due to a mix-up which has them marked for [Half-Moon Mill](/wiki/Skyrim:Half-Moon_Mill "Skyrim:Half-Moon Mill"). In addition, [Jorn](/wiki/Skyrim:Jorn "Skyrim:Jorn") of the [Bards College](/wiki/Skyrim:Bards_College "Skyrim:Bards College") still mentions her, and unused lines for [Hold Guards](/wiki/Skyrim:Guards "Skyrim:Guards") also make mention of her.

:   *"Come in, come in! Welcome to the Bear's Cave!"*
:   *"Company's a rare delight. Stay a spell."*
:   *"I trust the roads are safe? I take good care of my land."*
:   *"Do the bards still sing of my adventures?"*
:   *"Time was, the name Eyldi the Bear could make brigands flee for miles around."*
:   *"The world has changed since I was young. Let there be new heroes. I’ve earned a rest."*

Due to her heroic feats, Stormcloak-aligned guards would have been all too familiar with Eyldi and could have said:

:   *"I grew up hearing songs about Eyldi the Bear! She wrestled a storm out of the sky!"*

Imperial-aligned guards did not see Eyldi in the same light, however:

:   *"Just my luck, stuck in the sticks with this crazy old woman."*

Fellow Prisoner

The **Fellow Prisoner** is a random bandit who fights in [Windhelm's](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm") arena. He or she carries an [iron sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Sword "Skyrim:Iron Sword"), an [iron shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Shield "Skyrim:Iron Shield"), and a lockpick.

Grushnag

**Grushnag** is an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") warrior who travels with a [Khajiit caravan](/wiki/Skyrim:Khajiit_Traders "Skyrim:Khajiit Traders"). He wanders around the caravan when stopped. He wears [scaled horn armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Horn_Armor "Skyrim:Scaled Horn Armor"), [scaled boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Boots "Skyrim:Scaled Boots"), and [scaled bracers](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Bracers "Skyrim:Scaled Bracers").

They both wear executioner outfits and carry a [headsman's axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Headsman%27s_Axe "Skyrim:Headsman's Axe").

Henrik

**Henrik** is a dead [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)"). He wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a6 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a5 "Skyrim:Clothing"). He has no voicetype assigned, and carries nothing. He was meant to appear in [The Wolf Queen Awakened](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Wolf_Queen_Awakened "Skyrim:The Wolf Queen Awakened") if his Editor ID of `MS06Victim` is anything to go by.

Herebane Sorenshield

**Herebane Sorenshield** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") citizen. He wears an outfit of [Imperial armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear#Imperial_Soldier_Armor "Skyrim:Specialty Gear") with a full-faced helmet and carries a leveled amount of gold. He patrols a small area around him, but does not converse with anyone.

There are two items present in test cell `QASmoke` that appear to be named after him: an elven shield named [Herebane's Fortress](/wiki/Skyrim:Herebane%27s_Fortress "Skyrim:Herebane's Fortress") that resists frost damage and an elven sword named [Herebane's Courage](/wiki/Skyrim:Herebane%27s_Courage "Skyrim:Herebane's Courage") that does fire damage.

Hrolmir

**Hrolmir** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") involved in the quest [The Pursuit](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Pursuit "Skyrim:The Pursuit"). Besides his name and Editor ID, he has no other unique traits.

Huki Seven-Swords

**Huki Seven-Swords** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who works as jailkeeper in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm")'s arena. She is [Brond](#Brond) and [Brunwulf](/wiki/Skyrim:Brunwulf_Free-Winter "Skyrim:Brunwulf Free-Winter")'s friend, and [Benkum](#Benkum) is her employee. She stands in the Bloodworks all day. She wears [studded armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Studded_Armor "Skyrim:Studded Armor") and [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and carries an [iron greatsword](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Greatsword "Skyrim:Iron Greatsword").

**Dialogue:**

:   *"Looking for something?"*
:   *"Real Nords like to prove their mettle in the pit."*
:   *"Just between you and me, I wouldn't mind if the Empire just up and left for good."*
:   *"I'd love get one of those Imperial weasels under my boot..."*
:   *"All right, meat, it's time for you to earn your freedom."*

:   *"What is this, you think you're clever? If you want to fight your way out of here, you have to do it in the Pit. Gotta give the crowd a show. Luckily there's no shortage of Pit bait around here. Now get in there and earn your freedom."*
:   **What do I have to do?**
:   *"We don't just let people rot in cells until they've served their time. Here, you fight. If you win, you go free. If you lose, well, you'll know the freedom of Sovngarde if you're lucky."*
:   **I'm ready to go.**
:   *"All right. Head to the pit when you think you're ready."*
:   **I want to fight [Alain Dufont](/wiki/Skyrim:Alain_Dufont "Skyrim:Alain Dufont").** (Only if you have reached stage 20 and have not yet reached stage 40 of quest [Mourning Never Comes](/wiki/Skyrim:Mourning_Never_Comes "Skyrim:Mourning Never Comes").)
:   *"Well aren't you in luck. He's the next piece of gutter scum on the docket. Between you and me, I hope you cleave his arrogant head open."*

Imperial Officer

The **Imperial Officer** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") soldier. He wears an [Imperial soldier outfit](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear#Imperial_Soldier_Armor "Skyrim:Specialty Gear") without a helm, and carries an [Imperial light shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Light_Shield "Skyrim:Imperial Light Shield"), [Imperial sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Sword "Skyrim:Imperial Sword"), and [steel dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Dagger "Skyrim:Steel Dagger"). He was intended to be used for [Civil War](/wiki/Skyrim:Civil_War "Skyrim:Civil War") sieges. He patrols the area around him.

Imperial Wizard

The **Imperial Wizard** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") mage. He wears an [Imperial guard outfit](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear#Imperial_Soldier_Armor "Skyrim:Specialty Gear") (light armor), and carries a leveled warlock [dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Dagger "Skyrim:Dagger"). Possible spells include [Firebolt](/wiki/Skyrim:Firebolt "Skyrim:Firebolt"), [Healing](/wiki/Skyrim:Healing "Skyrim:Healing"), [Flames](/wiki/Skyrim:Flames "Skyrim:Flames"), [Oakflesh](/wiki/Skyrim:Oakflesh "Skyrim:Oakflesh"), and [Lesser Ward](/wiki/Skyrim:Lesser_Ward "Skyrim:Lesser Ward"), with the perks Regeneration and Augmented Flames. He was intended to be used for [Civil War](/wiki/Skyrim:Civil_War "Skyrim:Civil War") sieges.

Insane College Wizard

The **Insane College Wizard** is a [High Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:High_Elf "Skyrim:High Elf") mage used in the unimplemented quest [Rogue Wizard](/wiki/Skyrim:Unfinished_Quests#Rogue_Wizard "Skyrim:Unfinished Quests"). He has been wandering a hold of Skyrim, irritating the local Jarl, and the [College of Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:College_of_Winterhold_(faction) "Skyrim:College of Winterhold (faction)") has severed all ties with him but must save their reputation by killing him. He wears [expert robes of Destruction](/wiki/Skyrim:Expert_Robes_of_Destruction "Skyrim:Expert Robes of Destruction") with a pair of boots.

**Spells**:

- Fast Healing
- Fireball
- Firebolt
- Flame Cloak
- Flames
- Frostbite
- Frost Cloak
- Healing
- Ice Spike
- Ice Storm
- Lightning Bolt
- Sparks
- Greater Ward
- Lesser Ward
- Steadfast Ward

Jolf

**Jolf** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") citizen. He wears a leveled farmer's tunic.

Liesl

**Liesl** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who fights in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm")'s arena. She is [Edorfin](#Edorfin)'s rival and [Brond](#Brond)'s foe. She sleeps at midnight for seven hours, and then wanders around the Bloodworks for the rest of the time. She wears [scaled horn armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Horn_Armor "Skyrim:Scaled Horn Armor") and [scaled boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Boots "Skyrim:Scaled Boots"), and carries an [iron shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Shield "Skyrim:Iron Shield") and [iron battleaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Battleaxe "Skyrim:Iron Battleaxe").

**Dialogue:**

:   *"Stand away from me, or I'll crack you like an egg."*
:   *"What are you looking at? Scared of strong women?"*
:   *"You look mighty tasty. Better not stick around for supper."*

Makhel Abbas

**Makhel Abbas** is an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") bandit used in the nonexistent [Civil War](/wiki/Skyrim:Civil_War "Skyrim:Civil War") quest `CWMission11`. He hunts animals while the quest is active. He wears leveled bandit armor, with a 50% chance of having gauntlets or a helmet, and carries a leveled dagger, two-handed weapon, and gold.

### Marauder

**Marauders** are [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") bandits used in the E3 demo of Skyrim in the location Bleak Falls Tower.

The marauder archer wears ancient Nord [armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Armor "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Armor"), [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Boots "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Boots"), and [gauntlets](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Gauntlets "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Gauntlets"), and he carries an [Orcish bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Bow "Skyrim:Orcish Bow") with 11 [Orcish arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Arrow "Skyrim:Orcish Arrow"), along with two [steel daggers](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Dagger "Skyrim:Steel Dagger") and leveled gold, with a 25% chance to carry a lockpick. He has a few [Archery](/wiki/Skyrim:Archery "Skyrim:Archery") perks.

Mazgak

**Mazgak** is an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") bandit who would've appeared in the quest [Ill Met By Moonlight](/wiki/Skyrim:Ill_Met_By_Moonlight "Skyrim:Ill Met By Moonlight") as a hunter. Mithorpa Nasyal

**Mithorpa Nasyal** is a [Dark Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Elf "Skyrim:Dark Elf") [farmer](/wiki/Skyrim:Farmer_(class) "Skyrim:Farmer (class)") at Barleydark Farm, a location near [Sleeping Tree Camp](/wiki/Skyrim:Sleeping_Tree_Camp "Skyrim:Sleeping Tree Camp") that also does not appear in the game. He is the only one in his faction.

He wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothes#000261c0 "Skyrim:Clothes") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#000261bd "Skyrim:Boots"). He carries his [house key](/wiki/Skyrim:Mithorpa%27s_House_Key "Skyrim:Mithorpa's House Key") and an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

When interacted with, he will at first say, *"Come to gawk at the Dark Elf?"* and then apologize, *"I mean no offense. I am but a humble farmer. Not a... a great wanderer, like you."* If you are a Dark Elf, he may also be found saying, *"Well look at us. Like two gray specks in the snow."*

Muril

**Muril** is a [High Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:High_Elf "Skyrim:High Elf") [elemental mage](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatMageElemental "Skyrim:CombatMageElemental"). Along with [Ancano](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancano "Skyrim:Ancano") and his underling [Estormo](/wiki/Skyrim:Estormo "Skyrim:Estormo"), Muril is part of a [Thalmor](/wiki/Skyrim:Thalmor "Skyrim:Thalmor") infiltration unit attempting to undermine the [College of Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:College_of_Winterhold_(faction) "Skyrim:College of Winterhold (faction)"). He is set to follow Estormo during [The Staff of Magnus](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Staff_of_Magnus "Skyrim:The Staff of Magnus").

A Nord

This **Nord** warrior was to be used in a [random encounter](/wiki/Skyrim:World_Interactions "Skyrim:World Interactions"), or was to be a template for NPCs used in such an encounter. He wears leveled [light armor and boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Armor#Light_Armor "Skyrim:Armor"), and carries a number of leveled items, including food and a weapon.

### Pit Fan

.jpg)

A Pit Fan

**Pit Fans** are used in [Windhelm's](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm") arena. They exist in cell `WindhelmPitEntrance`. They ignore all combat. They are intended to cheer, but their AI packages are not actually set up to do so. They wear boots and clothes, with the outfits varying between fans. They have no voices. Presumably these Pit Fans were placeholder NPCs, and Windhelm citizens were meant to take up their spots if the Pit was finalized.

Player Friend

**Player Friend** is an unlucky [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") used in the quest [With Friends Like These...](/wiki/Skyrim:With_Friends_Like_These... "Skyrim:With Friends Like These..."). He is friendly with you. He wanders around [Candlehearth Hall](/wiki/Skyrim:Candlehearth_Hall "Skyrim:Candlehearth Hall"), acting as a normal NPC would. He wears and carries nothing.

His name hints at the original direction of the related quest. Pre-release reports for Skyrim claimed that the [Dark Brotherhood](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Brotherhood "Skyrim:Dark Brotherhood") would make you assassinate NPCs whom you had befriended, although this didn't make it into the final game.

Rogen

**Rogen** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") added by the [Dawnguard add-on](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard"). He wears a set of [vampire robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Robes "Skyrim:Vampire Robes") with matching [hood](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Hood "Skyrim:Vampire Hood"), [gloves](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Gloves "Skyrim:Vampire Gloves"), and [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Boots "Skyrim:Vampire Boots"). In combat, he relies on an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe").

Dark Elf Saarthal Miner

There are six **Saarthal Miners**, three of which are [Wood Elves](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Elf "Skyrim:Wood Elf") and three of which are [Dark Elves](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Elf "Skyrim:Dark Elf"). Each of them carries a [pickaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Pickaxe "Skyrim:Pickaxe"). They all wear [miner's clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Miner%27s_Clothes "Skyrim:Miner's Clothes") with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#00080699 "Skyrim:Boots"), and two of them also wear a pair of [gloves](/wiki/Skyrim:Gloves#000261c1 "Skyrim:Gloves"). They are present in [Saarthal](/wiki/Skyrim:Saarthal "Skyrim:Saarthal").

They all share the same dialogue:

:   *"I'm just doing what I'm told."*
:   *"Gods, it's cold. They'd better find something good in there to make this worthwhile."*
:   *"As long as the [College](/wiki/Skyrim:College_of_Winterhold_(faction) "Skyrim:College of Winterhold (faction)") keeps paying, I'll keep digging."*
:   *"For thousand-year old ruins, they're not much to look at."*
:   *"Can't imagine what the College expects to find in here."*

Spirit of the Ancient Traveler

He wears Steel Plate Armor, Steel Plate Boots, and Steel Plate Gauntlets—versions that you cannot wear—and carries a [steel sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Sword "Skyrim:Steel Sword").

Steirod

**Steirod** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [lumberjack](/wiki/Skyrim:Lumberjack "Skyrim:Lumberjack") who lives together with his brother [Trilf](#Trilf) at Irontree Mill. He wears some farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261c0 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261bd "Skyrim:Clothing"). He carries a [key](/wiki/Skyrim:Irontree_Mill_House_Key "Skyrim:Irontree Mill House Key") to the Irontree Mill and wields an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

Much unlike his brother Trilf, he will not want anything to do with you and will quickly turn you away:

:   *"We got everything in hand here. You aren't needed 'round here."*

He will be a little nicer if you continue conversation, giving a simple description of what he and Trilif do for a living:

:   *"We chop trees, we mill the wood. What more would you want to know?"*

He will then elaborate on this:

:   *"It's a hard way to make a living, but we find ways to make ends meet."*

He will then shift back to the same attitude he started the conversation with:

:   *"We don't take kindly to people snooping around our business."*

If his cold comments have not driven you away yet, he will state one last comment that might scare you away:

:   *"These axes can chop a lot more than wood, you know."*

Guards protecting their mill will also comment on the two brothers, stating things such as:

:   *"I used to serve with Trilf and Steirod. Good guys, but don't get on their bad side, you know?"*
:   *"These boys do good work for the Empire!"*

They also would have told you about Steirod's strength by recalling:

:   *"I once saw Steirod tear a whole tree out the ground with his bare hands. Glad he's on our side!"*

Sulvar the Steady

**Sulvar the Steady** is an [elderly](/wiki/Skyrim:Elder "Skyrim:Elder") [mystic](/wiki/Skyrim:Mystic "Skyrim:Mystic") who watches the Wheel in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm"). He wears a [belted tunic](/wiki/Skyrim:Belted_Tunic "Skyrim:Belted Tunic"), some [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#0001be1b "Skyrim:Boots"), and a [hat](/wiki/Skyrim:Hat#000209aa "Skyrim:Hat"). He also carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). He sleeps at noon for four hours and at 2am for four hours. He spends the rest of his time in the Wheelhouse.

**Dialogue:**

:   *"My job? Well, I'm the Watcher of the Wheel! Which is... exactly what it sounds like. I watch the Wheel."*
:   *"Watching the wheel isn't as simple as you might think. Have to inspect all the way around the ice, make sure it's not melting."*
:   *"Don't get too many folks up here these days. Sometimes I think they've forgotten about me."*

### Talib

| Talib | | | |

Talib

**Talib** is a [Redguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Redguard "Skyrim:Redguard") [mage](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatMageElemental "Skyrim:CombatMageElemental") living in [Stonehills](/wiki/Skyrim:Stonehills "Skyrim:Stonehills"). He is rivals with [Jesper](/wiki/Skyrim:Jesper "Skyrim:Jesper"). [Gestur Rockbreaker](/wiki/Skyrim:Gestur_Rockbreaker "Skyrim:Gestur Rockbreaker") is his father. He sleeps for eight hours at noon, then wanders around outside for two hours starting at 9pm. He works in the mine from 8am to 6pm, then visits [Argi](#Argi_Farseer) until 9pm. From 11pm to 8am he can be found inside his house. He wears a [belted tunic](/wiki/Skyrim:Belted_Tunic "Skyrim:Belted Tunic"), some [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#0001be1b "Skyrim:Boots"). He also carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). Despite being a mage, he knows no spells.

**Dialogue:**

:   **Gestur:** *"How goes it, son?"*
:   **Talib:** *"I'm fine, father. Work is fine."*
:   **Gestur:** *"Good, good. It's honest work, son. No shame in that."*
:   **Talib:** *"Of course not. I never thought otherwise."*
:   **Gestur:** *"Right. Well, you let me know if you need anything."*

Tasius Tragus

**Tasius Tragus** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") soldier living in [Dragon Bridge](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Bridge "Skyrim:Dragon Bridge"). He works for [General Tullius](/wiki/Skyrim:General_Tullius "Skyrim:General Tullius"), and [Varnius Junius](/wiki/Skyrim:Varnius_Junius "Skyrim:Varnius Junius") works for him. [Faida](/wiki/Skyrim:Faida "Skyrim:Faida") is his girlfriend and [Julienne Lylvieve](/wiki/Skyrim:Julienne_Lylvieve "Skyrim:Julienne Lylvieve") is his other lover. He sleeps for eight hours at midnight and spends the rest of his time wandering around a small area. He wears [scaled armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Armor "Skyrim:Scaled Armor") and [scaled boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Boots "Skyrim:Scaled Boots"), and carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). Tasius originally had the role that [Commander Maro](/wiki/Skyrim:Commander_Maro "Skyrim:Commander Maro") has in the final game, as he was meant to be the local Legion head. Varnius Junius still mentions Tasius in the final game.

Terek

**Terek** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") citizen who illegally lives in [Breezehome](/wiki/Skyrim:Breezehome "Skyrim:Breezehome"). He sleeps for eight hours at midnight. He wears farmer clothes and carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). A burial urn for Terek is present in Whiterun's Hall of the Dead, and will activate in case he dies.

**Dialogue:**

:   *"Don't let the door slam into your backside on the way out."*
:   *"Now if ya don't mind, my house could do with a bit of dusting."*
:   *"You're in my home. That's right. This here place, Breezehome, it belongs to old Terek."*
:   *"Oho! And what do you want, hmm?"*
:   *"You can't kick me out of here. You want me gone, you're gonna have to kill me."*
:   *"Now I don't care about no deeds, or laws, or other nonsense like that. This house was empty, so I settled in. And now it's mine."*
:   *"I'm a generous man, to those who treat me right. Might be I'll even let you live here with me, if you mind your manners."*

TestJeffBCarryWaterBucket

**TestJeffBCarryWaterBucket** is a Nord warrior. He wears default Redguard clothing when awake and a Psijic Monk outfit when sleeping, but never sleeps. He also carries iron armor and gauntlets, and knows the [Phantom Form](/wiki/Skyrim:Phantom_Form "Skyrim:Phantom Form") shout. He does not exist in any cell and does not say anything special. He does, however, use CarryActorScript, and was probably used to test how NPCs carry the bucket item used in specific animations. It's very likely that finalizing the process never got completed, seeing as the animation is broken in the final game.

Thaer

**Thaer** is a [Wood Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Elf "Skyrim:Wood Elf") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)") and [carriage](/wiki/Skyrim:Carriage "Skyrim:Carriage") driver added by the Dawnguard add-on. He wears farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#0006c1da "Skyrim:Clothing") and a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261bd "Skyrim:Clothing"), and carries an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe"). He shares his dialogue with other carriage drivers. He might have been planned as replacement for the Nord [Thaer](/wiki/Skyrim:Thaer "Skyrim:Thaer").

Thjollod

**Thjollod** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") bandit who was meant to appear as a hunter in [Ill Met By Moonlight](/wiki/Skyrim:Ill_Met_By_Moonlight "Skyrim:Ill Met By Moonlight"). He patrols [Bloated Man's Grotto](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloated_Man%27s_Grotto "Skyrim:Bloated Man's Grotto") and follows you in between stages 75 and 80 of the related quest. He wears [iron armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Armor "Skyrim:Iron Armor"), [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and [hide bracers](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Bracers "Skyrim:Hide Bracers"), and carries a leveled bandit sword, a leveled bandit bow, and leveled gold (plus 25 extra gold).

Trilf

**Trilf** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [lumberjack](/wiki/Skyrim:Lumberjack "Skyrim:Lumberjack") who lives together with his brother [Steirod](#Steirod) at Irontree Mill, located between the [Thalmor Embassy](/wiki/Skyrim:Thalmor_Embassy "Skyrim:Thalmor Embassy") and [Ironback Hideout](/wiki/Skyrim:Ironback_Hideout "Skyrim:Ironback Hideout"). He wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a6 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a5 "Skyrim:Clothing"). He carries a [key](/wiki/Skyrim:Irontree_Mill_House_Key "Skyrim:Irontree Mill House Key") to the Irontree Mill and wields an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe").

Trilf greets you quite warmly when you arrive at the mill:

:   *"Hey, welcome to the mill!"*

He will then reveal to you that his brother and him are veterans:

:   *"My brother and me bought this place when we left the army. Boy, did we lose a bundle, but at least we're not fighting the war!"*

He will then elaborate on his days in the army:

:   *"We served our time in the [Legion](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Legion "Skyrim:Imperial Legion"). Now we're just trying to make it day to day.*"

He will continue on about the financial difficulties he and Steirod are facing:

:   *"We're making our money back, bit by bit. Every extra bit of work helps..."*

Trius

**Trius** is a Breton bandit wizard. A [note](/wiki/Skyrim:For_Shelly "Skyrim:For Shelly") by him to someone named Shelly can be found at his camp near [Pilgrim's Trench](/wiki/Skyrim:Pilgrim%27s_Trench "Skyrim:Pilgrim's Trench"). He is involved in the unfinished quest [Trius' Trinket](/wiki/Skyrim:Trius%27_Trinket "Skyrim:Trius' Trinket"), where you would have had to fetch a jewelry box for him in exchange for a new spell.

Uglarz

**Uglarz** is an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H"). She wears [ragged trousers](/wiki/Skyrim:Ragged_Trousers "Skyrim:Ragged Trousers") with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#00080699 "Skyrim:Boots"), and carries an [Orcish bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Bow "Skyrim:Orcish Bow") and [Orcish dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Dagger "Skyrim:Orcish Dagger"), along with a selection of [lower-class items and gold](/wiki/Skyrim:Leveled_Lists#LootCitizenPocketsPoor "Skyrim:Leveled Lists"). She is placed inside of Mauhulakh's Longhouse, but her ref is permanently disabled.

Uglarz appears to have been intended to be the missing wife of [Chief Mauhulakh](/wiki/Skyrim:Chief_Mauhulakh "Skyrim:Chief Mauhulakh") in an [unfinished quest](/wiki/Skyrim:Unfinished_Quests "Skyrim:Unfinished Quests") called `NarzulburFreeFormWifeQuest` which also wasn't implemented in the game.

At the start of the quest to find her, Chief Mauhulakh speaks of her: *"I think of Uglarz. An old friend. When we were little she ran to the woods to hunt, and never came back. If someone found her, she would make an excellent mate."*

Before she comes back to the stronghold, Uglarz has a few lines of unique dialogue:

:   *"I am Uglarz, huntress of all."*
:   *"It was my home. But I left. The woods felt more alive, at least for a time."*
:   *"Mauhulakh. I remember him. Little, scrawny, fellow."*
:   *"Ha! Now that I would like to see. I may go back there some day."*
:   *"Always thought that old mine had more to it. Just like Mauhulakh to smash his way to good fortune."*
:   *"I'm going back to [Narzulbur](/wiki/Skyrim:Narzulbur "Skyrim:Narzulbur"). Want to see how it's come."*

After Uglarz returns to Narzulbur, she has a few more lines of unique dialogue:

:   *"You were right about this place. And about Mauhulakh."*
:   *"This was worth coming back for."*
:   *"[Urog](/wiki/Skyrim:Urog "Skyrim:Urog") is a good learner. I'm glad I have the chance to teach her. Makes me not miss the woods as much."*
:   *"Urog and I still hunt a lot, so it's not like I'm cooped up in the longhouse."*

When Uglarz is in Narzulbur, Urog will comment on her hunting skills, saying:

:   *"Uglarz is a great huntress. Perhaps she can teach me something new."*
:   *"I never would have thought, but Uglarz is an even better hunter than [Yatul](/wiki/Skyrim:Yatul "Skyrim:Yatul") was. The things I'm learning now..."*

Urchin

The **Urchin** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who exists in `WICourierCell`. He stays in a set location, never moving. He may have been meant for a [Dark Brotherhood](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Brotherhood "Skyrim:Dark Brotherhood") quest, as his Editor ID is `DBCourierUrchin`. He wears [ragged robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Ragged_Robes "Skyrim:Ragged Robes") along with [ragged boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Ragged_Boots "Skyrim:Ragged Boots").

Viding

**Viding** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who was meant to appear as a hunter in [Ill Met By Moonlight](/wiki/Skyrim:Ill_Met_By_Moonlight "Skyrim:Ill Met By Moonlight"). He patrols [Bloated Man's Grotto](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloated_Man%27s_Grotto "Skyrim:Bloated Man's Grotto") and follows you in between stages 66 and 80 of the related quest. He wears farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261c0 "Skyrim:Clothing"), [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and [hide bracers](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Bracers "Skyrim:Hide Bracers"), and carries a leveled potion of Restore Magicka, leveled gold, and an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

**Spells**:

- Firebolt
- Flames
- Healing
- Oakflesh
- Lesser Ward

### Yisra

This article is about the unused NPC in the base game. For the burnt corpse in the base game, see [Yisra](/wiki/Skyrim:Yisra "Skyrim:Yisra").

|  |

| Other Information | | | |

| [Stamina](/wiki/Skyrim:Stamina "Skyrim:Stamina") | 50 | | |
