Insane College Wizard

The **Insane College Wizard** is a [High Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:High_Elf "Skyrim:High Elf") mage used in the unimplemented quest [Rogue Wizard](/wiki/Skyrim:Unfinished_Quests#Rogue_Wizard "Skyrim:Unfinished Quests"). He has been wandering a hold of Skyrim, irritating the local Jarl, and the [College of Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:College_of_Winterhold_(faction) "Skyrim:College of Winterhold (faction)") has severed all ties with him but must save their reputation by killing him. He wears [expert robes of Destruction](/wiki/Skyrim:Expert_Robes_of_Destruction "Skyrim:Expert Robes of Destruction") with a pair of boots.

**Spells**:

- Fast Healing
- Fireball
- Firebolt
- Flame Cloak
- Flames
- Frostbite
- Frost Cloak
- Healing
- Ice Spike
- Ice Storm
- Lightning Bolt
- Sparks
- Greater Ward
- Lesser Ward
- Steadfast Ward

Jolf

| [Faction(s)](/wiki/Skyrim:Factions "Skyrim:Factions") | [CrimeFactionEastmarch](/wiki/Skyrim:CrimeFactionEastmarch "Skyrim:CrimeFactionEastmarch"); [TownWindhelmFaction](/wiki/Skyrim:TownWindhelmFaction "Skyrim:TownWindhelmFaction"); [Windhelm Pit](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm_Pit "Skyrim:Windhelm Pit") (Pit Lord) | | |

Liesl

**Liesl** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who fights in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm")'s arena. She is [Edorfin](#Edorfin)'s rival and [Brond](#Brond)'s foe. She sleeps at midnight for seven hours, and then wanders around the Bloodworks for the rest of the time. She wears [scaled horn armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Horn_Armor "Skyrim:Scaled Horn Armor") and [scaled boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Boots "Skyrim:Scaled Boots"), and carries an [iron shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Shield "Skyrim:Iron Shield") and [iron battleaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Battleaxe "Skyrim:Iron Battleaxe").

**Dialogue:**

:   *"Stand away from me, or I'll crack you like an egg."*
:   *"What are you looking at? Scared of strong women?"*
:   *"You look mighty tasty. Better not stick around for supper."*

Makhel Abbas

**Makhel Abbas** is an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") bandit used in the nonexistent [Civil War](/wiki/Skyrim:Civil_War "Skyrim:Civil War") quest `CWMission11`. He hunts animals while the quest is active. He wears leveled bandit armor, with a 50% chance of having gauntlets or a helmet, and carries a leveled dagger, two-handed weapon, and gold.

### Marauder

Marauder

This marauder wears [Orcish armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Armor "Skyrim:Orcish Armor"), [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Boots "Skyrim:Orcish Boots"), and [gauntlets](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Gauntlets "Skyrim:Orcish Gauntlets"), and carries an [Orcish sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Sword "Skyrim:Orcish Sword") and [shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Shield "Skyrim:Orcish Shield"), along with leveled gold and a leveled two-handed weapon. He has multiple perks fitting his class.

Marauder

The marauder archer wears ancient Nord [armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Armor "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Armor"), [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Boots "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Boots"), and [gauntlets](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Gauntlets "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Gauntlets"), and he carries an [Orcish bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Bow "Skyrim:Orcish Bow") with 11 [Orcish arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Arrow "Skyrim:Orcish Arrow"), along with two [steel daggers](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Dagger "Skyrim:Steel Dagger") and leveled gold, with a 25% chance to carry a lockpick. He has a few [Archery](/wiki/Skyrim:Archery "Skyrim:Archery") perks.

Mazgak

**Mazgak** is an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") bandit who would've appeared in the quest [Ill Met By Moonlight](/wiki/Skyrim:Ill_Met_By_Moonlight "Skyrim:Ill Met By Moonlight") as a hunter. She wears [hide armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Armor "Skyrim:Hide Armor") and [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and carries a [long bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Long_Bow "Skyrim:Long Bow"), leveled gold, a leveled poison of Damage Stamina, and a leveled [sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Sword "Skyrim:Sword"), with a 75% chance of carrying 10 [iron arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Arrow "Skyrim:Iron Arrow").

Mithorpa Nasyal

**Mithorpa Nasyal** is a [Dark Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Elf "Skyrim:Dark Elf") [farmer](/wiki/Skyrim:Farmer_(class) "Skyrim:Farmer (class)") at Barleydark Farm, a location near [Sleeping Tree Camp](/wiki/Skyrim:Sleeping_Tree_Camp "Skyrim:Sleeping Tree Camp") that also does not appear in the game. He is the only one in his faction.

He wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothes#000261c0 "Skyrim:Clothes") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#000261bd "Skyrim:Boots"). He carries his [house key](/wiki/Skyrim:Mithorpa%27s_House_Key "Skyrim:Mithorpa's House Key") and an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

When interacted with, he will at first say, *"Come to gawk at the Dark Elf?"* and then apologize, *"I mean no offense. I am but a humble farmer. Not a... a great wanderer, like you."* If you are a Dark Elf, he may also be found saying, *"Well look at us. Like two gray specks in the snow."*

Muril

**Muril** is a [High Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:High_Elf "Skyrim:High Elf") [elemental mage](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatMageElemental "Skyrim:CombatMageElemental"). Along with [Ancano](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancano "Skyrim:Ancano") and his underling [Estormo](/wiki/Skyrim:Estormo "Skyrim:Estormo"), Muril is part of a [Thalmor](/wiki/Skyrim:Thalmor "Skyrim:Thalmor") infiltration unit attempting to undermine the [College of Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:College_of_Winterhold_(faction) "Skyrim:College of Winterhold (faction)"). He is set to follow Estormo during [The Staff of Magnus](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Staff_of_Magnus "Skyrim:The Staff of Magnus").

.jpg)

A Pit Fan

**Pit Fans** are used in [Windhelm's](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm") arena. They exist in cell `WindhelmPitEntrance`. They ignore all combat. They are intended to cheer, but their AI packages are not actually set up to do so. They wear boots and clothes, with the outfits varying between fans. They have no voices. Presumably these Pit Fans were placeholder NPCs, and Windhelm citizens were meant to take up their spots if the Pit was finalized.

Player Friend

**Player Friend** is an unlucky [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") used in the quest [With Friends Like These...](/wiki/Skyrim:With_Friends_Like_These... "Skyrim:With Friends Like These..."). He is friendly with you. He wanders around [Candlehearth Hall](/wiki/Skyrim:Candlehearth_Hall "Skyrim:Candlehearth Hall"), acting as a normal NPC would. He wears and carries nothing.

His name hints at the original direction of the related quest. Pre-release reports for Skyrim claimed that the [Dark Brotherhood](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Brotherhood "Skyrim:Dark Brotherhood") would make you assassinate NPCs whom you had befriended, although this didn't make it into the final game.

Rogen

**Rogen** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") added by the [Dawnguard add-on](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard"). He wears a set of [vampire robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Robes "Skyrim:Vampire Robes") with matching [hood](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Hood "Skyrim:Vampire Hood"), [gloves](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Gloves "Skyrim:Vampire Gloves"), and [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Boots "Skyrim:Vampire Boots"). In combat, he relies on an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe").

Dark Elf Saarthal Miner

There are six **Saarthal Miners**, three of which are [Wood Elves](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Elf "Skyrim:Wood Elf") and three of which are [Dark Elves](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Elf "Skyrim:Dark Elf"). Each of them carries a [pickaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Pickaxe "Skyrim:Pickaxe"). They all wear [miner's clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Miner%27s_Clothes "Skyrim:Miner's Clothes") with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#00080699 "Skyrim:Boots"), and two of them also wear a pair of [gloves](/wiki/Skyrim:Gloves#000261c1 "Skyrim:Gloves"). They are present in [Saarthal](/wiki/Skyrim:Saarthal "Skyrim:Saarthal").

They all share the same dialogue:

:   *"I'm just doing what I'm told."*
:   *"Gods, it's cold. They'd better find something good in there to make this worthwhile."*
:   *"As long as the [College](/wiki/Skyrim:College_of_Winterhold_(faction) "Skyrim:College of Winterhold (faction)") keeps paying, I'll keep digging."*
:   *"For thousand-year old ruins, they're not much to look at."*
:   *"Can't imagine what the College expects to find in here."*

Skeggr

**Skeggr** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") used in the quest [The Pursuit](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Pursuit "Skyrim:The Pursuit"). Besides his name and Editor ID, he has no other unique traits.

Spirit of the Ancient Traveler

**Spirit of the Ancient Traveler** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") bandit ghost in [Wayward Pass](/wiki/Skyrim:Wayward_Pass "Skyrim:Wayward Pass"). He wears Steel Plate Armor, Steel Plate Boots, and Steel Plate Gauntlets—versions that you cannot wear—and carries a [steel sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Sword "Skyrim:Steel Sword").

Steirod

**Steirod** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [lumberjack](/wiki/Skyrim:Lumberjack "Skyrim:Lumberjack") who lives together with his brother [Trilf](#Trilf) at Irontree Mill. He wears some farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261c0 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261bd "Skyrim:Clothing"). He carries a [key](/wiki/Skyrim:Irontree_Mill_House_Key "Skyrim:Irontree Mill House Key") to the Irontree Mill and wields an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

Much unlike his brother Trilf, he will not want anything to do with you and will quickly turn you away:

:   *"We got everything in hand here. You aren't needed 'round here."*

He will be a little nicer if you continue conversation, giving a simple description of what he and Trilif do for a living:

:   *"We chop trees, we mill the wood. What more would you want to know?"*

He will then elaborate on this:

:   *"It's a hard way to make a living, but we find ways to make ends meet."*

He will then shift back to the same attitude he started the conversation with:

:   *"We don't take kindly to people snooping around our business."*

If his cold comments have not driven you away yet, he will state one last comment that might scare you away:

:   *"These axes can chop a lot more than wood, you know."*

Guards protecting their mill will also comment on the two brothers, stating things such as:

:   *"I used to serve with Trilf and Steirod. Good guys, but don't get on their bad side, you know?"*
:   *"These boys do good work for the Empire!"*

They also would have told you about Steirod's strength by recalling:

:   *"I once saw Steirod tear a whole tree out the ground with his bare hands. Glad he's on our side!"*

Sulvar the Steady

**Sulvar the Steady** is an [elderly](/wiki/Skyrim:Elder "Skyrim:Elder") [mystic](/wiki/Skyrim:Mystic "Skyrim:Mystic") who watches the Wheel in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm"). He wears a [belted tunic](/wiki/Skyrim:Belted_Tunic "Skyrim:Belted Tunic"), some [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#0001be1b "Skyrim:Boots"), and a [hat](/wiki/Skyrim:Hat#000209aa "Skyrim:Hat"). He also carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). He sleeps at noon for four hours and at 2am for four hours. He spends the rest of his time in the Wheelhouse.

**Dialogue:**

:   *"My job? Well, I'm the Watcher of the Wheel! Which is... exactly what it sounds like. I watch the Wheel."*
:   *"Watching the wheel isn't as simple as you might think. Have to inspect all the way around the ice, make sure it's not melting."*
:   *"Don't get too many folks up here these days. Sometimes I think they've forgotten about me."*

Talib

**Talib** is a [Redguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Redguard "Skyrim:Redguard") [mage](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatMageElemental "Skyrim:CombatMageElemental") living in [Stonehills](/wiki/Skyrim:Stonehills "Skyrim:Stonehills"). He is rivals with [Jesper](/wiki/Skyrim:Jesper "Skyrim:Jesper"). [Gestur Rockbreaker](/wiki/Skyrim:Gestur_Rockbreaker "Skyrim:Gestur Rockbreaker") is his father. He sleeps for eight hours at noon, then wanders around outside for two hours starting at 9pm. He works in the mine from 8am to 6pm, then visits [Argi](#Argi_Farseer) until 9pm. From 11pm to 8am he can be found inside his house. He wears a [belted tunic](/wiki/Skyrim:Belted_Tunic "Skyrim:Belted Tunic"), some [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#0001be1b "Skyrim:Boots"). He also carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). Despite being a mage, he knows no spells.

**Dialogue:**

:   **Gestur:** *"How goes it, son?"*
:   **Talib:** *"I'm fine, father. Work is fine."*
:   **Gestur:** *"Good, good. It's honest work, son. No shame in that."*
:   **Talib:** *"Of course not. I never thought otherwise."*
:   **Gestur:** *"Right. Well, you let me know if you need anything."*

Tasius Tragus

**Tasius Tragus** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") soldier living in [Dragon Bridge](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Bridge "Skyrim:Dragon Bridge"). He works for [General Tullius](/wiki/Skyrim:General_Tullius "Skyrim:General Tullius"), and [Varnius Junius](/wiki/Skyrim:Varnius_Junius "Skyrim:Varnius Junius") works for him. [Faida](/wiki/Skyrim:Faida "Skyrim:Faida") is his girlfriend and [Julienne Lylvieve](/wiki/Skyrim:Julienne_Lylvieve "Skyrim:Julienne Lylvieve") is his other lover. He sleeps for eight hours at midnight and spends the rest of his time wandering around a small area. He wears [scaled armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Armor "Skyrim:Scaled Armor") and [scaled boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaled_Boots "Skyrim:Scaled Boots"), and carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). Tasius originally had the role that [Commander Maro](/wiki/Skyrim:Commander_Maro "Skyrim:Commander Maro") has in the final game, as he was meant to be the local Legion head. Varnius Junius still mentions Tasius in the final game.

Terek

**Terek** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") citizen who illegally lives in [Breezehome](/wiki/Skyrim:Breezehome "Skyrim:Breezehome"). He sleeps for eight hours at midnight. He wears farmer clothes and carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). A burial urn for Terek is present in Whiterun's Hall of the Dead, and will activate in case he dies.

**Dialogue:**

:   *"Don't let the door slam into your backside on the way out."*
:   *"Now if ya don't mind, my house could do with a bit of dusting."*
:   *"You're in my home. That's right. This here place, Breezehome, it belongs to old Terek."*
:   *"Oho! And what do you want, hmm?"*
:   *"You can't kick me out of here. You want me gone, you're gonna have to kill me."*
:   *"Now I don't care about no deeds, or laws, or other nonsense like that. This house was empty, so I settled in. And now it's mine."*
:   *"I'm a generous man, to those who treat me right. Might be I'll even let you live here with me, if you mind your manners."*

TestJeffBCarryWaterBucket

**TestJeffBCarryWaterBucket** is a Nord warrior. He wears default Redguard clothing when awake and a Psijic Monk outfit when sleeping, but never sleeps. He also carries iron armor and gauntlets, and knows the [Phantom Form](/wiki/Skyrim:Phantom_Form "Skyrim:Phantom Form") shout. He does not exist in any cell and does not say anything special. He does, however, use CarryActorScript, and was probably used to test how NPCs carry the bucket item used in specific animations. It's very likely that finalizing the process never got completed, seeing as the animation is broken in the final game.

### TestJeremyBig

**TestJeremyBig** is a Nord warrior. He does not wear or carry anything, and appears in the TestTony cell. He was used to test sizes, and is set to be twice the size of an average human NPC.

Thaer

**Thaer** is a [Wood Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Elf "Skyrim:Wood Elf") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)") and [carriage](/wiki/Skyrim:Carriage "Skyrim:Carriage") driver added by the Dawnguard add-on. He wears farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#0006c1da "Skyrim:Clothing") and a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261bd "Skyrim:Clothing"), and carries an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe"). He shares his dialogue with other carriage drivers. He might have been planned as replacement for the Nord [Thaer](/wiki/Skyrim:Thaer "Skyrim:Thaer").

Thjollod

**Thjollod** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") bandit who was meant to appear as a hunter in [Ill Met By Moonlight](/wiki/Skyrim:Ill_Met_By_Moonlight "Skyrim:Ill Met By Moonlight"). He patrols [Bloated Man's Grotto](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloated_Man%27s_Grotto "Skyrim:Bloated Man's Grotto") and follows you in between stages 75 and 80 of the related quest. He wears [iron armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Armor "Skyrim:Iron Armor"), [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and [hide bracers](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Bracers "Skyrim:Hide Bracers"), and carries a leveled bandit sword, a leveled bandit bow, and leveled gold (plus 25 extra gold).

Trilf

**Trilf** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [lumberjack](/wiki/Skyrim:Lumberjack "Skyrim:Lumberjack") who lives together with his brother [Steirod](#Steirod) at Irontree Mill, located between the [Thalmor Embassy](/wiki/Skyrim:Thalmor_Embassy "Skyrim:Thalmor Embassy") and [Ironback Hideout](/wiki/Skyrim:Ironback_Hideout "Skyrim:Ironback Hideout"). He wears a set of farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a6 "Skyrim:Clothing") along with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000209a5 "Skyrim:Clothing"). He carries a [key](/wiki/Skyrim:Irontree_Mill_House_Key "Skyrim:Irontree Mill House Key") to the Irontree Mill and wields an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe").

Trilf greets you quite warmly when you arrive at the mill:

:   *"Hey, welcome to the mill!"*

He will then reveal to you that his brother and him are veterans:

:   *"My brother and me bought this place when we left the army. Boy, did we lose a bundle, but at least we're not fighting the war!"*

He will then elaborate on his days in the army:

:   *"We served our time in the [Legion](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Legion "Skyrim:Imperial Legion"). Now we're just trying to make it day to day.*"

He will continue on about the financial difficulties he and Steirod are facing:

:   *"We're making our money back, bit by bit. Every extra bit of work helps..."*

Trius

**Trius** is a Breton bandit wizard. A [note](/wiki/Skyrim:For_Shelly "Skyrim:For Shelly") by him to someone named Shelly can be found at his camp near [Pilgrim's Trench](/wiki/Skyrim:Pilgrim%27s_Trench "Skyrim:Pilgrim's Trench"). He is involved in the unfinished quest [Trius' Trinket](/wiki/Skyrim:Trius%27_Trinket "Skyrim:Trius' Trinket"), where you would have had to fetch a jewelry box for him in exchange for a new spell.

Uglarz

**Uglarz** is an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H"). She wears [ragged trousers](/wiki/Skyrim:Ragged_Trousers "Skyrim:Ragged Trousers") with a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots#00080699 "Skyrim:Boots"), and carries an [Orcish bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Bow "Skyrim:Orcish Bow") and [Orcish dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Dagger "Skyrim:Orcish Dagger"), along with a selection of [lower-class items and gold](/wiki/Skyrim:Leveled_Lists#LootCitizenPocketsPoor "Skyrim:Leveled Lists"). She is placed inside of Mauhulakh's Longhouse, but her ref is permanently disabled.

Uglarz appears to have been intended to be the missing wife of [Chief Mauhulakh](/wiki/Skyrim:Chief_Mauhulakh "Skyrim:Chief Mauhulakh") in an [unfinished quest](/wiki/Skyrim:Unfinished_Quests "Skyrim:Unfinished Quests") called `NarzulburFreeFormWifeQuest` which also wasn't implemented in the game.

At the start of the quest to find her, Chief Mauhulakh speaks of her: *"I think of Uglarz. An old friend. When we were little she ran to the woods to hunt, and never came back. If someone found her, she would make an excellent mate."*

Before she comes back to the stronghold, Uglarz has a few lines of unique dialogue:

:   *"I am Uglarz, huntress of all."*
:   *"It was my home. But I left. The woods felt more alive, at least for a time."*
:   *"Mauhulakh. I remember him. Little, scrawny, fellow."*
:   *"Ha! Now that I would like to see. I may go back there some day."*
:   *"Always thought that old mine had more to it. Just like Mauhulakh to smash his way to good fortune."*
:   *"I'm going back to [Narzulbur](/wiki/Skyrim:Narzulbur "Skyrim:Narzulbur"). Want to see how it's come."*

After Uglarz returns to Narzulbur, she has a few more lines of unique dialogue:

:   *"You were right about this place. And about Mauhulakh."*
:   *"This was worth coming back for."*
:   *"[Urog](/wiki/Skyrim:Urog "Skyrim:Urog") is a good learner. I'm glad I have the chance to teach her. Makes me not miss the woods as much."*
:   *"Urog and I still hunt a lot, so it's not like I'm cooped up in the longhouse."*

When Uglarz is in Narzulbur, Urog will comment on her hunting skills, saying:

:   *"Uglarz is a great huntress. Perhaps she can teach me something new."*
:   *"I never would have thought, but Uglarz is an even better hunter than [Yatul](/wiki/Skyrim:Yatul "Skyrim:Yatul") was. The things I'm learning now..."*

Urchin

The **Urchin** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who exists in `WICourierCell`. He stays in a set location, never moving. He may have been meant for a [Dark Brotherhood](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Brotherhood "Skyrim:Dark Brotherhood") quest, as his Editor ID is `DBCourierUrchin`. He wears [ragged robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Ragged_Robes "Skyrim:Ragged Robes") along with [ragged boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Ragged_Boots "Skyrim:Ragged Boots").

Viding

**Viding** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") who was meant to appear as a hunter in [Ill Met By Moonlight](/wiki/Skyrim:Ill_Met_By_Moonlight "Skyrim:Ill Met By Moonlight"). He patrols [Bloated Man's Grotto](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloated_Man%27s_Grotto "Skyrim:Bloated Man's Grotto") and follows you in between stages 66 and 80 of the related quest. He wears farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261c0 "Skyrim:Clothing"), [hide boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Boots "Skyrim:Hide Boots"), and [hide bracers](/wiki/Skyrim:Hide_Bracers "Skyrim:Hide Bracers"), and carries a leveled potion of Restore Magicka, leveled gold, and an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

**Spells**:

- Firebolt
- Flames
- Healing
- Oakflesh
- Lesser Ward

This article is about the unused NPC in the base game. For the burnt corpse in the base game, see [Yisra](/wiki/Skyrim:Yisra "Skyrim:Yisra").

| Voice Type | [FemaleCommoner](/wiki/Category:Skyrim-Voice-FemaleCommoner "Category:Skyrim-Voice-FemaleCommoner") | | |

An unused version of **Yisra**, the dead mage related to [The Missing Apprentices](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Missing_Apprentices "Skyrim:The Missing Apprentices"). She wears the standard court mage outfit consisting of [blue mage robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Blue_Mage_Robes "Skyrim:Blue Mage Robes") and a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#0006b46c "Skyrim:Clothing"). [Her necklace](/wiki/Skyrim:Yisra%27s_Necklace "Skyrim:Yisra's Necklace") and an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger") can also be found on her corpse. While in the final game, Yisra succumbed to a fire cloak, this version of her has the same frost corpse properties as [Rundi](/wiki/Skyrim:Rundi "Skyrim:Rundi") in the final game. Suggesting her death might have happened differently.

### Ysleif

**Ysleif** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)") and the [ferryman](/wiki/Skyrim:Ferryman "Skyrim:Ferryman") for [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar"). He wears farm [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#0006c1da "Skyrim:Clothing"), a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000261bd "Skyrim:Clothing"), and carries an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe").

In the [Creation Kit](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Creation_Kit "Skyrim Mod:Creation Kit"), his Editor ID is `ccKRTSSE001_FerrymanDawnstarBACKUP`, implying he was meant to be a backup in case [Harlaug](/wiki/Skyrim:Harlaug "Skyrim:Harlaug") was dead. However, Harlaug cannot be killed under normal conditions as he is essential and is also set to respawn.

## Behind-the-scenes NPCs

While these NPCs technically can't be encountered directly, they are in fact used by the game, usually in the background, for a variety of technical reasons.

Azura

**Azura** resides in her voice cell and acts as a speaker during the quest [The Black Star](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Black_Star "Skyrim:The Black Star") via a talking activator near the [Shrine of Azura](/wiki/Skyrim:Shrine_of_Azura "Skyrim:Shrine of Azura").

Clavicus Vile

**Clavicus Vile** is never instantiated. He is used as speaker for one line of dialogue and mentioned in three aliases for the quest [A Daedra's Best Friend](/wiki/Skyrim:A_Daedra%27s_Best_Friend "Skyrim:A Daedra's Best Friend") as *"Additional Valid Voice Type For Export"*. He carries an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"), and wears red colored [child's clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Child%27s_Clothes "Skyrim:Child's Clothes") and a pair of [shoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#000e80a8 "Skyrim:Clothing"). However, due to him being an adult NPC, the clothes don't appear at all, and while he appears to be wearing shoes, parts of his calves go invisible as a result.

Hrothmund

**Hrothmund** is a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") placeholder NPC used for when his spirit speaks during [Retaking Thirsk](/wiki/Skyrim:Retaking_Thirsk "Skyrim:Retaking Thirsk"). His axe can be found lodged in an altar inside the [barrow](/wiki/Skyrim:Hrothmund%27s_Barrow "Skyrim:Hrothmund's Barrow") where he is buried. For more information on the historical person, see [Hrothmund the Red](/wiki/Lore:Hrothmund_the_Red "Lore:Hrothmund the Red").

Malacath

**Malacath** resides in [Largashbur Longhouse](/wiki/Skyrim:Largashbur_Longhouse "Skyrim:Largashbur Longhouse") and acts as a speaker during [The Cursed Tribe](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Cursed_Tribe "Skyrim:The Cursed Tribe"). He can't be seen without console use, because he is always disabled. His reference has an editor ID of `ShagrolRef`. Shagrol is the name of the previous leader of the [Largashbur](/wiki/Skyrim:Largashbur "Skyrim:Largashbur") tribe and during the quest [Shagrol's Warhammer](/wiki/Skyrim:Shagrol%27s_Warhammer "Skyrim:Shagrol's Warhammer") needs to be recovered.

Mehrunes Dagon

**Mehrunes Dagon** is a [Daedric Prince](/wiki/Lore:Daedric_Princes "Lore:Daedric Princes") who is encountered during the quest [Pieces of the Past](/wiki/Skyrim:Pieces_of_the_Past "Skyrim:Pieces of the Past"). As a reward for betraying [Silus Vesuius](/wiki/Skyrim:Silus_Vesuius "Skyrim:Silus Vesuius") at the [Shrine of Mehrunes Dagon](/wiki/Skyrim:Shrine_of_Mehrunes_Dagon "Skyrim:Shrine of Mehrunes Dagon") you will receive the Daedric artifact [Mehrunes' Razor](/wiki/Skyrim:Mehrunes%27_Razor "Skyrim:Mehrunes' Razor").

Mephala

**Mephala** is a [Daedric Prince](/wiki/Lore:Daedric_Princes "Lore:Daedric Princes") who has a presence behind a locked door in [Dragonsreach](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonsreach "Skyrim:Dragonsreach"). She is encountered during [The Whispering Door](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Whispering_Door "Skyrim:The Whispering Door"), in which she tasks you with unsealing a locked room in [Dragonsreach](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonsreach "Skyrim:Dragonsreach"). As a reward for obeying her, you are gifted the [Ebony Blade](/wiki/Skyrim:Ebony_Blade "Skyrim:Ebony Blade").

Meridia

**Meridia** is a [Daedric Prince](/wiki/Lore:Daedric_Princes "Lore:Daedric Princes"). If you happen to find [Meridia's Beacon](/wiki/Skyrim:Meridia%27s_Beacon "Skyrim:Meridia's Beacon") by accident or by asking at [Meridia's Shrine](/wiki/Skyrim:Statue_to_Meridia "Skyrim:Statue to Meridia"), then the quest [The Break of Dawn](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Break_of_Dawn "Skyrim:The Break of Dawn") is started.

Meridia seeks for her Temple to be restored and sends you to defeat [Malkoran](/wiki/Skyrim:Malkoran "Skyrim:Malkoran") in [Kilkreath Ruins](/wiki/Skyrim:Kilkreath_Ruins "Skyrim:Kilkreath Ruins").

Vaermina

**Vaermina** is an [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H "Skyrim:CombatWarrior1H") placed in the `ThievesGuildHoldingCell`. She is used to play Vaermina's lines during [Waking Nightmare](/wiki/Skyrim:Waking_Nightmare "Skyrim:Waking Nightmare").

Voice of Boethiah

The **Voice of Boethiah** is a [Dark Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Elf "Skyrim:Dark Elf") [warrior](/wiki/Skyrim:Warrior_(class) "Skyrim:Warrior (class)") that isn't placed anywhere, but is used as the speaker for Boethiah's lines in [Boethiah's Calling](/wiki/Skyrim:Boethiah%27s_Calling "Skyrim:Boethiah's Calling").

Voice of Namira

The **Voice of Namira** is a [Wood Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Elf "Skyrim:Wood Elf") [nightblade](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightblade "Skyrim:Nightblade") who exists in a cell to the east of the dining room in [Reachcliff Cave](/wiki/Skyrim:Reachcliff_Cave "Skyrim:Reachcliff Cave"). She can only be seen by using either the Aura Whisper shout or Detect Life spell. Although she cannot be killed, it is possible to hit her with the Chain Lightning spell if you are pressed against the dining room's eastern wall, thus causing you to be charged with an assault. She wears no clothes, carries no items and cannot otherwise be interacted with.

If you do manage to get to her cell, you can attack her like other NPCs, but not kill her. Doing so will result in a bounty for [The Reach](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Reach "Skyrim:The Reach"). It is possible to reach her via the [Whirlwind Sprint exploit](/wiki/Skyrim:Whirlwind_Sprint#Notes "Skyrim:Whirlwind Sprint") at the rightmost sarcophagus on the eastern wall of the dining hall in Reachcliff Cave.

# Skyrim:Ustengrav

## Overview

Ustengrav

**Ustengrav** is a medium-sized [Nordic ruin](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Ruins "Skyrim:Nordic Ruins") northeast of [Morthal](/wiki/Skyrim:Morthal "Skyrim:Morthal") containing [bandits](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit "Skyrim:Bandit"), [warlocks](/wiki/Skyrim:Warlock "Skyrim:Warlock"), [draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Draugr "Skyrim:Draugr"), [skeletons](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeleton "Skyrim:Skeleton"), and [frostbite spiders](/wiki/Skyrim:Frostbite_Spider "Skyrim:Frostbite Spider"). It contains two zones: *[Ustengrav](#Ustengrav)* and *[Ustengrav Depths](#Ustengrav_Depths)*.

Its [word wall](/wiki/Skyrim:Word_Wall "Skyrim:Word Wall") teaches part of the [Become Ethereal](/wiki/Skyrim:Become_Ethereal "Skyrim:Become Ethereal") [dragon shout](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Shouts "Skyrim:Dragon Shouts"). Bandits have occupied the ruins and set up a camp outside the entrance. However, warlocks have tried to occupy the ruin, resulting in many casualties between the rival groups. The ongoing battles have alerted the sleeping draugr, who have engaged with both groups. Most of the ruin will be inaccessible before the quest [The Horn of Jurgen Windcaller](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Horn_of_Jurgen_Windcaller "Skyrim:The Horn of Jurgen Windcaller"); if the ruin is entered prior to this quest, bandits and warlocks will be engaged in battle in the first main chamber, the exit from which will be blocked.

## Related Quests

- **[Bandit Attack](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit_Attack "Skyrim:Bandit Attack")**[HF](/wiki/Skyrim:Hearthfire "Skyrim:Hearthfire"): Rescue your beloved from kidnappers. ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant"))
- **[The Horn of Jurgen Windcaller](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Horn_of_Jurgen_Windcaller "Skyrim:The Horn of Jurgen Windcaller")**: Retrieve a [Greybeard](/wiki/Skyrim:Greybeards "Skyrim:Greybeards") artifact from Ustengrav.

### Exterior

There are two bandits outside by the camp, and the corpses of two more can be found nearby: one by the campfire, and one slightly north of the fire. When you first approach the campsite, the living bandits are already zombies of a [necromancer](/wiki/Skyrim:Necromancer "Skyrim:Necromancer"), who will likely also raise one of the [dead bandits](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit_(dead) "Skyrim:Bandit (dead)"). If you kill the necromancer, the living bandits will die automatically.

The camp itself consists of two bedrolls (one under a lean-to shelter), a woodpile with a woodcutter's axe nearby, a bucket, and a tankard on a rock. There are four [deathbell](/wiki/Skyrim:Deathbell "Skyrim:Deathbell") plants growing alongside the rough path from the lake. Inside the barrow mound is another dead bandit, an unlocked [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest"), a shovel, a few sacks, and a barrel with two bottles of [skooma](/wiki/Skyrim:Skooma "Skyrim:Skooma") on it.

### Ustengrav

There is a dead bandit to the right as you enter the ruin, and human bones to the left with an iron weapon among the bones. There is another dead bandit near the bottom of the stairs ahead. There is a dead bandit by the pillar in the middle of the first room, and another at the far side; both are liable to be reanimated by warlocks during any fight. All of the remaining living bandits are zombies of a [High Elf](/wiki/Skyrim:High_Elf "Skyrim:High Elf") necromancer. If you remain hidden, shortly after you arrive, these enemies will head deeper into the ruin.

To the left of the entrance to the large chamber is a platform with a [dead draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Draugr "Skyrim:Containers"), a [potion of minor healing](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Minor_Healing "Skyrim:Potion of Minor Healing"), and a random potion. In the area with the warlocks is a table and a chest. On the table is a potion of minor healing, a loaf of [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread"), a [gourd](/wiki/Skyrim:Gourd "Skyrim:Gourd"), an [iron sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Sword "Skyrim:Iron Sword"), an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"), and two random books. The path continues to the north of the table.

Along the path are three dead warlocks and a draugr. The tunnel opens to a short corridor occupied by four draugr; another two warlocks have engaged in a fight with them. There is also a [spellcaster trap](/wiki/Skyrim:Spellcaster_Trap "Skyrim:Spellcaster Trap") to the left. In the first alcove to the right by the brazier are two potions of minor healing. Through the door to the left are two rooms filled with urns, one to the west and one to the northeast. The western room contains six intact [urns](/wiki/Skyrim:Urn "Skyrim:Urn"), one [burial urn](/wiki/Skyrim:Burial_Urn "Skyrim:Burial Urn"), one broken urn, a chest, and a potion of minor healing. In the broken urn are two additional potions. The northeastern room contains twelve intact urns, two burial urns, a [canis root](/wiki/Skyrim:Canis_Root "Skyrim:Canis Root"), a [chaurus egg](/wiki/Skyrim:Chaurus_Eggs "Skyrim:Chaurus Eggs") (inside a [cast iron pot](/wiki/Skyrim:Cast_Iron_Pot "Skyrim:Cast Iron Pot")), a potion of minor healing, a [potion of health](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Health "Skyrim:Potion of Health"), a [draught of the healer](/wiki/Skyrim:Draught_of_the_Healer "Skyrim:Draught of the Healer"), and a copy of the [Restoration](/wiki/Skyrim:Restoration "Skyrim:Restoration") [skill book](/wiki/Skyrim:Skill_Books "Skyrim:Skill Books") *[Mystery of Talara, v 2](/wiki/Skyrim:Mystery_of_Talara,_v_2 "Skyrim:Mystery of Talara, v 2")*.

Continuing east then south along the path, there is a partially hidden staircase to the right just before the next large room. There is a table in the room down the stairs holding eighteen loose gold coins and a random ancient Nord weapon. To the left of the table on the wall is a partially hidden pull chain, which reveals a secret passageway ending in a room with a chest.

The last room in the zone consists of a narrow, two-level main chamber with an overlooking balcony. A draugr will burst from its sarcophagus to the right as you enter, while another already patrols the far side of the room. Another slumbering draugr is between the two active ones, and a dead draugr lies by the first of three alcoves on the right. There is an iron battleaxe by the middle alcove, and an iron helmet by the last alcove. The stairs up to the balcony are at the far end of the room, and there is a chest, two potions of minor healing, and a random potion. A stone bridge leads to the door to *Ustengrav Depths*. In the corner before the final steps down to the door is a petty soul gem, and there is a large coin purse by the door.

### Ustengrav Depths

The depths

As you descend the stairs into the depths of the ruin, there is a hole in the wall to the right providing a view of the main chamber, with an iron sword on the floor nearby. Farther down the stairs, where the wall opens to reveal another view of the main chamber, is a patrolling draugr. An ancient Nord bow lies near a lantern and a spent brazier. On a rocky ledge just over the wall that the lantern sits upon is a leveled shield. Take precaution, as it is somewhat difficult to get back if you choose to retrieve the shield. This is a good spot to ambush the skeletons that patrol the main chamber with long-range attacks.

Down the stairs to the south is an [iron ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Iron Ore Vein") on the southwest wall. The path forward is to the left of the vein, but is trapped. There are four pressure plates on the ground, two of which are [flame spout traps](/wiki/Skyrim:Flame_Spout_Trap "Skyrim:Flame Spout Trap"). The traps can be safely bypassed by staying to the left, as only the two right-hand plates are traps. Farther along, the path is blocked by some rubble. The path forward is to the east, where the tunnel opens to a stone bridge over a feasting hall.

Two draugr patrol the area: one in the main area, and another on another stone bridge on the other side of the room. Across the bridge are some stairs leading in a rough circle down to the level below. This level of the room contains a table to the right of the lower entrance, five stone tables along the left-hand side of the raised platform ahead, two tables to the right in front of a throne, and one more table and an urn on the other side of the platform. Along with three loaves of bread and two [grilled leeks](/wiki/Skyrim:Grilled_Leeks "Skyrim:Grilled Leeks"), the five tables to the left hold a [charred skeever hide](/wiki/Skyrim:Charred_Skeever_Hide "Skyrim:Charred Skeever Hide"), a branch of [lavender](/wiki/Skyrim:Lavender "Skyrim:Lavender"), some [jazbay grapes](/wiki/Skyrim:Jazbay_Grapes "Skyrim:Jazbay Grapes"), and a [purple mountain flower](/wiki/Skyrim:Purple_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Purple Mountain Flower"). There are several cheeses on the tables by the throne, and a sheaf of [wheat](/wiki/Skyrim:Wheat "Skyrim:Wheat") is on the far table. It is entirely possible to avoid the first draugr here. There is a room to the east running the length of the main room, with doors on either side. There is a chest and two potions of minor healing in this eastern room.

After climbing stairs exiting the lower level to the south, then crossing the other bridge, the path returns to the tunnel on the other side of the blockage. The next room looks rather bare, but to the left is a small room. The entrance to the small room is blocked by two gates, which are raised by two separate handles. One handle is beside the gates, but the other is beside a sarcophagus to the west of the gates. Upon approaching the sarcophagus, a draugr will awaken, while another awakens from another sarcophagus to the north; there is also a third dead draugr that by the third sarcophagus. There is an [oil slick](/wiki/Skyrim:Oil_Slick "Skyrim:Oil Slick") between the sarcophagi that can be used to deal damage to the draugr. The small room contains an [arcane enchanter](/wiki/Skyrim:Arcane_Enchanters "Skyrim:Arcane Enchanters"), a random dagger, some ruined books, a chest, a potion of healing, an empty soul gem, and a random potion. Returning to the main room, through the tunnel to the west and up the stairs to the left is a table with a troll skull, some ruined books, and a poison. The path continues north from here.

Patrolling skeletons

The tunnel opens to a ledge in the main chamber. There is a stone path heading left that has collapsed and leads down to the main room. Three skeletons patrol this open chamber, and another two patrol a raised walkway along the south and west walls. There is one last skeleton sitting in the throne in the northwest corner; it will awaken if it detects any non-skeletons. There are pedestals to either side of the throne, each with a random potion. The stairs up to the walkway are to the left of the entrance in the room's southwest corner, and at the top is a flame spout trap. A dead draugr lies across the trap, leaving it in a state of perpetual activation. There are seven tiles on the ground by the trap, and the safe path is straight through the middle. Immediately after passing the trap, along the wall to the left and behind some rubble is a chest. Farther along is a small alcove to the left with a troll skull, a petty soul gem, a garnet, and a flawless garnet. At the end of the walkway, you can see a chest in a chamber blocked by rubble; there is an unorthodox way to reach it. By the first stone pillar are a series of pillars that were once connected to lead across the chamber, but the walkways between them have collapsed. The key to accessing the blocked chamber is the [Whirlwind Sprint](/wiki/Skyrim:Whirlwind_Sprint "Skyrim:Whirlwind Sprint") shout. The broken parts still point to each other, and careful use of the shout allows the gaps to be crossed. The chamber contains the chest, a dead draugr, and a lesser soul gem.

Just to the left of the bottom of the entrance pillar, there is a dirt path along the wall that leads southeast to a [corundum ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Corundum_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Corundum Ore Vein"). Note that it is very difficult to return up the path after this point. Dropping from the ledge past the vein, at the bottom and to the left is a well-lit alcove, with a chest, a random potion, and a potion of minor healing. To the northeast is another corundum ore vein on the same level, while from the alcove, along some descending ledges to the north and past a dead draugr, is a [silver ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Silver_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Silver Ore Vein"), with another dead draugr around the corner after the vein. The word wall to the east should now be clearly visible. Before approaching the wall, there is a secret room behind the nearby waterfall. This small treasure room contains a chest and a random sword, and is guarded by a slumbering draugr. The word wall teaches a word for the Become Ethereal shout.

Speed is of the essence

There is a dirt path behind the word wall that circles back up to the throne chamber. The earthen bridge to the east leads to the final part of the large main chamber. Three [stone posts](/wiki/Skyrim:Glowing_Stones "Skyrim:Glowing Stones") stand in a triangle, with a series of closed gates straight ahead. A skeleton patrols a balcony above and to the right, with two burial urns and a potion of minor healing on the balcony. To the left of the stones lies a dead draugr and a potion of minor magicka amid some rubble. This puzzle requires speed to solve, literally. Each stone activates one of the three gates blocking the path ahead, but on a timer. It is impossible to sprint through the stones and the gates. The Whirlwind Sprint shout needs to be used, but only once you have sprinted through all three stones. Between the first two gates are pull chains to open the preceding gates.

At the top of the stairs past the gates is a room with the entire floor covered in flame spout traps. However, none of the pressure plates on the raised section ahead work. The area may be passed safely by proceeding straight ahead, jumping over the piles of rubble into the next room, moving to the left, then jumping across to a small set of steps. This next room contains two frostbite spiders and a giant frostbite spider that drops from the ceiling, as well as three [spider egg sacs](/wiki/Skyrim:Egg_Sac "Skyrim:Egg Sac"). The exit to the east is blocked by two thick spider webs. There is a ledge above the exit that is accessible by a series of jumps using the wooden beams along the walls, but there is nothing on the ledge.

The statues

Beyond the exit, a short tunnel past a door leads to a closed gate. A pull chain to the left activates the gate, with another to the right on the other side. Passing the pillars causes four large stone statues to emerge from the water on either side of the room. This is the resting place of [Jurgen Windcaller](/wiki/Lore:Jurgen_Windcaller "Lore:Jurgen Windcaller"). There are two burial urns in front of the coffin, and a [mysterious note](/wiki/Skyrim:Mysterious_Note_(Delphine) "Skyrim:Mysterious Note (Delphine)") in the stone hand at the top of the coffin. There are two sarcophagi, one on either side of the coffin, with a dead draugr lying outside each. Behind the coffin is an urn and a door. Through the door is a room filled with six more urns, two burial urns, a chest, and twenty-four loose gold coins scattered near the chest. To the left is a tunnel leading up to a door back to *Ustengrav*. Farther up the tunnel, a lever opens a secret door to the [western urn room](#Secret_Room) found shortly after the main chamber.

## Notes

- When passing through the stone posts puzzle, it is possible to use the Whirlwind Sprint shout twice in a row in quick succession.
- The resurrected bandits here were originally intended to be named "Cursed Bandit". The unused message `CursedBanditName` would've applied this name, and the script `QF_dunUstengravQST_0002125B`  refers to them as such, although in the final game they're just normal bandits without any unique characteristics.
- The dead bandits and mages inside will never respawn or despawn, making them excellent targets to raise as [thralls](/wiki/Skyrim:Dead_Thrall "Skyrim:Dead Thrall").
- If you return to Jurgen Windcaller's coffin after completing The Horn of Jurgen Windcaller, you can activate it to release a dragon soul.
- With the *[Hearthfire](/wiki/Skyrim:Hearthfire "Skyrim:Hearthfire")* add-on installed, you can find [Windstad Manor](/wiki/Skyrim:Windstad_Manor "Skyrim:Windstad Manor") north of here.

## Bugs

- The frostbite spiders in the room before the giant frostbite spider may get stuck in the walls.
  - Disturbing the desiccated corpse on the ledge to the right will make them emerge.
- The three puzzle gates in *Ustengrav Depths* are supposed to stay open after you have sprinted through, but may not.
  - Waiting an hour once through the gates will teleport [followers](/wiki/Skyrim:Followers "Skyrim:Followers") to your side.
  -  You can get through using the [console](/wiki/Skyrim:Console "Skyrim:Console") command `tcl`, but this doesn't help followers. There are pull chains that can be activated on the other side of two of the gates. Through the console, you can also use the follower's [reference ID](/wiki/Skyrim:Form_ID "Skyrim:Form ID") and the `moveto player` command to move your follower to your location, should they get stuck on the other side.
  -  The puzzle gates can be tricked into staying open if you're quick enough to enter and exit each of the three stones' activation ranges, making them stay active.
- The Horn of Jurgen Windcaller, if you return to Ustengrav, appears hovering well above its placement on the tomb due to an animation block on the mesh that wasn't supposed to be there.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 1.3.1, fixes this bug.
- Scenes in the first large chamber where the necromancers are supposed to be tending the bandit thralls would be disrupted in which a necromancer attacks two bandits who are mining and stay idle (or do the mining animation) without fighting back. This is because the trigger to start the quest which holds the aliases that should pacify them is inside the dungeon and doesn't generally have enough time to run its script before the AI decides to attack each other.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 2.0.4, addresses this issue. The trigger box has now been moved to the outside of the dungeon so that the quest will be started and the pacification aliases will be properly filled before you enter.
- The bandits and necromancer outside of the dungeon respawn despite it being classed as a "no reset zone".
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 2.1.2, fixes this bug.

## Maps

- 

  Ustengrav
- 

  Ustengrav Depths

# Skyrim:Uthgerd's House

## Overview

|  |
| --- |
| **This article is being developed as part of the Skyrim Houses Redesign Project. If you see this tag it means the article can still be improved. Please see the [project page](/wiki/UESPWiki:SHRP "UESPWiki:SHRP") for guidelines.** |

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| |  | | --- | | Home: Uthgerd's House ([view on map](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=Skyrim&centeron=Uthgerd%27s+House)) | | |
| # of Zones | 1 |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | 10 days |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| WhiterunUthgerdTheUnbrokensHouse | |
| Region | |
| [Whiterun Hold](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun_Hold "Skyrim:Whiterun Hold") | |
| Location | |
| [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun "Skyrim:Whiterun") | |
| Special Features | |
| [Shadowmark](/wiki/Skyrim:Shadowmarks_(Thieves_Guild) "Skyrim:Shadowmarks (Thieves Guild)") | [Empty](/wiki/Skyrim:Shadowmarks_(Thieves_Guild) "Empty") Empty |

Uthgerd's House

**Uthgerd's House** is the home of [Uthgerd the Unbroken](/wiki/Skyrim:Uthgerd_the_Unbroken "Skyrim:Uthgerd the Unbroken"). It is located in the Wind District of [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun "Skyrim:Whiterun"), across the street from [Carlotta Valentia's House](/wiki/Skyrim:Carlotta_Valentia%27s_House "Skyrim:Carlotta Valentia's House").

It is owned by [Uthgerd the Unbroken](/wiki/Skyrim:Uthgerd_the_Unbroken "Skyrim:Uthgerd the Unbroken"), although she rarely sets foot in the house, spending most of her time inside [The Bannered Mare](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Bannered_Mare "Skyrim:The Bannered Mare"). It consists of one zone, *[Uthgerd's House](#Uthgerd.27s_House)*. She will always be in residence during the [Battle for Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Battle_for_Whiterun_(Imperial) "Skyrim:Battle for Whiterun (Imperial)") and may be found there at other times.

## Residents

| Residents |
| --- |
| [Uthgerd the Unbroken](/wiki/Skyrim:Uthgerd_the_Unbroken "Skyrim:Uthgerd the Unbroken") [Followers](/wiki/File:SR-icon-Follower.png "Followers") |

### Exterior

It is the first house on the right as you enter the Wind District of [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun "Skyrim:Whiterun") from the stairs nearest the gate. There are three [lavender](/wiki/Skyrim:Lavender "Skyrim:Lavender") plants around the house which is attached to [Amren's house](/wiki/Skyrim:Amren%27s_House "Skyrim:Amren's House").

### Uthgerd's House

Inside Uthgerd's House

The front door opens to the living room, with a ladder leading to the bedroom upstairs. Left of the door sits a small table with a bottle of [ale](/wiki/Skyrim:Ale "Skyrim:Ale") and a [sweet roll](/wiki/Skyrim:Sweet_Roll "Skyrim:Sweet Roll") on top. At the foot of the ladder is a dresser with some random [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing "Skyrim:Clothing"). Under the ladder are three [food](/wiki/Skyrim:Food "Skyrim:Food") sacks, two food barrels, a bowl with four [potatoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Potato "Skyrim:Potato"), an end table with clutter, and an unlocked [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") with a bit of gold. In the northwestern corner is a dinner table that seats three, laid with a bottle of ale and a piece of [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread"). In the adjacent corner is a cupboard with clutter, while the shelves above hold a [goat cheese wheel](/wiki/Skyrim:Goat_Cheese_Wheel "Skyrim:Goat Cheese Wheel") and an [eidar cheese wheel](/wiki/Skyrim:Eidar_Cheese_Wheel "Skyrim:Eidar Cheese Wheel"). At the foot of the cupboard, scattered on the floor, are three [carrots](/wiki/Skyrim:Carrot "Skyrim:Carrot"). A small table in front of the fireplace holds a bottle of [alto wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Alto_Wine "Skyrim:Alto Wine"), an [eidar cheese wedge](/wiki/Skyrim:Eidar_Cheese_Wedge "Skyrim:Eidar Cheese Wedge"), and a [boiled creme treat](/wiki/Skyrim:Boiled_Creme_Treat "Skyrim:Boiled Creme Treat"). The alcove to the south has a small cupboard with clutter with four [tomatoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Tomato "Skyrim:Tomato") on top. A bookshelf holds several [common books](/wiki/Skyrim:Books "Skyrim:Books").

The ladder leads upstairs where a wardrobe contains some clutter, clothes, and a slim chance of some [jewelry](/wiki/Skyrim:Jewelry "Skyrim:Jewelry"), while an unlocked chest holds a bit of gold. A display case contains two [iron daggers](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"). The bedroom has a dresser with random clothing and the end table near the bed contains clutter. At the foot of the table sits a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#Boots "Skyrim:Clothing") and on top of the table is a bottle of alto wine and a bowl with five [red apples](/wiki/Skyrim:Red_Apple "Skyrim:Red Apple").

## Notes

- Once you recruit Uthgerd as a [follower](/wiki/Skyrim:Followers "Skyrim:Followers"), all items, including the bed, are free to use.
- There is an  "Empty") Empty [shadowmark](/wiki/Skyrim:Thieves_Guild_(faction)#Shadowmarks "Skyrim:Thieves Guild (faction)") to the left of the entrance door due to the sparseness of loot; however, the location will no longer be so empty if you are on one of [Vex's](/wiki/Skyrim:Vex "Skyrim:Vex") burglary or sweep jobs.

# Skyrim:Uttering Hills Cave

## Overview

|  |  |  |  |

| |  |  | | --- | --- | | [SR-mapicon-Cave.png](/wiki/File:SR-mapicon-Cave.png) | Cave: Uttering Hills Cave ([view on map](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=Skyrim&centeron=Uttering+Hills+Cave)) | | |
| # of Zones | 1 |

Uttering Hills Cave

**Uttering Hills Cave** is a small [cave](/wiki/Skyrim:Caves "Skyrim:Caves") southwest of [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm") containing [bandits](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit "Skyrim:Bandit"). It contains only one zone, *[Uttering Hills Cave](#Uttering_Hills_Cave)*.

## Related Quests

- **[Summerset Shadows](/wiki/Skyrim:Summerset_Shadows "Skyrim:Summerset Shadows")**: Remove a rival guild from the picture.

#### Radiant Quests

- **[Amulet of the Moon](/wiki/Skyrim:Amulet_of_the_Moon "Skyrim:Amulet of the Moon")**: Find the [Moon Amulet](/wiki/Skyrim:Moon_Amulet "Skyrim:Moon Amulet") for [Kharjo](/wiki/Skyrim:Kharjo "Skyrim:Kharjo").
- **[Dungeon Delving](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeon_Delving_(Caves) "Skyrim:Dungeon Delving (Caves)")**: Do a favor by retrieving an item from a cave.
- **[Find Shahvee's Amulet of Zenithar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeon_Delving_(Bandits) "Skyrim:Dungeon Delving (Bandits)")**: Do a favor by retrieving an item from a bandit hideout.
- **[Harsh Master](/wiki/Skyrim:Harsh_Master "Skyrim:Harsh Master")**: Take care of a runaway crew member.
- **[Kill the Bandit Leader](/wiki/Skyrim:Kill_the_Bandit_Leader "Skyrim:Kill the Bandit Leader")**: Do a favor by slaying a bandit leader.

- This location is one of many potential targets for [one or more of the Radiant quests](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant#Quest_Locations "Skyrim:Radiant").

### Exterior

The cave only be reached from directly north or south. The easiest approach is to travel to [Anga's Mill](/wiki/Skyrim:Anga%27s_Mill "Skyrim:Anga's Mill"), cross the river to the south, and climb the path. Outside the cave is a small bandit camp, with two bandits guarding the entrance: one is an archer, and the other is a melee fighter. There are two small tents, both containing unowned bedrolls, and three [food](/wiki/Skyrim:Food "Skyrim:Food") barrels and one food sack around the tents. In front of the tents is an old campfire, with a metal pot containing an [ingredient](/wiki/Skyrim:Ingredients "Skyrim:Ingredients") hanging over it. An [iron ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Iron Ore Vein") is among the rocks to the southwest, and a number of [snowberry](/wiki/Skyrim:Snowberries "Skyrim:Snowberries") bushes are in the vicinity. A wooden door serves as the entrance to the cave.

### Uttering Hills Cave

Bandits by the fire

The entrance leads into an icy tunnel that descends to the north, passing a small table with a random [poison](/wiki/Skyrim:Poison "Skyrim:Poison") and a random [mace](/wiki/Skyrim:Mace "Skyrim:Mace"). The mined area turns into snowy cave tunnels and has a few corners. Just before the tunnel opens into a small chamber, there is a [mammoth tusk](/wiki/Skyrim:Mammoth_Tusk "Skyrim:Mammoth Tusk") on the ground beside some mammoth ribs. In the chamber, the path diverges into paths to the left and right, each patrolled by a single leveled bandit. Following either tunnel leads to two leveled bandits warming themselves by a campfire with a [cooking pot](/wiki/Skyrim:Cooking_Pot "Skyrim:Cooking Pot") hanging over it. There is an unlocked [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") beside the fire, a [wood chopping block](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Chopping_Block "Skyrim:Wood Chopping Block") to the southwest, and a [corundum ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Corundum_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Corundum Ore Vein") to the west.

A wooden door to the north leads to a circular tower room with stairs descending. At the bottom is a table with a built-in [alchemy lab](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemy_Lab "Skyrim:Alchemy Lab") and a random [potion](/wiki/Skyrim:Potions "Skyrim:Potions"). Hanging above the lab is a bunch of dried [elves ear](/wiki/Skyrim:Elves_Ear "Skyrim:Elves Ear"), a bunch of dried [frost mirriam](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Mirriam "Skyrim:Frost Mirriam"), and two [pheasants](/wiki/Skyrim:Pheasant "Skyrim:Pheasant"). A leveled bandit is patrolling between the room and the next set of descending stairs, and another bandit is sitting at the far end of the passage below. To the left of the stairs is an adept-locked chest. At the far end of the passage down the stairs are three small cells that can be opened with pull chains, but these never seem to contain prisoners.

Partway along the passage is a room on the right containing a long wooden table holding a [cabbage](/wiki/Skyrim:Cabbage "Skyrim:Cabbage"), some [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread"), and some cheese. Two barrels are in the southeast corner, one containing [ingredients](/wiki/Skyrim:Ingredients "Skyrim:Ingredients"), the other foodstuffs. To the left, against the east wall, is a set of shelves holding many more pieces of cheese. There are wooden doors on both the east and west walls. During the related quest, there is a [Summerset Shadow](/wiki/Skyrim:Summerset_Shadow "Skyrim:Summerset Shadow") banner which can be burned hanging from the ceiling by the north wall (see the [Summerset Shadows](/wiki/Skyrim:Summerset_Shadows "Skyrim:Summerset Shadows") quest page for details).

The room to the west contains up to two bandits, one awake, and one sleeping. The room also features four unowned single beds, with two each against the north and south walls. Two sets of shelves against the west wall hold several bottles of [alto wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Alto_Wine "Skyrim:Alto Wine"), a bottle of [wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Wine "Skyrim:Wine"), a [salt pile](/wiki/Skyrim:Salt_Pile "Skyrim:Salt Pile"), a [knapsack](/wiki/Skyrim:Knapsack "Skyrim:Knapsack"), and a leveled [invisibility potion](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Brief_Invisibility "Skyrim:Potion of Brief Invisibility"). The room to the east contains the bandit chief, along with an unowned single bed against the east wall. Opposite the bed is a set of shelves holding six common [books](/wiki/Skyrim:Books "Skyrim:Books"), an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"), and a copy of the [One-handed](/wiki/Skyrim:One-handed "Skyrim:One-handed") [skill book](/wiki/Skyrim:Skill_Books "Skyrim:Skill Books") *[Mace Etiquette](/wiki/Skyrim:Mace_Etiquette "Skyrim:Mace Etiquette")*. Against the south wall is a tall wardrobe containing [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothes "Skyrim:Clothes"), with an unlocked boss chest to the right.

## Notes

- A [gold ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Gold_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Gold Ore Vein") and a corundum ore vein are east of the cave, beneath a flag near an unmarked [frost troll](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Troll "Skyrim:Frost Troll")'s lair.

## Bugs

- After the quest Summerset Shadows, members of the Summerset Shadows replace the bandits and follow the bandits' routines. If you have an objective to kill the bandit leader here after the quest, it will be impossible to complete it, as the bandit leader will be the corpse of [Linwe](/wiki/Skyrim:Linwe "Skyrim:Linwe"), who is already dead. Similarly, in order to mark this location as cleared, the bandit chief must be killed prior to starting the quest Summerset Shadows.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 1.3.3, fixes this bug.
  -  The first issue can be fixed by resurrecting Linwe with the console by targeting his corpse and typing `resurrect 1`, then killing him again. See the [quest page](/wiki/Skyrim:Kill_the_Bandit_Leader#Bugs "Skyrim:Kill the Bandit Leader") for more details on this and other possible fixes.

## Maps

- 

  Uttering Hills Cave

# Skyrim:Vahlok's Tomb

## Overview

| # of Zones | 1 |
| [Clearable](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons#Clearing "Skyrim:Dungeons") | Yes |
| [Dungeon](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons "Skyrim:Dungeons") | Yes |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | Never (storage is [safe](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Safe_Locations "Skyrim:Containers")) |
| [Level](/wiki/Skyrim:Encounter_Zone_Level "Skyrim:Encounter Zone Level") | Min: 30 |
| Occupants | |
| [Corrupted Shades](/wiki/Skyrim:Corrupted_Shade "Skyrim:Corrupted Shade"), [Draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Draugr "Skyrim:Draugr"), [Vahlok the Jailor](/wiki/Skyrim:Vahlok_the_Jailor "Skyrim:Vahlok the Jailor") | |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| DLC2VahloksTombExterior01, DLC2VahloksTomb01 | |
| Region | |

| Location | |
| South-southeast of [Thirsk Mead Hall](/wiki/Skyrim:Thirsk_Mead_Hall "Skyrim:Thirsk Mead Hall") North-northeast of [Ashfallow Citadel](/wiki/Skyrim:Ashfallow_Citadel "Skyrim:Ashfallow Citadel") | |
| Special Features | |
| [Word Wall](/wiki/Skyrim:Word_Wall "Skyrim:Word Wall") | [Battle Fury](/wiki/Skyrim:Battle_Fury "Skyrim:Battle Fury") |

Vahlok's Tomb

**Vahlok's Tomb** is a small [Nordic ruin](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Ruins "Skyrim:Nordic Ruins") south-southeast of [Thirsk Mead Hall](/wiki/Skyrim:Thirsk_Mead_Hall "Skyrim:Thirsk Mead Hall") containing [corrupted shades](/wiki/Skyrim:Corrupted_Shade "Skyrim:Corrupted Shade"), [draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Draugr "Skyrim:Draugr"), and the [dragon priest](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Priest "Skyrim:Dragon Priest") [Vahlok the Jailor](/wiki/Skyrim:Vahlok_the_Jailor "Skyrim:Vahlok the Jailor"). It contains only one zone, *[Vahlok's Tomb](#Vahlok.27s_Tomb)*.

The tomb contains three [word walls](/wiki/Skyrim:Word_Wall "Skyrim:Word Wall"), which teach all three words of the [Battle Fury](/wiki/Skyrim:Battle_Fury "Skyrim:Battle Fury") [dragon shout](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Shouts "Skyrim:Dragon Shouts"). The tomb will not be accessible and its map marker will not appear until after starting the quest [Lost Legacy](/wiki/Skyrim:Lost_Legacy "Skyrim:Lost Legacy"); before starting the quest, a landslide blocks the entrance.

## Related Quests

- **[Lost Legacy](/wiki/Skyrim:Lost_Legacy "Skyrim:Lost Legacy")**: Travel to Vahlok's Tomb and defeat [Vahlok the Jailor](/wiki/Skyrim:Vahlok_the_Jailor "Skyrim:Vahlok the Jailor").

### Vahlok's Tomb

The main chamber

The entrance leads into a passage that descends to the south and turns west to open into the large main chamber. A short flight of stairs leads down to a platform with a pedestal holding an etched tablet and a handle. Two more sets of stairs on either side of the platform descend to the west to a larger central platform, in the middle of which is a trap door over a firepit. North and south of the firepit are stairs and short bridges leading to gates on the north and south sides of the chamber. West of the firepit is another short flight of stairs ascending to a cage containing another pedestal holding an etched tablet and a handle. The cage is initially locked, with two keyholes flanking the cage; during the related quest, you must first proceed through the areas beyond the north and south gates to obtain the [left](/wiki/Skyrim:Amethyst_Claw,_Left_Half "Skyrim:Amethyst Claw, Left Half") and [right](/wiki/Skyrim:Amethyst_Claw,_Right_Half "Skyrim:Amethyst Claw, Right Half") halves of the dragon claw required for the keyholes. Eight dead draugr are found on and around the platforms and bridges. To open the north and south gates, you must drag one of the draugr on top of the trap door over the firepit, then use the handle on the first platform to open the trap door and "sacrifice" the draugr body.

North and south of the first platform are stairs descending to the flooded bottom level of the chamber. Near the bottom of the north set of stairs on a ledge just above the water level is a [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest"). Under the water are two more chests, with one near the northwest corner of the chamber next to a large [coin purse](/wiki/Skyrim:Coin_Purse "Skyrim:Coin Purse"), and the other southeast of the first and west of the locked cage partly buried near an underwater slope. Southeast of the second chest under the water is a [potion of plentiful healing](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Plentiful_Healing "Skyrim:Potion of Plentiful Healing"). Returning to the central platform and crossing the short bridge to the area in front of the south gate, on top of the angled pillar with a sculpted face nearby to the west is another large coin purse.

See the [quest page](/wiki/Skyrim:Lost_Legacy#A_Tomb_Uncovered "Skyrim:Lost Legacy") for details on the areas beyond the north and south gates. In addition to the word wall and dragon claw piece, the area beyond the north gate contains three [burial urns](/wiki/Skyrim:Burial_Urn "Skyrim:Burial Urn"), a [soul gem](/wiki/Skyrim:Soul_Gems "Skyrim:Soul Gems"), a medium coin purse, an adept-locked chest, and another chest. Also, upon exiting the north area and returning to the main chamber, immediately below the exit on a lower ledge at the base of the large stone arch spanning the chamber is a chest. Similarly, the area beyond the south gate contains a [staff of flames](/wiki/Skyrim:Staff_of_Flames "Skyrim:Staff of Flames"), an [ancient Nord sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Sword "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Sword"), an [ancient Nord bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Bow "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Bow"), a quiver of [ancient Nord arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Arrow "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Arrow"), and two chests.

Once you have both halves of the dragon claw and use them to unlock the cage door west of the firepit, the handle in the cage creates a glowing blue magical bridge to a ledge on the west side of the chamber. Cross the bridge to the ledge, descend a short set of stairs, and proceed west through the doorway into a short corridor. On the south side of the corridor is a wooden door barred from the other side. At the west end of the corridor is a handle on a plinth, with a [potion of healing](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Healing "Skyrim:Potion of Healing") on a sconce just above the plinth. Looking through the opening next to the plinth, you will see you are near the ceiling of a large partly flooded room with waterfalls cascading down the walls. Corrupted shades wander the water far below at the bottom of the room, but may not engage you at this time.

The handle creates another magical bridge that crosses the room to a second short corridor a short distance to the northwest. At the west end of this second corridor are two burial urns, a [garnet](/wiki/Skyrim:Garnet "Skyrim:Garnet"), an [emerald](/wiki/Skyrim:Emerald "Skyrim:Emerald"), and another barred wooden door to the south. At the north end of the corridor is another handle on a plinth, which creates another magical bridge that crosses the room to a third short corridor a short distance to the northwest. At the north end of this third corridor is a lootable [skeleton](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeleton "Skyrim:Skeleton"), an empty [common soul gem](/wiki/Skyrim:Common_Soul_Gem "Skyrim:Common Soul Gem"), and another barred wooden door to the east. At the west end of the corridor is another handle on a plinth, which creates a final magical bridge to a set of iron doors at the north end of the room. If at any point you fall to the flooded bottom level of the room, you can battle the shades, optionally harvest two [white cap](/wiki/Skyrim:White_Cap "Skyrim:White Cap") mushrooms that grow near two of the fallen stones along the walls to the northeast and northwest, and pass through one of three doorways leading to passages ascending to the far sides of the three barred doors.

Beyond the iron doors to the north is the usual wide corridor leading to a [puzzle door](/wiki/Skyrim:Puzzle_Door "Skyrim:Puzzle Door"), with the solution **Eagle—Wolf—Dragon** starting from the outer ring and working inwards. Beyond the puzzle door, a long flight of stairs ascends to a portcullis raised using the pull chain to the left. Beyond the portcullis is the [boss room](/wiki/Skyrim:Lost_Legacy#Vahlok_the_Jailor "Skyrim:Lost Legacy"), which contains the sarcophagus of [Vahlok the Jailor](/wiki/Skyrim:Vahlok_the_Jailor "Skyrim:Vahlok the Jailor"), a boss chest, and the final word wall, but nothing else of note. Beware of the numerous [flame spout traps](/wiki/Skyrim:Flame_Spout_Trap "Skyrim:Flame Spout Trap") which cover the floor here. As there is no shortcut from this room to the exit of the tomb, after defeating the dragon priest, you must retrace your steps to the entrance.

## Notes

- If you attempt to read the etched tablets before [Tharstan](/wiki/Skyrim:Tharstan "Skyrim:Tharstan") interprets them for you, a message will display indicating you can't understand the writing.

## Bugs

- If the puzzle door does not work, check [here](/wiki/Skyrim:Puzzle_Totems#Bugs "Skyrim:Puzzle Totems") for possible fixes.
- Vahlok will respawn, even though the tomb does not. He is the only named dragon priest to do so.
  -  The [Unofficial Dragonborn Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Dragonborn_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Dragonborn Patch"), version 1.0.1, fixes this bug.


---
