# Skyrim:Vald's Debt

## Overview

|  |
| --- |
| **This page is currently being rewritten as part of the [Skyrim Quest Redesign Project](/wiki/UESPWiki:Skyrim_Quest_Redesign_Project "UESPWiki:Skyrim Quest Redesign Project").** The page is being rewritten and checked in several stages. All users are welcome to make changes to the page. If you make a change that is relevant to the project, please update this template accordingly, and make sure you have observed the [project guidelines](/wiki/UESPWiki:Skyrim_Quest_Redesign_Project#Guidelines "UESPWiki:Skyrim Quest Redesign Project").  Details  **Walkthrough**: written by [Beezer1029](/wiki/User:Beezer1029 "User:Beezer1029"), *not checked*  **Reward**: written by [Nephele](/wiki/User:Nephele "User:Nephele"), checked by [Riliane](/wiki/User:Riliane "User:Riliane") |

This article is about the quest. For the note, see [Vald's Debt (note)](/wiki/Skyrim:Vald%27s_Debt_(note) "Skyrim:Vald's Debt (note)").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Find the [Quill of Gemination](/wiki/Skyrim:Quill_of_Gemination "Skyrim:Quill of Gemination") to clear [Vald](/wiki/Skyrim:Vald "Skyrim:Vald")'s debt to [Maven Black-Briar](/wiki/Skyrim:Maven_Black-Briar "Skyrim:Maven Black-Briar").** | |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Location(s): | None |
| Reward: | The [key](/wiki/Skyrim:Mercer%27s_House_Key "Skyrim:Mercer's House Key") to [Riftweald Manor](/wiki/Skyrim:Riftweald_Manor "Skyrim:Riftweald Manor") |
| ID: | FreeformValdDebt |
| --- | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | |  | **Concurrent** [The Pursuit](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Pursuit "Skyrim:The Pursuit") |  | | |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | Concurrent | [The Pursuit](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Pursuit "Skyrim:The Pursuit") | | |

Vald's Strongbox

## Quick Walkthrough

1. Talk to [Maven Black-Briar](/wiki/Skyrim:Maven_Black-Briar "Skyrim:Maven Black-Briar") about erasing [Vald](/wiki/Skyrim:Vald "Skyrim:Vald")'s debt.
2. Find the [Quill of Gemination](/wiki/Skyrim:Quill_of_Gemination "Skyrim:Quill of Gemination") in [Lake Honrich](/wiki/Skyrim:Lake_Honrich "Skyrim:Lake Honrich").
3. Bring the quill to Maven.
4. Give the note to Vald.

### To Catch a Thief

With hard evidence in hand, you and [Karliah](/wiki/Skyrim:Karliah "Skyrim:Karliah") inform [Brynjolf](/wiki/Skyrim:Brynjolf "Skyrim:Brynjolf") of [Mercer Frey's](/wiki/Skyrim:Mercer_Frey "Skyrim:Mercer Frey") nefarious deeds. Brynjolf decides that more intelligence is needed on Mercer's plans and asks you break into [Riftweald Manor](/wiki/Skyrim:Riftweald_Manor "Skyrim:Riftweald Manor") to see what you can find out—with impunity.

### The Watchdog

Ask Brynjolf about Riftweald Manor and he will mention a "trained watchdog" guarding the house; a man named [Vald](/wiki/Skyrim:Vald "Skyrim:Vald") that Mercer is extorting. Brynjolf suggests you speak to [Vex](/wiki/Skyrim:Vex "Skyrim:Vex") for more on Vald. Vex has a few choice words about Vald, but will also tell you that he is working for Mercer Frey because of a debt he owes to [Maven Black-Briar](/wiki/Skyrim:Maven_Black-Briar "Skyrim:Maven Black-Briar"). Clearing the debt is suggested as one way to get Vald to stop guarding Mercer's house.

### The Queen Bee and the Quill

To learn the details of the debt, track down Maven, who can typically be found at [The Bee and Barb](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Bee_and_Barb "Skyrim:The Bee and Barb"). She tells you that years ago she commissioned a [unique quill](/wiki/Skyrim:Quill_of_Gemination "Skyrim:Quill of Gemination") from Winterhold. Vald was to deliver the quill to her, but he lost it at the bottom of [Lake Honrich](/wiki/Skyrim:Lake_Honrich "Skyrim:Lake Honrich"), outside of [Riften](/wiki/Skyrim:Riften "Skyrim:Riften").

The location of the quill shown on the local map

From [Riften Stables](/wiki/Skyrim:Riften_Stables "Skyrim:Riften Stables"), head southwest until you reach the edge of the lake. Ahead, you should see a large ship docked at Riften. Swim towards the westernmost end of that ship and look west across the lake towards [Goldenglow Estate](/wiki/Skyrim:Goldenglow_Estate "Skyrim:Goldenglow Estate") to see a similar ship docked there. Halfway to the other boat, the prow of Vald's sunken rowboat can be found at the bottom of the lake. For reference, a small island to the north will mark the halfway point. You'll see half a boat prow sticking out of the ground and the quill is located in Vald's Strongbox (leveled lock) [(map)](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=Skyrim&centeron=Vald%27s+Strongbox). Pick it, take the quill and a leveled amount of gold, then head back to find Maven. She'll hand you a [note](/wiki/Skyrim:Vald%27s_Debt_(note) "Skyrim:Vald's Debt (note)") to take to Vald.

### Clearing the way

Head to the back gate of Riftweald Manor and wait there until Vald comes to talk to you. Inform him that you have erased his debt, and he will want to do you a good turn as well, giving you the key to the Manor and opening the back gate for you.

## Notes

- The quill does not spawn until you have spoken to Maven about it, so you cannot skip this step.
- It's possible to keep the quill for yourself. To do this, talk to Maven to get the quill to spawn, go and obtain the quill, then go to Vald and intimidate/bribe him to let you pass. The miscellaneous quest will disappear from your quest log and the quill will no longer be marked as a quest item and is free to be dropped/deposited. This was done with the Unofficial Skyrim Patch installed. Results may vary. [Confirmed in USSEP 4.3.0]

## Bugs

- Even if you kill Vald, you will still have the dialogue option to erase his debt with Maven. Attempting to erase Vald's debt after he is dead will still earn you leveled gold, but you will be unable to complete the quest by delivering the letter of pardon. The quest and item will stay active.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 1.2.4, fixes this bug.
  - If the undelivered letter bothers you, it seems you can place it on owned bookshelves. You can also leave it on his corpse.
- The dialogue option to tell Vald his debt is gone appears as soon as you've picked up the quill. Turning it in to Maven is therefore optional.
- It's possible that Vald won't come talk to you at the gate when you bring him the note to clear his debt, becoming hostile toward you instead. This can occur if you've walked too close to the yard after the quest has started, but before erasing Vald's debt.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 2.0.4, fixes this bug.
  - Sometimes the bug occurs even with the Unofficial Patch.
  - You can also try using [Pacify](/wiki/Skyrim:Pacify_(effect) "Skyrim:Pacify (effect)") if he becomes hostile and you don't wish to kill him.
  - Alternatively, approach him with your weapon out and when he attacks go into the console, click him, type `setav aggression 0`, exit the console, then sheathe your weapon.

## Quest Stages

| Vald's Debt (FreeformValdDebt) | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Stage | Finishes Quest | Journal Entry |
| 10 |  | *Objective 10:* Talk to Maven about Vald's debt |
| 20 |  | *Objective 20:* Locate the Quill of Gemination under Lake Honrich |
| 30 |  | *Objective 30:* Bring the Quill of Gemination to Maven |

- The following empty quest stages were omitted from the table: 0, 40, 200.

Notes

- Any text displayed in angle brackets (e.g., `<Alias=LocationHold>`) is dynamically set by the Radiant Quest system, and will be filled in with the appropriate word(s) when seen in game.
- Not all Journal Entries may appear in your journal; which entries appear and which entries do not depends on the manner in which the quest is done.
- Stages are not always in order of progress. This is usually the case with quests that have multiple possible outcomes or quests where certain tasks may be done in any order. Some stages may therefore repeat objectives seen in other stages.
- If an entry is marked as "Finishes Quest" it means the quest disappears from the Active Quest list, but you may still receive new entries for that quest.
- On the PC, it is possible to use the [console](/wiki/Skyrim:Console "Skyrim:Console") to advance through the quest by entering `setstage FreeformValdDebt stage`, where `stage` is the number of the stage you wish to complete. It is not possible to un-complete (i.e. go back) quest stages, but it is possible to clear all stages of the quest using `resetquest FreeformValdDebt`.

*This [Skyrim](/wiki/Skyrim:Skyrim "Skyrim:Skyrim")-related article is a [stub](/wiki/UESPWiki:Stub "UESPWiki:Stub"). You can help by [expanding it](https://en.uesp.net/w/index.php?title=Skyrim:Vald%27s_Debt&action=edit).*

# Skyrim:Valindor's House

## Overview

|  |
| --- |
| **This article is being developed as part of the Skyrim Houses Redesign Project. If you see this tag it means the article can still be improved. Please see the [project page](/wiki/UESPWiki:SHRP "UESPWiki:SHRP") for guidelines.** |

| RiftenValindorsHouse | |
| Region | |

| Location | |
| [Riften](/wiki/Skyrim:Riften "Skyrim:Riften") | |

Valindor's House

**Valindor's House** is a house which is owned by [Valindor](/wiki/Skyrim:Valindor "Skyrim:Valindor").

It is located on the lower levels of [Riften](/wiki/Skyrim:Riften "Skyrim:Riften"), alongside the canal which flows through the city. It is next door to [Marise Aravel's House](/wiki/Skyrim:Marise_Aravel%27s_House "Skyrim:Marise Aravel's House"), and below the [Temple of Mara](/wiki/Skyrim:Temple_of_Mara "Skyrim:Temple of Mara").

## Residents

| Residents |
| --- |
| [Valindor](/wiki/Skyrim:Valindor "Skyrim:Valindor") |

## Walkthrough

Valindor's house

This is a single-room dwelling, with a partial wall dividing it into separate areas. The main living space consists of a fireplace against the east wall, two chairs pulled up in front, a small table with a bottle of [wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Wine "Skyrim:Wine") on it between them, and a [cooking pot](/wiki/Skyrim:Cooking_Pot "Skyrim:Cooking Pot") on the hearth. Along the north wall, a set of shelves holds two bottles of [Nord mead](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord_Mead "Skyrim:Nord Mead"), a [sweet roll](/wiki/Skyrim:Sweet_Roll "Skyrim:Sweet Roll"), several [potatoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Potato "Skyrim:Potato"), an [apple pie](/wiki/Skyrim:Apple_Pie "Skyrim:Apple Pie"), several pieces of cheese, and a bottle of [Alto wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Alto_Wine "Skyrim:Alto Wine"). Against the west wall is a dresser, with a copy of the book *[Of Crossed Daggers](/wiki/Skyrim:Of_Crossed_Daggers "Skyrim:Of Crossed Daggers")* on top. A dining table, a bench, some barrels and some sacks of [food](/wiki/Skyrim:Food "Skyrim:Food") stand beside the partial wall; hanging above are two [garlic](/wiki/Skyrim:Garlic "Skyrim:Garlic") braids, two bunches of [elves ear](/wiki/Skyrim:Elves_Ear "Skyrim:Elves Ear"), and two bunches of [frost mirriam](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Mirriam "Skyrim:Frost Mirriam"). The table is lain with a [baked potato](/wiki/Skyrim:Baked_Potatoes "Skyrim:Baked Potatoes"), a portion of [grilled leeks](/wiki/Skyrim:Grilled_Leeks "Skyrim:Grilled Leeks"), a loaf of [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread"), an [Eidar cheese wheel](/wiki/Skyrim:Eidar_Cheese_Wheel "Skyrim:Eidar Cheese Wheel"), a bottle of Alto wine, and a bottle of Nord mead.

Beyond the partition is a sparse bedroom, consisting of an owned, single bed; on an end table beside it lies a copy of the book *[Horker Attacks](/wiki/Skyrim:Horker_Attacks "Skyrim:Horker Attacks").* To the right is a wardrobe, storing [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothes "Skyrim:Clothes"); opposite this, along the south wall, is an unlocked wooden [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") containing minor loot.

## Notes

- There is a  "Loot") Loot [shadowmark](/wiki/Skyrim:Thieves_Guild_(faction)#Shadowmarks "Skyrim:Thieves Guild (faction)") on the left doorstep.
- Once you have joined the [Thieves Guild](/wiki/Skyrim:Thieves_Guild_(faction) "Skyrim:Thieves Guild (faction)"), a barrel that is carved with the  "Cache") Cache shadowmark appears opposite the entrance, on the walkway that travels alongside the canal.
- If you infect Valindor with vampirism during the [New Allegiances](/wiki/Skyrim:New_Allegiances "Skyrim:New Allegiances")[DG](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard") quest, a coffin will appear in his house[*verification needed — location?*] and can be used to sleep and get the vampire rested bonus. Valindor does not seem to use it however, and will still sleep in his old bed.
- During [New Allegiances](/wiki/Skyrim:New_Allegiances "Skyrim:New Allegiances")[DG](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard") quest, the door to Valindor's House will lock if night falls while inside it. Unlocking the door at this point is considered a crime. Just wait until 04:00 AM for the door to unlock itself.

# Skyrim:Valtheim Towers

## Overview

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | [SR-mapicon-Nordic Tower.png](/wiki/File:SR-mapicon-Nordic_Tower.png) | Nordic Tower: Valtheim Towers ([view on map](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=Skyrim&centeron=Valtheim+Towers)) | | |
| [Clearable](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons#Clearing "Skyrim:Dungeons") | Yes |
| [Dungeon](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons "Skyrim:Dungeons") | Yes |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | 10 days or 30 days |
| [Level](/wiki/Skyrim:Encounter_Zone_Level "Skyrim:Encounter Zone Level") | Min: 6 |
| Occupants | |

| *[The Black Arrow, v2](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Black_Arrow,_v2 "Skyrim:The Black Arrow, v2")* | |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| ValtheimKeepExterior01, ValtheimKeepExterior02, ValtheimKeepExterior03 | |
| Region | |
| [Whiterun Hold](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun_Hold "Skyrim:Whiterun Hold") | |
| Location | |
| East of [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun "Skyrim:Whiterun") West of [Darkshade](/wiki/Skyrim:Darkshade "Skyrim:Darkshade") | |
| Special Features | |
| # of [Alchemy Labs](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemy_Labs "Skyrim:Alchemy Labs") | 1 |

| # of [Clay Deposits](/wiki/Skyrim:Clay "Skyrim:Clay")[HF](/wiki/Skyrim:Hearthfire "Skyrim:Hearthfire") | 1 |
| [Ore Veins](/wiki/Skyrim:Mining "Skyrim:Mining") | |
| # of [Corundum](/wiki/Skyrim:Corundum_Ore "Skyrim:Corundum Ore") | 1 |

Valtheim Towers

**Valtheim Towers** is a small pair of linked ruined [Nordic towers](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Towers "Skyrim:Nordic Towers") east of [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun "Skyrim:Whiterun") containing [bandits](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit "Skyrim:Bandit").

## Related Quests

- **[Amulet of the Moon](/wiki/Skyrim:Amulet_of_the_Moon "Skyrim:Amulet of the Moon")**: Find the [Moon Amulet](/wiki/Skyrim:Moon_Amulet "Skyrim:Moon Amulet") for [Kharjo](/wiki/Skyrim:Kharjo "Skyrim:Kharjo"). ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant"))
- **[Dungeon Delving](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeon_Delving_(Bandits) "Skyrim:Dungeon Delving (Bandits)")**: Do a favor by retrieving an item from a bandit hideout. ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant"))
- **[Trouble in Skyrim](/wiki/Skyrim:Trouble_in_Skyrim "Skyrim:Trouble in Skyrim")**: Fulfill a contract for the [Companions](/wiki/Skyrim:Companions "Skyrim:Companions") to clear out a troublesome area. ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant"))

- This location is one of many potential targets for [one or more of the Radiant quests](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant#Quest_Locations "Skyrim:Radiant").

## Walkthrough

The towers straddle the [White River](/wiki/Skyrim:White_River "Skyrim:White River") along the main road east of Whiterun. They support a stone bridge spanning the river high above. Along the road just outside the southern tower is a [cooking pot](/wiki/Skyrim:Cooking_Pot "Skyrim:Cooking Pot"). The treasure chest marked on [Treasure Map II](/wiki/Skyrim:Treasure_Map_II "Skyrim:Treasure Map II") is on the rocky riverbank just east of the southern tower.

Approaching from the main road, a Bandit by the cooking pot guards the passage, extorting a toll from the player when they come in the vicinity. She will demand 200 gold, which can be paid to keep her and her gang calm. Alternatively, by passing a low-level [Speech](/wiki/Skyrim:Speech "Skyrim:Speech") check, she will accept only 50 gold, granting Speech XP (see Notes). Refusing to pay, entering the tower, or loitering in the area for too long will cause her and all the other Bandits to become hostile.

The southern tower contains a [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") at its base rigged with a [swinging mace](/wiki/Skyrim:Swinging_Mace "Skyrim:Swinging Mace") trap. The trigger may be deactivated by [picking](/wiki/Skyrim:Lockpicking "Skyrim:Lockpicking") a leveled lock. Stairs inside rise to the second level, from which an exterior wooden ramp leads up to the stone bridge and the third floor of the tower. At the top is a platform normally guarded by an archer. A second archer patrols the bridge between the towers.

Across the bridge, the northern tower has two interior levels, with a dresser on the first floor and an [alchemy lab](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemy_Lab "Skyrim:Alchemy Lab") on the second. A copy of the [Archery](/wiki/Skyrim:Archery "Skyrim:Archery") [skill book](/wiki/Skyrim:Skill_Books "Skyrim:Skill Books") *[The Black Arrow, v2](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Black_Arrow,_v2 "Skyrim:The Black Arrow, v2")* is on the bedside table on the third level. Above this is a platform where you can find the bandit chief, who carries a random [enchanted weapon](/wiki/Skyrim:Generic_Magic_Weapons "Skyrim:Generic Magic Weapons"). A random enchanted bow can normally be found on the table here.

The first floor has a second door leading out to the mountains. An [iron ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Iron Ore Vein") is immediately north of the door, and to the right is another archer whose chair is near a [corundum ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Corundum_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Corundum Ore Vein"). A [clay deposit](/wiki/Skyrim:Clay_Deposit "Skyrim:Clay Deposit")[HF](/wiki/Skyrim:Hearthfire "Skyrim:Hearthfire") is near the riverbank just west of the northern tower.

## Notes

- In the local map and quest journal, Valtheim Towers is designated "Valtheim Keep".
- If you approach the bandit who demands the toll in a different form, such as [beast form](/wiki/Skyrim:Lycanthropy "Skyrim:Lycanthropy") or [Vampire Lord](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Lord "Skyrim:Vampire Lord")[DG](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard"), they will still demand money, even if you are attacking.
- Persuading or intimidating the toll bandit gives [Speech](/wiki/Skyrim:Speech "Skyrim:Speech") skill experience. Moving away from the towers sufficiently far resets the toll demand; going west a short distance to a fallen tree that half covers the road is sufficient. This allows for fast Speech leveling.
- If the location is set as the destination for a radiant quest, the Bandit outside will not ask for a toll and all Bandits will be hostile.

## Bugs

- There seems to be an issue with the "Intimidate" option when dealing with the bandit who demands the toll. Even if your intimidation is successful, 200 gold is removed from your inventory.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 1.0, fixes this bug.
- If you succeed in persuading the bandit to accept 50 gold instead of 200, the 50 gold is never added to the bandit's inventory.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 2.0.5, fixes this bug.
- The intimidation option for the bandits here doesn't have a goodbye flag set if you succeed.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Legendary Edition Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Legendary_Edition_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Legendary Edition Patch"), version 3.0.6, fixes this bug.
- [Aela the Huntress](/wiki/Skyrim:Aela_the_Huntress "Skyrim:Aela the Huntress") might send you here for the quest [Striking the Heart](/wiki/Skyrim:Striking_the_Heart "Skyrim:Striking the Heart"), but there may be no one here to kill. This prevents you from completing the quest and advancing in the [Companions](/wiki/Skyrim:Companions "Skyrim:Companions") questline.
  - Reload a save from before you were assigned the quest and obtain one with a different target location.
- It may not be possible to pick up the enchanted bow in the northern tower.
  - Saving and loading the new save fixes the problem.
- Sometimes when the bandit leader is killed, this location will not be marked as cleared. Reloading a save and killing him again does not solve this problem.
  - Use the [console](/wiki/Skyrim:Console "Skyrim:Console") command `setlocationcleared 000192c1 1` to force the game to mark it as cleared.

# Skyrim:Valtheim Towers

## Overview

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | [SR-mapicon-Nordic Tower.png](/wiki/File:SR-mapicon-Nordic_Tower.png) | Nordic Tower: Valtheim Towers ([view on map](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=Skyrim&centeron=Valtheim+Towers)) | | |
| [Clearable](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons#Clearing "Skyrim:Dungeons") | Yes |
| [Dungeon](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons "Skyrim:Dungeons") | Yes |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | 10 days or 30 days |
| [Level](/wiki/Skyrim:Encounter_Zone_Level "Skyrim:Encounter Zone Level") | Min: 6 |
| Occupants | |
| [Bandits](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit "Skyrim:Bandit") | |
| Important Treasure | |
| *[The Black Arrow, v2](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Black_Arrow,_v2 "Skyrim:The Black Arrow, v2")* | |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| ValtheimKeepExterior01, ValtheimKeepExterior02, ValtheimKeepExterior03 | |
| Region | |
| [Whiterun Hold](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun_Hold "Skyrim:Whiterun Hold") | |
| Location | |
| East of [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun "Skyrim:Whiterun") West of [Darkshade](/wiki/Skyrim:Darkshade "Skyrim:Darkshade") | |
| Special Features | |

| [Ore Veins](/wiki/Skyrim:Mining "Skyrim:Mining") | |
| # of [Corundum](/wiki/Skyrim:Corundum_Ore "Skyrim:Corundum Ore") | 1 |
| # of [Iron](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Ore "Skyrim:Iron Ore") | 1 |

Valtheim Towers

**Valtheim Towers** is a small pair of linked ruined [Nordic towers](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Towers "Skyrim:Nordic Towers") east of [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun "Skyrim:Whiterun") containing [bandits](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit "Skyrim:Bandit").

## Related Quests

- **[Amulet of the Moon](/wiki/Skyrim:Amulet_of_the_Moon "Skyrim:Amulet of the Moon")**: Find the [Moon Amulet](/wiki/Skyrim:Moon_Amulet "Skyrim:Moon Amulet") for [Kharjo](/wiki/Skyrim:Kharjo "Skyrim:Kharjo"). ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant"))
- **[Dungeon Delving](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeon_Delving_(Bandits) "Skyrim:Dungeon Delving (Bandits)")**: Do a favor by retrieving an item from a bandit hideout. ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant"))
- **[Trouble in Skyrim](/wiki/Skyrim:Trouble_in_Skyrim "Skyrim:Trouble in Skyrim")**: Fulfill a contract for the [Companions](/wiki/Skyrim:Companions "Skyrim:Companions") to clear out a troublesome area. ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant"))

- This location is one of many potential targets for [one or more of the Radiant quests](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant#Quest_Locations "Skyrim:Radiant").

## Walkthrough

The towers straddle the [White River](/wiki/Skyrim:White_River "Skyrim:White River") along the main road east of Whiterun. They support a stone bridge spanning the river high above. Along the road just outside the southern tower is a [cooking pot](/wiki/Skyrim:Cooking_Pot "Skyrim:Cooking Pot"). The treasure chest marked on [Treasure Map II](/wiki/Skyrim:Treasure_Map_II "Skyrim:Treasure Map II") is on the rocky riverbank just east of the southern tower.

Approaching from the main road, a Bandit by the cooking pot guards the passage, extorting a toll from the player when they come in the vicinity. She will demand 200 gold, which can be paid to keep her and her gang calm. Alternatively, by passing a low-level [Speech](/wiki/Skyrim:Speech "Skyrim:Speech") check, she will accept only 50 gold, granting Speech XP (see Notes). Refusing to pay, entering the tower, or loitering in the area for too long will cause her and all the other Bandits to become hostile.

The southern tower contains a [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") at its base rigged with a [swinging mace](/wiki/Skyrim:Swinging_Mace "Skyrim:Swinging Mace") trap. The trigger may be deactivated by [picking](/wiki/Skyrim:Lockpicking "Skyrim:Lockpicking") a leveled lock. Stairs inside rise to the second level, from which an exterior wooden ramp leads up to the stone bridge and the third floor of the tower. At the top is a platform normally guarded by an archer. A second archer patrols the bridge between the towers.

Across the bridge, the northern tower has two interior levels, with a dresser on the first floor and an [alchemy lab](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemy_Lab "Skyrim:Alchemy Lab") on the second. A copy of the [Archery](/wiki/Skyrim:Archery "Skyrim:Archery") [skill book](/wiki/Skyrim:Skill_Books "Skyrim:Skill Books") *[The Black Arrow, v2](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Black_Arrow,_v2 "Skyrim:The Black Arrow, v2")* is on the bedside table on the third level. Above this is a platform where you can find the bandit chief, who carries a random [enchanted weapon](/wiki/Skyrim:Generic_Magic_Weapons "Skyrim:Generic Magic Weapons"). A random enchanted bow can normally be found on the table here.

The first floor has a second door leading out to the mountains. An [iron ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Iron Ore Vein") is immediately north of the door, and to the right is another archer whose chair is near a [corundum ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Corundum_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Corundum Ore Vein"). A [clay deposit](/wiki/Skyrim:Clay_Deposit "Skyrim:Clay Deposit")[HF](/wiki/Skyrim:Hearthfire "Skyrim:Hearthfire") is near the riverbank just west of the northern tower.

## Notes

- In the local map and quest journal, Valtheim Towers is designated "Valtheim Keep".
- If you approach the bandit who demands the toll in a different form, such as [beast form](/wiki/Skyrim:Lycanthropy "Skyrim:Lycanthropy") or [Vampire Lord](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Lord "Skyrim:Vampire Lord")[DG](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard"), they will still demand money, even if you are attacking.
- Persuading or intimidating the toll bandit gives [Speech](/wiki/Skyrim:Speech "Skyrim:Speech") skill experience. Moving away from the towers sufficiently far resets the toll demand; going west a short distance to a fallen tree that half covers the road is sufficient. This allows for fast Speech leveling.
- If the location is set as the destination for a radiant quest, the Bandit outside will not ask for a toll and all Bandits will be hostile.

## Bugs

- There seems to be an issue with the "Intimidate" option when dealing with the bandit who demands the toll. Even if your intimidation is successful, 200 gold is removed from your inventory.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 1.0, fixes this bug.
- If you succeed in persuading the bandit to accept 50 gold instead of 200, the 50 gold is never added to the bandit's inventory.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 2.0.5, fixes this bug.
- The intimidation option for the bandits here doesn't have a goodbye flag set if you succeed.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Legendary Edition Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Legendary_Edition_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Legendary Edition Patch"), version 3.0.6, fixes this bug.
- [Aela the Huntress](/wiki/Skyrim:Aela_the_Huntress "Skyrim:Aela the Huntress") might send you here for the quest [Striking the Heart](/wiki/Skyrim:Striking_the_Heart "Skyrim:Striking the Heart"), but there may be no one here to kill. This prevents you from completing the quest and advancing in the [Companions](/wiki/Skyrim:Companions "Skyrim:Companions") questline.
  - Reload a save from before you were assigned the quest and obtain one with a different target location.
- It may not be possible to pick up the enchanted bow in the northern tower.
  - Saving and loading the new save fixes the problem.
- Sometimes when the bandit leader is killed, this location will not be marked as cleared. Reloading a save and killing him again does not solve this problem.
  - Use the [console](/wiki/Skyrim:Console "Skyrim:Console") command `setlocationcleared 000192c1 1` to force the game to mark it as cleared.

Valthume

**Valthume** is a medium-sized [Nordic ruin](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Ruins "Skyrim:Nordic Ruins") southeast of [Markarth](/wiki/Skyrim:Markarth "Skyrim:Markarth") containing [draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Draugr "Skyrim:Draugr"), [frostbite spiders](/wiki/Skyrim:Frostbite_Spider "Skyrim:Frostbite Spider"), [skeevers](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeever "Skyrim:Skeever"), and the [dragon priest](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Priest "Skyrim:Dragon Priest") [Hevnoraak](/wiki/Skyrim:Hevnoraak_(creature) "Skyrim:Hevnoraak (creature)"). It contains three zones: *[Valthume Vestibule](#Valthume_Vestibule)*, *[Valthume](#Valthume)*, and *[Valthume Catacombs](#Valthume_Catacombs)*.

The word wall

Its [word wall](/wiki/Skyrim:Word_Wall "Skyrim:Word Wall") teaches part of the [Aura Whisper](/wiki/Skyrim:Aura_Whisper "Skyrim:Aura Whisper") [dragon shout](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Shouts "Skyrim:Dragon Shouts").

## Related Quests

- **[Alteration Ritual Spell](/wiki/Skyrim:Alteration_Ritual_Spell "Skyrim:Alteration Ritual Spell")**: Acquire [Kahvozein's Fang](/wiki/Skyrim:Kahvozein%27s_Fang "Skyrim:Kahvozein's Fang") and use it to get [heartscales](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Heartscales "Skyrim:Dragon Heartscales") from a [dragon](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon "Skyrim:Dragon") for [Tolfdir](/wiki/Skyrim:Tolfdir "Skyrim:Tolfdir"). ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant"))
- **[Evil in Waiting](/wiki/Skyrim:Evil_in_Waiting "Skyrim:Evil in Waiting")**: Seal away an [ancient evil](/wiki/Skyrim:Hevnoraak_(creature) "Skyrim:Hevnoraak (creature)").
- **[The Words of Power](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Words_of_Power "Skyrim:The Words of Power")**: Follow the [Greybeards](/wiki/Skyrim:Greybeards "Skyrim:Greybeards")' advice to learn a [Word of Power](/wiki/Skyrim:Word_of_Power "Skyrim:Word of Power") from a [word wall](/wiki/Skyrim:Word_Wall "Skyrim:Word Wall"). ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant"))

### Valthume Vestibule

Upon entering, a ghostly figure named [Valdar](/wiki/Skyrim:Valdar "Skyrim:Valdar") immediately greets you and explains that evil is lurking in this barrow, asking you to help him (see the [Evil in Waiting](/wiki/Skyrim:Evil_in_Waiting "Skyrim:Evil in Waiting") quest page for details). There is a throne bathed in natural sunlight from a grating above, with Valdar's skeleton still sitting where he has been keeping vigil. Next to the throne is an [ancient Nord helmet](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Helmet "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Helmet"). Once you have agreed to help, Valdar will instruct you on what is required, and will lower a hidden door at the rear of the room. The hidden door reveals a tunnel strewn with thin cobwebs leading to an ornate double door that opens into Valthume itself.

### Valthume

The area is filled with [urns](/wiki/Skyrim:Urn "Skyrim:Urn"), [burial urns](/wiki/Skyrim:Burial_Urn "Skyrim:Burial Urn"), other containers holding minor loot, and empty coffins.

The first room you enter is huge, with a sarcophagus in the center of the room with a stone carving at each corner, various stone pillars supporting the massive ceiling, and lit braziers providing light. Beyond the sarcophagus, at the rear of the room is a throne on a raised platform. Behind the throne to the left is a caved-in area with a [dead draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Draugr "Skyrim:Containers"), and to the right is a doorway that leads into a narrow corridor with a burial urn at the end and a [pressure plate](/wiki/Skyrim:Pressure_Plate "Skyrim:Pressure Plate") near the start that triggers a [battering ram](/wiki/Skyrim:Battering_Ram "Skyrim:Battering Ram") in the next corridor. This trap can be used to kill or injure the first living draugr standing in this corridor. The corridor heading south opens into a small room with a second draugr. Along the west wall of this room are two urns near the entrance, a couple sideboards with a bowl of [bone meal](/wiki/Skyrim:Bone_Meal "Skyrim:Bone Meal") on the left sideboard, and a stone table between the sideboards with a burial urn and a draugr on top. Against the south wall is a set of shelves holding two bowls of [troll fat](/wiki/Skyrim:Troll_Fat "Skyrim:Troll Fat"), two [skeever tails](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeever_Tail "Skyrim:Skeever Tail"), and two bowls of bone meal, with a sack of [food](/wiki/Skyrim:Food "Skyrim:Food") beside it. There are two exits from this room: one to the left of the shelves to the south, and the other in the southeast corner heading east. There is also a doorway in the northeastern corner, but that has caved in; however, you can peer through a gap in the rubble and see deeper into the barrow.

The doorway to the south descends a flight of stairs to a wooden door, but this is unfortunately barred from the other side. Through the doorway heading east, descend a flight of stairs to a balcony above a room, with another balcony opposite where a ghostly figure drifts away out of sight. Before descending the stairs, there are two [imp stool](/wiki/Skyrim:Imp_Stool "Skyrim:Imp Stool") fungi and an [apothecary's satchel](/wiki/Skyrim:Apothecary%27s_Satchel "Skyrim:Apothecary's Satchel") among the rubble on your left, and a sideboard with two burial urns ahead. Wooden stairs lead down from the balcony you are on. It is also possible to jump from the balcony to wooden planking along the north wall, then at the end either jump via the small ledge in the corner or use the [Whirlwind Sprint](/wiki/Skyrim:Whirlwind_Sprint "Skyrim:Whirlwind Sprint") shout to reach the other balcony where the ghostly figure was. Behind this second balcony is a short corridor that leads to a random [shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Shield "Skyrim:Shield") and an unlocked [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest"). The room's lower level features several rows of benches facing an altar, with a throne on either side in the corners. Beware a [trap door](/wiki/Skyrim:Trap_Door "Skyrim:Trap Door") in the floor before the benches. Descending the stairs and approaching the altar will cause the two draugr sitting in the thrones to rise and attack; the one on the left is leveled. On the altar is a dead draugr, and in front of it is an unlocked chest. There are three exits from this room: one through the trap door into the room below, and doorways to the north and south. The northern doorway leads to the return route from beneath the trap door, while the doorway to the south leads farther into the barrow.

The room beneath the trap door holds an [iron sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Sword "Skyrim:Iron Sword"), a [banded iron shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Banded_Iron_Shield "Skyrim:Banded Iron Shield"), and a bottle of poison. An iron door opens into a frostbite spider lair containing up to four frostbite spiders and an [egg sac](/wiki/Skyrim:Egg_Sac "Skyrim:Egg Sac") that can be harvested for a [spider egg](/wiki/Skyrim:Spider_Egg "Skyrim:Spider Egg"). Just on the other side of the iron door and slightly to the right, a gold diamond ring lies on the floor by the hand of a skeleton. The ring is not always easily visible, so scan the floor carefully. To the northeast, just past a pair of unharvestable egg sacs, is a draugr standing slumbering against the wall, hidden behind webbing; it will be carrying the [Valthume Cellar key](/wiki/Skyrim:Valthume_Cellar_Key "Skyrim:Valthume Cellar Key"), and will be a magic user. Opposite where it is standing is an iron door with an expert-level lock, which is opened using the draugr's key. Behind the door is a wooden spiral staircase leading up, with two growths of [bleeding crown](/wiki/Skyrim:Bleeding_Crown "Skyrim:Bleeding Crown") fungi at the bottom. At the top of the stairs is the room to the north of the room with the trap door. This room contains a couple sets of shelves, one of which holds two burial urns and a plate with two [amethysts](/wiki/Skyrim:Amethyst "Skyrim:Amethyst") and a [flawless emerald](/wiki/Skyrim:Flawless_Emerald "Skyrim:Flawless Emerald"). Beside the shelves, on the floor is a stone bowl holding two [spider eggs](/wiki/Skyrim:Spider_Egg "Skyrim:Spider Egg").

The corridor heading south from the room with the benches and altar has a row of [oil lanterns](/wiki/Skyrim:Oil_Lanterns "Skyrim:Oil Lanterns") hanging from the ceiling. There is a doorway into a room partway along the corridor. This room also has several oil lanterns hanging from the ceiling, and contains a stone table against the north wall, with a [woodcutter's axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Woodcutter%27s_Axe "Skyrim:Woodcutter's Axe") and an [iron mace](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Mace "Skyrim:Iron Mace") on top and a [knapsack](/wiki/Skyrim:Knapsack "Skyrim:Knapsack") underneath. A broken table to the west holds a [leather strip](/wiki/Skyrim:Leather_Strips "Skyrim:Leather Strips"), with an open door to the left of it leading to another small room. In this room is a stone table and bench opposite the entrance holding a copy of the [Restoration](/wiki/Skyrim:Restoration "Skyrim:Restoration") [skill book](/wiki/Skyrim:Skill_Books "Skyrim:Skill Books") *[Withershins](/wiki/Skyrim:Withershins "Skyrim:Withershins")*, and a sideboard holding three [potatoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Potato "Skyrim:Potato") and a leather strip to the left. In the main room, against the south wall is a sideboard holding a leather strip, two sheaves of [wheat](/wiki/Skyrim:Wheat "Skyrim:Wheat"), and a [snow bear pelt](/wiki/Skyrim:Snow_Bear_Pelt "Skyrim:Snow Bear Pelt"), with a set of shelves to the left holding another snow bear pelt, a [bear pelt](/wiki/Skyrim:Bear_Pelt "Skyrim:Bear Pelt"), and a [wolf pelt](/wiki/Skyrim:Wolf_Pelt "Skyrim:Wolf Pelt").

The corridor continues past the room and ends at an unlocked iron door. Behind the door is a room with more oil lanterns above and an [oil slick](/wiki/Skyrim:Oil_Slick "Skyrim:Oil Slick") on the floor. There are two skeevers and a draugr in this room; setting fire to the oil slick should take care of them. When you enter the room, you should see the ghostly figure through a window to the south. There are three pressure plates in the room that trigger some feeble [poison dart traps](/wiki/Skyrim:Dart_Trap "Skyrim:Dart Trap"), which don't seem to fire more than one or two darts each. At the end of the room is a gated corridor to the west and a concealed door to the east; the handle to open the concealed door is to the left of the door. This reveals a tunnel that leads to a naturally lit room, with an upright sarcophagus opposite and the first of the required opaque vessels on a pedestal to the left. The sarcophagus will burst open to reveal a leveled draugr. In the northwest corner is a growth of [blisterwort](/wiki/Skyrim:Blisterwort "Skyrim:Blisterwort") fungi, a partly buried unlootable skeleton, an iron dagger, and a silver ruby ring. Returning to the previous room, you will find that taking the opaque vessel has raised the gate to the west, allowing further exploration.

After a few paces, the corridor turns to the south and opens into a larger corridor with roots crisscrossing the floor. Upon reaching a carved face that has fallen to the floor, the corridor staggers to the left and opens into a large room. You enter the room on a balcony area, with a couple stone tables in front of you. The tables hold three [potions of regeneration](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Regeneration "Skyrim:Potion of Regeneration"), two bunches of [tundra cotton](/wiki/Skyrim:Tundra_Cotton "Skyrim:Tundra Cotton"), a [baked potato](/wiki/Skyrim:Baked_Potatoes "Skyrim:Baked Potatoes"), and several wheels of cheese. A bridge behind the tables leads across to another small area, with a magic-using draugr guarding an adept-locked iron door, and a ramp descending to the right to the room's lower level.

The adept-locked door opens into a large room with a sideboard holding a bulb of [garlic](/wiki/Skyrim:Garlic "Skyrim:Garlic") next to an unlocked chest against the west wall. In the southwestern corner is a bookcase holding eleven common books, a random [potion of stamina](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Stamina "Skyrim:Potion of Stamina"), a random [potion of strength](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Strength "Skyrim:Potion of Strength"), and a sliced cheese. There is a throne in the middle of the south wall, with a table in front of it holding two potions of regeneration, a random [potion of magicka](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Magicka "Skyrim:Potion of Magicka"), a random [potion of health](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Health "Skyrim:Potion of Health"), and another random potion of strength. An urn is in the southeast corner. Finally, a second bookcase at the eastern end of the room holds a [flute](/wiki/Skyrim:Flute "Skyrim:Flute"), a [drum](/wiki/Skyrim:Drum "Skyrim:Drum"), a bowl of bone meal, eight more common books on and around it, and another potion of health on the floor in front of it.

In the previous room's lower level are two more draugr; the first is leveled, and patrols between the bottom of the ramp and the other end of the room. There are several skeletons either shackled to the walls or lying broken beneath them around this lower level; however, none are lootable. In the southeastern corner of the room on a raised section is another draugr standing next to a stone table, with a lootable skeleton on the table and various torture equipment around. An urn and two burial urns are in the northwestern corner. There is an exit to the east, and another to the north behind another raised section.

The room to the north has a couple sets of shelves holding six burial urns and some cheese, with an urn to the left of the shelves, and a concealed door to the east; the handle to open the door is to its left. The tunnel beyond the concealed door twists and turns until it reaches a room with a stone table holding a dead draugr in the center, an [alchemy lab](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemy_Lab "Skyrim:Alchemy Lab") on your immediate left, and a sideboard to the right of the alchemy lab holding a sword, a burial urn, and three random potions: one [healing](/wiki/Skyrim:Healing "Skyrim:Healing"), one magicka, and one stamina. Above the sideboard is a rack holding a garlic braid, two bunches of dried [elves ear](/wiki/Skyrim:Elves_Ear "Skyrim:Elves Ear"), and a bunch of dried [frost mirriam](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Mirriam "Skyrim:Frost Mirriam"). Two other garlic braids hang from a second rack in the middle of the room. Against the west wall is a sideboard holding an unlocked chest. To the north is a window looking into the previous room with the two skeevers. Back in the main chamber, the door to the east leads to *Valthume Catacombs*.

### Valthume Catacombs

Entering the catacombs leads directly onto stairs descending to the north. At the bottom, the corridor turns east, then opens into a room with two burial urns and an apprentice-trapped chest ahead; the trap opens a trap door in the ceiling, releasing a giant frostbite spider from above. There are two smaller additional frostbite spiders in alcoves to the sides of the room, and stairs on either side of the chest leading down to the floor. Underneath the chest, behind thick webbing that has to be cut or burnt though, is a lootable skeleton, and opposite at the bottom of more ascending stairs between two statues is an altar holding another lootable skeleton. There are two egg sacs that can be harvested in this room, one in the southwest corner, and the other at the top of the stairs in the northeast corner. At the top of the stairs is a gated doorway in the middle of the east wall, with the chain to open it on the right.

As you enter the catacombs proper, there is a draugr standing slumbering in an alcove in front of you. The path turns to the north, then back to the east. Take care, as there is a draugr patrolling up and down this corridor, and a pressure plate at this end triggers a [swinging blade trap](/wiki/Skyrim:Swinging_Blades "Skyrim:Swinging Blades") ahead. After the trapped section, the path turns south, then east again, and enters a brightly lit room with two urns and an altar holding another dead draugr. The path continues to the south, with another slumbering draugr in an alcove at the far end. Here the path turns to the west, with an urn to the left and yet another draugr ahead, then turns north, where another draugr is in an alcove on your left. To the west is a gated doorway opened by a chain in an alcove on the right wall, between stacks of coffins. There is also a narrow corridor to the south.

Beyond the gated doorway is a room guarded by a leveled draugr containing numerous sarcophagi, all of which are inactive, the second opaque vessel on a pedestal, and a row of three spellcaster traps. The traps only shoot through the windows into the next corridor, so you are safe to remove the [soul gems](/wiki/Skyrim:Soul_Gems "Skyrim:Soul Gems") from this side. This makes the next corridor much simpler and safer. Backtracking and taking the corridor to the south, the passage turns to the west after a few feet, then enters a corridor with a lootable skeleton on the ground and three windows into the previous room, where the spellcaster traps would have shot [flames](/wiki/Skyrim:Flames "Skyrim:Flames") at you. At the far end is a doorway to the south entering a room, where the path turns east and descends past rows of alcoves for the dead. Two urns and eight burial urns can be found near the next corner, where the passage turns back to the south, with an altar ahead holding a bow and a dead draugr. To the west are two draugr wandering and two slumbering in alcoves, one along the north wall, and the other at the far end along the west wall; several of the draugr are leveled. In the southern half of the room is a pressure plate that triggers a [spike wall](/wiki/Skyrim:Spike_Wall "Skyrim:Spike Wall") that is hinged on the left. At the far end is another gated doorway in the southwestern corner, with the chain to open it on the west wall.

Through the doorway, there are sets of shelves on either side of you, but the only items of interest are a [canis root](/wiki/Skyrim:Canis_Root "Skyrim:Canis Root") sample on the top shelf to the right, an urn, and four burial urns. Wooden walls separate the raised central corridor ahead from the sides of the room, with five burial urns along the corridor, a small gap to the east by stairs leading down to the lower level, and another gap farther along to the east by a bridge to an outer balcony housing an unlocked chest and a dead draugr in an open sarcophagus. The balcony and the floor below have many sarcophagi, but only a few contain slumbering draugr that will burst out. There is also one draugr patrolling the eastern balcony. After looting three urns and two dead draugr on the lower level, head west into a corridor choked with rubble and roots. After a few paces, the corridor staggers to the left and ends at an iron door.

The iron claw and its door

Beyond the door is a corridor guarded by three frostbite spiders and containing the [iron claw](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Claw "Skyrim:Iron Claw"). Take care, as removing the claw from the pedestal will trigger the four [flamethrower statues](/wiki/Skyrim:Flamethrower_Statues "Skyrim:Flamethrower Statues") around it. At the far end of the room is the puzzle door, the solution to which is written on the underside of the claw: **Dragon—Hawk—Wolf**. Past the door are stairs leading up to a doorway, which opens into another room with a total of six urns and four draugr; several of the draugr may be deathlords, so be prepared for a tough battle. In the center of this room is the third pedestal holding the last of the opaque vessels, behind which is a row of spears blocking access to the word wall and boss chest. If you have been sent here to retrieve a quest item, it will be in this chest. Taking the third vessel should lower the spears; if not, see [notes](#Notes) or [bugs](#Bugs). The word wall teaches part of the Aura Whisper shout. To the north of the word wall is a path to a gated doorway, opened by the chain on the north wall. Follow the corridor beyond this doorway, passing through an iron door to the first zone, and continuing until you reach the barred door you found early in the barrow; you can now raise the bar to provide a shortcut back to near the entrance.

At the end of the quest Evil in Waiting, Hevnoraak will burst from the sarcophagus in the center of the large room at the start of *Valthume*. Don't forget to loot his ash pile for his [mask](/wiki/Skyrim:Hevnoraak_(item) "Skyrim:Hevnoraak (item)") and [unique staff](/wiki/Skyrim:Hevnoraak%27s_Staff "Skyrim:Hevnoraak's Staff").

## Notes

- You can get the word without raising the gate by standing by the wall to the right of the word wall (the side with the door to the next chamber) and pressing yourself as close to the wall as possible. The word will go through the wall to you.
- If you have picked up all three vessels and the bars have not retracted, stand on the podium for a few seconds then jump off, and the bars will retract.
- As you approach the area around Valthume, you will experience short, strong earthquakes. The quakes are unrelated to Valthume, as can be discovered by traversing the nearby Dwemer ruin of [Arkngthamz](/wiki/Skyrim:Arkngthamz "Skyrim:Arkngthamz")[DG](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard"). In your search for the [Aetherium Forge](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Aetherium_Forge "Skyrim:The Aetherium Forge") during the quest [Lost to the Ages](/wiki/Skyrim:Lost_to_the_Ages "Skyrim:Lost to the Ages"), you will discover the quakes are echoes of a vast subterranean network of Aetherium foundries within the massive caverns under the mountains of southern Skyrim, and of the [Tonal Architecture](/wiki/Lore:Tonal_Architecture "Lore:Tonal Architecture") weaved by the ancient Dwemer to guard and house them.
- Valthume also [appears](/wiki/Online:Valthume "Online:Valthume") in [ESO](/wiki/Online:Online "Online:Online").

## Bugs

- If the puzzle door does not work, check [here](/wiki/Skyrim:Puzzle_Totems#Bugs "Skyrim:Puzzle Totems") for possible fixes.
- It is possible for the quest vial to be knocked off the pedestal during the fight. This may cause some complications:
  - It may be blown away to where you can't find it. You can try jumping on the pedestal or placing an item on it, and the bars may retract.
  - It may press the switch twice. If you are outside the gate, you can jump on the pedestal, and your weight may cause the bars to retract. If you are inside the gate, you can try using a shout such as [Unrelenting Force](/wiki/Skyrim:Unrelenting_Force "Skyrim:Unrelenting Force") or [Fire Breath](/wiki/Skyrim:Fire_Breath "Skyrim:Fire Breath") on the pedestal.
  - Reloading the autosave from when you entered the Catacombs, then exiting, reentering, and making your way back to the door will correct this.

## Maps

- 

  Valthume Vestibule
- 

  Valthume
- 

  Valthume Catacombs

# Skyrim:Varlais Cavern

## Overview

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | [SR-mapicon-Cave.png](/wiki/File:SR-mapicon-Cave.png) | Cave: Varlais Cavern ([view on map](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=Skyrim&centeron=Varlais+Cavern)) | | |
| Added by | [The Cause](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Cause "Skyrim:The Cause") |
| [Clearable](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons#Clearing "Skyrim:Dungeons") | No |
| [Dungeon](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons "Skyrim:Dungeons") | Yes |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | ? |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| ArcwindPointExterior09 | |

Varlais Cavern

**Varlais Cavern** is a [cave](/wiki/Skyrim:Caves "Skyrim:Caves") containing the [Ayleid](/wiki/Lore:Ayleid "Lore:Ayleid") ruins of [Rielle](/wiki/Skyrim:Rielle "Skyrim:Rielle"). It is added by [The Cause](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Cause "Skyrim:The Cause") Creation.

The cavern's interior is not actually a separate location, but rather forms part of the first [zone](#Rielle) of Rielle.

## Related Quests

- **[The Cause](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Cause_(quest) "Skyrim:The Cause (quest)")**[CC](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Cause "Skyrim:The Cause"): Investigate the activities of the [Mythic Dawn](/wiki/Lore:Mythic_Dawn "Lore:Mythic Dawn") cult.

## Walkthrough

Before it is open

Boards removed

It is located just off the vast temple complex of [Arcwind Point](/wiki/Skyrim:Arcwind_Point "Skyrim:Arcwind Point"), being south along the path from the central sarcophagus and northwest of the round cairn. Mythic Dawn cultists have been digging through the back of the cavern to reach the back wall of the Ayleid tomb of [Rielle](/wiki/Skyrim:Rielle "Skyrim:Rielle") that abuts it. Consequently, the cave no longer has its own zone and instead of "to Varlais Cavern", you will see "to Rielle" as you approach the entrance. A pickaxe lies atop a crate to the left of the cave opening.

Before a certain point in the quest [The Cause](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Cause_(quest) "Skyrim:The Cause (quest)") added by the Creation, the cave contains a hole in Rielle's now-exposed back wall, but it is boarded up and inaccessible. Whoever left behind pickaxes, wagons, and sacks around the interior of the cave is nowhere to be seen and there are no notes to identify them. The distinctive jingling sounds of Ayleid ruins can be heard from the other side of the wall.

Once the [Mythic Dawn Camp](/wiki/Skyrim:Mythic_Dawn_Camp "Skyrim:Mythic Dawn Camp") at the foot of the Throat of the World nearby has been cleared and notes on the cult's plans discovered, you will be directed to Varlais Cavern where the excavation team has now removed the boards and entered the ruins. None of them will be alive to confront you.

## Bugs

- When attempting to fast travel to the [Alchemist's Shack](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemist%27s_Shack "Skyrim:Alchemist's Shack") while standing outside the entrance of Varlais Cavern, you may end up at Arcwind Point instead. **?**

# Skyrim:Velothi Mountains

## Overview

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| |  | | --- | | Landmark: Velothi Mountains ([view on map](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=Skyrim&centeron=Velothi+Mountains)) ([lore page](/wiki/Lore:Velothi_Mountains "Lore:Velothi Mountains")) | | |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | 10 days |
| Regions | |
| [Eastmarch](/wiki/Skyrim:Eastmarch "Skyrim:Eastmarch"), [The Rift](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Rift "Skyrim:The Rift") | |
| Location | |
| Eastern border of [Skyrim](/wiki/Skyrim:Skyrim "Skyrim:Skyrim") | |

The Velothi Mountains as viewed from [Stendarr's Beacon](/wiki/Skyrim:Stendarr%27s_Beacon "Skyrim:Stendarr's Beacon")

The **Velothi Mountains** are a mountain range that stretches along the eastern border of [Skyrim](/wiki/Skyrim:Skyrim "Skyrim:Skyrim").

They are the mountain range separating [Morrowind](/wiki/Lore:Morrowind "Lore:Morrowind") from [Skyrim](/wiki/Lore:Skyrim "Lore:Skyrim").

The mountains form the eastern border of the Skyrim regions of [Eastmarch](/wiki/Skyrim:Eastmarch "Skyrim:Eastmarch") and [The Rift](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Rift "Skyrim:The Rift"). In the south the mountain range meets the [Jerall Mountains](/wiki/Skyrim:Jerall_Mountains "Skyrim:Jerall Mountains").

Some points of interest include the orcish stronghold of [Narzulbur](/wiki/Skyrim:Narzulbur "Skyrim:Narzulbur"), [Sacellum of Boethiah](/wiki/Skyrim:Sacellum_of_Boethiah "Skyrim:Sacellum of Boethiah"), [Fort Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Fort_Dawnguard "Skyrim:Fort Dawnguard"), or the Dwemer ruins of [Kagrenzel](/wiki/Skyrim:Kagrenzel "Skyrim:Kagrenzel") and [Mzulft](/wiki/Skyrim:Mzulft "Skyrim:Mzulft"). The [Dunmeth Pass](/wiki/Skyrim:Dunmeth_Pass "Skyrim:Dunmeth Pass") leads through the mountains and into northern Morrowind. High in the mountains lies the ancient temple of [Skuldafn](/wiki/Skyrim:Skuldafn "Skyrim:Skuldafn"), along the [Eastmarch](/wiki/Lore:Eastmarch "Lore:Eastmarch") border.

For more information, see the [main lore article](/wiki/Lore:Velothi_Mountains "Lore:Velothi Mountains").

# Skyrim:Verner and Annekke's House

## Overview

Verner and Annekke's House

**Verner and Annekke's House** is the only house in [Darkwater Crossing](/wiki/Skyrim:Darkwater_Crossing "Skyrim:Darkwater Crossing").

Verner and Annekke's home

It is a one-room dwelling home to [Annekke Crag-Jumper](/wiki/Skyrim:Annekke_Crag-Jumper "Skyrim:Annekke Crag-Jumper") and her husband [Verner Rock-Chucker](/wiki/Skyrim:Verner_Rock-Chucker "Skyrim:Verner Rock-Chucker"), the owners of [Goldenrock Mine](/wiki/Skyrim:Goldenrock_Mine "Skyrim:Goldenrock Mine") and the original settlers of [Darkwater Crossing](/wiki/Skyrim:Darkwater_Crossing "Skyrim:Darkwater Crossing").

## Residents

| Name |
| --- |
| [Annekke Crag-Jumper](/wiki/Skyrim:Annekke_Crag-Jumper "Skyrim:Annekke Crag-Jumper") [Follower](/wiki/Skyrim:Followers "Follower")  [Verner Rock-Chucker](/wiki/Skyrim:Verner_Rock-Chucker "Skyrim:Verner Rock-Chucker") |

### Exterior

The house has a field attached, enclosed with wicker fencing and planted with seven bushels of [wheat](/wiki/Skyrim:Wheat "Skyrim:Wheat"). There is a lean-to being used as a chicken coop with two [nests](/wiki/Skyrim:Chicken%27s_Egg "Skyrim:Chicken's Egg") in it, with two [chickens](/wiki/Skyrim:Chicken "Skyrim:Chicken") and a [goat](/wiki/Skyrim:Goat "Skyrim:Goat") wandering near the house. To the left of the chicken coop is a [tanning rack](/wiki/Skyrim:Tanning_Rack "Skyrim:Tanning Rack"), and in front of the house by the southwestern corner are a [grindstone](/wiki/Skyrim:Grindstone "Skyrim:Grindstone") and a [wood chopping block](/wiki/Skyrim:Wood_Chopping_Block "Skyrim:Wood Chopping Block").

### Verner and Annekke's House

The entrance is novice-locked at night, but will be unlocked during the day. Against the west wall there are two [food](/wiki/Skyrim:Food "Skyrim:Food") barrels in each corner separated by a cupboard holding three bottles of [alto wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Alto_Wine "Skyrim:Alto Wine") and two bottles of [wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Wine "Skyrim:Wine"). There is a long wooden table against the north wall with a bottle of alto wine, a bottle of [Nord mead](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord_Mead "Skyrim:Nord Mead"), and a bucket containing a few [potatoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Potato "Skyrim:Potato") on it. Above this, hanging from the ceiling, are three [garlic](/wiki/Skyrim:Garlic "Skyrim:Garlic") braids, two bunches of dried [elves ear](/wiki/Skyrim:Elves_Ear "Skyrim:Elves Ear"), a bunch of dried [frost mirriam](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Mirriam "Skyrim:Frost Mirriam"), and a rack that holds three [rabbits](/wiki/Skyrim:Rabbit_(dead) "Skyrim:Rabbit (dead)") and three [pheasants](/wiki/Skyrim:Pheasant "Skyrim:Pheasant"). To the right of the table are another food barrel and a set of shelves; the shelves hold four more bottles of alto wine and four more bottles of wine. To the right of the shelves is a fireplace with a round wooden table and two chairs in front. On the table are a bottle of alto wine, a bottle of wine, and some [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread") and cheese. On the hearth are a [cooking pot](/wiki/Skyrim:Cooking_Pot "Skyrim:Cooking Pot") and a kettle containing two [leeks](/wiki/Skyrim:Leek "Skyrim:Leek"). On the east wall is an owned double bed with an end table to the left and an unlocked [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") at the foot of the bed. There is a bottle of Nord mead on the end table. To the right of the bed against the south wall is a tall wardrobe with a food barrel and dresser to the right. On the dresser are six [corundum ingots](/wiki/Skyrim:Corundum_Ingot "Skyrim:Corundum Ingot"), two pieces of [corundum ore](/wiki/Skyrim:Corundum_Ore "Skyrim:Corundum Ore"), a [pickaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Pickaxe "Skyrim:Pickaxe"), and a bottle of [Black-Briar mead](/wiki/Skyrim:Black-Briar_Mead "Skyrim:Black-Briar Mead").

# Skyrim:Vile Whispers

## Overview

|  |
| --- |
| **This page is currently being rewritten as part of the [Skyrim Quest Redesign Project](/wiki/UESPWiki:Skyrim_Quest_Redesign_Project "UESPWiki:Skyrim Quest Redesign Project").** The page is being rewritten and checked in several stages. All users are welcome to make changes to the page. If you make a change that is relevant to the project, please update this template accordingly, and make sure you have observed the [project guidelines](/wiki/UESPWiki:Skyrim_Quest_Redesign_Project#Guidelines "UESPWiki:Skyrim Quest Redesign Project").  Details  **Walkthrough**: written by [Legoless](/wiki/User:Legoless "User:Legoless"), *not checked*  **Reward**: written by [Legoless](/wiki/User:Legoless "User:Legoless"), *not checked* |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [SR-qico-Miscellaneous.png](/wiki/File:SR-qico-Miscellaneous.png) | **A [malevolent Daedric entity](/wiki/Lore:Umbra "Lore:Umbra") long thought destroyed has resurfaced in the [Velothi Mountains](/wiki/Skyrim:Velothi_Mountains "Skyrim:Velothi Mountains").** |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Quest Giver: | Reading the [Vigilant's report](/wiki/Skyrim:Vigilant%27s_Report "Skyrim:Vigilant's Report") |
| Location(s): | [Champion's Rest](/wiki/Skyrim:Champion%27s_Rest "Skyrim:Champion's Rest") |
| Reward: | [Umbra](/wiki/Skyrim:Umbra_(item) "Skyrim:Umbra (item)") |
| ID: | ccBGSSSE016\_UmbraMainQuest |
| Added by: | [Umbra](/wiki/Skyrim:Umbra "Skyrim:Umbra") |

Umbra has returned...

## Quick Walkthrough

1. Enter [Champion's Rest](/wiki/Skyrim:Champion%27s_Rest "Skyrim:Champion's Rest") and read the *[Vigilant's Report](/wiki/Skyrim:Vigilant%27s_Report "Skyrim:Vigilant's Report")*.
2. Explore the dungeon until you come face-to-face with [Umbra](/wiki/Skyrim:Umbra_(person) "Skyrim:Umbra (person)").
3. Attack Umbra when he loses his invulnerability and take the [Umbra Sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Umbra_(item) "Skyrim:Umbra (item)") from his corpse.

### Ghostly Sightings

Head to [Champion's Rest](/wiki/Skyrim:Champion%27s_Rest "Skyrim:Champion's Rest"), a [Nordic ruin](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Ruins "Skyrim:Nordic Ruins") which has recently been uncovered in a [silver](/wiki/Skyrim:Silver "Skyrim:Silver") mine east of [Shor's Stone](/wiki/Skyrim:Shor%27s_Stone "Skyrim:Shor's Stone"). To start the quest, find and read the *[Vigilant's Report](/wiki/Skyrim:Vigilant%27s_Report "Skyrim:Vigilant's Report")* from a [Vigilant of Stendarr](/wiki/Skyrim:Vigilant_of_Stendarr "Skyrim:Vigilant of Stendarr") who previously investigated the site after reports that the miners saw a ghost within the ruins wielding a whispering sword. The Vigilant identified the sword as [Umbra](/wiki/Skyrim:Umbra_(item) "Skyrim:Umbra (item)"), a sentient [Daedric artifact](/wiki/Skyrim:Daedric_Artifacts "Skyrim:Daedric Artifacts") long thought to have been destroyed. After reading the report, you will task yourself with finding the source of this dark presence to prevent any harm befalling Shor's Stone.

.jpg)

Puzzle solution for the western chamber

.jpg)

Puzzle solution for the eastern chamber

After passing through the short silver mine, you will enter the Nordic ruins themselves. Several [skeevers](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeever "Skyrim:Skeever") can be found in a chamber to the left; to the right, you will enter a chamber only to come face-to-face with the [ghost](/w/index.php?title=Skyrim:Ghost_(Umbra)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Skyrim:Ghost (Umbra) (page does not exist)") himself: a purple spirit who quickly vanishes. Venture deeper inside and you will find yourself in a huge chamber surrounded by sealed [draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Draugr "Skyrim:Draugr") sarcophagi and unlit blue braziers. At the opposite end of the chamber is a doubly-locked gate. Activating the nearby levers will do nothing. You will instead need to explore the two side passages, both of which contain [spinning totem puzzles](/wiki/Skyrim:Puzzles#Spinning_Symbol.2FRune_Totems "Skyrim:Puzzles").

Both side passages contain leveled draugr enemies. The totem solutions themselves are partially obscured, with one totem being almost totally hidden beneath rubble. The eastern chamber's solution is **Snake—Hawk—Whale**. Pull the nearby lever to activate the eastern braziers, but be careful as you will be hit by a [dart trap](/wiki/Skyrim:Dart_Trap "Skyrim:Dart Trap") if you select the wrong totems. Once one side of the chamber is lit up, you will be able to activate the corresponding lever to partially lower the gate. However, solving each puzzle will also release a wave of draugr from their tombs. The western chamber's solution is **Snake—Snake—Whale**, but again beware that the level will activate a [flamethrower statue trap](/wiki/Skyrim:Flamethrower_Statues "Skyrim:Flamethrower Statues"), which will ignite the surrounding [oil slick](/wiki/Skyrim:Oil_Slicks "Skyrim:Oil Slicks") if you haven't done so already.

With both puzzles solved, the braziers on both sides of the main chamber will be lit by a cold blue flame and you will be able to fully lower the gate. Progress further in and defeat the boss-level draugr (which may be a [dragon priest](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Priest "Skyrim:Dragon Priest") at higher levels) and enter the second area of the ruins, Champion's Rest Amphitheater.

### A Vile Presence

The first section of the Amphitheater zone consists of narrow waterlogged hallways filled with various traps activated by hidden pressure plates. You will come to an open area containing [oil lanterns](/wiki/Skyrim:Oil_Lanterns "Skyrim:Oil Lanterns"); descend to the bottom level and continue onward into the eerie blue crypts. You will again come across the ghost as you explore this area.

Umbra awaits you in an ancient Nordic arena

At the end of the crypts, you will enter into a huge natural cavern featuring a rock-hewn gladiatorial arena. In the center of the arena, you will find the "ghost", who is in fact an [ebony](/wiki/Skyrim:Ebony "Skyrim:Ebony")-clad [Imperial](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial "Skyrim:Imperial") warrior named [Umbra](/wiki/Skyrim:Umbra_(person) "Skyrim:Umbra (person)"). He is immune to all damage while purple and translucent, but he will repeatedly summon three [soul images](/w/index.php?title=Skyrim:Umbra%27s_Soul_Image&action=edit&redlink=1 "Skyrim:Umbra's Soul Image (page does not exist)") to fight you, during which time he will materialize and will be vulnerable to damage. Be sure not to allow the soul images to gang up on you, as two of them use [bound swords](/wiki/Skyrim:Bound_Sword "Skyrim:Bound Sword") and another will shoot from a distance with a [bound bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Bound_Bow "Skyrim:Bound Bow").

After a short period, Umbra will reabsorb his soul images and cast an extremely powerful area of effect [Destruction](/wiki/Skyrim:Destruction "Skyrim:Destruction") spell, which includes [frost](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Damage "Skyrim:Frost Damage") and [fire](/wiki/Skyrim:Fire_Damage "Skyrim:Fire Damage") damage spells. Should you survive the boss fight and do enough damage to Umbra while he is not invulnerable, he will fall dead, allowing you to claim the sword as well as the [Champion's Rest Passage Key](/wiki/Skyrim:Champion%27s_Rest_Passage_Key "Skyrim:Champion's Rest Passage Key") granting access to his inner sanctum and a short escape route out of the dungeon. Taking the sword will end the quest.

Loot the chest in the northeast corner of the arena and make your way to the southern exit. Inside Umbra's living quarters, you will find a small camp along with a bowl full of [skeever tails](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeever_Tail "Skyrim:Skeever Tail") and a *[Treasure Hunter's Journal](/wiki/Skyrim:Treasure_Hunter%27s_Journal "Skyrim:Treasure Hunter's Journal")* explaining how the now deceased Imperial man had ended up in the tomb and how Umbra bent him to his will until their psyches fused. A secret tunnel will lead you back to the ruin's entrance, allowing for a quick escape.

## Notes

- The name of this quest is a reference to [Clavicus Vile](/wiki/Skyrim:Clavicus_Vile "Skyrim:Clavicus Vile"), the creator of Umbra.
- Umbra was destroyed at the climax of [*Lord of Souls*](/wiki/Books:Lord_of_Souls "Books:Lord of Souls"), the second of two novels written by Greg Keyes. This took place in [4E 48](/wiki/Lore:Fourth_Era#4E_48 "Lore:Fourth Era"), over 150 years before the events of *Skyrim*.
- The song that plays during the Umbra boss fight is "Daedra in Flight" from the [*Oblivion* soundtrack](/wiki/Oblivion:Music "Oblivion:Music").

## Bugs

- The song may not necessarily play during the boss fight. **?**
- Items in the final boss fight room may become bugged. **?**
- It's possible the sword (Umbra) does not show up in the NPC's inventory (Cresitus/Umbra); if that happens reloading an earlier save can fix the issue. **?**

## Quest Stages

| Vile Whispers (ccBGSSSE016\_UmbraMainQuest) | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Stage | Finishes Quest | Journal Entry |
| 10 |  | I've heard rumors that a Nordic ruin, Champion's Rest, was recently uncovered in a silver mine near Shor's Stone. The miners reportedly fled, leaving their belongings behind. I should investigate the miner's campsite to see if I can learn anything about what caused them to flee. *Objective 0:* Search the miner's camp |
| 20 |  | The miners witnessed an armor-clad ghost wielding a menacing blade that struck fear into them, causing them to flee. A Vigilant of Stendarr investigated, and suspects that this blade may be Umbra, a Daedric artifact with the power to steal souls and bend its bearer to its will. I should investigate the ruins to find the source of this dark presence. As long as this threat remains, Shor's Stone is not safe. |
| 20 |  | I witnessed an armor-clad ghost that quickly disappeared while exploring Champion's Rest. The sighting filled me with a sense of dread. I should investigate the ruins to find the source of this dark presence. *Objective 10:* Investigate the dark presence in Champion's Rest |
| 30 |  | I've come to the great battle arena within Champion's Rest, and discovered an armored specter wielding Umbra. I will need to defeat the phantom in order to rid Shor's Stone of the danger posed by this powerful force. |
| 30 |  | I've come to the great battle arena within Champion's Rest, and discovered an armored specter. I will need to defeat the phantom in order to rid Shor's Stone of the danger posed by this powerful force. *Objective 20:* Defeat Umbra |
| 40 |  | The Daedric artifact Umbra awaits a new owner. I should take it to prevent it from falling into the wrong hands again. |
| 40 |  | The blade of the defeated spectral warrior awaits a new owner. When I gaze upon it, a single word is whispered in my mind: "Umbra". I should take it to prevent it from falling into the wrong hands again. *Objective 30:* Retrieve the artifact Umbra |
| 200 | ☑ | I was able to rid Shor's Stone of the danger posed by the artifact sword Umbra by defeating its former bearer and retrieving it for myself. |

- The following empty quest stages were omitted from the table: 0.

Notes

- Any text displayed in angle brackets (e.g., `<Alias=LocationHold>`) is dynamically set by the Radiant Quest system, and will be filled in with the appropriate word(s) when seen in game.
- Not all Journal Entries may appear in your journal; which entries appear and which entries do not depends on the manner in which the quest is done.
- Stages are not always in order of progress. This is usually the case with quests that have multiple possible outcomes or quests where certain tasks may be done in any order. Some stages may therefore repeat objectives seen in other stages.
- If an entry is marked as "Finishes Quest" it means the quest disappears from the Active Quest list, but you may still receive new entries for that quest.
- On the PC, it is possible to use the [console](/wiki/Skyrim:Console "Skyrim:Console") to advance through the quest by entering `setstage ccBGSSSE016_UmbraMainQuest stage`, where `stage` is the number of the stage you wish to complete. It is not possible to un-complete (i.e. go back) quest stages, but it is possible to clear all stages of the quest using `resetquest ccBGSSSE016_UmbraMainQuest`.

Vilemyr Inn

**Vilemyr Inn** is an inn located in the town of [Ivarstead](/wiki/Skyrim:Ivarstead "Skyrim:Ivarstead"), that sits at the base of the 7,000 steps that lead up to the home of the reclusive Greybeards, [High Hrothgar](/wiki/Skyrim:High_Hrothgar "Skyrim:High Hrothgar").

## Residents

| Residents |
| --- |
| [Gwilin](/wiki/Skyrim:Gwilin "Skyrim:Gwilin")  [Lynly Star-Sung](/wiki/Skyrim:Lynly_Star-Sung "Skyrim:Lynly Star-Sung") ([bard](/wiki/Skyrim:Bardic_Performances "Skyrim:Bardic Performances"))  [Temba Wide-Arm](/wiki/Skyrim:Temba_Wide-Arm "Skyrim:Temba Wide-Arm")  [Wilhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Wilhelm "Skyrim:Wilhelm") [Innkeeper](/wiki/Skyrim:Inns "Innkeeper") [Food and Drink](/wiki/Skyrim:Merchants "Food and Drink") |

## Related Quests

- **[Lifting the Shroud](/wiki/Skyrim:Lifting_the_Shroud "Skyrim:Lifting the Shroud")**: Investigate the haunted [barrow](/wiki/Skyrim:Shroud_Hearth_Barrow "Skyrim:Shroud Hearth Barrow") outside of [Ivarstead](/wiki/Skyrim:Ivarstead "Skyrim:Ivarstead").
- **[The Straw that Broke](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Straw_that_Broke "Skyrim:The Straw that Broke")**: Discover what is plaguing [Narfi](/wiki/Skyrim:Narfi "Skyrim:Narfi")'s mind.
- **[The Book of Love](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Book_of_Love "Skyrim:The Book of Love")**: Help the [Temple of Mara](/wiki/Skyrim:Temple_of_Mara "Skyrim:Temple of Mara") spread the cause of love throughout Skyrim.
- **[The Lover's Requital](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Lover%27s_Requital "Skyrim:The Lover's Requital")**: Help [Sibbi Black-Briar](/wiki/Skyrim:Sibbi_Black-Briar "Skyrim:Sibbi Black-Briar") locate his former [fiancée](/wiki/Skyrim:Svidi "Skyrim:Svidi"), so he can kill her.

## Vilemyr Inn

It is a typical Nord inn, with a fire pit down the middle of the common room. The L-shaped counter is at the southeast end, with five long wooden tables along the southwest and northeast walls. There are three doorways leading to bedrooms, two on the northeast side and a larger one on the southwest side. Against the southeast wall is a large vat, several food barrels and a pile of firewood, with racks hanging from the ceiling in front of them. These hold eight [rabbits](/wiki/Skyrim:Rabbit_(dead) "Skyrim:Rabbit (dead)") and two [pheasants](/wiki/Skyrim:Pheasant "Skyrim:Pheasant"), also hanging from the ceiling are two [garlic](/wiki/Skyrim:Garlic "Skyrim:Garlic") braids, a bunch of [frost mirriam](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Mirriam "Skyrim:Frost Mirriam") and a bunch of [elves ear](/wiki/Skyrim:Elves_Ear "Skyrim:Elves Ear"). These are in front of a set of shelves holding five bottles of [wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Wine "Skyrim:Wine") and three bottles of [alto wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Alto_Wine "Skyrim:Alto Wine"). On the counter are several pieces of cheese, a [salmon steak](/wiki/Skyrim:Salmon_Steak "Skyrim:Salmon Steak"), a bottle of alto wine and a bottle of wine. Under the counter is a copy of *[Herbane's Bestiary: Hagravens](/wiki/Skyrim:Herbane%27s_Bestiary:_Hagravens "Skyrim:Herbane's Bestiary: Hagravens")*, five loose [septims](/wiki/Skyrim:Gold "Skyrim:Gold"), a small [coin purse](/wiki/Skyrim:Coin_Purse "Skyrim:Coin Purse") and an [iron war axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_War_Axe "Skyrim:Iron War Axe").

Either side of the doorway into the larger bedroom are a set of shelves holding several loaves of [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread"), six bottles of wine, two bottles of alto wine, seven bottles of [Nord mead](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord_Mead "Skyrim:Nord Mead") and three bunches of elves ear. The long wooden table against the walls typically hold bread and a selection of wine and alto wine. However the table in the middle of the northeast wall also holds two [chicken's eggs](/wiki/Skyrim:Chicken%27s_Egg "Skyrim:Chicken's Egg") and a [pine thrush egg](/wiki/Skyrim:Pine_Thrush_Egg "Skyrim:Pine Thrush Egg"), while the table in the northern corner of the room also holds a selection of cheeses and two salmon steaks.

The larger bedroom is the room you will be shown to if renting a room for the night. It consists of two single beds; one in the southern corner, one in the western corner. The second of these is used by [Lynly Star-Sung](/wiki/Skyrim:Lynly_Star-Sung "Skyrim:Lynly Star-Sung"). There is a long [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") against the northwest wall that contains only minor loot. There is also a small square wooden table and chair against the northeast wall holding a small coin purse, four loose septims, and an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger").

The two smaller bedrooms are opposite the larger bedroom. The left-hand bedroom is used by [Gwilin](/wiki/Skyrim:Gwilin "Skyrim:Gwilin") and contains a single owned bed in the northern corner with an unlocked chest and wardrobe at the foot of the bed and a small round wooden table and chair beside the bed. On the table is a bottle of [Black-Briar mead](/wiki/Skyrim:Black-Briar_Mead "Skyrim:Black-Briar Mead"). The right-hand bedroom is used by [Temba Wide-Arm](/wiki/Skyrim:Temba_Wide-Arm "Skyrim:Temba Wide-Arm") and has a single owned bed in the eastern corner, with an unlocked chest and dresser at the foot of the bed and a small round wooden table and chair beside it. Both of these chests contain only minor loot as well.

Many of the town's residents will often visit the inn during the evening.

## Standard Merchandise

This is the merchandise found in the inn's [merchant chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Merchant_Chest "Skyrim:Merchant Chest"). These items are only available if purchased from the merchant; they cannot be [pickpocketed](/wiki/Skyrim:Pickpocket "Skyrim:Pickpocket") or stolen. They are restocked every two days.

| Count | Item |
| --- | --- |
| 7 | Random [drinks](/wiki/Skyrim:Food#Beverages "Skyrim:Food") |
| 10 | Random inn [food](/wiki/Skyrim:Food "Skyrim:Food") |
| 1 | [Salt Pile](/wiki/Skyrim:Salt_Pile "Skyrim:Salt Pile") |

# Skyrim:Viola Giordano's House

## Overview

|  |
| --- |
| **This article is being developed as part of the Skyrim Houses Redesign Project. If you see this tag it means the article can still be improved. Please see the [project page](/wiki/UESPWiki:SHRP "UESPWiki:SHRP") for guidelines.** |
