Viola Giordano's house

**Viola Giordano's House** is the home of [Viola Giordano](/wiki/Skyrim:Viola_Giordano "Skyrim:Viola Giordano") in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm")'s Valunstrad district.

Viola Giordano is the sole occupant of the large two-story Nordic-style house in one of the better neighborhoods of the city directly opposite the [house of Clan Shatter-Shield](/wiki/Skyrim:House_of_Clan_Shatter-Shield "Skyrim:House of Clan Shatter-Shield") and to the north of the graveyard and [Hall of the Dead](/wiki/Skyrim:Hall_of_the_Dead_(Windhelm) "Skyrim:Hall of the Dead (Windhelm)"), but she rarely spends much time there.

## Residents

| Name |
| --- |
| [Viola Giordano](/wiki/Skyrim:Viola_Giordano "Skyrim:Viola Giordano") |

## Related Quests

- **[That Was Always There](/wiki/Skyrim:That_Was_Always_There "Skyrim:That Was Always There")**: Return a [ring](/wiki/Skyrim:Viola%27s_Gold_Ring "Skyrim:Viola's Gold Ring") to its rightful [owner](/wiki/Skyrim:Viola_Giordano "Skyrim:Viola Giordano").

## Viola Giordano's House

The interior of Viola Giordano's House

The house is completely open-plan downstairs with a single room extending the length of the house and support pillars along the right-hand third of the house. The house has many food sacks around the place. Upon entering there is a chair in the northwestern corner, with some crates beside it and a [drum](/wiki/Skyrim:Drum "Skyrim:Drum") on the floor. There is a wooden table in an alcove on the north wall, with a [knapsack](/wiki/Skyrim:Knapsack "Skyrim:Knapsack"), a [pickaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Pickaxe "Skyrim:Pickaxe") and [plate](/wiki/Skyrim:Plate "Skyrim:Plate") on top. At the other end of the room is a matching alcove with another table in it, which has a [gourd](/wiki/Skyrim:Gourd "Skyrim:Gourd"), a [skeever tail](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeever_Tail "Skyrim:Skeever Tail"), two [Nordic barnacles](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Barnacle "Skyrim:Nordic Barnacle"), three [rock warbler eggs](/wiki/Skyrim:Rock_Warbler_Egg "Skyrim:Rock Warbler Egg"), a [salt pile](/wiki/Skyrim:Salt_Pile "Skyrim:Salt Pile"), a [pheasant breast](/wiki/Skyrim:Pheasant_Breast "Skyrim:Pheasant Breast"), a rack of [dog meat](/wiki/Skyrim:Dog_Meat "Skyrim:Dog Meat") and a fillet of [horker meat](/wiki/Skyrim:Horker_Meat "Skyrim:Horker Meat") on the table and a bowl of [bone meal](/wiki/Skyrim:Bone_Meal "Skyrim:Bone Meal") on the floor under it. To the left of the alcove is a set of shelves holing a bottle of [alto wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Alto_Wine "Skyrim:Alto Wine"), a bottle of [Nord mead](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord_Mead "Skyrim:Nord Mead") and three [goblets](/wiki/Skyrim:Goblet "Skyrim:Goblet").

On the rear wall is a large fireplace with a [cooking pot](/wiki/Skyrim:Cooking_Pot "Skyrim:Cooking Pot") hanging in front of it and tables on either side. The table to the left has a salt pile on top and a basket in front containing a [cabbage](/wiki/Skyrim:Cabbage "Skyrim:Cabbage") and [potatoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Potato "Skyrim:Potato"), while the table to the right has a pile of [firewood](/wiki/Skyrim:Firewood "Skyrim:Firewood") including two loose pieces on top. There is a long wooden table and benches in front of the fireplace with two bottles of alto wine, a bowl of [red](/wiki/Skyrim:Red_Apple "Skyrim:Red Apple") and [green apples](/wiki/Skyrim:Green_Apple "Skyrim:Green Apple"), a plate of three [salmon steaks](/wiki/Skyrim:Salmon_Steak "Skyrim:Salmon Steak"), another with a [baked potato](/wiki/Skyrim:Baked_Potatoes "Skyrim:Baked Potatoes") and [grilled leeks](/wiki/Skyrim:Grilled_Leeks "Skyrim:Grilled Leeks"), a salt pile and two goblets. In the southeastern corner is a square wooden table with a mead barrel on top, with another alcove to the right with a long unlocked [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") in it and a set of shelves to the right holding various pieces of cheese and a bottle of Nord mead.

The stairs leading up to the top floor are against the south wall with a wall screening them from downstairs. On this south wall is a set of shelves holding a bottle of alto wine and three common books, next to this is a chair. There are a couple of large empty vases next to the pillars and to the right when entering is a dresser which is used as the drop off point for returning the ring without Viola's notice which has a [flute](/wiki/Skyrim:Flute "Skyrim:Flute") on top of it. There is a fourth alcove to the left of the dresser with a few barrels in it and some more crates against the south wall, to the left of the alcove.

The upstairs of Viola Giordano's house

At the top of the stairs there is an alcove on the right with three [cow hides](/wiki/Skyrim:Cow_Hide "Skyrim:Cow Hide") piled on the floor. In front is a tall wardrobe containing [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothes "Skyrim:Clothes"). Along the edge of the stairwell are two more large empty vases and a low table and chair. On this table is a [solution of extra magicka](/wiki/Skyrim:Solution_of_Extra_Magicka "Skyrim:Solution of Extra Magicka"), a copy of *[The Book of Daedra](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Book_of_Daedra "Skyrim:The Book of Daedra")* and some [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread") and cheese. Against the south wall is a tall wardrobe with a square wooden table to the right of it and a copy of *[Spirit of Nirn](/wiki/Skyrim:Spirit_of_Nirn "Skyrim:Spirit of Nirn")* on top. Against the west wall, to the left of a doorway into [Viola](/wiki/Skyrim:Viola_Giordano "Skyrim:Viola Giordano")'s bedroom are a couple of barrels and a long end table with a [lute](/wiki/Skyrim:Lute "Skyrim:Lute") on top. To the right, against the north wall, is a set of shelves holding three common books and a bottle of each, alto wine, [wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Wine "Skyrim:Wine") and Nord mead. To the right of this is a chair and farther along an empty open crate and then an end table which has a [leather strip](/wiki/Skyrim:Leather_Strips "Skyrim:Leather Strips") and a fancy [jug](/wiki/Skyrim:Jug "Skyrim:Jug") and [candlestick](/wiki/Skyrim:Candlestick "Skyrim:Candlestick") on top, with a drum on the floor beside it. Farther to the right, next to another alcove is a set of shelves holding seven more common books.

The L-shaped bedroom through the doorway at the western end of the floor contains a double bed coming out from the west wall with a long unlocked chest at the foot of the bed. To the left in the southwestern corner is an end table with a wash basin on a stand around the corner, with a medium [coin purse](/wiki/Skyrim:Coin_Purse "Skyrim:Coin Purse") on the floor beside it. To the right of another alcove to the south is a set of shelves holding a [potion of regeneration](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Regeneration "Skyrim:Potion of Regeneration"). In the alcove are two corner shelves with a bottle of wine on one and a solution of extra magicka on the other. In the middle of this space is a large round wooden table with a bottle of alto wine, a jug and goblet and bread and cheese on top. Beside the doorway is a bookcase holding nine common books, while on the other side of it is an open crate containing two pairs of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots "Skyrim:Boots"). There is another long unlocked chest in yet another alcove to the north with a tall wardrobe to the left of the alcove with a small wardrobe on the west wall to the left of the larger one.

## Notes

- There is a  "Loot") Loot [shadowmark](/wiki/Skyrim:Thieves_Guild_(faction)#Shadowmarks "Skyrim:Thieves Guild (faction)") to the bottom right of the entrance door and another one to the right before the arch in front of her house.
- [Vignar Gray-Mane](/wiki/Skyrim:Vignar_Gray-Mane "Skyrim:Vignar Gray-Mane") and his court (i.e., [Brill](/wiki/Skyrim:Brill "Skyrim:Brill") and [Olfina Gray-Mane](/wiki/Skyrim:Olfina_Gray-Mane "Skyrim:Olfina Gray-Mane")), as well as [Avulstein Gray-Mane](/wiki/Skyrim:Avulstein_Gray-Mane "Skyrim:Avulstein Gray-Mane"), were supposed to go here if exiled, but it is impossible for this to happen, so they will never be seen here.

## Bugs

- The house and its front door are owned by the `WindhelmViolaFaction`, which Viola is not a member of.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 1.2.2, fixes this bug.

# Skyrim:Visit the hunters at Frostmoon Crag

## Overview

|  |
| --- |
| **This page is currently being rewritten as part of the [Skyrim Quest Redesign Project](/wiki/UESPWiki:Skyrim_Quest_Redesign_Project "UESPWiki:Skyrim Quest Redesign Project").** The page is being rewritten and checked in several stages. All users are welcome to make changes to the page. If you make a change that is relevant to the project, please update this template accordingly, and make sure you have observed the [project guidelines](/wiki/UESPWiki:Skyrim_Quest_Redesign_Project#Guidelines "UESPWiki:Skyrim Quest Redesign Project").  Details  **Walkthrough**: written by [Dreamshadow](/wiki/User:Dreamshadow "User:Dreamshadow"), checked by [MolagBallet](/wiki/User:MolagBallet "User:MolagBallet")  **Reward**: written by [Secunda](/wiki/User:Secunda "User:Secunda"), *not checked* |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Talk to the hunters at [Frostmoon Crag](/wiki/Skyrim:Frostmoon_Crag "Skyrim:Frostmoon Crag").** | |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Quest Giver: | [Geldis Sadri](/wiki/Skyrim:Geldis_Sadri "Skyrim:Geldis Sadri") |
| Location(s): | [Frostmoon Crag](/wiki/Skyrim:Frostmoon_Crag "Skyrim:Frostmoon Crag") |
| Reward: | Ability to buy four [unique rings](/wiki/Skyrim:Unique_Items#Unique_Jewelry "Skyrim:Unique Items") from [Majni](/wiki/Skyrim:Majni "Skyrim:Majni") (if you are a [werewolf](/wiki/Skyrim:Lycanthropy "Skyrim:Lycanthropy")) |
| ID: | DLC2dunFrostmoonQSTMisc |

What's the secret behind these hunters?

## Quick Walkthrough

1. Talk to the innkeeper of [The Retching Netch](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Retching_Netch "Skyrim:The Retching Netch") in Raven Rock.
2. Talk to [Majni](/wiki/Skyrim:Majni "Skyrim:Majni") in [Frostmoon Crag](/wiki/Skyrim:Frostmoon_Crag "Skyrim:Frostmoon Crag").

## Detailed Walkthrough

The Frostmoon Pack

You can ask [Geldis Sadri](/wiki/Skyrim:Geldis_Sadri "Skyrim:Geldis Sadri") in [The Retching Netch](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Retching_Netch "Skyrim:The Retching Netch") for any recent rumors. He may tell you about a pack of [werewolves](/wiki/Skyrim:Lycanthropy "Skyrim:Lycanthropy") in the mountains and advise you to talk to the hunters up at Frostmoon Crag to know more. Alternatively, you might run into a wandering hunter in the northern areas of Solstheim who will tell you about the pack at Frostmoon Crag. Although he will mention the pack to anyone, the quest will only start this way if you're a werewolf and you haven't already heard the rumors from Geldis.

Travel to Frostmoon Crag and you can find four hunters there. [Rakel](/wiki/Skyrim:Rakel "Skyrim:Rakel") will come up to you and stop you from getting too close. *"Hold, traveler. You have no business here."* If you're not a werewolf, she will ask you to go away and leave them in peace. *"We mean you no harm, but... be on your way."* Staying around will lead her to give you some warnings. They will transform into werewolves and attack you after the last warning. If she finds the beast blood in your body, she will say *"...Wait. You... you're one of us, aren't you? A werewolf?"* Then she will introduce the Frostmoon Pack to you and welcome you as a brother or sister.

Now, you may talk to Majni, Alpha of the Frostmoon Pack, and complete the quest. He will tell you *"You are welcome here, so long as you respect our law."* You can talk to the members of the pack to learn more about them. Once you have, you will be able to buy four [unique rings](/wiki/Skyrim:Unique_Jewelry "Skyrim:Unique Jewelry") that affect your beast form from Majni.

## Notes

- \* If you are not a werewolf, the quest will remain in your journal unless you come back as a werewolf. If the Companions quest was completed and you cured your lycanthropy, you can go back to Aela the Huntress in Jorrvaskr and ask her: "I wish to regain the gift of beast blood".
  -  The [Unofficial Dragonborn Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Dragonborn_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Dragonborn Patch"), version 1.0.4, fixes this bug.
  -  Use the console command `setstage DLC2dunFrostmoonQSTMisc 20` to update it and remove it from your journal.
  - Attacking the hunters will also remove the quest.
- If your lycanthropy is cured during the quest, Majni will no longer speak with you or sell you the rings.
- The quest can also be started by a [randomly encountered](/wiki/Skyrim:World_Interactions "Skyrim:World Interactions"), generic lycanthropic [hunter](/wiki/Skyrim:Hunter "Skyrim:Hunter") who does not turn hostile. They will initiate the following dialogue: *"Well, now... a fellow beast-blood. Well met, friend. Not many of our kind still hunt these lands. If you ever need aid, seek out Majni's pack, up at Frostmoon Crag. They've long welcomed outsiders. Now... my prey awaits. Good hunting to you."*

## Bugs

- If [Geldis Sadri](/wiki/Skyrim:Geldis_Sadri "Skyrim:Geldis Sadri") sends you to talk to the hunters here, the quest objective is not cleared when they tell you to go away.
  -  The [Unofficial Dragonborn Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Dragonborn_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Dragonborn Patch"), version 1.0.4, fixes this bug.

## Quest Stages

| Visit the hunters at Frostmoon Crag (DLC2dunFrostmoonQSTMisc) | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Stage | Finishes Quest | Journal Entry |
| 10 |  | *Objective 10:* Visit the hunters at Frostmoon Crag |
| 11 |  | *Objective 11:* Visit the werewolves at Frostmoon Crag |

- The following empty quest stages were omitted from the table: 20, 30.

Notes

- Any text displayed in angle brackets (e.g., `<Alias=LocationHold>`) is dynamically set by the Radiant Quest system, and will be filled in with the appropriate word(s) when seen in game.
- Not all Journal Entries may appear in your journal; which entries appear and which entries do not depends on the manner in which the quest is done.
- Stages are not always in order of progress. This is usually the case with quests that have multiple possible outcomes or quests where certain tasks may be done in any order. Some stages may therefore repeat objectives seen in other stages.
- If an entry is marked as "Finishes Quest" it means the quest disappears from the Active Quest list, but you may still receive new entries for that quest.
- On the PC, it is possible to use the [console](/wiki/Skyrim:Console "Skyrim:Console") to advance through the quest by entering `setstage DLC2dunFrostmoonQSTMisc stage`, where `stage` is the number of the stage you wish to complete. It is not possible to un-complete (i.e. go back) quest stages, but it is possible to clear all stages of the quest using `resetquest DLC2dunFrostmoonQSTMisc`.

# Skyrim:Vittoria Vici's House

## Overview

|  |
| --- |
| **This article is being developed as part of the Skyrim Houses Redesign Project. If you see this tag it means the article can still be improved. Please see the [project page](/wiki/UESPWiki:SHRP "UESPWiki:SHRP") for guidelines.** |

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| # of Zones | 1 |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | 10 days |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| SolitudeVittoriaVicisHouse | |
| Location | |
| In [Solitude](/wiki/Skyrim:Solitude "Skyrim:Solitude"), between [Castle Dour](/wiki/Skyrim:Castle_Dour "Skyrim:Castle Dour") and [Proudspire Manor](/wiki/Skyrim:Proudspire_Manor "Skyrim:Proudspire Manor") | |
| Special Features | |
| # of [Cooking Pots/Spits](/wiki/Skyrim:Cooking "Skyrim:Cooking") | 1 |
| [Shadowmark](/wiki/Skyrim:Shadowmarks_(Thieves_Guild) "Skyrim:Shadowmarks (Thieves Guild)") | [Loot](/wiki/Skyrim:Shadowmarks_(Thieves_Guild) "Loot") Loot |

Vittoria Vici's House

**Vittoria Vici's House** is a large house in [Solitude](/wiki/Skyrim:Solitude "Skyrim:Solitude") and home of [Vittoria Vici](/wiki/Skyrim:Vittoria_Vici "Skyrim:Vittoria Vici").

However, if you have killed her during the [Dark Brotherhood](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Brotherhood "Skyrim:Dark Brotherhood") quest, it will be inhabited by her [friend](/wiki/Skyrim:Aquillius_Aeresius "Skyrim:Aquillius Aeresius").

## Residents

| Residents |
| --- |
| [Aquillius Aeresius](/wiki/Skyrim:Aquillius_Aeresius "Skyrim:Aquillius Aeresius")  [Vittoria Vici](/wiki/Skyrim:Vittoria_Vici "Skyrim:Vittoria Vici") |

### Exterior

It is next door to [Proudspire Manor](/wiki/Skyrim:Proudspire_Manor "Skyrim:Proudspire Manor") and across the street from Solitude's [Hall of the Dead](/wiki/Skyrim:Hall_of_the_Dead_(Solitude) "Skyrim:Hall of the Dead (Solitude)"). There are three entrances and three floors, The main door is up a flight of stairs opposite Proudspire Manor. The second, also up a flight of stairs and is at the other end of the building and the third is below the second and leads directly into the first floor. the first two entrances both lead to the Second floor. They are all apprentice-locked. There is a  "Loot") Loot [shadowmark](/wiki/Skyrim:Shadowmarks_(Thieves_Guild) "Skyrim:Shadowmarks (Thieves Guild)") on the wall to the left of the main door. The second floor overhangs the ground floor and has arches supporting it at the front. Under this overhang are a couple of piles of firewood and several [food](/wiki/Skyrim:Food "Skyrim:Food") barrels. At the rear of the building, once you have completed the [Thieves Guild](/wiki/Skyrim:Thieves_Guild_(faction) "Skyrim:Thieves Guild (faction)") reputation quest in Solitude, is a barrel marked with the  "Cache") Cache shadowmark. There is a [hanging moss](/wiki/Skyrim:Hanging_Moss "Skyrim:Hanging Moss") growing from the roof, but it is out of reach.

### Vittoria Vici's House

Second Floor main room

#### Second Floor

Upon entering you are in a large room with two narrow tables against the northeast and southeast walls, they hold an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger") and a [bowl](/wiki/Skyrim:Bowl "Skyrim:Bowl") containing [tundra cotton](/wiki/Skyrim:Tundra_Cotton "Skyrim:Tundra Cotton"). There are two bookcases against the southwest wall holding mainly common books, but also a copy of *[Arcana Restored](/wiki/Skyrim:Arcana_Restored "Skyrim:Arcana Restored")*, a [flute](/wiki/Skyrim:Flute "Skyrim:Flute"), a [potion of extreme stamina](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Extreme_Stamina "Skyrim:Potion of Extreme Stamina") and a [solution of strength](/wiki/Skyrim:Solution_of_Strength "Skyrim:Solution of Strength"). In the center of the room is a low table with a chair and a wooden bench. On the table is a [goblet](/wiki/Skyrim:Goblet "Skyrim:Goblet"), a bottle of [spiced wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Spiced_Wine "Skyrim:Spiced Wine"), a common book and a [plate](/wiki/Skyrim:Plate "Skyrim:Plate") holding a [sliced eidar cheese](/wiki/Skyrim:Sliced_Eidar_Cheese "Skyrim:Sliced Eidar Cheese").

There is an alcove to the northeast at the rear of the room with a cupboard holding a [deathbell](/wiki/Skyrim:Deathbell "Skyrim:Deathbell") flower and a copy of *[The Firmament](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Firmament "Skyrim:The Firmament")* against the northwest wall. Above this is a wall shelf with two [cabbages](/wiki/Skyrim:Cabbage "Skyrim:Cabbage") on it. To the left is a second wooden bench. Against the northeast wall is a set of shelves holding a copy of *[The Cake and The Diamond](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Cake_and_The_Diamond "Skyrim:The Cake and The Diamond")*, a bunch of tundra cotton, two goblets, a [draught of strength](/wiki/Skyrim:Draught_of_Strength "Skyrim:Draught of Strength"), a [potion of health](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Health "Skyrim:Potion of Health"), a [potion of regeneration](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Regeneration "Skyrim:Potion of Regeneration") and a basket containing two bunches of [lavender](/wiki/Skyrim:Lavender "Skyrim:Lavender"). Beside this is a sack of foodstuffs. To the northwest is an opening with a central pillar. An open corridor leads to the stairwell, with stairs leading up and down.

To the left of the pillar is an open corridor leading to the second entrance. There is a wooden bench against the southeast wall. Against the southwest wall is a long open box containing a bunch of tundra cotton, a [skull](/wiki/Skyrim:Skull "Skyrim:Skull") and a [sabre cat pelt](/wiki/Skyrim:Sabre_Cat_Pelt "Skyrim:Sabre Cat Pelt"). Further along the wall to the right is a small cupboard holding a [jug](/wiki/Skyrim:Jug "Skyrim:Jug"). Against the same wall, right by the entrance is an unlocked [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") and a set of shelves holding two [slaughterfish scales](/wiki/Skyrim:Slaughterfish_Scales "Skyrim:Slaughterfish Scales"), a bowl of [glow dust](/wiki/Skyrim:Glow_Dust "Skyrim:Glow Dust"), a [potion of haggling](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Haggling "Skyrim:Potion of Haggling"), two bottles of [frostbite venom](/wiki/Skyrim:Frostbite_Venom "Skyrim:Frostbite Venom"), a deathbell flower, a bowl of [void salts](/wiki/Skyrim:Void_Salts "Skyrim:Void Salts") and a basket containing two bunches of lavender. Opposite this is another set of shelves holding copies of *[Beggar Prince](/wiki/Skyrim:Beggar_Prince "Skyrim:Beggar Prince")*, *[A Gentleman's Guide to Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:A_Gentleman%27s_Guide_to_Whiterun "Skyrim:A Gentleman's Guide to Whiterun")*, *[The Windhelm Letters](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Windhelm_Letters "Skyrim:The Windhelm Letters")*, *[Biography of Barenziah, v1](/wiki/Skyrim:Biography_of_Barenziah,_v1 "Skyrim:Biography of Barenziah, v1")* and *[v2](/wiki/Skyrim:Biography_of_Barenziah,_v2 "Skyrim:Biography of Barenziah, v2")*, a goblet, a deathbell flower and a basket containing a bunch of tundra cotton.

To the northeast is a door to the kitchen, containing a cupboard opposite the door, with the main part of the room to the left. On the northeast wall is a large fireplace with a stone mantle holding a [bowl](/wiki/Skyrim:Bowl "Skyrim:Bowl"), two goblets and a bottle of frostbite venom. On the hearth is a piece of [raw beef](/wiki/Skyrim:Raw_Beef "Skyrim:Raw Beef") and a cabbage in a cast iron pot. To the left is a vase containing a [snowberry](/wiki/Skyrim:Snowberries "Skyrim:Snowberries") bush and to the left of this is a unit holding a bottle of [alto wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Alto_Wine "Skyrim:Alto Wine"), a goblet and two [cups](/wiki/Skyrim:Cup "Skyrim:Cup"). Against the northwest wall are two sets of shelves holding more goblet, a jug, two goblets, two slaughterfish scales, a bowl of glow dust, a potion of haggling, two bottles of frostbite venom, a deathbell flower, a bowl of void salts and a basket containing two bunches of lavender. Further to the left on the same wall is a cupboard with a [platter](/wiki/Skyrim:Platter "Skyrim:Platter") holding two [sweet rolls](/wiki/Skyrim:Sweet_Roll "Skyrim:Sweet Roll"), two [boiled creme treats](/wiki/Skyrim:Boiled_Creme_Treat "Skyrim:Boiled Creme Treat") and four [red apples](/wiki/Skyrim:Red_Apple "Skyrim:Red Apple"). In the middle of the room is a large table with three chairs holding a potion of regeneration, a bunch of tundra cotton, three goblets, two [seared slaughterfish](/wiki/Skyrim:Seared_Slaughterfish "Skyrim:Seared Slaughterfish"), three [salmon steaks](/wiki/Skyrim:Salmon_Steak "Skyrim:Salmon Steak"), a [baked potato](/wiki/Skyrim:Baked_Potatoes "Skyrim:Baked Potatoes"), on three plates, a loaf of [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread") and two [goat cheese wheels](/wiki/Skyrim:Goat_Cheese_Wheel "Skyrim:Goat Cheese Wheel"). Against the southwest wall is a rack hanging two [elves ears](/wiki/Skyrim:Elves_Ear "Skyrim:Elves Ear"), a [garlic](/wiki/Skyrim:Garlic "Skyrim:Garlic") braid and a bunch of [frost mirriam](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Mirriam "Skyrim:Frost Mirriam"). To the left of this is a narrow table holding an iron dagger, bowl of bread and plate holding a [sliced eidar cheese](/wiki/Skyrim:Sliced_Eidar_Cheese "Skyrim:Sliced Eidar Cheese"). Above this is a wall shelf holding a bottle of [Nord mead](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord_Mead "Skyrim:Nord Mead"). Finally against the southwest wall is another cupboard.

First Floor kitchen

#### First Floor

At the bottom of the stairs is an alcove to the right with a cupboard holding a couple of sacks, a bowl and cups and a set of shelves full of mead barrels, a bottle of [wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Wine "Skyrim:Wine") and two bottles of Nord mead. Opposite the alcove is a room containing a display cabinet holding a [steel battleaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Battleaxe "Skyrim:Steel Battleaxe"), to the left of this is a small wardrobe with a chair beside it holding a goblet and jug. Against the northeast wall is a cupboard, with a [cooking pot](/wiki/Skyrim:Cooking_Pot "Skyrim:Cooking Pot") on a stand to the left of this. On the other side of the cooking pot is a long wooden table and bench holding exactly the same as the table upstairs. Against the southwest wall is a cupboard with an open crate on top holding five red apples and three [potatoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Potato "Skyrim:Potato"). To the northwest is a short corridor leading to the third entrance with a small wardrobe against the northeast wall.

Straight ahead from the bottom of the stairs there is a cupboard against the northeast wall holding several cheese wheels, two loaves of bread and a sheaf of [wheat](/wiki/Skyrim:Wheat "Skyrim:Wheat"). Hanging from the ceiling are a total of six garlic braids. To the right of the cupboard is a rack hanging two elves ears, a garlic braid and a bunch of frost mirriam. Against the southeast wall is a cupboard holding a [gourd](/wiki/Skyrim:Gourd "Skyrim:Gourd"), three garlic bulbs, a set of [fine clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Fine_Clothes "Skyrim:Fine Clothes") and a pair of [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Boots "Skyrim:Boots"). Against the southwest wall are two alcoves housing two large vats with food barrels behind.

Third Floor bedroom

#### Third Floor

Up the first flight of stairs you pass a large vase holding a snowberry bush. At the top of the stairs is an L-shaped balcony with a unit holding a bottle of alto wine, a goblet and two cups. To your right against the southwest wall is a narrow table holding a bowl containing a bunch of tundra cotton, with a small table holding a large vase containing another bunch. To the left of the table are two more bookcases holding several common books as well as a copy of *[Charwich-Koniinge Letters, v3](/wiki/Skyrim:Charwich-Koniinge_Letters,_v3 "Skyrim:Charwich-Koniinge Letters, v3")*, a potion of extreme stamina and a solution of strength. There is a low table and chair in front of the bookcases holding a plate of bread and cheese and a common book. Against the southeast wall is a wooden bench.

Turning left at the top of the stairs, there is a cupboard against the northwest wall holding a flute, a [drum](/wiki/Skyrim:Drum "Skyrim:Drum") and a potion of health, with a wall shelf above holding a deathbell flower and a copy of *[A Minor Maze](/wiki/Skyrim:A_Minor_Maze "Skyrim:A Minor Maze")*. There is a door to the northeast the opens into an L-shaped bedroom, to your right, is a narrow table against the southeast holding a plate of three red apples and a platter holding a sweet roll, a [baked potato](/wiki/Skyrim:Baked_Potatoes "Skyrim:Baked Potatoes") and a [sliced goat cheese](/wiki/Skyrim:Sliced_Goat_Cheese "Skyrim:Sliced Goat Cheese"), and in front of this is a low table and chair holding a goblet, a potion of regeneration and a plate of bread and an [eidar cheese wedge](/wiki/Skyrim:Eidar_Cheese_Wedge "Skyrim:Eidar Cheese Wedge"). Opposite the door is a large vase containing a bunch of tundra cotton on a small table and the rest of the bedroom to the left. The large double bed is coming diagonally out from the western corner, with a long unlocked chest at the foot of the bed. In the southern corner is a small wardrobe holding a jug and two goblets, with a chair to the right of it and a washbasin on a stand to the left. Beside this is a basket hiding a small [coin purse](/wiki/Skyrim:Coin_Purse "Skyrim:Coin Purse"). Against the northwest wall is an unlocked [safe](/wiki/Skyrim:Safe "Skyrim:Safe") and tall wardrobe containing fine clothes. Against the northeast wall is a second long unlocked chest, with a small wardrobe to the right of it holding a goblet.

## Notes

- The [keys to the house](/wiki/Skyrim:Key_to_Vittoria_Vici%27s_House "Skyrim:Key to Vittoria Vici's House") are carried by Aquillius and Vittoria.

# Skyrim:Vlindrel Hall

## Overview

|  |
| --- |
| **This article is being developed as part of the Skyrim Houses Redesign Project. If you see this tag it means the article can still be improved. Please see the [project page](/wiki/UESPWiki:SHRP "UESPWiki:SHRP") for guidelines.** |

| # of Zones | 1 |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | Never (storage is [safe](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Safe_Locations "Skyrim:Containers")) |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| MarkarthVlindrelHall | |
| Region | |
| [The Reach](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Reach "Skyrim:The Reach") | |
| Location | |
| [Markarth](/wiki/Skyrim:Markarth "Skyrim:Markarth") | |
| Special Features | |

| [Shadowmark](/wiki/Skyrim:Shadowmarks_(Thieves_Guild) "Skyrim:Shadowmarks (Thieves Guild)") | [Cache](/wiki/Skyrim:Shadowmarks_(Thieves_Guild) "Cache") Cache |

Vlindrel Hall

**Vlindrel Hall** is a [house](/wiki/Skyrim:Houses "Skyrim:Houses") available for purchase in [Markarth](/wiki/Skyrim:Markarth "Skyrim:Markarth").

To purchase this home, you must first complete the prerequisite quests for [Thane of the Reach](/wiki/Skyrim:Thane_of_the_Reach "Skyrim:Thane of the Reach"). If [Igmund](/wiki/Skyrim:Igmund "Skyrim:Igmund") is Jarl, you must complete [Kill the Forsworn Leader](/wiki/Skyrim:Kill_the_Forsworn_Leader_(Jarl) "Skyrim:Kill the Forsworn Leader (Jarl)"), followed by [Dungeon Delving](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeon_Delving_(Jarl_-_Hagravens) "Skyrim:Dungeon Delving (Jarl - Hagravens)"), the latter of which requires you to be at least level 20. If [Thongvor Silver-Blood](/wiki/Skyrim:Thongvor_Silver-Blood "Skyrim:Thongvor Silver-Blood") becomes Jarl, either due to the events of [The Battle for Fort Sungard](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Battle_for_Fort_Sungard_(Stormcloaks) "Skyrim:The Battle for Fort Sungard (Stormcloaks)") or [Season Unending](/wiki/Skyrim:Season_Unending "Skyrim:Season Unending"), he will grant you permission to buy it out of gratitude for your efforts in making him Jarl. You can purchase the house from the Jarl's [Steward](/wiki/Skyrim:Steward "Skyrim:Steward") for 8,000 gold once you have been given permission.

Once purchased, the Steward will provide you with a book, *[Markarth Home Decorating Guide](/wiki/Skyrim:Markarth_Home_Decorating_Guide "Skyrim:Markarth Home Decorating Guide")*, which details the various house upgrades available from him. The upgrades cost 3,900 or 4,200 gold, putting the final cost of Vlindrel Hall at 11,900 or 12,200 gold, depending on whether you choose the child's bedroom[HF](/wiki/Skyrim:Hearthfire "Skyrim:Hearthfire") or the alchemy laboratory. [Argis the Bulwark](/wiki/Skyrim:Argis_the_Bulwark "Skyrim:Argis the Bulwark") will be appointed your housecarl and will move to Vlindrel Hall after you become a thane.

## Residents

| Residents |
| --- |
| [Argis the Bulwark](/wiki/Skyrim:Argis_the_Bulwark "Skyrim:Argis the Bulwark") [Follower](/wiki/Skyrim:Followers "Follower") ‡ |

‡ This person moves here once you become [Thane of the Reach](/wiki/Skyrim:Thane_of_the_Reach "Skyrim:Thane of the Reach").

### Exterior

The house is located in the top tier of houses over the market place. It is next door to [Nepos's house](/wiki/Skyrim:Nepos%27s_House "Skyrim:Nepos's House") and above [Ogmund's house](/wiki/Skyrim:Ogmund%27s_House "Skyrim:Ogmund's House"), both of which have related quests that can leave them uninhabited. Outside and to the right of the single entrance is a lit brazier and [food](/wiki/Skyrim:Food "Skyrim:Food") sack. Once you have completed the [reputation quest](/wiki/Skyrim:Silver_Lining "Skyrim:Silver Lining") for the [Thieves Guild](/wiki/Skyrim:Thieves_Guild_(faction) "Skyrim:Thieves Guild (faction)") in [Markarth](/wiki/Skyrim:Markarth "Skyrim:Markarth"), a barrel marked with the  "Cache") Cache [shadowmark](/wiki/Skyrim:Shadowmarks_(Thieves_Guild) "Skyrim:Shadowmarks (Thieves Guild)") will also be found by the brazier.

#### Prior to Purchasing any Upgrades

The whole place is filthy, with cobwebs and dust throughout. There are many pieces of cheap, broken, and unusable wooden furniture. In the living room, a large stone table is centrally situated with a single wooden chair drawn up to it; the others have been knocked over or are resting upside-down on the edge of the table. There is a large fireplace with a lit fire to the north, which has a sunken area in front of it. Hanging over the fire is a metal pot. There is also a food barrel by the rear wall to the left of an open door that leads back to a room which separates the bedrooms. This room also has a large fireplace with a lit fire opposite the door, with a [cooking pot](/wiki/Skyrim:Cooking_Pot "Skyrim:Cooking Pot"), a [carrot](/wiki/Skyrim:Carrot "Skyrim:Carrot"), and a cast iron pot containing a [baked potato](/wiki/Skyrim:Baked_Potatoes "Skyrim:Baked Potatoes") and [cabbage](/wiki/Skyrim:Cabbage "Skyrim:Cabbage") on the hearth. To the left is a loaf of [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread"), and to the right is a wooden block with a rack of [dog meat](/wiki/Skyrim:Dog_Meat "Skyrim:Dog Meat") on top. The central third of this room is also sunken, like the area around the fireplace in the first room, and another stone table stands in the middle. There are a couple chairs, a crate, and a round wooden table on the raised section to the left, as well as a large doorway that leads to the master bedroom. On the raised section to the right of the fireplace is a dagger-rack display case on a stone ledge, with an end table and open crate to the right of it. On the end table is a [red apple](/wiki/Skyrim:Red_Apple "Skyrim:Red Apple") and a jug; the open crate contains more fine crockery. To the right of a doorway leading through into the [housecarl](/wiki/Skyrim:Housecarl "Skyrim:Housecarl")'s bedroom are a crate, a food barrel, and a sack. There is also a set of shelves that holds two bottles of [alto wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Alto_Wine "Skyrim:Alto Wine"), a bottle of [wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Wine "Skyrim:Wine"), two bottles of [Nord mead](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord_Mead "Skyrim:Nord Mead"), a [garlic](/wiki/Skyrim:Garlic "Skyrim:Garlic") bulb, a cabbage, a [gourd](/wiki/Skyrim:Gourd "Skyrim:Gourd"), a cast iron pot containing two carrots and a cheese wheel, a haunch of [venison](/wiki/Skyrim:Venison "Skyrim:Venison"), a [goat cheese wheel](/wiki/Skyrim:Goat_Cheese_Wheel "Skyrim:Goat Cheese Wheel"), a [leek](/wiki/Skyrim:Leek "Skyrim:Leek"), and a [rock warbler egg](/wiki/Skyrim:Rock_Warbler_Egg "Skyrim:Rock Warbler Egg") in a wooden bowl.

The master bedroom contains a large, poor quality double bed with an empty [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") at its foot and a crate to the right.

At the other end of the main room are two more open doors. The right-hand door leads to a darkened room that is empty except for a single crate behind the door, while the left-hand door leads into another room with a lit fireplace in front of which are a block of wood, a chair, and stool. In the corner behind the left-hand door there is a crate with three food sacks on top, as well as a mead barrel containing bottles of Nord mead and clutter.

## Costs and Upgrades

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Vlindrel Hall** | | | | | | |
| Home and Upgrades purchased from | [Raerek](/wiki/Skyrim:Raerek "Skyrim:Raerek") or [Reburrus Quintilius](/wiki/Skyrim:Reburrus_Quintilius "Skyrim:Reburrus Quintilius") | Price to purchase home | [Value](/wiki/File:SR-item-Gold-heads.png "Value")  8,000 | Housecarl | [Argis the Bulwark](/wiki/Skyrim:Argis_the_Bulwark "Skyrim:Argis the Bulwark") | |
| Total cost of all upgrades | [Value](/wiki/File:SR-item-Gold-heads.png "Value")  3,900[HF](/wiki/Skyrim:Hearthfire "Skyrim:Hearthfire") to 4,200 |
| **Upgrades** | | | | | | |
| Bedroom furnishings | Living room furnishings | Alchemy laboratory | Enchanting laboratory | Entrance hall | Child's Bedroom[†](#intnote_ChildroomVH)[HF](/wiki/Skyrim:Hearthfire "Skyrim:Hearthfire") | Housecarl's Quarters |
| ID: (000e2d52) | ID: (000e2d53) | ID: (000e2d55) | ID: (000e2d56) | ID: (000e2d57) | ID: ([xx](/wiki/Skyrim:Form_ID "Skyrim:Form ID")004206) | ID: (000e2d54) |
| 800 gold | 900 gold | 1000 gold | 1000 gold | 500 gold | 700 gold | Become [Thane of the Reach](/wiki/Skyrim:Thane_of_the_Reach "Skyrim:Thane of the Reach") |
| 1 Double Bed  1 Chest  4 Cupboards  2 Wardrobes  2 Corner Shelf stands (2 shelves)  1 Shield plaque  3 Dagger Rack Display cases  1 Table  4 Chairs  1 Dresser  1 Wall Shelf  8 Rugs | 9 Chairs  3 Tables  1 End Table  1 Dresser  1 Cupboard  3 Bookshelves (3 shelves  holding 18 books each)  2 Shelf stands (3 shelves)  1 Dagger Rack Display case  8 Rugs | 1 [Alchemy Lab](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemy_Lab "Skyrim:Alchemy Lab")  2 Wall Shelves  1 Chair  1 Stool  1 Rug  2 Barrels   Harvestable plants:  1 [Garlic Braid](/wiki/Skyrim:Garlic_Braid "Skyrim:Garlic Braid")  2 [Dried Frost Mirriam](/wiki/Skyrim:Dried_Frost_Mirriam "Skyrim:Dried Frost Mirriam")  2 [Dried Elves Ear](/wiki/Skyrim:Dried_Elves_Ear "Skyrim:Dried Elves Ear")   Harvestable animals:  1 [Pheasant](/wiki/Skyrim:Pheasant "Skyrim:Pheasant")  2 [Rabbits](/wiki/Skyrim:Rabbit_(dead) "Skyrim:Rabbit (dead)") | 1 [Arcane Enchanter](/wiki/Skyrim:Arcane_Enchanter "Skyrim:Arcane Enchanter")  2 Weapon plaques  1 Wall Shelf  2 Shelf stands (3 shelves)  2 Weapon racks (2 slots)  2 Shield plaques  1 Mannequin  1 Rug | 2 Rugs   Harvestable plants:  2 Gourd  1 [Red Mountain Flower](/wiki/Skyrim:Red_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Red Mountain Flower")  1 [Lavender](/wiki/Skyrim:Lavender "Skyrim:Lavender")  1 [Snowberries](/wiki/Skyrim:Snowberries "Skyrim:Snowberries")  2 [Jazbay Grapes](/wiki/Skyrim:Jazbay_Grapes "Skyrim:Jazbay Grapes") | 2 Child's Single Beds  1 Child's Chest  1 Tall Wardrobe  1 End Table  1 Child's Practice Dummy  1 Small Sideboard  1 Rug | 1 Bed  1 Table  1 Chair  1 Dresser  1 Cupboard (2 shelves)  3 Rugs |

[†](#note_ChildroomVH)The Child's Bedroom is only available if [Hearthfire](/wiki/Skyrim:Hearthfire "Skyrim:Hearthfire") is installed. Choosing this upgrade will replace the Alchemy Lab and is repeatably reversible.

### Walkthrough - Room by Room

As each upgrade is purchased, the corresponding area is cleaned up, rugs are scattered over the floors, and mounted animal heads are hung from the walls. Most of the existing items are kept, though often upgraded to be higher quality. Throughout the property fine quality crockery and goat horn lamps (or candles on stands) are added.

#### Bedroom

Master bedroom

This upgrade actually upgrades both the master bedroom and the room between the bedrooms. The double bed in the master bedroom is upgraded to a finer quality bed and corner shelves are added on either side. (The left-hand shelf holds a rock warbler egg in a wooden bowl.) The empty chest remains at the foot of the bed, and four small cupboards are added around the room. These cupboards hold two [red](/wiki/Skyrim:Red_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Red Mountain Flower") and one [blue mountain flower](/wiki/Skyrim:Blue_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Blue Mountain Flower"), a [thistle branch](/wiki/Skyrim:Thistle_Branch "Skyrim:Thistle Branch") and a [sweet roll](/wiki/Skyrim:Sweet_Roll "Skyrim:Sweet Roll"), a [potion of minor healing](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Minor_Healing "Skyrim:Potion of Minor Healing"), and a bottle of [Black-Briar Mead](/wiki/Skyrim:Black-Briar_Mead "Skyrim:Black-Briar Mead"), respectively. There are two wardrobes, one small - with a bottle of alto wine and a gourd on top - and one tall; each contains a selection of [fine clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Fine_Clothes "Skyrim:Fine Clothes") and [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Fine_Boots "Skyrim:Fine Boots"), and to the left are a washbasin, a dagger-rack display case with a wall shelf above it, and a bowl containing two sprigs of [snowberries](/wiki/Skyrim:Snowberries "Skyrim:Snowberries"), as well as three food sacks. On the wall above the head of the bed hangs a shield rack sporting an [iron shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Shield "Skyrim:Iron Shield") and crossed [iron greatswords](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Greatsword "Skyrim:Iron Greatsword").

The middle room has kept all its original items. The central stone table has now been laid with a platter holding several red and [green apples](/wiki/Skyrim:Green_Apple "Skyrim:Green Apple") and wedges of cheese, two bottles of alto wine, a bottle of wine, a copy of *[On the Great Collapse](/wiki/Skyrim:On_the_Great_Collapse "Skyrim:On the Great Collapse")*, and a wooden bowl containing two [luna moth wings](/wiki/Skyrim:Luna_Moth_Wing "Skyrim:Luna Moth Wing"). To the left of the fireplace is a second dagger-rack display case. The round wooden table outside the master bedroom has been laid with a potion of minor healing, a [boiled creme treat](/wiki/Skyrim:Boiled_Creme_Treat "Skyrim:Boiled Creme Treat"), and a [honey nut treat](/wiki/Skyrim:Honey_Nut_Treat "Skyrim:Honey Nut Treat"). The chairs in the room have been upgraded to a higher quality and are arranged in pairs either side of the stone table and the doorway to the master bedroom.

#### Housecarl's Bedroom

Once you have been made [Thane](/wiki/Skyrim:Thane "Skyrim:Thane"), the housecarl's room is furnished with an owned single bed against a stone ledge to the southeast. On the ledge are a small mead barrel and a wooden bowl containing one bunch of [juniper berries](/wiki/Skyrim:Juniper_Berries "Skyrim:Juniper Berries") and two bunches of [jazbay grapes](/wiki/Skyrim:Jazbay_Grapes "Skyrim:Jazbay Grapes"). Beside the bed stands a small table with a loaf of bread on top and a kettle beside it containing two cabbages and two [potatoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Potato "Skyrim:Potato"). Against the northeast wall is a cupboard and a square wooden table, both of which hold more fine crockery - of which there is even more in a basket on the floor. Opposite the bed hanging from a rack are two bunches of dried frost mirriam, a bunch of dried elves ear, and a garlic braid, with a pile of firewood, a wooden block, and a bucket containing a [woodcutter's axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Woodcutter%27s_Axe "Skyrim:Woodcutter's Axe") and two [goat cheese wedges](/wiki/Skyrim:Goat_Cheese_Wedge "Skyrim:Goat Cheese Wedge") underneath. To the left is a dresser with an [apple pie](/wiki/Skyrim:Apple_Pie "Skyrim:Apple Pie") and a bottle of alto wine on top. Beside the dresser is a washbasin and a basket containing three leeks and a potato.

#### Living Room

Living room

The central stone table now has three chairs drawn up to it and has been laid with two bottles of alto wine, a bottle of wine, a baked potato, a [salmon steak](/wiki/Skyrim:Salmon_Steak "Skyrim:Salmon Steak"), an apple pie, two portions of [grilled leeks](/wiki/Skyrim:Grilled_Leeks "Skyrim:Grilled Leeks"), a [pheasant breast](/wiki/Skyrim:Pheasant_Breast "Skyrim:Pheasant Breast"), a cabbage, a [tomato](/wiki/Skyrim:Tomato "Skyrim:Tomato"), a [raw rabbit leg](/wiki/Skyrim:Raw_Rabbit_Leg "Skyrim:Raw Rabbit Leg"), a [salt pile](/wiki/Skyrim:Salt_Pile "Skyrim:Salt Pile"), and some bread and cheese. Two large round tables stand on either side of the fireplace. Both have a single chair, and they are laid with a bottle of alto wine, a bottle of wine, a green apple, several loaves of bread and pieces of cheese, a sweet roll, a wooden bowl containing three bunches of jazbay grapes, a [drum](/wiki/Skyrim:Drum "Skyrim:Drum"), a [flute](/wiki/Skyrim:Flute "Skyrim:Flute"), and a copy of *[The Third Door](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Third_Door "Skyrim:The Third Door")*. To the right is a small cupboard with a [lute](/wiki/Skyrim:Lute "Skyrim:Lute") on top. To the left of the fireplace is a set of shelves holding an [iron mace](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Mace "Skyrim:Iron Mace"), a sample of [ice wraith teeth](/wiki/Skyrim:Ice_Wraith_Teeth "Skyrim:Ice Wraith Teeth"), and copies of *[Galerion The Mystic](/wiki/Skyrim:Galerion_The_Mystic "Skyrim:Galerion The Mystic")* and *[Beggar Prince](/wiki/Skyrim:Beggar_Prince "Skyrim:Beggar Prince")*. Beside these shelves is a washbasin.

A square wooden table stands to the left of the entrance doorway, with a bottle of alto wine, two honey nut treats, and a copy of *[Chance's Folly](/wiki/Skyrim:Chance%27s_Folly "Skyrim:Chance's Folly")* on top and a single chair pulled up to it. Three tall, three-shelf bookcases are arranged in an L-shape nearby, one of which is backed up against the table and two of which stand along the wall. Against the wall at the southwest end of the room is a dresser with a dagger-rack display case on top and chairs on either side. To the right of the doorway is a mannequin, an end table with a [long taffy treat](/wiki/Skyrim:Long_Taffy_Treat "Skyrim:Long Taffy Treat") on top, and a set of shelves holding a [deer hide](/wiki/Skyrim:Deer_Hide "Skyrim:Deer Hide"), a bottle of alto wine, a bottle of wine, and a set of [iron armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Armor "Skyrim:Iron Armor") and [boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Boots "Skyrim:Iron Boots"). On one side of the shelves is a half-height stone wall with a quiver of [iron arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Arrow "Skyrim:Iron Arrow") on top. Nearby are a chair and stool, on top of which sits a bowl of red apples and cheese; a bottle of alto wine sits on the floor beside it.

#### Alchemy Lab

Alchemy Laboratory

The left-hand room off of the main room still contains its original items, but now also houses an alchemy lab, with three racks hanging above it which hold two rabbits, a pheasant, two bunches of dried elves ear, two bunches of dried frost mirriam, and a garlic braid. On the small stone shelves on either side of the table sit a bowl of [vampire dust](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Dust "Skyrim:Vampire Dust"), a salt pile, a bottle of [frostbite venom](/wiki/Skyrim:Frostbite_Venom "Skyrim:Frostbite Venom"), a bowl of [ectoplasm](/wiki/Skyrim:Ectoplasm "Skyrim:Ectoplasm"), and a [draught of vigor](/wiki/Skyrim:Draught_of_Vigor "Skyrim:Draught of Vigor"). To the left of the lab is a washbasin holding two samples of [slaughterfish scales](/wiki/Skyrim:Slaughterfish_Scales "Skyrim:Slaughterfish Scales") and four [slaughterfish eggs](/wiki/Skyrim:Slaughterfish_Egg "Skyrim:Slaughterfish Egg"). On the floor to the right is an open crate containing a cabbage and three apples, two red and one green. Beside the previously existing mead barrel, atop which now sit a sheaf of [wheat](/wiki/Skyrim:Wheat "Skyrim:Wheat") and a bowl of [bone meal](/wiki/Skyrim:Bone_Meal "Skyrim:Bone Meal"), a barrel containing [ingredients](/wiki/Skyrim:Ingredients "Skyrim:Ingredients") has been added. There are two wall shelves above the stone shelf that runs along the rear wall; these hold two bowls of [troll fat](/wiki/Skyrim:Troll_Fat "Skyrim:Troll Fat"), two sprigs of [frost mirriam](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Mirriam "Skyrim:Frost Mirriam"), a sample of [scaly pholiota](/wiki/Skyrim:Scaly_Pholiota "Skyrim:Scaly Pholiota") fungus, a [mammoth cheese bowl](/wiki/Skyrim:Mammoth_Cheese_Bowl "Skyrim:Mammoth Cheese Bowl"), a [potion of vigor](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Vigor "Skyrim:Potion of Vigor"), a [potion of vigorous magicka](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Vigorous_Magicka "Skyrim:Potion of Vigorous Magicka"), a [weak vigor poison](/wiki/Skyrim:Weak_Vigor_Poison "Skyrim:Weak Vigor Poison"), a [potion of vigorous healing](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Vigorous_Healing "Skyrim:Potion of Vigorous Healing"), a [potent lingering poison](/wiki/Skyrim:Potent_Lingering_Poison "Skyrim:Potent Lingering Poison"), a [fear poison](/wiki/Skyrim:Fear_Poison "Skyrim:Fear Poison"), and a [potion of brief invisibility](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Brief_Invisibility "Skyrim:Potion of Brief Invisibility"). On the stone shelf are two bowls of [fire salts](/wiki/Skyrim:Fire_Salts "Skyrim:Fire Salts"), two bowls of [glow dust](/wiki/Skyrim:Glow_Dust "Skyrim:Glow Dust"), a [torchbug thorax](/wiki/Skyrim:Torchbug_Thorax "Skyrim:Torchbug Thorax"), a bulb of garlic, two bowls of [frost salts](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Salts "Skyrim:Frost Salts"), a bunch of [tundra cotton](/wiki/Skyrim:Tundra_Cotton "Skyrim:Tundra Cotton"), a [draught of strength](/wiki/Skyrim:Draught_of_Strength "Skyrim:Draught of Strength"), a small mead barrel and two open crates, which contain two [swamp fungal pods](/wiki/Skyrim:Swamp_Fungal_Pod "Skyrim:Swamp Fungal Pod"), three rock warbler eggs, two [deathbell](/wiki/Skyrim:Deathbell "Skyrim:Deathbell") flowers, and three [nightshade](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightshade "Skyrim:Nightshade") flowers. There is an [apothecary's satchel](/wiki/Skyrim:Apothecary%27s_Satchel "Skyrim:Apothecary's Satchel") on the stool, and sitting by the hearth are a wooden bowl containing two [hagraven claws](/wiki/Skyrim:Hagraven_Claw "Skyrim:Hagraven Claw") and a cast iron pot holding a [taproot](/wiki/Skyrim:Taproot "Skyrim:Taproot"). Beside the hearth is a kettle containing a rack of dog meat, a cut of [horker meat](/wiki/Skyrim:Horker_Meat "Skyrim:Horker Meat"), and a potato.

#### Enchanting Room

Enchanting room

The right-hand room off of the main room still contains the single crate behind the door, but it now has a small cage hanging above it which contains several human skulls. Beside the crate is an empty soul gem stand, and to the right of the stand is a stone shelf with a copy of *[Magic from the Sky](/wiki/Skyrim:Magic_from_the_Sky "Skyrim:Magic from the Sky")*, a bowl of bone meal, three [soul gem fragments](/wiki/Skyrim:Soul_Gem_Fragment "Skyrim:Soul Gem Fragment"), a bowl containing an empty [lesser](/wiki/Skyrim:Lesser_Soul_Gem "Skyrim:Lesser Soul Gem") and filled [petty soul gem](/wiki/Skyrim:Petty_Soul_Gem "Skyrim:Petty Soul Gem"), and a copy of *[The Firmament](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Firmament "Skyrim:The Firmament")* which may have fallen to the floor. Above the stone shelf is a wall shelf holding a skull and an empty [grand soul gem](/wiki/Skyrim:Grand_Soul_Gem "Skyrim:Grand Soul Gem"). Above the shelf hangs a shield rack bearing an iron shield and crossed iron greatswords. To the side is a washbasin holding two bottles of wine.

Against the rear wall are two weapon plaques, one above the other, above which is a second shield plaque holding another pair of iron greatswords and an iron shield. A sets of shelves on the left holds an empty petty soul gem in a stand, three more soul gem fragments, an empty soul gem holder, a bowl containing an empty petty soul gem and filled [common soul gem](/wiki/Skyrim:Common_Soul_Gem "Skyrim:Common Soul Gem"), a set of [blue mage robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Blue_Mage_Robes "Skyrim:Blue Mage Robes") and a copy of the non-skill book version of *[Vernaccus and Bourlor](/wiki/Skyrim:Vernaccus_and_Bourlor "Skyrim:Vernaccus and Bourlor")*. An additional set of shelves on the right holds another empty petty soul gem, an animal skull, a soul gem fragment, and a [dragon's tongue](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon%27s_Tongue "Skyrim:Dragon's Tongue") flower in another soul gem holder. On the floor between them is an open crate containing a sample of ice wraith teeth and an animal skull. Against the wall to the right is an arcane enchanter with two weapon racks on either side. The left-hand rack holds an [iron sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Sword "Skyrim:Iron Sword"). Above the enchanter hangs another cage containing human skulls.

#### Entryway

A washbasin is added immediately on your left when entering, with two pairs of lit braziers on either side along the passage leading up to the main living room. There are planters between the first and second sets of braziers. The one on the left holds two gourds plants, a lavender bush, and a red mountain flower plant, while the one on the right holds two patches of jazbay grapes and a snowberry bush. At either end of both planters are metal plates; atop one sits a bowl containing five red mountain flowers and two bunches of jazbay grapes. Beside this is a basket holding two gourds. To the right there are a bowl containing two sprigs of snowberries and a basket holding a bunch of lavender, as well as a cabbage and a [goat cheese wheel](/wiki/Skyrim:Goat_Cheese_Wheel "Skyrim:Goat Cheese Wheel"). On a stone shelf by the doorway into the living room is an empty bowl that is added by the living room upgrade; opposite this sits an empty basket.

#### Children's Bedroom

Children's bedroom

When purchasing this upgrade, any items left in containers are found in the tall wardrobe after installing the children's bedroom. To the left of the fireplace is an empty, unlocked child's chest. To the right, coming out along the front of the fireplace, is a child's bed; a second bed sits along the rear wall. Between the beds is a tall wardrobe. To the right of the second bed is an end table with a copy of *[Olaf and the Dragon](/wiki/Skyrim:Olaf_and_the_Dragon "Skyrim:Olaf and the Dragon")* on top. Beside this is a child's practice dummy and on the far side is a small sideboard holding a [satchel](/wiki/Skyrim:Satchel "Skyrim:Satchel"), two red apples, a honey nut treat, and piece of bread; on a shelf underneath are a flute, a drum, and copies of *[Yellow Book of Riddles](/wiki/Skyrim:Yellow_Book_of_Riddles "Skyrim:Yellow Book of Riddles")*, *[An Explorer's Guide to Skyrim](/wiki/Skyrim:An_Explorer%27s_Guide_to_Skyrim "Skyrim:An Explorer's Guide to Skyrim")*, and *[A Children's Anuad](/wiki/Skyrim:A_Children%27s_Anuad "Skyrim:A Children's Anuad")*. In front is a flower basket containing two [chicken's eggs](/wiki/Skyrim:Chicken%27s_Egg "Skyrim:Chicken's Egg"), and a blue, a [purple](/wiki/Skyrim:Purple_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Purple Mountain Flower") and a red mountain flower.

Purchasing the upgrade to convert the alchemy lab to a children's bedroom is reversible as often as required, for repeat costs of 1000 [septims](/wiki/Skyrim:Gold "Skyrim:Gold") and 700 septims respectively. This does not reset the room or replace any loose items picked up previously, but any loose items remain on display once you revert the room's function. Any items left in any of the [containers](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers "Skyrim:Containers") in the alchemy lab room, when replacing the lab with the bedroom, will be found in the tall wardrobe. Any items left in any of the containers in the bedroom, when reverting the children's bedroom to an alchemy lab, will be found in the large mead barrel behind the door.

- [Adopted](/wiki/Skyrim:Adoption "Skyrim:Adoption") children living here will have unique dialogue comments about Markarth:
  - *I climbed all the way up to the [temple](/wiki/Skyrim:Temple_of_Dibella "Skyrim:Temple of Dibella"), but they said I wasn't old enough to get in. They're just mean.* (girls)
  - *[Banning](/wiki/Skyrim:Banning "Skyrim:Banning") said I could come play with his [dogs](/wiki/Skyrim:Dog "Skyrim:Dog"). I want a dog.* (girls)
  - *[Aicantar](/wiki/Skyrim:Aicantar "Skyrim:Aicantar") let me see his [museum](/wiki/Skyrim:Dwemer_Museum "Skyrim:Dwemer Museum"). It's really nice.* (boys)
  - *I hate Markarth. There's nowhere to play! It's all just rocks. Rocks rocks rocks.* (boys)
- If you ask your [spouse](/wiki/Skyrim:Marriage "Skyrim:Marriage") "How are the kids?" they will reply with one of the following, depending on their [voicetype](/wiki/Skyrim:Generic_Dialogue#Adoption_Dialogue "Skyrim:Generic Dialogue"):
  - *You tell me. Between the [Forsworn](/wiki/Skyrim:Forsworn "Skyrim:Forsworn") and the mines, Markarth is no place to raise a child.* (Arrogant)
  - *All right. I do worry, though, no matter how many times the guards say Markarth is safe.* (Neutral)
  - *Honestly, I'm a little worried. Markarth is nice enough, but... I wonder if it's really safe for children.* (Nord)
  - *Fine, fine. The city's not safe, but... that's good. Keeps a kid on edge, alert. Could keep 'em alive when they grow up.* (Orc)

## Notes

- Vlindrel Hall also [appears](/wiki/Online:Vlindrel_Hall "Online:Vlindrel Hall") in the [Markarth](/wiki/Online:Markarth_(DLC) "Online:Markarth (DLC)") DLC for [ESO](/wiki/Online:Online "Online:Online").
- If you clip through the front door while inside the house, then walk off the ledge and aim the camera down, you'll come across 5 merchant-style chests. None of them are considered owned, and their origins are unknown.

## Bugs

- There are a few bugs which affect all houses; see the [bugs](/wiki/Skyrim:Houses#Bugs "Skyrim:Houses") section of the Houses page for details and fixes.
- Filling the bookshelves with books and then quickly exiting the menu may result in the books disappearing forever.
  - The [Official Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim:Patch "Skyrim:Patch"), version 1.4, fixes this bug.
- A barrel in the building is placed underneath the floor and behind a wall, making it unsearchable.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 2.1.3, fixes this bug.
- After completing [The Forsworn Conspiracy](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Forsworn_Conspiracy "Skyrim:The Forsworn Conspiracy") and [No One Escapes Cidhna Mine](/wiki/Skyrim:No_One_Escapes_Cidhna_Mine "Skyrim:No One Escapes Cidhna Mine"), you may not be able to buy this house because there will be no dialogue option to do something for the Jarl of Markarth.
  - If you completed these two quests before you were level 20, there is still a way to buy the house. During the quest [Season Unending](/wiki/Skyrim:Season_Unending "Skyrim:Season Unending"), [General Tullius](/wiki/Skyrim:General_Tullius "Skyrim:General Tullius") and [Ulfric Stormcloak](/wiki/Skyrim:Ulfric_Stormcloak "Skyrim:Ulfric Stormcloak") will negotiate an exchange of territories, which will include [the Reach](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Reach "Skyrim:The Reach") unless you are a Stormcloak. If the Reach is handed over to or conquered by the [Stormcloaks](/wiki/Skyrim:Stormcloaks "Skyrim:Stormcloaks") during [Liberation of Skyrim](/wiki/Skyrim:Liberation_of_Skyrim "Skyrim:Liberation of Skyrim"), [Thongvor Silver-Blood](/wiki/Skyrim:Thongvor_Silver-Blood "Skyrim:Thongvor Silver-Blood") will become the [Jarl](/wiki/Skyrim:Jarl "Skyrim:Jarl") of Markarth. He will then give you the opportunity to become thane, and the subsequent option to purchase Vlindrel Hall. If you have joined the [Legion](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Legion "Skyrim:Imperial Legion") and completed [Reunification of Skyrim](/wiki/Skyrim:Reunification_of_Skyrim "Skyrim:Reunification of Skyrim") then this workaround is unavailable.
- Do not speak to Steward [Raerek](/wiki/Skyrim:Raerek "Skyrim:Raerek") unless you have at least 8,000 gold. Speaking to him and not buying the home immediately has caused many players to lose the option to buy Vlindrel Hall forever.
  -  This can be fixed by entering `setstage Favor250 10` in the console. The dialogue to become thane will reappear if you talk to Jarl Igmund, and once you are thane, the option to buy the house from Raerek will appear.
  -  If the above solution doesn't work, you can enter `setstage Favor250 15`, which will advance the quest to the "help x citizens" stage. Speak with the jarl to become thane and afterwards speak with Raerek. The option to buy the house should reappear.
  - This can also be fixed by doing another quest to help a citizen of Markarth if one is available.[*verification needed*]
- After purchasing the house, and gaining the quest to visit your new home, the quest marker may not point to Vlindrel Hall. **?**


---

# Skyrim:Volkihar Courtyard

## Overview

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| |  | | --- | | Worldspace: Volkihar Courtyard | | |
| Added by | [Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard") |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | 10 days |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| DLC1VampireCastleCourtyard | |
| Location | |
| East of [Volkihar Keep](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Keep "Skyrim:Volkihar Keep") | |

.jpg)

Volkihar Courtyard Passage (not repaired)

**Volkihar Courtyard** is the courtyard attached to [Castle Volkihar](/wiki/Skyrim:Castle_Volkihar "Skyrim:Castle Volkihar") and [Volkihar Keep](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Keep "Skyrim:Volkihar Keep").

.jpg)

Volkihar Courtyard Passage (repaired)

It is first encountered during the quest [Chasing Echoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Chasing_Echoes "Skyrim:Chasing Echoes"). After fighting your way through *[Volkihar Undercroft](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Undercroft "Skyrim:Volkihar Undercroft")*, you come across the courtyard, which then leads to *[Volkihar Ruins](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Ruins "Skyrim:Volkihar Ruins")*. You first come out of the undercroft along the southernmost wall, with the moondial directly in front of you in the center of the courtyard. Off to the west lies the ruined passage leading from the courtyard to the main body of the keep (which can be repaired if allied with the vampires; see [notes](#Notes)). There is a small pool of water to the northwest that contains the [Half Moon Crest](/wiki/Skyrim:Half_Moon_Crest "Skyrim:Half Moon Crest"), with five tufts of [spiky grass](/wiki/Skyrim:Spiky_Grass "Skyrim:Spiky Grass"), four [swamp fungal pods](/wiki/Skyrim:Swamp_Fungal_Pod "Skyrim:Swamp Fungal Pod"), and one patch of [blisterwort](/wiki/Skyrim:Blisterwort "Skyrim:Blisterwort") fungus growing around the edge. To the east is Valerica's garden, containing the [Full Moon Crest](/wiki/Skyrim:Full_Moon_Crest "Skyrim:Full Moon Crest"), as well as nine [nightshade](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightshade "Skyrim:Nightshade") plants; after Valerica's return, the garden will also contain all colors of mountain flowers, deathbells, dragon's tongues, and a snowberry. Finally, there are two ruined towers containing minor loot and more [skeletons](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeleton "Skyrim:Skeleton"), one to the north and one to the east (aptly named *[Volkihar North Tower](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_North_Tower "Skyrim:Volkihar North Tower")* and *[Volkihar East Tower](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_East_Tower "Skyrim:Volkihar East Tower")*). Up the stairs to the right of the entrance to the north tower is the [Crescent Moon Crest](/wiki/Skyrim:Crescent_Moon_Crest "Skyrim:Crescent Moon Crest").

## Related Quests

- **[Chasing Echoes](/wiki/Skyrim:Chasing_Echoes "Skyrim:Chasing Echoes")**: Explore [Castle Volkihar](/wiki/Skyrim:Castle_Volkihar "Skyrim:Castle Volkihar").

## Notes

- If you complete the quest [Kindred Judgment](/wiki/Skyrim:Kindred_Judgment "Skyrim:Kindred Judgment") for the Volkihar, you can repair the passage from [Volkihar Keep](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Keep "Skyrim:Volkihar Keep") to the courtyard by speaking to [Garan Marethi](/wiki/Skyrim:Garan_Marethi "Skyrim:Garan Marethi"). The repair is free, and the passage will be fixed the next time you enter the keep. If the quest was completed for the Dawnguard, you must continue to use the undercroft to access the courtyard.
- If you return to the Soul Cairn after the Dawnguard questline is complete and inform [Valerica](/wiki/Skyrim:Valerica "Skyrim:Valerica") of Harkon's death, she will return to Castle Volkihar and replant her garden.

- The following ingredients are found in the courtyard:

:   | Before Valerica moves back | After Valerica moves back | |
    | --- | --- | --- |
    | - 3 [Blisterwort](/wiki/Skyrim:Blisterwort "Skyrim:Blisterwort") - 1 [Canis Root](/wiki/Skyrim:Canis_Root "Skyrim:Canis Root") - 1 [Deathbell](/wiki/Skyrim:Deathbell "Skyrim:Deathbell") - 24 [Hanging Moss](/wiki/Skyrim:Hanging_Moss "Skyrim:Hanging Moss") - 1 [Jazbay Grapes](/wiki/Skyrim:Jazbay_Grapes "Skyrim:Jazbay Grapes") - 1 [Namira's Rot](/wiki/Skyrim:Namira%27s_Rot "Skyrim:Namira's Rot") - 9 [Nightshade](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightshade "Skyrim:Nightshade") - 5 [Spiky Grass](/wiki/Skyrim:Grass_Pod "Skyrim:Grass Pod") - 4 [Swamp Fungal Pod](/wiki/Skyrim:Swamp_Fungal_Pod "Skyrim:Swamp Fungal Pod") | - 3 [Blisterwort](/wiki/Skyrim:Blisterwort "Skyrim:Blisterwort") - 2 [Blue Mountain Flower](/wiki/Skyrim:Blue_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Blue Mountain Flower") - 1 [Canis Root](/wiki/Skyrim:Canis_Root "Skyrim:Canis Root") - 3 [Deathbell](/wiki/Skyrim:Deathbell "Skyrim:Deathbell") - 3 [Dragon's Tongue](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon%27s_Tongue "Skyrim:Dragon's Tongue") - 22 [Hanging Moss](/wiki/Skyrim:Hanging_Moss "Skyrim:Hanging Moss") - 1 [Jazbay Grapes](/wiki/Skyrim:Jazbay_Grapes "Skyrim:Jazbay Grapes") - 2 [Juniper Berries](/wiki/Skyrim:Juniper_Berries "Skyrim:Juniper Berries") | - 1 [Namira's Rot](/wiki/Skyrim:Namira%27s_Rot "Skyrim:Namira's Rot") - 3 [Nightshade](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightshade "Skyrim:Nightshade") - 2 [Purple Mountain Flower](/wiki/Skyrim:Purple_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Purple Mountain Flower") - 1 [Red Mountain Flower](/wiki/Skyrim:Red_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Red Mountain Flower") - 5 [Spiky Grass](/wiki/Skyrim:Grass_Pod "Skyrim:Grass Pod") - 1 [Snowberries](/wiki/Skyrim:Snowberries "Skyrim:Snowberries") - 4 [Swamp Fungal Pod](/wiki/Skyrim:Swamp_Fungal_Pod "Skyrim:Swamp Fungal Pod") - 2 [Yellow Mountain Flower](/wiki/Skyrim:Yellow_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Yellow Mountain Flower")[DG](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard") |

## Bugs

- The walls surrounding the courtyard may become invisible upon entry.
  -  The [Unofficial Dawnguard Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Dawnguard_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Dawnguard Patch"), version 2.0.1, fixes this bug.
  - This can be fixed by setting the Object Fade slider in the game Display settings to roughly the middle.

# Skyrim:Volkihar Keep

## Overview

This article is about the area of Castle Volkihar used by the Volkihar vampire clan. For the dungeon area of the castle, see [Castle Volkihar](/wiki/Skyrim:Castle_Volkihar "Skyrim:Castle Volkihar").

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | [SR-mapicon-Fort.png](/wiki/File:SR-mapicon-Fort.png) | Castle: Volkihar Keep ([view on map](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=Skyrim&centeron=Castle+Volkihar+%28DG%29)) | | |
| Added by | [Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard") |
| # of Zones | 2 |
| [Clearable](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons#Clearing "Skyrim:Dungeons") | No |

| Occupants | |

| *[The Aetherium Wars](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Aetherium_Wars "Skyrim:The Aetherium Wars")* [Bone Hawk Amulet](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#Bone_Hawk_Amulet "Skyrim:Clothing") [Bone Hawk Ring](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#Bone_Hawk_Ring "Skyrim:Clothing") [Dragon Priest Dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Priest_Dagger "Skyrim:Dragon Priest Dagger") | |

Volkihar Keep

**Volkihar Keep** is a large [fortress](/wiki/Skyrim:Castles "Skyrim:Castles") on an island far northwest of [Solitude](/wiki/Skyrim:Solitude "Skyrim:Solitude") connected to [Castle Volkihar](/wiki/Skyrim:Castle_Volkihar "Skyrim:Castle Volkihar") which serves as the [Volkihar vampire clan](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Vampire_Clan "Skyrim:Volkihar Vampire Clan")'s home. It contains two zones: *[Volkihar Keep](#Volkihar_Keep)* and *[Volkihar Cathedral](#Volkihar_Cathedral)*.

Though you can travel to the island immediately upon installing the *[Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard")* [official add-on](/wiki/Skyrim:Add-ons "Skyrim:Add-ons"), the keep is initially inaccessible. You must work with the [Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard_(faction) "Skyrim:Dawnguard (faction)") vampire hunters for a short time in order to gain entry, even if you wish to side with the Volkihar vampire clan during the add-on's [main questline](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard_Quests#Main_Quests "Skyrim:Dawnguard Quests"). Once you locate [Serana](/wiki/Skyrim:Serana "Skyrim:Serana"), she tells you to take her here. After reaching the island, you will be able to gain entrance to the keep by crossing the bridge connecting to the island. On your first visit, she will stop you on the bridge to say a few words before the [watchman](/wiki/Skyrim:Watchman "Skyrim:Watchman") standing guard will open the gate to the keep.

Outside the keep, there is little of note besides the [bone hawks](/wiki/Skyrim:Bone_Hawk "Skyrim:Bone Hawk") circling the castle. Throughout the inside of the keep are a large number of fully stocked bookcases, as well as various poisonous flowers in planters scattered around. Some of the keep is destroyed, making certain areas inaccessible, though the path to the [courtyard](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Courtyard "Skyrim:Volkihar Courtyard") at the back end of the top floor may be repaired after completing *Dawnguard'*s main questline on the Volkihar side by talking to [Garan Marethi](/wiki/Skyrim:Garan_Marethi "Skyrim:Garan Marethi"). This repair is free of charge, and is completed the next time you enter the keep.
