#### Dawnguard Main Quests

- **[Bloodline](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloodline "Skyrim:Bloodline")**: Choose your side!
- **[Kindred Judgment](/wiki/Skyrim:Kindred_Judgment "Skyrim:Kindred Judgment")**: Confront [Lord Harkon](/wiki/Skyrim:Lord_Harkon "Skyrim:Lord Harkon").

### Vampire Quests

| Name | Quest Giver |
| --- | --- |
| **[Amulets of Night Power](/wiki/Skyrim:Amulets_of_Night_Power "Skyrim:Amulets of Night Power")**: Retrieve the Amulets of Night Power. ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant")) | [Feran Sadri](/wiki/Skyrim:Feran_Sadri "Skyrim:Feran Sadri") |
| **[Ancient Power](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Power "Skyrim:Ancient Power")**: Retrieve [vampire parts](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Vampire_Arm "Skyrim:Ancient Vampire Arm") from varied locations around Skyrim. ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant")) | [Feran Sadri](/wiki/Skyrim:Feran_Sadri "Skyrim:Feran Sadri") |
| **[Deceiving the Herd](/wiki/Skyrim:Deceiving_the_Herd "Skyrim:Deceiving the Herd")**: Frame the [Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard_(faction) "Skyrim:Dawnguard (faction)") for murder. ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant")) | [Feran Sadri](/wiki/Skyrim:Feran_Sadri "Skyrim:Feran Sadri") |
| **[Rings of Blood Magic](/wiki/Skyrim:Rings_of_Blood_Magic "Skyrim:Rings of Blood Magic")**: Retrieve the [Ring of the Erudite](/wiki/Skyrim:Ring_of_the_Erudite "Skyrim:Ring of the Erudite") and the [Ring of The Beast](/wiki/Skyrim:Ring_of_The_Beast "Skyrim:Ring of The Beast"). ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant")) | [Feran Sadri](/wiki/Skyrim:Feran_Sadri "Skyrim:Feran Sadri") |
| **[Culling the Beast](/wiki/Skyrim:Culling_the_Beast "Skyrim:Culling the Beast")**: Kill the thin-blooded [vampires](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire "Skyrim:Vampire"). ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant")) | [Fura Bloodmouth](/wiki/Skyrim:Fura_Bloodmouth "Skyrim:Fura Bloodmouth") or [Garan Marethi](/wiki/Skyrim:Garan_Marethi "Skyrim:Garan Marethi") |
| **[Destroying the Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Destroying_the_Dawnguard "Skyrim:Destroying the Dawnguard")**: Kill the [Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard_(faction) "Skyrim:Dawnguard (faction)") leaders. | [Fura Bloodmouth](/wiki/Skyrim:Fura_Bloodmouth "Skyrim:Fura Bloodmouth") or [Garan Marethi](/wiki/Skyrim:Garan_Marethi "Skyrim:Garan Marethi") |
| **[The Hunt](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Hunt "Skyrim:The Hunt")**: Kill the [Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard_(faction) "Skyrim:Dawnguard (faction)") spy masquerading as a citizen. ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant")) | [Fura Bloodmouth](/wiki/Skyrim:Fura_Bloodmouth "Skyrim:Fura Bloodmouth") or [Garan Marethi](/wiki/Skyrim:Garan_Marethi "Skyrim:Garan Marethi") |
| **[Protecting the Bloodline](/wiki/Skyrim:Protecting_the_Bloodline "Skyrim:Protecting the Bloodline")**: Destroy the [vampire](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire "Skyrim:Vampire") before it turns its allies. ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant")) | [Fura Bloodmouth](/wiki/Skyrim:Fura_Bloodmouth "Skyrim:Fura Bloodmouth") or [Garan Marethi](/wiki/Skyrim:Garan_Marethi "Skyrim:Garan Marethi") |
| **[The Gift](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Gift "Skyrim:The Gift")**: Turn your [spouse](/wiki/Skyrim:Marriage "Skyrim:Marriage") into a [creature of the night](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire "Skyrim:Vampire"). | [Vingalmo](/wiki/Skyrim:Vingalmo "Skyrim:Vingalmo") |
| **[New Allegiances](/wiki/Skyrim:New_Allegiances "Skyrim:New Allegiances")**: Turn the target into a [vampire](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire "Skyrim:Vampire"). ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant")) | [Vingalmo](/wiki/Skyrim:Vingalmo "Skyrim:Vingalmo") |

### Volkihar Keep

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Map of Volkihar Keep](/wiki/File:SR-map-Volkihar_Castle_Interior.jpg "Map of Volkihar Keep") [Icewater Jetty](/wiki/Skyrim:Icewater_Jetty "Icewater Jetty")  [A](/wiki/Skyrim:Icewater_Jetty "Skyrim:Icewater Jetty")  [Volkihar Cathedral](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Keep#Volkihar_Cathedral "Volkihar Cathedral")  [B](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Keep#Volkihar_Cathedral "Skyrim:Volkihar Keep")  [Volkihar Courtyard](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Courtyard "Volkihar Courtyard")  [C](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Courtyard "Skyrim:Volkihar Courtyard")  [Dining Hall](/wiki/File:White_pog.png "Dining Hall")  1  [Smith](/wiki/File:White_pog.png "Smith")  2  [Chalice Room](/wiki/File:White_pog.png "Chalice Room")  3  [Alchemy Lab](/wiki/File:White_pog.png "Alchemy Lab")  4  [Coffin Room](/wiki/File:White_pog.png "Coffin Room")  5  [Lord Harkon's Quarters](/wiki/File:White_pog.png "Lord Harkon's Quarters")  6  [Serana's Room](/wiki/File:White_pog.png "Serana's Room")  7  [Kitchen](/wiki/File:White_pog.png "Kitchen")  8  [Thrall Pen](/wiki/File:White_pog.png "Thrall Pen")  9  Map of Volkihar Keep |  | Doors A: Castle Exterior  B: [Volkihar Cathedral](#Volkihar_Cathedral)  C: [Volkihar Courtyard](/wiki/Skyrim:Volkihar_Courtyard "Skyrim:Volkihar Courtyard") (only after repair) Rooms  1. Dining Hall 2. Smith 3. Chalice Room 4. Lab 5. Coffin Room 6. Lord Harkon's Quarters 7. Serana's Room 8. Kitchen 9. Thrall Pen |

**Dining Hall**: This is generally where most NPCs can be found if they aren't in their usual locations. The only items of note are three [Potions of Blood](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Blood "Skyrim:Potion of Blood"), one on each table, and nine portions of [human flesh](/wiki/Skyrim:Human_Flesh "Skyrim:Human Flesh"). As with all items in the keep, these will respawn after you spend ten days outside the castle. Some [Vampire Cattle](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Cattle "Skyrim:Vampire Cattle") can be seen here serving drinks. [Platters](/wiki/Skyrim:Platter "Skyrim:Platter") and a [nightshade](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightshade "Skyrim:Nightshade") planter can also be found here.

**Smith**: [Fura Bloodmouth](/wiki/Skyrim:Fura_Bloodmouth "Skyrim:Fura Bloodmouth") and [Hestla](/wiki/Skyrim:Hestla "Skyrim:Hestla") can usually be found here, the former attacking the dummies, and the latter working in the smithing area. The room features the usual smithing amenities, including a [grindstone](/wiki/Skyrim:Grindstone "Skyrim:Grindstone"), [workbench](/wiki/Skyrim:Workbench "Skyrim:Workbench"), [tanning rack](/wiki/Skyrim:Tanning_Rack "Skyrim:Tanning Rack"), and [forge](/wiki/Skyrim:Forge "Skyrim:Forge"), though the area lacks a [smelter](/wiki/Skyrim:Smelting "Skyrim:Smelting"). The four weapon racks around the room each contain one leveled weapon. The forge area itself contains a few [iron](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron "Skyrim:Iron") ingots, [steel](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel "Skyrim:Steel") ingots, [gold](/wiki/Skyrim:Gold "Skyrim:Gold") ore, a copy of *[The Aetherium Wars](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Aetherium_Wars "Skyrim:The Aetherium Wars")*, two weapon plaques above the forge (one with an [iron warhammer](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Warhammer "Skyrim:Iron Warhammer"), the other with a leveled [sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Sword "Skyrim:Sword")), and miscellaneous smithing items. At the southern end is a small archery range with two [Imperial bows](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Bow "Skyrim:Imperial Bow") mounted behind the stand and various [arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Arrow "Skyrim:Arrow") lying around. There are two coffin areas, one in the northeast and one in the southwest, both of which contain minor loot. You can also find a white [blacksmith's apron](/wiki/Skyrim:Blacksmith%27s_Apron "Skyrim:Blacksmith's Apron") here.

**Chalice Room**: This room holds the [Bloodstone Chalice](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloodstone_Chalice "Skyrim:Bloodstone Chalice"), which, if you drink from it as a [vampire](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire "Skyrim:Vampire"), will allow your [Vampiric Drain](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampiric_Drain "Skyrim:Vampiric Drain") spell to also absorb magicka and stamina for the duration of the effect (which is lengthened every time you bring an [Ancient Vampire body part](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Vampire_Arm "Skyrim:Ancient Vampire Arm") back to [Feran Sadri](/wiki/Skyrim:Feran_Sadri "Skyrim:Feran Sadri") in [Ancient Power](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Power "Skyrim:Ancient Power")). In addition, two bottles of [skooma](/wiki/Skyrim:Skooma "Skyrim:Skooma"), a bottle of [spiced wine](/wiki/Skyrim:Spiced_Wine "Skyrim:Spiced Wine"), and a bowl of [moon sugar](/wiki/Skyrim:Moon_Sugar "Skyrim:Moon Sugar") are on a wardrobe, a [lute](/wiki/Skyrim:Lute "Skyrim:Lute") is in between the bookcases, an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger") is on a table, and a pair of [vampire boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Boots "Skyrim:Vampire Boots") is on the floor, next to a chair.

**Lab**: [Feran Sadri](/wiki/Skyrim:Feran_Sadri "Skyrim:Feran Sadri") and [Ronthil](/wiki/Skyrim:Ronthil "Skyrim:Ronthil") can both usually be found here working on the first floor, and [Garan Marethi](/wiki/Skyrim:Garan_Marethi "Skyrim:Garan Marethi") can be found on the second. [Lord Harkon](/wiki/Skyrim:Lord_Harkon "Skyrim:Lord Harkon") frequents the first floor as well. The first floor holds an [alchemy lab](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemy_Lab "Skyrim:Alchemy Lab"), two locked display cases containing alchemy ingredients including a [Daedra heart](/wiki/Skyrim:Daedra_Heart "Skyrim:Daedra Heart"), one locked display case containing poisons and a [Frenzy](/wiki/Skyrim:Frenzy_(effect) "Skyrim:Frenzy (effect)") poison recipe, and a set of shelves containing more ingredients. A dead [alchemist](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemist "Skyrim:Alchemist") can be found on the table in the first floor. Ronthil can sometimes be found sleeping on the floor behind the angled bookcase in the southeast corner. The second floor holds an [arcane enchanter](/wiki/Skyrim:Arcane_Enchanter "Skyrim:Arcane Enchanter"), a locked display case containing a black [soul gem](/wiki/Skyrim:Soul_Gems "Skyrim:Soul Gems"), and another locked display case containing one of each soul gem except grand and an [Elven Dagger of Binding](/wiki/Skyrim:Elven_Dagger_of_Binding "Skyrim:Elven Dagger of Binding"). On the balcony overlooking the dining hall, another copy of *The Aetherium Wars* is on a table, while a set of [vampire armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Armor "Skyrim:Vampire Armor") and a pair of vampire boots are on a shelf just behind the balcony. Near the shelf is a coffin that Garan Marethi frequently uses.

**Coffin Room**: West of the lab along the stairwell connecting the first and second floors is a small room containing eight coffins, with two weapon racks against the east wall, and two wardrobes and a table holding some clothing and a locked display case on the west wall. The clothing includes four sets of vampire armor (two black, one light gray, and one red) and two pairs of vampire boots, and the display case contains two regular unenchanted amulets and a [Bone Hawk amulet](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#Bone_Hawk_Amulet "Skyrim:Clothing"). This room serves as the sleeping quarters for most of the less important vampires of the keep and those who work in the alchemy section of the lab.

**Lord Harkon's Quarters**: Frequented by Lord Harkon, the torture room contains a bloody rack, a throne and a chair facing the fire, and a large empty cage near the chairs. Strangely, a [Mythic Dawn](/wiki/Oblivion:Mythic_Dawn "Oblivion:Mythic Dawn") tapestry hangs above the torture rack. A [werewolf pelt](/wiki/Skyrim:Werewolf_Pelt "Skyrim:Werewolf Pelt") is in the unlocked display case to the immediate right of the entrance, and various poisons, [embalming tools](/wiki/Skyrim:Embalming_Tool "Skyrim:Embalming Tool"), and a [woodcutter's axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Woodcutter%27s_Axe "Skyrim:Woodcutter's Axe") are on the table between the case and the rack. On a high wall shelf above the display case and bookcases are two samples each of [deathbell](/wiki/Skyrim:Deathbell "Skyrim:Deathbell") and nightshade. Up the stairs to the north, four locked display cases on the west wall contain, from left to right: a Bone Hawk amulet, a [Bone Hawk ring](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#Bone_Hawk_Ring "Skyrim:Clothing"), and a diamond in the first, an enchanted necklace in the second, a leveled scroll in the third, and four [human hearts](/wiki/Skyrim:Human_Heart "Skyrim:Human Heart") in the last. Two common soul gems, a Potion of Blood, a scroll, and an unlocked display case containing a [Dragon Priest dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Priest_Dagger "Skyrim:Dragon Priest Dagger") are opposite the first four cases to the east. A long chest under the table to the right of the unlocked display case is safe for storage. Past the door to the north is Lord Harkon's personal coffin room, containing a coffin, a leveled weapon on the rack to the left of the coffin, and a plate to the right of the coffin holding gold and gems.

**Serana's Room**: This room was originally owned by Serana, and is derelict until the completion of the quest [The Bloodstone Chalice](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Bloodstone_Chalice "Skyrim:The Bloodstone Chalice"). No one seems to enter it at any point, including Serana herself, possibly because her bed is erroneously marked as belonging to [Valerica](/wiki/Skyrim:Valerica "Skyrim:Valerica"). An arcane enchanter and an alchemy lab are in the southwest corner, with a few potions and soul gems around them. In the northwest part of the room is a small closet containing a wardrobe and two locked display cases, with one containing two unenchanted necklaces, and the other containing an enchanted dagger and an unenchanted ring. Also scattered around the room are various planters and pots holding assorted flora, as well as minor loot around the two nightstands flanking the bed.

**Kitchen**: After passing through a small corridor northeast of the dining hall with kegs of dripping blood, you will find the kitchen. There is little of note here aside from minor items, a blooding rack, a dead [Vampire Cattle](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Cattle "Skyrim:Vampire Cattle") on a table, a small closet with a hanging [elves ear](/wiki/Skyrim:Elves_Ear "Skyrim:Elves Ear") plant, and a coffin usually used by [Rargal Thrallmaster](/wiki/Skyrim:Rargal_Thrallmaster "Skyrim:Rargal Thrallmaster"). On the east side of the room near the coffin are three sacks; the one to the left is not safe for storage, but the other two are.

**Thrall Pen**: North of the kitchen is the thrall pen. Before going down the stairs, you can find the uncommon [tavern clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Tavern_Clothes "Skyrim:Tavern Clothes") on a wardrobe to the left, along with various other low-value apparel. Down the stairs, you'll find two large cages, each with three [Vampire Cattle](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Cattle "Skyrim:Vampire Cattle") inside. These Cattle have no dialogue options; when they are selected, you have the option of feeding on them with no adverse side effects.

### Volkihar Cathedral

Shrine to Molag Bal

The cathedral is a room attached to the northwest of the keep, unused aside from the related quests. An unusable shrine to [Molag Bal](/wiki/Lore:Molag_Bal "Lore:Molag Bal") is at the apex of the room to the west, with several short sets of stairs leading to raised platforms and small connected balconies on the north and south sides. Lord Harkon brings you here at the end of the quest [Bloodline](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloodline "Skyrim:Bloodline") if you choose to become a [Vampire Lord](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Lord "Skyrim:Vampire Lord"), at which time a [Vampire Cattle](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Cattle "Skyrim:Vampire Cattle") stands near the door. The final battle also occurs here during the quest [Kindred Judgment](/wiki/Skyrim:Kindred_Judgment "Skyrim:Kindred Judgment"). It is generally quite bare, with a chest by the door containing minor leveled loot appearing during Kindred Judgement.

Two versions of the cathedral exist; one is actually part of the same cell as the rest of the keep, while the other is a separate cell that is used for the battle during Kindred Judgment. The door leading to the separate cell is positioned in front of the regular door and permanently remains in place after the quest, rendering the regular cathedral inaccessible through legitimate means.

## Notes

- Although technically in [Haafingar](/wiki/Skyrim:Haafingar "Skyrim:Haafingar"), anything you steal from the keep doesn't count towards completing a [bedlam job](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Bedlam_Job "Skyrim:The Bedlam Job") for [Delvin Mallory](/wiki/Skyrim:Delvin_Mallory "Skyrim:Delvin Mallory") in [Solitude](/wiki/Skyrim:Solitude "Skyrim:Solitude").
- All wardrobes and bookcases within the keep are safe for storage. Unless otherwise noted, other containers respawn after ten days along with the rest of the keep.
- With 38 of the 41 containers being safe for storage, all regular [crafting](/wiki/Skyrim:Activities#Crafting "Skyrim:Activities") amenities except a smelter, and three [merchants](/wiki/Skyrim:Merchants "Skyrim:Merchants") available within the keep, this location can be ideal for use as a home if you side with the Volkihar vampire clan.
- The fact that the inside of the keep will respawn every ten days can be especially beneficial to vampire characters who did not side with the Volkihar, as it allows access to four respawning Potions of Blood, with one on each table in the main dining room and another in Lord Harkon's quarters. However, these potions will have to be stolen.
- If you choose to become a vampire in Bloodline and [survival mode](/wiki/Skyrim:Survival_Mode "Skyrim:Survival Mode") is active, you'll be able to keep your [Hunger](/wiki/Skyrim:Hunger "Skyrim:Hunger") under control by feeding on the Vampire Cattle. Potions of Blood can also be used to restore Hunger with version 1.6 of the [Official Skyrim Special Edition Patch](/wiki/Skyrim:Special_Edition_Patch "Skyrim:Special Edition Patch").
- You can find a [Mythic Dawn](/wiki/Lore:Mythic_Dawn "Lore:Mythic Dawn") Banner in Lord Harkon's room.
- Serana's room will only be furnished after completing the quest The Bloodstone Chalice, though she never stays or sleeps there.
- If you lose your [vampirism](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampirism "Skyrim:Vampirism") after siding with the Volkihar vampires in the Dawnguard questline, NPCs in the keep will refuse to speak to you until you have Lord Harkon or Serana change you back. However, you are not required to become a Vampire Lord again; being a regular vampire will work. All NPCs will behave the same regardless of your vampire type.
- The inhabitants here can be seen eating what looks like bloody meat, instead of the [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread") that normal NPC's eat throughout the game.
- The first time you enter the thrall pen, you will hear the same dungeon music that plays when you discover [Nordic ruins](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Ruins "Skyrim:Nordic Ruins"), [Dwemer ruins](/wiki/Skyrim:Dwarven_Ruins "Skyrim:Dwarven Ruins"), [caves](/wiki/Skyrim:Caves "Skyrim:Caves"), and [mines](/wiki/Skyrim:Mines "Skyrim:Mines").
- If you [cure yourself](/wiki/Skyrim:Rising_at_Dawn "Skyrim:Rising at Dawn") of vampirism or if you parade around the castle in [Vampire Lord](/wiki/Skyrim:Vampire_Lord "Skyrim:Vampire Lord") form, a good majority of the residents here will comment about it.
- If you clip through the stone pillar in the smithing room and turn around, you'll come across 4 chests. Three of them are owned by the castle merchants. The fourth isn't, though it is empty.
- If you have allied with the Volkihar vampire clan, [Dawnguard scouts](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard_Scout "Skyrim:Dawnguard Scout") will occasionally appear near the fast travel arrival point to the castle.
- If you have allied with the Dawnguard, all the bodies of named NPCs (except Lord Harkon) killed during the quest Kindred Judgment will never disappear.
- If the portcullis is closed, you can glitch through it by holding a wooden plate or platter against it then running against the plate or using the [Whirlwind Sprint](/wiki/Skyrim:Whirlwind_Sprint "Skyrim:Whirlwind Sprint") [shout](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Shouts "Skyrim:Dragon Shouts") until you clip through the portcullis. Alternatively, you can hold the plate against the diagonal walls surrounding the portcullis and sprint your way across. In this case, the vampires inside will be friendly and treat you as a vampire, and all items in the keep will be free to take, allowing you to potentially stock up on materials before proceeding with the Dawnguard questline.[*verification needed — see [talk page](https://en.uesp.net/wiki/Skyrim_talk:Volkihar_Keep)*] If you glitch your way in before "Bloodline" is active, you can take almost everything from the keep freely, though there is a chance the vampires may act as if you hadn't spoken to Lord Harkon yet. If that is the case, you won't be able to ask Feran Sadri, Hestla, or Ronthil what they have for sale, nor will you be able to ask anyone for training. You might also be able to interact with the vampires if you glitch in during [A New Order](/wiki/Skyrim:A_New_Order "Skyrim:A New Order"), though the items in the castle will have to be stolen.
- If you have not allied with the Volkihar, the Bloodstone Chalice still has the option to be interacted with, even though you can't. It still cannot be interacted with if you side with the Dawnguard and then become a Vampire Lord later on, though the hover text indicates otherwise.
- You cannot invest with any of the merchants here, and none of them benefit from the [Master Trader](/wiki/Skyrim:Master_Trader "Skyrim:Master Trader") perk.
- All of the cattle in the slave pens will vanish without a trace once the Dawnguard are joined and Kindred Judgment is started.
- If you take up Harkon's offer of the Vampire Lord, you will wake up in the version of the cathedral that is seen when you fight him during Kindred Judgment (the boss chest is there in the corner), but the next time you visit, the "normal" version of the cathedral is seen, instead (there is no boss chest).
  - Because the cathedral has the two separate versions mentioned above, you should avoid dropping anything in there, as they may disappear.
- If you finish the *Dawnguard* questline for the Vampires, you will be the new owner of the castle, and will now be able to take everything for free without being flagged for theft.
- If you finish the *Dawnguard* questline for the Dawnguard, everything in the castle will still be treated as owned, and taking them will still count as theft. However, you can remove the stolen tags by either placing them in the boss chest in the cathedral and ordering a follower to retrieve them from it, or by placing them into the cupboard behind the counter at the [Riverwood Trader](/wiki/Skyrim:Riverwood_Trader "Skyrim:Riverwood Trader") and purchasing them from [Lucan](/wiki/Skyrim:Lucan_Valerius "Skyrim:Lucan Valerius").

## Bugs

- Although the keep is located within [Haafingar](/wiki/Skyrim:Haafingar "Skyrim:Haafingar"), crimes committed here may earn a bounty in [Whiterun Hold](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun_Hold "Skyrim:Whiterun Hold").
  - This behavior also means that even though the castle is in Haafingar, any Bedlam quests from the Thieves Guild to be done in Haafingar also will not work, as the castle is part of Whiterun.
- If you discover the castle before starting [Bloodline](/wiki/Skyrim:Bloodline "Skyrim:Bloodline"), the [watchman](/wiki/Skyrim:Watchman "Skyrim:Watchman") may not notice you with Serana, and the portcullis may not open.
  - Use any of the plate tricks mentioned above, and enter. The Dawnguard questline should continue normally from there.
  - Wait until Bloodline is active before discovering the castle. The watchman should recognize Serana properly and open the portcullis.
- Both of the death hound followers have the same stats and level as a normal death hound instead of the usual 10-25 level range on other [dog](/wiki/Skyrim:Dog "Skyrim:Dog") followers and are therefore quite weak in comparison.
  -  The [Unofficial Dawnguard Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Dawnguard_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Dawnguard Patch"), version 1.1.4, fixes this bug.
- The bodies of the vampires here are never cleaned up once Kindred Judgment is completed on the Dawnguard side.
  -  The [Unofficial Dawnguard Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Dawnguard_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Dawnguard Patch"), version 2.0.0, fixes this bug.
- The blood fountain in the Cathedral doesn't make any sound of flowing fluid.
  -  The [Unofficial Dawnguard Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Dawnguard_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Dawnguard Patch"), version 2.0.3, addresses this issue. A sound marker for the fountain has been added to resolve this.
- Hello topics for the occupants here may put generic ones before their quest-related ones, resulting in them being blocked.
  -  The [Unofficial Dawnguard Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Dawnguard_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Dawnguard Patch"), version 2.1.3, fixes this bug.
- If you remove the Dragon Priest Dagger from the display case in Lord Harkon's room, you may not be able to put anything in there. **?**

# Skyrim:Volskygge (quest)

## Overview

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Climb your way to the [evil](/wiki/Skyrim:Volsung_(creature) "Skyrim:Volsung (creature)") of [Volskygge](/wiki/Skyrim:Volskygge "Skyrim:Volskygge").** | |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Location(s): | [Volskygge](/wiki/Skyrim:Volskygge "Skyrim:Volskygge") |
| Reward: | [Volsung](/wiki/Skyrim:Volsung_(item) "Skyrim:Volsung (item)"), Word for the [Whirlwind Sprint](/wiki/Skyrim:Whirlwind_Sprint "Skyrim:Whirlwind Sprint") shout |
| ID: | dunVolskyggeQST |
| Suggested Level: | 24 |

The evil of [Volskygge](/wiki/Skyrim:Volskygge "Skyrim:Volskygge")

## Quick Walkthrough

**Note**: This quest does not appear in your journal.

1. Locate [Volskygge](/wiki/Skyrim:Volskygge "Skyrim:Volskygge").
2. Fight your way through the ruin.
3. Defeat the [dragon priest](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Priest "Skyrim:Dragon Priest") [Volsung](/wiki/Skyrim:Volsung_(creature) "Skyrim:Volsung (creature)").

### A Wall, Black as Night

*"It is a cursed place...!"*

When you visit [Solitude](/wiki/Skyrim:Solitude "Skyrim:Solitude"), the local [guards](/wiki/Skyrim:Solitude_Guard "Skyrim:Solitude Guard") may warn you about an ancient [Nordic ruin](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Ruins "Skyrim:Nordic Ruins") called [Volskygge](/wiki/Skyrim:Volskygge "Skyrim:Volskygge"), saying: *"If you must venture southwest, stay clear of Volskygge. It is a cursed place, a tomb where the dead still live...."* Talking to experienced traveler [Jawanan](/wiki/Skyrim:Jawanan "Skyrim:Jawanan") will net you even more information: *"Inside, there is said to be a wall, black as night. And it speaks! In the ancient Dragon tongue!"* If you heard about Volskygge from the guards, a map marker will be added; otherwise you will have to locate it yourself. The ruin lies far to the west of Solitude, past [Dragon Bridge](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Bridge "Skyrim:Dragon Bridge"), and just south of [Lost Echo Cave](/wiki/Skyrim:Lost_Echo_Cave "Skyrim:Lost Echo Cave"). Three leveled [bandits](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit "Skyrim:Bandit") guard its expansive exterior and [draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Draugr "Skyrim:Draugr") inhabit hidden, booby-trapped rooms, portending dangers to come. The ruin's large interior contains many locks and hosts myriad enemies, so carry a good supply of health potions and [lockpicks](/wiki/Skyrim:Lockpick "Skyrim:Lockpick") or the Skeleton Key before entering.

### Volskygge

Inside the ruin, pass through the first gate and into a throne room protected by a [dart trap](/wiki/Skyrim:Traps#Dart_Trap "Skyrim:Traps") and two bandits patrolling back and forth. Proceed westward through a tunnel, wary of a [pressure plate](/wiki/Skyrim:Traps#Pressure_Plate "Skyrim:Traps") that triggers a [spiked wall](/wiki/Skyrim:Traps#Spike_Wall "Skyrim:Traps") trap. Ahead stands another bandit near a locked chest that contains minor loot. The northern tunnel leads to an intersection; the path to the west from this leads to a room in which a [Nord](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord "Skyrim:Nord") and an [Orc](/wiki/Skyrim:Orc "Skyrim:Orc") bandit try to break into a cage (see [this section](#Bugs)).

Fighting the bandits here is optional, although unlocking the cage leads to a handle that will lower a nearby wall and provide access to a secret room with minor loot. You will find a copy of the [Lockpicking](/wiki/Skyrim:Lockpicking "Skyrim:Lockpicking") [skill book](/wiki/Skyrim:Skill_Books "Skyrim:Skill Books") *[Surfeit of Thieves](/wiki/Skyrim:Surfeit_of_Thieves "Skyrim:Surfeit of Thieves")* on the shelves near the secret wall. From here, backtrack to the intersection and continue east into a huge dining hall littered with dead [draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Draugr "Skyrim:Draugr") through which two more bandits wander. The rooms to the north contain little of interest, so leave the area through the eastern door, but tread carefully: the dead bandit you'll see on the floor was killed by a [battering ram](/wiki/Skyrim:Traps#Battering_Ram "Skyrim:Traps") triggered by a pressure plate just inside. Don't meet the same fate.

### Four Totems

*"All must be activated in order, if you wish to go home."*

The ruin's puzzle room is situated around the corner. A huge structure in the middle of the room points to four handles, each of which must be activated in the correct order. If you fail, undead enemies will break out of the central structure and attack. If done correctly, the torches in the middle of the room will light up as each lever is pulled in the correct order. Find the pedestal supporting the book *[The Four Totems of Volskygge](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Four_Totems_of_Volskygge "Skyrim:The Four Totems of Volskygge")* and read it for the clues to the puzzle:

:   *The first fears all, The second fears none.*
:   *The third eats what it can, Preferably number one.*
:   *The fourth fears the second, But only when alone.*
:   *All must be activated in order, If you wish to go home.*

The puzzle solution is:

:   **Snake** (fears all)
:   **Bear** (fears none)
:   **Fox** (eats the snake)
:   **Wolf** (fears the bear as an individual but not in packs)

If done correctly, the adjacent iron gate will open, providing access to another room containing a spiral staircase, a chest and a secret passage. The passage is opened via a lever on the left of the two pillars on the northern wall, leading to a dart trap and a few minor items. Turn back and take the spiral staircase down, which leads to the next zone of the ruin, Volskygge Passages.

### Volskygge Passages

The first few rooms of Volskygge Passages are empty, except for a [soul gem](/wiki/Skyrim:Soul_Gems "Skyrim:Soul Gems") on a pedestal and a handle that opens to a small room holding absolutely nothing (see [notes](#Notes)). In the third chamber, a leveled draugr lurks before an altar that holds two more soul gems and a [skeever tail](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeever_Tail "Skyrim:Skeever Tail"). There is also a chest with an apprentice locked [tension cable](/wiki/Skyrim:Traps#Tension_Cable "Skyrim:Traps") in the northeast corner of the room. After the battle, pull the handle next to the iron gate and head west, past the inactive trap door in the first chamber and a set of [swinging blades](/wiki/Skyrim:Traps#Swinging_Blades "Skyrim:Traps") triggered by a [pressure plate](/wiki/Skyrim:Traps#Pressure_Plate "Skyrim:Traps"). In the next chamber, beyond an active [trap door](/wiki/Skyrim:Traps#Trap_Door "Skyrim:Traps"), you will find a dead bandit near a handle which provides access to yet another secret chamber in which a [potion of ultimate healing](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Ultimate_Healing "Skyrim:Potion of Ultimate Healing") stands atop a [weight sensitive plate](/wiki/Skyrim:Traps#Weight_Sensitive_Plate "Skyrim:Traps"). An adept locked chest sits in the northwest corner of the room. The trap door near the dead bandit will drop you into a tower with a broken spiral staircase and a [spike pit](/wiki/Skyrim:Traps#Spike_Pit "Skyrim:Traps") at the bottom. A draugr patrols the passage beyond the spike pit, and the passage leads to the southeast corner of a large room. If you did not fall down the trap door, the passage heading west contains typical draugr catacombs with an [urn](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Urn "Skyrim:Containers") and inactive draugr on the shelves to be looted and enters the north side of the large room. Several draugr will emerge from their shelves in this room, and there is a pressure plate on the floor which triggers a spike wall. The passage to the south contains a [burial urn](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Burial_Urn "Skyrim:Containers") and an apprentice locked chest in the corner where the passage turns west. As you move forward, two more draugr will emerge. The passage will then turn north and enter a room. Two urns sit in front of the four stone pillars and impenetrable vines in the room's center. From this point, proceed with caution lest the five draugr in this room gang up on you. Up the northern stairs, a strong draugr patrols the passage. There are more urns and inactive draugr to loot in this passage along with another draugr sleeping on a shelf unless it is alerted by nearby combat. Continue north to find yet another draugr in the northern room where you will find more urns to loot and three healing potions on an altar. A pressure plate halfway up the western stairs triggers another battering ram, and another draugr waits in the passage beyond.

Past this draugr, webs become more prominent, the ruins become more overgrown, and three [frostbite spiders](/wiki/Skyrim:Frostbite_Spider "Skyrim:Frostbite Spider") wait. A couple of [web sacs](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Web_Sac "Skyrim:Containers") contain [spider eggs](/wiki/Skyrim:Spider_Egg "Skyrim:Spider Egg") and [dessicated corpses](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Dessicated_Corpse "Skyrim:Containers") may yield some gold or lockpicks. Beyond a web barrier, more spiders await along with [egg sacs](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Egg_Sac "Skyrim:Containers") containing more spider eggs and another web sac and dessicated corpse. Beyond the next web barrier is a room that looks even more like a natural cavern and that contains a waterfall feeding a stream. Another frostbite spider waits here, and three more will drop from the ceiling as you enter. There are also a few more dessicated corpses and another web sac containing a skeever tail. Destroy another web barrier to enter the last part of the ruin which is less overgrown where you will find more urns and a chest with an adept locked tension cable before the passage turns south. Head south to reach a room patrolled by two more draugr. There are a few more urns here, and halfway across the room, two stone bridges cross an indoor stream. Follow the stream to its western end to find an [orichalcum ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Orichalcum_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Orichalcum Ore Vein"), a [pickaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear#Pickaxe "Skyrim:Specialty Gear"), and an unlocked chest. At the southern end of the room, a wide staircase leads up to a platform where another draugr patrols. The room beyond contains a few urns and a staircase heading up and east where the boss draugr awaits in a room that contains a boss chest, two more urns, a few potions on a shelf, and a coin purse, a few loose coins, and an [ancient nord bow](/wiki/Skyrim:Weapons#Ancient_Nord_Bow "Skyrim:Weapons") on another shelf along with 12 [ancient nord arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Arrow "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Arrow"). The stairs heading farther up and east lead to a door that opens onto the external area [Volskygge Peak](/wiki/Skyrim:Volskygge#Volskygge_Peak "Skyrim:Volskygge").

### Volskygge Peak

You will now stand atop the mountain where you initially entered. There is one emerald on each side of the statue at the foot of the stairs. Head up the stairs and approach the [word wall](/wiki/Skyrim:Word_Wall "Skyrim:Word Wall") for one of the words for [Whirlwind Sprint](/wiki/Skyrim:Whirlwind_Sprint "Skyrim:Whirlwind Sprint"). As soon as you have learned the word, [Volsung](/wiki/Skyrim:Volsung_(creature) "Skyrim:Volsung (creature)"), a [dragon priest](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Priest "Skyrim:Dragon Priest") will burst from the sarcophagus behind you. A powerful foe, he uses only [frost attacks](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Damage "Skyrim:Frost Damage") in combat. After the battle, loot his remains for his unique [dragon priest mask](/wiki/Skyrim:Volsung_(item) "Skyrim:Volsung (item)").

## Notes

- This "quest" only controls the dialogue for the bandits and will automatically complete entirely within the first section (but see [bugs](#Bugs)). Killing Volsung is not actually part of the quest.
- It's possible to reach both Volsung and the word wall by simply running up the mountain.
- A [strange gem](/wiki/Skyrim:Strange_Gem "Skyrim:Strange Gem") was meant to be part of the Volskygge puzzle, but never made it into the final game.

## Bugs

- Some dialogue between the [bandits](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit "Skyrim:Bandit") was supposed to add a bit more spice to the story about Volskygge. However, the quest trigger was incorrectly placed in the dining hall just before the entrance to Volskygge Passages, so the dialogue will rarely be heard.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 1.3.2, fixes this bug.
- A second, similar bug also causes the dialogue to remain unheard.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Legendary Edition Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Legendary_Edition_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Legendary Edition Patch"), version 3.0.1, fixes this bug. ([details](https://afktrack.afkmods.com/index.php?a=issues&i=19846))
  - Sneak through Volskygge without killing anyone until you reach the spiral staircase just before Volskygge Passages, then make your way backwards to the entrance. The dialogue will be heard normally along the way.
  - Do Volskygge once, normally, and wait for it to reset. The second time through, the dialogue may be heard.[*verification needed — Not sure if this works, needs in-game verification.*]

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| | Speaker | Dialogue | | --- | --- | | Nord | *"This is getting ridiculous. If we can't get this open soon, the boss will have our heads."* | | *"I can't get it open."* | | Orc | *"You've been at it for five minutes. Here, let me try."* | | *"I think I almost got it."* | | *"Come on, just open."* | | | Speaker | Dialogue | | --- | --- | | Male | *"Find anything worthwhile yet?"* | | Female | *"Can't say that I have."* | | Male | *"Quiet! I think I heard something."* | | Female | *"This place has had you shaking in your armor ever since we arrived."* | | Male | *"I'm fine, I'm fine. Let's move on."* | |

- Sometimes the word wall won't activate, therefore Volsung won't wake up. **?**
  - Use `TCL` to "dive" into the wall where the emanation comes from until the word is activated.

## Quest Stages

- All of the quest stages are empty: 0, 10, 20.

Notes

- Any text displayed in angle brackets (e.g., `<Alias=LocationHold>`) is dynamically set by the Radiant Quest system, and will be filled in with the appropriate word(s) when seen in game.
- Not all Journal Entries may appear in your journal; which entries appear and which entries do not depends on the manner in which the quest is done.
- Stages are not always in order of progress. This is usually the case with quests that have multiple possible outcomes or quests where certain tasks may be done in any order. Some stages may therefore repeat objectives seen in other stages.
- If an entry is marked as "Finishes Quest" it means the quest disappears from the Active Quest list, but you may still receive new entries for that quest.
- On the PC, it is possible to use the [console](/wiki/Skyrim:Console "Skyrim:Console") to advance through the quest by entering `setstage dunVolskyggeQST stage`, where `stage` is the number of the stage you wish to complete. It is not possible to un-complete (i.e. go back) quest stages, but it is possible to clear all stages of the quest using `resetquest dunVolskyggeQST`.

# Skyrim:Volskygge

## Overview

|  |  |  |  |

Volskygge

**Volskygge** is a medium-sized [Nordic ruin](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Ruins "Skyrim:Nordic Ruins") west of [Solitude](/wiki/Skyrim:Solitude "Skyrim:Solitude") containing [bandits](/wiki/Skyrim:Bandit "Skyrim:Bandit"), [draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Draugr "Skyrim:Draugr"), [frostbite spiders](/wiki/Skyrim:Frostbite_Spider "Skyrim:Frostbite Spider"), and the [dragon priest](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Priest "Skyrim:Dragon Priest") [Volsung](/wiki/Skyrim:Volsung_(creature) "Skyrim:Volsung (creature)"). It contains two zones: *[Volskygge](#Volskygge)* and *[Volskygge Passages](#Volskygge_Passages)*.

Its [word wall](/wiki/Skyrim:Word_Wall "Skyrim:Word Wall") teaches part of the [Whirlwind Sprint](/wiki/Skyrim:Whirlwind_Sprint "Skyrim:Whirlwind Sprint") [dragon shout](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Shouts "Skyrim:Dragon Shouts").

## Related Quests

- **[Alteration Ritual Spell](/wiki/Skyrim:Alteration_Ritual_Spell "Skyrim:Alteration Ritual Spell")**: Acquire [Kahvozein's Fang](/wiki/Skyrim:Kahvozein%27s_Fang "Skyrim:Kahvozein's Fang") and use it to get [heartscales](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Heartscales "Skyrim:Dragon Heartscales") from a [dragon](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon "Skyrim:Dragon") for [Tolfdir](/wiki/Skyrim:Tolfdir "Skyrim:Tolfdir"). ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant"))
- **[Volskygge](/wiki/Skyrim:Volskygge_(quest) "Skyrim:Volskygge (quest)")**: Climb your way to the [evil](/wiki/Skyrim:Volsung_(creature) "Skyrim:Volsung (creature)") of Volskygge.

### Exterior

There are three bandits near the entrance, two of which are melee fighters, while the third is an archer. As you approach, there is a long flight of stairs leading down to a [pressure plate](/wiki/Skyrim:Pressure_Plate "Skyrim:Pressure Plate") at the bottom, which triggers a [battering ram](/wiki/Skyrim:Battering_Ram "Skyrim:Battering Ram") from a doorway on the left. Through the doorway is a room with two upright sarcophagi against the rear wall. The one on the right is already open and has a [dead draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Containers#Draugr "Skyrim:Containers") lying on the ground in front, but the one on the left conceals a slumbering leveled draugr. Between the sarcophagi is a doorway that is completely blocked by rubble. At either end of the room are stone tables; the one to the east has four [burial urns](/wiki/Skyrim:Burial_Urn "Skyrim:Burial Urn") on top, and the other is broken with a single burial urn among the remains. Stairs lead back up to ground level past the pressure plate outside. There are stairs leading up to walls, with a walkway linking them in front of the entrance. On a raised section to the left is a campfire with a [charred skeever](/wiki/Skyrim:Charred_Skeever_Hide "Skyrim:Charred Skeever Hide") cooking over it and a wooden table nearby with a medium [coin purse](/wiki/Skyrim:Coin_Purse "Skyrim:Coin Purse"), a [skeever tail](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeever_Tail "Skyrim:Skeever Tail"), and an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger") on top. The entrance is an iron door under a covered area.

The fast travel point will take you to a point a few paces in front of a stone hut to the east of the entrance, with a sunken path to the right of the hut that takes you to a round-topped barrow with a circular passage around a central chamber that has an adept-trapped iron door. Beyond the door is the circular chamber, with two sideboards to the left as you enter. The first has two burial urns on top, while the second is broken. Beside the second sideboard is one of two large [urns](/wiki/Skyrim:Urn "Skyrim:Urn") flanking an altar with some skeletal remains on top. Farther to the right is a set of shelves holding two random [potions of healing](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Minor_Healing "Skyrim:Potion of Minor Healing").

### Volskygge

The banqueting hall inside Volskygge

The entrance leads into an antechamber with stone benches along both side walls and another iron door ahead. This door leads into a large chamber, with banners on posts flanking a short flight of stairs leading up to a raised platform with a throne at the top. The raised platform is in front of you as you enter. In front of the throne is a pressure plate that triggers [poison darts](/wiki/Skyrim:Dart_Trap "Skyrim:Dart Trap"). To the west is a wide passage that descends to the north. Two bandits will emerge from this passage. There are roots descending from the ceiling and covering the floor of this passage, which turns west at the end. There are blood splatters on the floor ahead, followed by a pressure plate that triggers a [spike wall](/wiki/Skyrim:Spike_Wall "Skyrim:Spike Wall"). Past this trap are stairs leading down to an apprentice-locked [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") at the bottom, guarded by a leveled bandit. At this point, the passage turns north and descends further. At the bottom is a T-junction, with a doorway on the left, and the passage continuing to the right. The room through the doorway contains two leveled bandits hidden by a stone pillar. Against the west wall is a set of shelves holding a random potion of healing and a copy of the [Lockpicking](/wiki/Skyrim:Lockpicking "Skyrim:Lockpicking") [skill book](/wiki/Skyrim:Skill_Books "Skyrim:Skill Books") *[Surfeit of Thieves](/wiki/Skyrim:Surfeit_of_Thieves "Skyrim:Surfeit of Thieves")*. To the left is a concealed door that is opened using a lever locked behind an adept-locked cage door on the left. The door opens to reveal a tunnel descending to a small room containing an unlocked chest and a sideboard with two random [potions of magicka](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Minor_Magicka "Skyrim:Potion of Minor Magicka") on top.

The passage heads down, passing a set of shelves set back against the wall on the right with three random [potions of health](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Health "Skyrim:Potion of Health") atop them. Beyond is an entrance into a large banqueting hall, with two doorways along the hall's north wall. These lead into a narrow horseshoe-shaped corridor, with a room immediately on the left, and a leveled bandit patrolling between this and a second room farther along. In the second room is another bandit who will be alerted by any fighting and converge on you. The first room, to the west, contains several sets of shelves holding ancient crockery and four large urns. The second room, to the north with two doorways leading into it, contains more shelves of crockery and a central stone table and benches with two [potions of minor healing](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Minor_Healing "Skyrim:Potion of Minor Healing") on top. Continuing along the narrow corridor to the east, there is a third room containing nothing much of interest.

Back in the banqueting hall, there are sets of shelves along the north wall holding more clutter, two random potions of healing, and a random potion of health. Down the center of the room is a very long stone table with stone benches on either side and a throne at the far end. There is the body of a draugr on the floor, and another lying across part of the table. At the far end of the room is an iron door.

In the middle of the doorway is a pressure plate that triggers a battering ram, with a dead bandit lying beyond it. There are three large urns along the corridor beyond, which then turns north. Ahead is a chamber containing four upright sarcophagi in a square in the middle of the room, with a doorway to the north blocked by a lowered gate. In each of the corners of the room are raised sections, with plinths holding handles and symbols of one of four animals beneath them. In front of the exit is a fifth plinth with a copy of *[The Four Totems of Volskygge](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Four_Totems_of_Volskygge "Skyrim:The Four Totems of Volskygge")* on top. This book contains a riddle for the solution to raise the gate. The four handles must be operated in the correct order to raise the gate, which is **Snake—Bear—Fox—Wolf**. A fire will light atop the central pillar each time you pull the correct handle. The first four times an incorrect handle is pulled, one of the four sarcophagi will open. Three of the sarcophagi contain slumbering draugr, and the fourth contains skeletal remains and some loose low-level equipment.

The room beyond the gate contains a central spiral stairwell that descends to a passage heading west to an iron door to *Volskygge Passages*. There is also an unlocked chest at the back of the room, and a skeleton to the left in front of another hidden door. The lever to open it is to the left. The door opens to reveal another tunnel descending to a small room, with a pressure plate in front that triggers a poison dart trap. The room contains a sideboard with a random [potion of stamina](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Minor_Stamina "Skyrim:Potion of Minor Stamina") and two random [potions of resist cold](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Resist_Cold "Skyrim:Potion of Resist Cold") on top, and a large urn opposite.

### Volskygge Passages

A Draugr Death Overlord rising from his throne

This area contains several leveled draugr. It starts with a wide passage descending stairs heading west. At the bottom, the path turns north and passes four statues, then reaches an open door into a chamber with five large urns around the sides, an iron door opposite, and a random empty [soul gem](/wiki/Skyrim:Soul_Gems "Skyrim:Soul Gems") on a stand. Beside the stand is a lever that raises a gate to a tiny alcove that is empty. If you want to be sneaky, do not pull the lever where the soul gem is, as doing so will alert the draugr in the next room. Through the iron door is a room containing a magic-using draugr standing near an altar on a raised section with a [skeever tail](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeever_Tail "Skyrim:Skeever Tail") and two empty [petty soul gems](/wiki/Skyrim:Petty_Soul_Gem "Skyrim:Petty Soul Gem") on stands. Flanking the altar are two urns, and in the northeast corner is an apprentice-trapped chest. To the west is a doorway blocked by a gate; the lever to operate it is to the right.

Through the doorway is a small room with a large [trap door](/wiki/Skyrim:Trap_Door "Skyrim:Trap Door") in the floor with sharp stakes beneath it. There are two urns and two burial urns in the room, and a doorway to the west. Between the trap door and the doorway is a pressure plate that triggers a [swinging blade](/wiki/Skyrim:Swinging_Blades "Skyrim:Swinging Blades") trap in the doorway. The next room looks similar to the last, with a large trap door in the floor. Beneath this trap door are small broken sections of a wooden staircase above stakes. The trap door will probably open as you approach, but can be closed again using the screen prompt when it appears. Once closed, it can be safely walked on. If you choose to go down through the trap door, carefully descending the broken staircase will bring you to the end of a passage patrolled by a single draugr (see next paragraph); it is not possible to climb back up the broken staircase, but killing the lone draugr and climbing the stone stairs at the far end of the passage will lead you further into the ruin (see below). In the northwestern corner of the room with the trap door is a doorway, and in the north wall is a concealed door with a dead bandit in front under the lever that opens it, while to the right are two more urns. Behind the hidden door is a second with the lever on the right. Activating the second lever opens the second door and closes the first. Past the second lever is a room with some grating on the floor, two urns, an adept-locked chest at the rear, and a [weight sensitive](/wiki/Skyrim:Weight_Sensitive_Plate "Skyrim:Weight Sensitive Plate") plinth in the center with a leveled potion of healing on top. Taking the potion triggers [spears](/wiki/Skyrim:Spear_Trap "Skyrim:Spear Trap") to come up from the floor through the grating.

The doorway in the northwestern corner enters a catacomb area, where there is a large urn and several dead draugr and slumbering draugr in alcoves as you descend. There is a pressure plate at a crossroads that triggers a spike wall. To the west is an alcove area with three burial urns; a fourth burial urn is next to the spike wall. To the east is a narrow flight of stairs descending and passing through a small catacomb with a single draugr patrolling. At the end are the sharpened stakes underneath the second trap door over the broken staircase (see previous paragraph), with a dead bandit lying among the stakes. Climbing back to the crossroads and heading south leads to an apprentice-locked chest and a burial urn where the passage turns west. At the end, after passing a large urn on the left, turn north and descend to another area with even more leveled draugr in alcoves. There is a central area with many roots descending from the ceiling and pathways to either side. In front of the central area are two large urns, while a dead draugr is in an alcove on the western wall. To the north after the pathways merge are ascending stairs with a leveled draugr patrolling at the top. The area at the top of the stairs contains a large urn and three dead draugr in wall alcoves. To the north is a room patrolled by another leveled draugr, containing five large urns, an altar on a raised section opposite the door with three random potions of health, and an open sarcophagus at either end.

To the west is another long flight of stairs with a pressure plate near the top that triggers a battering ram. The passage enters a natural cave system with cobwebs everywhere, winding as it continues to rise. There are a number of frostbite spiders in this area, as well as several web sacs that can be looted. The passage eventually opens into a narrow cave, with several levels above you, many more spiders and egg sacs, and a [dessicated corpse](/wiki/Skyrim:Dessicated_Corpse "Skyrim:Dessicated Corpse") hanging from the ceiling. Keep following the passage as it twists and turns, passing thick cobwebs you have to burn or chop your way through, until you reach a brightly lit cavern with waterfalls dropping from above and two hanging dessicated corpses. Three frostbite spiders will descend from above.

At the far end, break through more cobwebs and follow the passage as it rises to the east. The passage soon reenters the ruin, where after climbing a flight of stairs you will find an adept-trapped chest and three large urns. The passage then turns south and descends to a large chamber with three bridges, one of which is broken, crossing a fast-moving stream. There are two draugr patrolling back and forth across the bridges, and a third standing at the top of stairs to the rear.

The chamber contains three large urns, while downstream to the west is an unlocked chest and a pickaxe on the ground next to an [orichalcum ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Orichalcum_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Orichalcum Ore Vein"). Across a bridge and up the stairs is another room with three large urns. A second flight of stairs to the east then ascends to a small room with a Draugr Death Overlord sitting on a throne opposite, who will arise when you enter. There are two large urns and a sideboard in the northwestern corner with three leveled potions: one of health, one of healing, and one of magicka. In the southwestern corner is an unlocked boss chest, and in the southeastern corner is a set of shelves holding an ancient Nord bow and quiver of arrows, a medium coin purse, and eight loose coins. Behind the throne to the east is a narrow flight of stairs leading up to an iron door and the exit to *Volskygge Peak*.

### Exterior: Volskygge Peak

Volskygge Peak

Once you exit *Volskygge Passages*, you find yourself atop a nearby mountain, at the base of a staircase. At the top of the staircase is a word wall that teaches part of the Whirlwind Sprint shout. Once you have learned the word, the sarcophagus at the top of the stairs will open, and the dragon priest Volsung will emerge. He is a powerful undead foe who uses strong frost magic and carries the dragon priest mask [Volsung](/wiki/Skyrim:Volsung_(item) "Skyrim:Volsung (item)"). The only other loot is a pair of emeralds, embedded in the eyes of the statue at the bottom of the staircase. Be wary of using area-of-effect spells or shouts (such as Fireball and Kyne's Peace) near the statue, as this can dislodge and scatter the emeralds across the snow. The emeralds may also have fallen from the eye sockets before you reach them (perhaps due to the activity of a battle or a dragon before you reach the immediate area, for example when you encounter the initial bandits before entering Volkskygge further down), in which case they will be on the snow near the adjacent statue.

## Notes

- It is possible to reach Volsung and the word wall by simply running up the mountain. Volsung will rise from his grave after you learn the word from the wall. You can then go through the dungeon from the exit until almost the start of the second zone, where a gate just beyond the trap door rooms only opens using a lever on the other side. You can open the gate by crouching, getting close to the gate on the left side, and using the lever through the gap. In this way, as also noted in the Prima Guide, Volskygge can be entered and explored from both the bottom and the top (near the word wall). It is therefore possible to play through the dungeon in the opposite direction from that described in the above walkthrough. (However, note that a follower may not appear if you pass through the ruin in this direction, as they may be stuck on the other side of the gate.)
- Scaling the peak to reach the word wall (versus going through the entire ruin) has caused confusion about the puzzle room. Returning from the direction of the peak and trying to solve the puzzles, they will be backwards compared to the walkthrough. The simple way to avoid such confusion is to explore the ruin from the main entrance and solve the puzzle as you go along.
- You may want to complete the puzzle incorrectly to start with, in order to loot the three draugr and the dead skeleton with loose armor and weapons. Alternatively, casting a rune spell next to one of the sarcophagi will cause them to burst open.
- There is no return shortcut from within the ruin, so the simplest methods to leave the area are to fast travel from the peak or descend the mountain to return to the entrance.
- The Draugr Death Overlord at the end (or beginning, depending on which door you used to enter the ruin) is present regardless of level, meaning a tough fight for low-level characters. However, victory is rewarded with a guaranteed one-handed ebony weapon, which would usually only be available elsewhere at level 30+.
- Volskygge also appears in [ESO](/wiki/Online:Online "Online:Online") as [Lendoran Ruin](/wiki/Online:Lendoran_Ruin "Online:Lendoran Ruin") and [Dragon's Belly](/wiki/Online:Dragon%27s_Belly "Online:Dragon's Belly").

## Bugs

- If you have completed the puzzle but fast-traveled from the mountains without killing Volsung, the puzzle gate may be closed when you return, with the handles locked in place, preventing you from reaching the summit.
- The swinging blade trap in *Volskygge Passages* can become stuck in the "open" position after being triggered, trapping followers behind it. Clicking on and disabling the trap with the console will free the follower. This may happen more often if you have more than one follower, such as one humanoid and one Riekling/dog/automaton. **?**
- A scene trigger is placed too far from where the scene plays, resulting in the dialogue rarely being heard.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 1.3.2, fixes this bug.

## Maps

- 

  Volskygge
- 

  Volskygge Passages

# Skyrim:Volunruud

## Overview

|  |  |  |  |

| [Dungeon](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons "Skyrim:Dungeons") | Yes |

Volunruud

**Volunruud** is a medium-sized [Nordic ruin](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Ruins "Skyrim:Nordic Ruins") southeast of [Morthal](/wiki/Skyrim:Morthal "Skyrim:Morthal") containing [draugr](/wiki/Skyrim:Draugr "Skyrim:Draugr") and the ghost of [Kvenel the Tongue](/wiki/Skyrim:Kvenel_the_Tongue "Skyrim:Kvenel the Tongue"). It contains two zones: *[Volunruud](#Volunruud)* and *[Volunruud Elder's Cairn](#Volunruud_Elder.27s_Cairn)*.

Its [word wall](/wiki/Skyrim:Word_Wall "Skyrim:Word Wall") teaches part of the [Aura Whisper](/wiki/Skyrim:Aura_Whisper "Skyrim:Aura Whisper") [dragon shout](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Shouts "Skyrim:Dragon Shouts"). The ruin is supposedly the final resting place of one "Tongue Chieftain Kvenel". Replicas of his two weapons are the key to his burial chamber. A man named [Heddic](/wiki/Skyrim:Heddic_Volunnar "Skyrim:Heddic Volunnar"), according to his *[notes](/wiki/Skyrim:Heddic%27s_Volunruud_Notes "Skyrim:Heddic's Volunruud Notes")*, attempted to find Kvenel's tomb, presumably for the treasure and historical significance. However, the explorer met his untimely end at the hands of the dead "living" in the ruin.

## Related Quests

- **[Hail Sithis!](/wiki/Skyrim:Hail_Sithis! "Skyrim:Hail Sithis!")**: One of the biggest [contracts](/wiki/Skyrim:Emperor_Titus_Mede_II_(real) "Skyrim:Emperor Titus Mede II (real)") in [Brotherhood](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Brotherhood "Skyrim:Dark Brotherhood") history is yours to fulfill.
- **[The Silence Has Been Broken](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Silence_Has_Been_Broken "Skyrim:The Silence Has Been Broken")**: Meet [Amaund Motierre](/wiki/Skyrim:Amaund_Motierre "Skyrim:Amaund Motierre") inside Volunruud and learn who he wants assassinated.
- **[Silenced Tongues](/wiki/Skyrim:Silenced_Tongues "Skyrim:Silenced Tongues")**: Unlock the burial chamber of the Tongue Chieftain [Kvenel](/wiki/Skyrim:Kvenel_the_Tongue "Skyrim:Kvenel the Tongue").

### Exterior

There are several large standing stones to the east of the barrow. An unlocked chest is in an alcove on the left side, and to its right is a [burial urn](/wiki/Skyrim:Burial_Urn "Skyrim:Burial Urn"). Farther to the right in another alcove are two more burial urns. Climb the side of the barrow and descend the curved stone stairs, marked at the top by an unlit candle on a stand. Under the top of the stairs is another unlocked chest, and to the right of the entrance in an alcove is another burial urn.

### Volunruud

Upon entering, there are stairs leading down to the north, with a landing a few steps down with the corpse of Heddic lying on the ground. To the left of the body is a throne with a slumbering [skeleton](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeleton "Skyrim:Skeleton") sitting in it. The skeleton will become aggressive if disturbed, so it is best to kill it in one hit. Beside Heddic's body on the ground are three loose gold [coins](/wiki/Skyrim:Gold "Skyrim:Gold"), an [iron dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"), an [iron helmet](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Helmet "Skyrim:Iron Helmet"), and *[Heddic's Volunruud Notes](/wiki/Skyrim:Heddic%27s_Volunruud_Notes "Skyrim:Heddic's Volunruud Notes")*. Reading these will initiate the quest [Silenced Tongues](/wiki/Skyrim:Silenced_Tongues "Skyrim:Silenced Tongues").

Following the stairs down past an [ancient Nord battle axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Battle_Axe "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Battle Axe"), you come to a room with four exits, located to the northeast, north, northwest, and southwest. Around the room is some rubble, an empty cart, a [pickaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Pickaxe "Skyrim:Pickaxe"), various human and animal bones, and a lit brazier to the east behind a rock pillar. In front of the exit to the southwest is an [iron ore vein](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Iron Ore Vein") on the floor. Ignore the path up the stairs to the north for now.

Meeting the client

Through the exit to the southwest is a short corridor with steps leading down into a small room full of vegetation, where a wooden door exits to the northwest. If you are here during the [Dark Brotherhood](/wiki/Skyrim:Dark_Brotherhood "Skyrim:Dark Brotherhood") quest [The Silence Has Been Broken](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Silence_Has_Been_Broken "Skyrim:The Silence Has Been Broken"), six draugr will be lying on the floor, three of which are leveled, and through the door will be [Amaund Motierre](/wiki/Skyrim:Amaund_Motierre "Skyrim:Amaund Motierre") and his bodyguard [Rexus](/wiki/Skyrim:Rexus "Skyrim:Rexus"). In addition, if The Silence Has Been Broken is active, the room past the door will contain a bedroll, an [eidar cheese wheel](/wiki/Skyrim:Eidar_Cheese_Wheel "Skyrim:Eidar Cheese Wheel"), and a loaf of [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread"), while the alcove to the north-northeast will be where Amaund clearly completed the Black Sacrament, as it will feature a [human heart](/wiki/Skyrim:Human_Heart "Skyrim:Human Heart") and [flesh](/wiki/Skyrim:Human_Flesh "Skyrim:Human Flesh"), a [nightshade](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightshade "Skyrim:Nightshade") flower, an iron dagger, and a copy of *[A Kiss, Sweet Mother](/wiki/Skyrim:A_Kiss,_Sweet_Mother "Skyrim:A Kiss, Sweet Mother")*, along with a skeleton covered in blood. Regardless of whether The Silence Has Been Broken is active, the room past the door will at least contain a large [urn](/wiki/Skyrim:Urn "Skyrim:Urn") against the west wall and a chest against the northwest wall.

Head back to the main room, this time taking the exit to the northwest. Proceed down a short corridor to an unlocked iron door. Opening the door will reveal a crypt area, with alcoves along the walls for the dead. From your left following the wall around, the second and fourth alcoves contain draugr that will arise if they detect you. Above the second draugr some [hanging moss](/wiki/Skyrim:Hanging_Moss "Skyrim:Hanging Moss") grows, and to the right are stairs leading down to the northeast. At the bottom of the stairs are alcoves on your right. There is a slumbering draugr in the second alcove. There is a doorway opposite with a path that heads down an earthen ramp to the north-northeast before turning back on itself. There are a couple draugr patrolling up and down; the second draugr is a magic user. Once they have been dealt with, proceed down. After the switchback, there is an iron ore vein to the left on the wall. At the bottom, there is an unlocked iron door to the northwest.

Through the iron door is a small room with a leveled draugr sitting in a throne against the north wall. To the left of the throne is an open upright sarcophagus with another leveled draugr. As soon as you enter the room, the standing draugr will awaken and start looking for you. As soon as you attack, the draugr on the throne will rise and join the fight. There are seven [pressure plates](/wiki/Skyrim:Pressure_Plate "Skyrim:Pressure Plate") scattered around the room that trigger short spikes to thrust upwards from their center, causing minor damage. In the western corner of the room and to the northwest are two short caved-in areas with nothing of interest. At the rear of the room are many lit candles on stands. On the left arm of the throne is a copy of the [One-handed](/wiki/Skyrim:One-handed "Skyrim:One-handed") [skill book](/wiki/Skyrim:Skill_Books "Skyrim:Skill Books") *[Mace Etiquette](/wiki/Skyrim:Mace_Etiquette "Skyrim:Mace Etiquette")*, and above the throne is a [ceremonial axe](/wiki/Skyrim:Ceremonial_Axe "Skyrim:Ceremonial Axe"), one of two replicas of the weapons carried by Kvenel the Tongue that are needed to unlock the door to *Volunruud Elder's Cairn*. Return to the main room the way you came.

The path to the northeast twists and turns and leads to a multi-leveled room. You enter the room on a balcony area directly opposite another balcony with two draugr standing on it. In front of you is a wooden ramp down to the main room. A stone walkway can be seen overhead. The room below is empty, except for a [spike wall](/wiki/Skyrim:Spike_Wall "Skyrim:Spike Wall") trap and the pressure plate that activates it just before the corridor to the northwest; [bone chimes](/wiki/Skyrim:Bone_Chimes "Skyrim:Bone Chimes") hang in front of this corridor. Avoiding the pressure plate and bone chimes, continue northeast through the corridor, up a short flight of stairs, and through an open gate into an area with a lit brazier on your left. Human bones, a pickaxe, and an iron dagger can be found scattered around the floor. There is a corridor to the southeast, and another corridor in the northern corner heading back to the southwest.

The path to the southwest heads up some stairs and comes out onto the balcony you observed earlier with the two draugr. There are no items of interest here except the draugr you likely killed with ranged weapons from the other side. The path to the southeast climbs a set of stairs, widens as it turns to the southwest, then climbs two more flights of stairs, where you will find a magic-using draugr patrolling up and down. At the top of the stairs, the path turns to the northwest and crosses the stone walkway over the previous empty room. When you reach the other side, you will enter a small catacomb area facing an [ancient Nord helmet](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Helmet "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Helmet") on a pedestal surrounded by candles on stands. A draugr rests in an alcove on your right, and will awaken when you enter the room. To the northeast is an unlocked iron door.

Through the door, you will find a short ramp flanked by two statues leading up to a platform overlooked by a large stone head. Behind the two statues are upright sarcophagi, with a third horizontal sarcophagus under the stone head. As you pass the upright sarcophagi, they will open and release hostile draugr. Once the draugr have been dispatched, you will find the [ceremonial sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Ceremonial_Sword "Skyrim:Ceremonial Sword") needed to open the door to *Volunruud Elder's Cairn* in the third sarcophagus, together with the remains of a skeleton.

Return to the main area with both ceremonial weapons and head north up the stairs to the door to *Volunruud Elder's Cairn*. Just before the door is a stone table holding a random [shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Armor#Shields "Skyrim:Armor"), a [salt pile](/wiki/Skyrim:Salt_Pile "Skyrim:Salt Pile"), and a bowl of [troll fat](/wiki/Skyrim:Troll_Fat "Skyrim:Troll Fat"). Activating the set of double doors places the ceremonial weapons directly into the two doors, with the sword on the left and the axe on the right, and unlocks access to *Volunruud Elder's Cairn*.

### Volunruud Elder's Cairn

You enter this area at the top of a corridor, with a lit brazier on your right and a pickaxe on the floor. Follow the path as it slopes down to the north, passing a burial urn on your left and a large urn on your right. You will be able to hear the music and noises for a word wall all the way down and along the corridor. Through an arch is a cave-in on your left; to the right where the path jogs is a burial urn, a large urn, a bookcase, and a fireplace with a [burnt corpse](/wiki/Skyrim:Burnt_Corpse "Skyrim:Burnt Corpse") and a salt pile in front of it. The bookcase holds copies of two random common books, a random potion, an embalming tool, and several ruined books. Underneath the burnt corpse is a [scroll of Ice Storm](/wiki/Skyrim:Scroll_of_Ice_Storm "Skyrim:Scroll of Ice Storm").

Kvenel the Tongue

As you continue along, you will see a broken brazier hanging down on your left. Just behind and above this brazier is a stone walkway, with a draugr guarding the area. Continuing ahead to the north, you enter a tunnel that turns to the east. As you approach a lit brazier on the right, a leveled draugr will be alerted and start looking for you. The path will then open into a cavern, with a throne on the other side to the southeast occupied by the ghostly form of Kvenel. He will attack as soon as you are detected. There is a boss chest to the left of the throne on a raised area, during [Silenced Tongues](/wiki/Skyrim:Silenced_Tongues "Skyrim:Silenced Tongues"), the chest will contain the skill book *[Night Falls on Sentinel](/wiki/Skyrim:Night_Falls_on_Sentinel "Skyrim:Night Falls on Sentinel")*. To the right of the throne is a sarcophagus from which a magic-using draugr will rise to assist Kvenel. The draugr may summon a [frost atronach](/wiki/Skyrim:Frost_Atronach "Skyrim:Frost Atronach"). Once you have defeated these enemies, you may need to search Kvenel twice to get both unique weapons: [Eduj](/wiki/Skyrim:Eduj "Skyrim:Eduj"), a frost damage sword, and [Okin](/wiki/Skyrim:Okin "Skyrim:Okin"), a frost damage war axe.

On the ground on the bottom step of the raised area are two potions, one an [elixir of true shot](/wiki/Skyrim:Elixir_of_True_Shot "Skyrim:Elixir of True Shot"), and the other a [potion of resist magic](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Resist_Magic "Skyrim:Potion of Resist Magic"), while farther up of the third step is a [potion of plentiful stamina](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Plentiful_Stamina "Skyrim:Potion of Plentiful Stamina"). To the right of the steps leading up to the throne is a [potion of resist fire](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Resist_Fire "Skyrim:Potion of Resist Fire"), and on the left arm of the throne is another leveled potion. The boss chest is unlocked. Inside the sarcophagus to the right of the throne and chest are two potions, one a [potion of light feet](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Light_Feet "Skyrim:Potion of Light Feet"), and the other a [potion of brief invisibility](/wiki/Skyrim:Potion_of_Brief_Invisibility "Skyrim:Potion of Brief Invisibility"). There are also two [ancient Nord greatswords](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Nord_Greatsword "Skyrim:Ancient Nord Greatsword") and an [iron warhammer](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Warhammer "Skyrim:Iron Warhammer") on the ground just in front of the sarcophagus. Finally, a large urn is found among some rubble by the wall to the northeast. The fighting with Kvenel can alert another draugr to come to his aid from the west.

Once the fighting and looting are finished, proceed to the west. After climbing a few stairs, there is a word wall for the Aura Whisper shout on your left, and more stairs climbing farther to the north. Ascending these stairs, you will pass a burial urn and a large urn in the middle of the stairs. At the top, the path crosses the stone walkway previously seen from below. In front of you is an unlocked chest, a large urn, three burial urns, an ancient Nord battleaxe, a random shield, a random helmet, a [dragon priest dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Priest_Dagger "Skyrim:Dragon Priest Dagger"), and a copy of *[A Minor Maze](/wiki/Skyrim:A_Minor_Maze "Skyrim:A Minor Maze")*. Once you have looted everything here, return to the walkway and face south. Using [Whirlwind Sprint](/wiki/Skyrim:Whirlwind_Sprint "Skyrim:Whirlwind Sprint") or careful jumping, you can reach a small ledge with another unlocked chest.

There is no shortcut or back exit out of the dungeon.

## Notes

- During the Dark Brotherhood quest The Silence Has Been Broken, your map will be updated with the location of Volunruud, as you need to speak to a certain person within the tomb. However, the mission doesn't require you to explore the whole area.
- During the Dark Brotherhood quest [Hail Sithis!](/wiki/Skyrim:Hail_Sithis! "Skyrim:Hail Sithis!"), an urn full of gold in the room to the left serves as a reward.
- The [Notched Pickaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Notched_Pickaxe "Skyrim:Notched Pickaxe")'s editor ID is dunVolunruudPickaxe even though the weapon isn't related to this location in any way.

## Bugs

- The spike wall may get stuck on the skeletons connected to the wall.
  - The [Official Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim:Patch "Skyrim:Patch"), version 1.9.26.0.8, fixes this bug.
- Kvenel respawns when the dungeon resets.
  - The [Unofficial Skyrim Special Edition Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Special_Edition_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Special Edition Patch"), version 4.3.5, addresses this issue. A leveled draugr boss will now replace him.
- The *Volunruud Elder's Cairn* door may not open, even with both ceremonial weapons in your inventory. See [the quest page](/wiki/Skyrim:Silenced_Tongues#Bugs "Skyrim:Silenced Tongues") for details. **?**

## Maps

- 

  Volunruud
- 

  Volunruud Elder's Cairn
