# Skyrim:Waking Nightmare

## Overview

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [SR-qico-Daedric.png](/wiki/File:SR-qico-Daedric.png) | **Relieve the people of [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar") from [Vaermina](/wiki/Lore:Vaermina "Lore:Vaermina")'s curse.** |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Quest Giver: | [Erandur](/wiki/Skyrim:Erandur "Skyrim:Erandur") |
| Location(s): | The [Windpeak Inn](/wiki/Skyrim:Windpeak_Inn "Skyrim:Windpeak Inn") in [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar"), [Nightcaller Temple](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightcaller_Temple "Skyrim:Nightcaller Temple") |
| Reward: | [Skull of Corruption](/wiki/Skyrim:Skull_of_Corruption "Skyrim:Skull of Corruption") *or* [Erandur](/wiki/Skyrim:Erandur "Skyrim:Erandur") as a [Follower](/wiki/Skyrim:Followers "Skyrim:Followers") |
| [Disposition](/wiki/Skyrim:Disposition "Skyrim:Disposition"): | =3 (Erandur, if spared)  =1 (Skald, if still Jarl) |
| ID: | DA16 |
| Suggested Level: | 18 |
| --- | |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | **Next →** [Kill the Giant (Jarl)](/wiki/Skyrim:Kill_the_Giant_(Jarl) "Skyrim:Kill the Giant (Jarl)") | | |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | → Next | [Kill the Giant (Jarl)](/wiki/Skyrim:Kill_the_Giant_(Jarl) "Skyrim:Kill the Giant (Jarl)") | | |

The cause of Dawnstar's nightmares

## Quick Walkthrough

1. Learn of the nightmares of the people of [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar").
2. Follow Erandur to the [Nightcaller Temple](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightcaller_Temple "Skyrim:Nightcaller Temple").
3. Fight your way with Erandur to the library, and then find the book *[The Dreamstride](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Dreamstride "Skyrim:The Dreamstride")*.
4. Locate and drink [Vaermina's Torpor](/wiki/Skyrim:Vaermina%27s_Torpor "Skyrim:Vaermina's Torpor") in the laboratory.
5. In the Dreamstride, find and activate the chain that will release the Miasma.
6. Take the soul gem to stop the force field.
7. Make your way to the sanctum and kill [Veren Duleri](/wiki/Skyrim:Veren_Duleri "Skyrim:Veren Duleri") and [Thorek](/wiki/Skyrim:Thorek "Skyrim:Thorek").
8. Choose whether or not to kill Erandur.

### Restless Nights

Upon entering [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar") you hear talk of the inhabitants suffering from terrible nightmares and lack of sleep. Even the Jarl himself is having trouble sleeping, and tension is high. Make your way to the [Windpeak Inn](/wiki/Skyrim:Windpeak_Inn "Skyrim:Windpeak Inn"), where you will find [Erandur](/wiki/Skyrim:Erandur "Skyrim:Erandur"), a priest of [Mara](/wiki/Lore:Mara "Lore:Mara"). He warns that the inhabitants of Dawnstar are in terrible danger, as the nightmares are a symptom of having their memories stolen by the Daedric Prince [Vaermina](/wiki/Lore:Vaermina "Lore:Vaermina"). He explains that the nightmares are being caused by an event that happened in [Nightcaller Temple](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightcaller_Temple "Skyrim:Nightcaller Temple"), a ruin located on the clifftop overlooking Dawnstar. Erandur will prompt you to follow him to Nightcaller Temple to put an end to the nightmares.

### Temple of Doom

Erandur opening the way

Make your way with Erandur to the Temple, and speak to him once more outside the door. He will explain why the nightmares are occurring: Many years ago, a group of [Orcish invaders](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Invader "Skyrim:Orcish Invader") attacked the temple, which was inhabited by priests of Vaermina. To defend themselves, the priests released a gas called "The Miasma", which the priests used to elongate their lives. The concentration of Miasma sent both the Orc invaders and the priests into a sleep that lasted for years. After speaking to him, head inside the Temple. If you have a follower, the follower will not enter.

There is a working shrine of Mara on the left side wall inside the ruined temple. On the bottom shelf of the podium in the middle of the first room there is a book *[Lost Legends](/wiki/Skyrim:Lost_Legends "Skyrim:Lost Legends")* which activates the quest [Forbidden Legend](/wiki/Skyrim:Forbidden_Legend "Skyrim:Forbidden Legend"). Two [skeevers](/wiki/Skyrim:Skeever "Skyrim:Skeever") inhabit the hall near the back, and a lectern on the raised area contains books. A novice-level chest is on the right side of the large carving on the back wall. Once the room is cleared, Erandur will unlock the carving with a spell, after which the wall will turn purple and reveal a doorway. Make your way through the opening and follow Erandur to the balcony overlooking the tower's inner sanctum.

The inner sanctum contains the [Skull of Corruption](/wiki/Skyrim:Skull_of_Corruption "Skyrim:Skull of Corruption"), one of Vaermina's artifacts. Erandur explains that the Skull has gained the ability to reach out to people without being wielded, and is stealing memories. The Skull must be destroyed in order for the nightmares to stop. Follow him down the stairs, where you will meet two Orcish invaders at the bottom. After confronting them, you will find the way ahead blocked by a force field. Erandur will say that there may be a way of opening it, but he would have to search for the answer in an alchemy book located in the locked Library upstairs. Luckily, he has a key—a memento from when he was himself a Priest of Vaermina. When the Orcs attacked, he had fled, leaving his companions to die, and in his shame over this, he turned to following Mara.

### Words of Power

Follow Erandur back up the stairs and allow him to unlock the door to the library. The inhabitants—Orcish invaders and [Vaermina Devotees](/wiki/Skyrim:Vaermina_Devotee "Skyrim:Vaermina Devotee")—will awaken and attack. The Vaermina Devotees fight with lightning as well as conventional weapons, so a Resist Shock potion and other defenses against magic may be useful here. The library is split into two levels. The bookcases on the lower level make it quite a tight squeeze but provide useful cover from incoming spells.

After all the enemies are defeated, Erandur will ask you to help find the book *[The Dreamstride](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Dreamstride "Skyrim:The Dreamstride")*. This contains the information needed to breach the inner sanctum. The majority of the books in the library are burnt, but a few remain intact. The Dreamstride is located on the upper level in the northeast corner of the room, on a lectern. Take it back to Erandur, who will decipher the book and state that you must use a potion called "Vaermina's Torpor." This allows the taker to use dreams to travel across distances in the real world. As a Priest of Mara, he is unable to use the potion, but says that you will be able to do so. Only the unaffiliated or the followers of Vaermina can use it. To find the potion, go to the laboratory, which adjoins the library.

### Mad Alchemist

Follow Erandur to the laboratory, where you will encounter another Orcish Invader and a Vaermina Devotee. Continue down the corridor, and fight off more Vaermina Devotees and Orcish invaders. You may find it easier to back through the doorway and tackle the enemies one at a time; Erandur is quite capable of dealing with them. After they are defeated, you can look for the torpor—a tall red bottle located on the lower level in a bookcase near the northeast corner of the room. Numerous other alchemical ingredients and potions line the shelves, and on the top level near to where Erandur is waiting, there is a copy of the [Alchemy](/wiki/Skyrim:Alchemy "Skyrim:Alchemy") [skill book](/wiki/Skyrim:Skill_Books "Skyrim:Skill Books") *[Mannimarco, King of Worms](/wiki/Skyrim:Mannimarco,_King_of_Worms "Skyrim:Mannimarco, King of Worms")*, on a half-buried bookshelf. Speak to Erandur, and he will command you to drink the potion. It's a good idea to save your game here.

### Dreamwalker

Dreamwalker

Upon taking the potion, you are transported to the inner sanctum in the tower, which you experience in a blurry, psychedelic vision. Two of Vaermina's Devotees are talking. Though everything is quite hazy, you can discern from what they are saying that you have traveled back in time to the moment of the Orc invasion of the temple. One of the men addresses you as "Brother Casimir", and asks if you are ready to release the Miasma.

There is a clattering noise in the background. You cannot access your inventory while in the Dreamwalk, nor can you wait, pick up items, or fight; you will not get attacked, however. Reply that you are ready, then head down the corridor and into the dining hall. You will see battles and corpses along the way. Make a left into a bedchamber. Head up the stairs and then down the passage on the right. Navigate a winding path onward until you reach the upper level overlooking the sanctum. Locate a chain with a large ring-shaped pull and activate it. This will release the Miasma. You will then awaken.

### Breaking the Barrier

Old friends, Veren and Thorek

When your objective updates to "Disable the Magical Barrier", take the soul gem from just to the left of the pull chain. The forcefield will deactivate. Talk to Erandur, and he will report that you disappeared and reappeared on the other side of the barrier, and that you can now access the [Skull of Corruption](/wiki/Skyrim:Skull_of_Corruption "Skyrim:Skull of Corruption"). Follow Erandur toward the Skull, taking care of several Orcs and Vaermina Devotees you will encounter along the way. You may confront some challenging battles here. Hanging back behind Erandur and some strategic retreats can be an effective tactic, but you will likely face the powerful battleaxes of some of the Orcs on a few occasions, or the Devotees's shock spells, both of which can deal heavy damage. Erandur will pause if he begins to get very far ahead of you.

When you are nearing the Skull shrine, there is an Orc lying in a room who is especially deadly. He won't wake up spontaneously, but if you try to search him, he will come to and say *"This better be good."* He's very hard to kill and can bring you down with a single strike, according to the skill level you play at. You can awaken him even after completing the quest. You then enter the door to a new cutscene where you can only watch, and it is revealed Erandur is not who he says he is.

:   **Erandur**: *Wait... Veren, Thorek. You're alive.*
:   **Veren**: *No thanks to you, Cassimir.*
:   **Erandur**: *I no longer use that name. I'm Erandur, priest of Mara.*
:   **Veren**: *You're a traitor. You left us to die, then ran before the miasma took you.*
:   **Erandur**: *No, I-I was scared. I wasn't ready to sleep.*
:   **Veren**: *Enough of your lies! I can't allow you to destroy the skull, priest of Mara.*
:   **Erandur**: *Then you leave me no choice!*

Defeat them, then Erandur will begin to take down a barrier protecting the Skull.

:   **Erandur**: *I knew Veren and Thorek. They were my friends. Is this punishment for my past? Is it Mara's will to torment me so?*

    :   **We had no choice.**

        :   **Erandur**: *Yes, you're right. If they needed to die for Dawnstar to live, then it was worth the price.*
    :   **They were trying to kill us.**

        :   **Erandur**: *And had they succeeded, Dawnstar's fate would be sealed. You have a unique way of looking at things, my friend.*
    :   **Maybe they were right. Don't destroy the staff.**

        :   **Erandur**: *No! You mustn't listen to them. They speak only lies and deception. Had we aided them in releasing the skull, they would wreak havoc across Skyrim.*

Erandur calling upon the power of Mara

Either way, he will say "*It's time. The Skull must be destroyed. If you'll stand back, I'll perform an incantation gifted to me by Lady Mara. First, an incantation will remove the barrier. I call upon you Lady Mara. The skull hungers for memories and leaves nightmares in its wake. Grant me the power to break through this barrier and send the skull to the depths of Oblivion*!"

Then the barrier will suddenly disappear. Vaermina will speak to you:

:   *"He's deceiving you. When the ritual's complete, the Skull will be free and then Erandur will turn on you. Quickly! Kill him now. Kill him and claim the Skull for your own! Vaermina commands you!"*

At this point, you can kill Erandur and take the skull, or spare him and take him on as a follower. Killing him is not considered a crime.

If you ignore Vaermina's command, [Erandur](/wiki/Skyrim:Erandur "Skyrim:Erandur") will destroy the skull and then offer to travel with you as a [follower](/wiki/Skyrim:Followers "Skyrim:Followers"); he does not betray you as Vaermina would have had you believe. If you choose this option, you will be one Daedric Artifact short of the 15 you need for the [Oblivion Walker](/wiki/Skyrim:Oblivion_Walker "Skyrim:Oblivion Walker") Achievement. Obtaining two Artifacts from one quest will then be required, and the only way you can do that is by obtaining both the [Ring of Hircine](/wiki/Skyrim:Ring_of_Hircine "Skyrim:Ring of Hircine") and the [Savior's Hide](/wiki/Skyrim:Savior%27s_Hide "Skyrim:Savior's Hide") from the Daedric quest [Ill Met By Moonlight](/wiki/Skyrim:Ill_Met_By_Moonlight "Skyrim:Ill Met By Moonlight").

After making your choice, the quest will complete, and the nightmares will be lifted from Dawnstar.

## Nightmare Reactions

Before you finish the quest, the following Dawnstar residents will complain about the nightmares:

| Name | Dialogue |
| --- | --- |
| [Beitild](/wiki/Skyrim:Beitild "Skyrim:Beitild") | *"You're wrong to come here, stranger. No rest in Dawnstar."* |
| [Brina Merilis](/wiki/Skyrim:Brina_Merilis "Skyrim:Brina Merilis") | *"You come to Dawnstar at a bad time. Tempers are... fiery."* |
| [Bulfrek](/wiki/Skyrim:Bulfrek "Skyrim:Bulfrek") | *"By the Divines, I'm tired."* |
| [Dawnstar Guards](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar_Guard "Skyrim:Dawnstar Guard") | *"You come to Dawnstar at a strange time, friend. Seems everyone in the town is having nightmares..."* *"The people here are still plagued with nightmares. Its... unnatural."* |
| [Frida](/wiki/Skyrim:Frida "Skyrim:Frida") | *"No. I don't have anything for bad dreams, so don't ask."* |
| [Fruki](/wiki/Skyrim:Fruki "Skyrim:Fruki") | *"Lady Mara will save us from these nightmares. You'll see."* |
| [Irgnir](/wiki/Skyrim:Irgnir "Skyrim:Irgnir") | *"These dreams are real, I tell you. Real!"* |
| [Jod](/wiki/Skyrim:Jod "Skyrim:Jod") | *"The Jarl is... tired. Be mindful of that."* |
| [Karita](/wiki/Skyrim:Karita_(bard) "Skyrim:Karita (bard)") | *"I envy you travelers. Dreams aren't affecting anyone not from here."* |
| [Karl](/wiki/Skyrim:Karl "Skyrim:Karl") | *"Can't sleep well no matter how much drink I put in me."* |
| [Leigelf](/wiki/Skyrim:Leigelf "Skyrim:Leigelf") | *"What is it? I haven't had much sleep."* |
| [Lond](/wiki/Skyrim:Lond "Skyrim:Lond") | *"If I get any rest soon, I'll count myself richer than a king."* |
| [Madena](/wiki/Skyrim:Madena "Skyrim:Madena") | *"If you're here about the nightmares, I'm afraid my pleas to the College of Winterhold go unheard."* |
| [Rustleif](/wiki/Skyrim:Rustleif "Skyrim:Rustleif") | *"Seems like no one is getting a decent night's rest in Dawnstar, stranger. Best buy your steel and move on."* |
| [Seren](/wiki/Skyrim:Seren "Skyrim:Seren") | *"Gods I'm so tired."* |
| [Skald the Elder](/wiki/Skyrim:Skald "Skyrim:Skald") | *"Unless you're here to solve this nightmare problem, I don't need you."* |
| [Thoring](/wiki/Skyrim:Thoring "Skyrim:Thoring") | *"Need a room? Don't worry. Nightmares don't seem to happen to travelers."* |

## Notes

- Unless [Skald](/wiki/Skyrim:Skald "Skyrim:Skald") has been replaced as [Jarl](/wiki/Skyrim:Jarl "Skyrim:Jarl") of [the Pale](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Pale "Skyrim:The Pale"), this quest is one of two required to become [Thane of the Pale](/wiki/Skyrim:Thane_of_the_Pale "Skyrim:Thane of the Pale"). If Brina is Jarl (or if Skald has been removed from power and then reinstated), this quest is not required to become Thane.
- After the quest is finished, Skald will say "The nightmares in Dawnstar have lifted, and I hear you helped that happen. You have my thanks." the first time you talk to him if he is Jarl. Skald will then treat you as a friend after that. Dawnstar guards will also occasionally say "No more nightmares. Thank the gods for that."
- You can bypass the barrier that blocks the way to the Skull by putting a plate against the wall that separates you from the Skull room and sprinting your way across. By doing so, you can take the soul gem and disable the barrier without drinking the Torpor.
  - If you do so with the Dreamstride and Torpor in your inventory, you can finish the quest and keep the Torpor.
- If you use [conjuration](/wiki/Skyrim:Conjuration "Skyrim:Conjuration") spells to resurrect the devotees and have them fight the Orcs, they will have unique dialogue.
- If you get between Erandur and the wall when he uses flames against it and block their path, the passage will still open up.
- If you are over-encumbered when you drink the Torpor, you will be over-encumbered for the entire flashback scene, and will be unable to access your inventory to drop items.
- If you enter the Dreamstride while wearing [Miraak's Robes](/wiki/Skyrim:Miraak%27s_Robes "Skyrim:Miraak's Robes"), despite being in the memories of someone else, the robe's tentacle explosions will still occur in response if you are hit.
- During the flashback scene, a priest can be seen at the end of the first hallway sitting on a bench drinking from a tankard. You won't see his body after you exit, though.
- During the flashback scene, Veren can be seen equipped with a [steel mace](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Mace "Skyrim:Steel Mace"), and Thorek can be seen equipped with a [steel sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Sword "Skyrim:Steel Sword"). However, their equipped weapons may be different when you and Erandur advance to the Skull room afterwards.
- If you have a [follower](/wiki/Skyrim:Followers "Skyrim:Followers") present, they won't enter Nightcaller Temple with you.
- If you knock anything down during the flashback scene, they will reset to where they originally were once you exit.
- After you finish the Dreamstride, all of the loot will respawn on the dead enemies.
- If, when exiting the Dreamstride, you don't disable the barrier, or do disable the barrier but don't talk to Erandur and instead continue on to the skull yourself, Erandur will eventually teleport to the skull room, after a long walk through the dungeon in the opposite direction. Veren and Thorek won't be in the skull room, though.
- If you didn't undress the two Orc attackers you killed outside of the barrier, they still won't have their clothes on but in their inventory, when you exit the Dreamstride.
- You can collect Orc and Dark Elf blood during this quest for [Discerning the Transmundane](/wiki/Skyrim:Discerning_the_Transmundane "Skyrim:Discerning the Transmundane").
- You can "talk" to some of the Orcs if you're right next to them when they wake up, though they will only say general dialogue and still attack afterwards.
- If you are in third person when drinking the Torpor, you will instantly be forced into first person while experiencing it and remain in that view when it ends on the other side of the barrier. You can then switch back to third person at this point if you desire.
- You can get the [Oblivion Walker](/wiki/Skyrim:Oblivion_Walker "Skyrim:Oblivion Walker") achievement without the Skull due to a bug in [Ill Met By Moonlight](/wiki/Skyrim:Ill_Met_By_Moonlight "Skyrim:Ill Met By Moonlight"), where you can get two Daedric artifacts.
- If you spare Erandur, he won't mention anything related to the quest once you finish the latter, you cannot ask him about them any longer, and he will switch to standard Dunmer combat dialogue instead.
- Each Vaermina Devotee has one leveled magicka potion and one random [ingredient](/wiki/Skyrim:Ingredients "Skyrim:Ingredients"). Each Orcish Invader has a small amount of gold and a piece of either [iron](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron "Skyrim:Iron") or [steel](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel "Skyrim:Steel") weaponry.
- Although the Miasma is supposed to degrade the minds of those exposed to its effects, Veren and Thorek don't appear to have been affected like everyone else.
- Erandur refers to the Torpor as three different sizes: first as a "small bottle" when asking what the potion looks like, but his end of conversation floating dialogue calls it a "tall bottle". After finding it, when asking him if he wants you to drink the Torpor here and now, he will then refer to it as "a tiny bottle". The Torpor itself shares the same model as Elixirs of [Fortify Health](/wiki/Skyrim:Fortify_Health "Skyrim:Fortify Health").

## Bugs

|  |
| --- |
| **To meet our site's higher standard of quality, this article or section may require cleanup.**  **The user who placed this here had the following concern:** needs yet another cleanup, or at least a few confirmed bugs To leave a message about the cleanup for this article, please add it to this article's [talk page](https://en.uesp.net/wiki/Skyrim_talk:Waking_Nightmare). |

- After you release the Miasma, the prompt to activate the chain may still be there, but nothing will happen if you do. **?**
- Erandur's line "Perhaps my prayers were answered and your reason for stumbling across Dawnstar is more than a mere coincidence." may simply appear in the subtitles despite not actually being said. **?**
- Saving (manually or automatically) during or after the Dreamstride portion of the quest will cause the game to freeze on consoles or crash to desktop on PC.
  - The [Official Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim:Patch "Skyrim:Patch"), version 1.9.26.0.8, fixes this bug.
  - To prevent this, avoid saving during this quest, and disable autosaves as well.
  - Alternatively, after removing the barrier to speak with Erandur, if you head outside and save your game, you should be able to re-enter the Temple and save normally.
  - This bug may be related to conflicting [follower](/wiki/Skyrim:Followers "Skyrim:Followers") protocols. Leaving the Temple and dismissing any followers you have, or recruiting and dismissing different followers, may fix this.
  -  As the bug originates during the Dreamstride, use the console command `tcl`, cross through the barrier, and remove the soul gem. Since the quest progress is scripted through the status of the soul gem, this will progress the quest just as if you had used the Dreamstride. The only impact it will cause on the game is that there will be no enemies except two Orcs in the dining room, Thorek and Veren. To remove Vaermina's Torpor, use the command `player.removeitem 5f6df 1`. If you keep the Torpor and drink it later outside of Nightcaller Temple, a message will appear informing you that it only works inside that location, and the potion will be replaced in your inventory. After the quest is finished the Torpor can be dropped or placed in a container.
    - The enemies can be made to appear using two more console commands: `Prid C4DB9` followed by `enable`.
  - Similar to the fix above, using the [Become Ethereal](/wiki/Skyrim:Become_Ethereal "Skyrim:Become Ethereal") shout may allow you to cross the barrier and grab the soul gem, skipping the Dreamstride sequence altogether.
  -  The least complicated method to avoid this bug is to exit the Temple before and after the Dreamstride. Exiting before the dreamstride will enable you to do it again after the dream, or else you will not be able to exit the temple at all.
- After drinking the Torpor, or shortly after pulling the chain to release the miasma, the game may crash.
  -  You can bypass the dream sequence entirely using the console commands `player.setstage da16 160` and `movetoqt da16 2`.
- If [survival mode](/wiki/Skyrim:Survival_Mode "Skyrim:Survival Mode") is active, you may continue to suffer from Survival-based penalties while in the Dreamstride.
  - The [Official Skyrim Special Edition Patch](/wiki/Skyrim:Special_Edition_Patch "Skyrim:Special Edition Patch"), version 1.6, fixes this bug.
- Sometimes Erandur will not lead you to the temple, and will stand in place at the inn.
  - The [Unofficial Skyrim Special Edition Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Special_Edition_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Special Edition Patch"), version 4.2.0, fixes this bug.
  - Using [Unrelenting Force](/wiki/Skyrim:Unrelenting_Force "Skyrim:Unrelenting Force") on him may cause him to act normally and start walking.
  - Simply sprinting and bumping into him will sometimes make him move as well, especially if he is stuck next to the bar.
  - Leaving the inn and re-entering may fix the issue.
  - Talking to him, quitting the dialogue and immediately leaving the inn, while he's still talking, will make him teleport next to you outside. After finishing his line, he may lead you to the temple as intended.
  - Use the command `prid 24280`, followed by `moveto player` when just outside the temple to move him directly to the entrance of the temple.
- While following him to Nightcaller Temple, if he engages in combat, is jostled by running, or if you walk too far ahead of him, Erandur may stop moving or walk back to the beginning of his route.
  - Clearing the area of enemies beforehand can help prevent this from occurring.
  - This can be fixed using the prid/moveto method described above.
- Similarly, Erandur may go up to the door to Nightcaller Temple, or the library door within it, and just stop.
  - Saving then reloading this save should joggle him to continue. Failing that, revert to an earlier save, and if any combat happened before the door in question, use Wait for an hour immediately after the combat, before he goes to the door.
  - Alternatively, you can attack Erandur until he becomes hostile and the door should open when he uses his magic on you. Sheathe your weapon and he will then become passive again.
- If you let Erandur enter the Nightcaller Temple first, he may not be at the entrance once you enter, but rather farther inside the temple, a place you cannot get to without Erandur's help. This will as a result make the entire quest impossible to complete.
  - To avoid this, make sure to enter the temple before Erandur. (Note contrary advice below.)
  - This issue can be fixed by entering `prid 00024280` into the console, followed by `moveto player`, which will bring him to your location.
- If you enter Nightcaller Temple before Erandur, he may be immediately transported farther in without opening the entrance, making the quest impossible to finish.
  - To avoid this, make sure you let Erandur enter the temple before you. (Note contrary advice above.)
  - It is possible to use the [clipping glitch](/wiki/Skyrim:Glitches#Clipping "Skyrim:Glitches") to progress past the initial door. Use the object on the right hand wall if you've just entered the keep, fall down to the room off to the side, and run forward to catch up to Erandur. After completing quest you can use buckets in the temple for easy clipping.
- At the point where Erandur is supposed to lead you up the staircase to the library, he may get stuck halfway up the stairs.
  - The [Official Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim:Patch "Skyrim:Patch"), version 1.5, fixes this bug.
- Once you are in the library and have killed the Orcs and Vaermina's followers there, Erandur may simply stand around on the lower level without telling you what to do next. It may require talking to him two or more times to get the next instruction to search on the upper level.
  - This may be fixed by using the "resurrect" command on the corpse of an enemy, and killing them again.
- Once you have removed the soul gem to take down the barrier, the barrier may not come down. This leaves you unable to talk with Erandur. **?**
  - To bypass this, proceed to the last room with the skull on your own. Erandur should be waiting for you there. Make sure you speak with him straight away. If not, he will simply go back behind the barrier. If he isn't waiting for you, then load an earlier gamesave from when you were at the inn. He is bound to show up there. In case you're completely stuck and none of the above helps, go back into the temple, open the console and type these commands: `prid 24280` and `moveto player`.
- After you have killed Veren and Thorek and are at the point when you have to speak to Erandur, no interaction with him may be possible, including talking.
  - If this happens, run all the way back to the front door of the temple. Go outside and then come back in.
  -  Alternatively, open the console, select Erandur, and use the console commands `disable` followed by `enable`
- During the Dreamwalker flashback, you may be unable to pull the chain to release the miasma.
  - A possible fix is to avoid drinking the torpor and use the console command `TCL` to run to the chain outside of the flashback; while there, you should take the soul gem used to form the miasma. This will skip the entire flashback. Running to the central room with the skull, you will find Erandur and the quest will proceed normally.
- In addition to attacking Erandur, Veren and Thorek may also attack each other, making this fight particularly easy.
- In some cases, it may happen that Orcish Invaders and Vaermina Devotees that were defeated before drinking Vaermina's Torpor get replaced with naked bodies, while still having default items in their inventory. Occasionally, they may have standing animations, but horizontally and with their eyes closed, after the dream sequence. In addition, the Vaermina Devotees' inventories are refilled; if they were looted already, this allows you to gain twice as many items from them. The defeated Orc Invaders are not affected by this inventory resetting. **?**
  - To fix the animation once the quest is finished, simply save the game after leaving the area and reload it; this should replace the bugged corpses with normal ones (though still naked).
- If the Dreamwalk sequence did not start for you, you might find that it starts inappropriately at a much later stage in the quest, e.g. when you are supposed to help Erandur fight Veren and Thorek. **?**
  - The workaround is to do the Dreamwalk sequence if you like (though you'll likely remain stuck in it even after triggering the Miasma), then open the Console and enter `SetStage` commands, one after another, for every quest stage up to the point where you are supposed to be in the quest; then do `CoC NightCallerTemple01`, and go back to where you left Erandur (who may be fighting Veren and Thorek on his own, though there is no need to hurry, as he is Essential and thus unkillable until the ending sequence of the quest).
- Sometimes Erandur may not do anything to remove the illusion in the entry hall.
  - To fix this you have to hit him a few times and the illusion will be removed when he casts his flame spell.
- Sometimes after the dream sequence you will be stuck in first person **?**
  - fixable by any transformation

## Quest Stages

| Waking Nightmare (DA16) | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Stage | Finishes Quest | Journal Entry |
| 20 |  | I've overheard two citizens of Dawnstar complaining about horrible nightmares. They seem to be relying on a priest of Mara named Erandur for assistance. *Objective 20:* Speak to Erandur |
| 25 |  | Erandur has assured me he can end the nightmares, the source of which seems to be within Nightcaller Temple, a place of worship for Vaermina. I have agreed to assist him in this endeavor and we are now on our way. *Objective 25:* Follow Erandur to Nightcaller Temple |
| 28 |  | *Objective 28:* Speak to Erandur |
| 30 |  | *Objective 30:* Follow Erandur |
| 50 |  | Erandur has revealed that Nightcaller Temple contains the legendary Skull of Corruption, an artifact of great power. In order to end the nightmares, we must breach the inner sanctum and allow Erandur to perform a ritual on the Skull which will render it inert. *Objective 40:* Follow Erandur to the Library |
| 60 |  | *Objective 50:* Speak to Erandur |
| 70 |  | *Objective 55:* Locate "The Dreamstride" |
| 80 |  | *Objective 57:* Speak to Erandur |
| 90 |  | From a tome obtained in the temple's library, Erandur has discovered a way to breach the inner sanctum. By obtaining a special liquid, we'll be able to perform a Dreamstride... travel a distance using another subject's dreams. *Objective 60:* Follow Erandur to the Laboratory |
| 100 |  | *Objective 65:* Speak to Erandur |
| 110 |  | *Objective 70:* Locate Vaermina's Torpor |
| 120 |  | *Objective 80:* Speak to Erandur |
| 140 |  | With Vaermina's Torpor in hand, I am ready to undergo the Dreamstride. I must drink the liquid which will enable me to enter the dreams of those who still slumber within these walls and use them to breach the inner sanctum. *Objective 100:* Drink Vaermina's Torpor |
| 150 |  | I've found myself within the dreams of a priest of Vaermina. I am experiencing the Orc invasion of the temple over a decade ago. In the guise of the priest, I've been tasked with releasing the Miasma and placing everyone inside the temple in a deep sleep. *Objective 110:* Release the Miasma |
| 160 |  | *Objective 120:* Disable the Magical Barrier |
| 170 |  | *Objective 130:* Speak to Erandur |
| 180 |  | After awakening from the Dreamstride, I was able to remove the barrier to the inner sanctum. I must now follow Erandur to the Skull of Corruption so it can be rendered inert. *Objective 140:* Follow Erandur to the Skull of Corruption |
| 191 |  | *Objective 150:* Defeat Veren and Thorek |
| 192 |  | *Objective 160:* Speak to Erandur |
| 195 |  | *Objective 165:* Wait for Erandur to dispel the barrier |
| 197 |  | I've heard the words of the goddess Vaermina, who claims that Erandur will kill me after the ritual to destroy the Skull of Corruption is complete. I must make a choice; either allow Erandur to complete the ritual and destroy the Skull, or kill Erandur and wield the Skull of Corruption as my own. *Objective 170 or 180:* Murder Erandur *or* Allow Erandur to complete his ritual |
| 198 |  | *Objective 200:* Speak to Erandur |
| 199 |  | *Objective 190:* Take the Skull of Corruption |
| 200 | ☑ | I've made my decision, and allowed Erandur to live. The Skull of Corruption has been destroyed and Erandur has offered his services as a companion if I should ever need his assistance. |
| ☑ | I've made my decision and Erandur has died by my hands. Unfortunately, the Skull of Corruption was destroyed before I could take his life. Such moments are the misfortunes of the indecisive. |
| ☑ | I've made my decision and Erandur has died by my hands. I now possess the Skull of Corruption and look forward to performing my duties for Lady Vaermina. |

- The following empty quest stages were omitted from the table: 0, 5, 10, 22, 40, 145, 148, 155, 190, 196.

Notes

- Any text displayed in angle brackets (e.g., `<Alias=LocationHold>`) is dynamically set by the Radiant Quest system, and will be filled in with the appropriate word(s) when seen in game.
- Not all Journal Entries may appear in your journal; which entries appear and which entries do not depends on the manner in which the quest is done.
- Stages are not always in order of progress. This is usually the case with quests that have multiple possible outcomes or quests where certain tasks may be done in any order. Some stages may therefore repeat objectives seen in other stages.
- If an entry is marked as "Finishes Quest" it means the quest disappears from the Active Quest list, but you may still receive new entries for that quest.
- On the PC, it is possible to use the [console](/wiki/Skyrim:Console "Skyrim:Console") to advance through the quest by entering `setstage DA16 stage`, where `stage` is the number of the stage you wish to complete. It is not possible to un-complete (i.e. go back) quest stages, but it is possible to clear all stages of the quest using `resetquest DA16`.

First room of the War Quarters

The **War Quarters** is a small [Dwarven building](/wiki/Skyrim:Dwarven_Ruins "Skyrim:Dwarven Ruins") in [Blackreach](/wiki/Skyrim:Blackreach "Skyrim:Blackreach"). It is set into the southeastern wall of the cavern, northeast of the [Great Lift at Raldbthar](/wiki/Skyrim:Great_Lift_at_Raldbthar "Skyrim:Great Lift at Raldbthar"). The building is devoid of any [creatures](/wiki/Skyrim:Creatures "Skyrim:Creatures") or [automatons](/wiki/Skyrim:Dwarven_Automatons "Skyrim:Dwarven Automatons").

## War Quarters

The sleeping chamber

It is a typical deserted Dwemer building. Upon entering, the first room has a balcony at the rear with a door accessing it, and a doorway in front of the balcony to the south. The southern doorway leads into another empty room, with an unlocked door to the east that opens into a T-junction. To the south is a sleeping chamber, with seven beds arranged in a fan formation. To the north is another empty room, with a stone double bed in an alcove to the north and a door to the west. This door opens onto the balcony at the rear of the first room, which has two unlocked Dwemer [chests](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") along its edge.

## Notes

- One copy of the [Heavy Armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Heavy_Armor "Skyrim:Heavy Armor") [skill book](/wiki/Skyrim:Skill_Books "Skyrim:Skill Books") *[2920, MidYear, v6](/wiki/Skyrim:2920,_MidYear,_v6 "Skyrim:2920, MidYear, v6")* can be found on a table at the top of the stands overlooking the arena-like ring just west of the War Quarters.

## Bugs

- You may hear the sound of active Dwarven traps in certain areas. **?**
  - Saving and reloading will fix this.

## Maps

- 

  War Quarters

# Skyrim:Warehouse

## Overview

| Region | |
| [Eastmarch](/wiki/Skyrim:Eastmarch "Skyrim:Eastmarch") | |
| Location | |
| [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm")'s docks | |

The Windhelm Warehouse

The **Warehouse** is a location in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm") which serves as a storage for the docks. Inside are a plethora of barrels, crates, and sacks to store the various goods in the warehouse, as well as two carts for transporting them.

The interior of the warehouse

It can be located on the quieter eastern end of the docks, just to the left of the gates to Windhelm proper when exiting the city and entering the harbor. The double doors that serve as the entrance are surrounded by a multitude of barrels on one end, not currently properly stored inside the building, and the steps leading to the higher portion of the docks. A lone brazier provides illumination for the entrance. While the warehouse is not properly secured with a lock and it is not illegal to ever enter it, all of the contents inside of it are formally owned, and cannot be taken without stealing. However, as no one will ever enter here as part of their normal schedule, it is fairly easy to rob.

The building itself is a bit disorderly, with many random containers filling up the room in disorderly piles. The room is well-lit, with three chandeliers providing lighting. Six giant wooden beams, with three on both sides of the building, provide support to the heavy stone structure. Less sturdy wooden rafters provide additional support.

While most of the containers in here are entirely static and cannot be opened, the few that can be opened for the most part contain foodstuffs. Notably, a knapsack hidden behind some crates at the far side of the building from the entrance has some more valuable items, such as jewelry, [books](/wiki/Skyrim:Books "Skyrim:Books"), a [potion](/wiki/Skyrim:Potions "Skyrim:Potions") of [restore health](/wiki/Skyrim:Restore_Health "Skyrim:Restore Health"), gold, as well as less valuable items such as food or common clothing. Additionally, a few items randomly sprawled across the room are of note. A [solution of enhanced stamina](/wiki/Skyrim:Solution_of_Enhanced_Stamina "Skyrim:Solution of Enhanced Stamina") can be found on the floor next to a chair to the right when entering the room. To the left of the entrance is a [philter of strength](/wiki/Skyrim:Philter_of_Strength "Skyrim:Philter of Strength"). Besides that, there are a few random [drinks](/wiki/Skyrim:Beverages "Skyrim:Beverages"), baskets, gold pieces, plates, mugs, and a broom littered throughout the room.

Warmaiden's

**Warmaiden's** is a smithy and weapons store in [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun "Skyrim:Whiterun"). It is located just inside the main gate, right next to [Breezehome](/wiki/Skyrim:Breezehome "Skyrim:Breezehome").

The store is owned by [Ulfberth War-Bear](/wiki/Skyrim:Ulfberth_War-Bear "Skyrim:Ulfberth War-Bear") and his wife [Adrianne Avenicci](/wiki/Skyrim:Adrianne_Avenicci "Skyrim:Adrianne Avenicci"). It consists of one area, *[Warmaiden's](#Warmaiden.27s)*.

Like all other stores, Warmaiden's has a number of fixed wares on display scattered around the main trading room. These can be bought from the proprietors and will disappear from the room when purchased. For a list of these special items, see [this section](#Displayed_Merchandise).

## Residents

| Residents |
| --- |
| [Adrianne Avenicci](/wiki/Skyrim:Adrianne_Avenicci "Skyrim:Adrianne Avenicci") [Blacksmith](/wiki/Skyrim:Blacksmith "Blacksmith")  [Ulfberth War-Bear](/wiki/Skyrim:Ulfberth_War-Bear "Skyrim:Ulfberth War-Bear") [Blacksmith](/wiki/Skyrim:Blacksmith "Blacksmith") |

## Related Quests

- **[Blacksmithing Tutorial](/wiki/Skyrim:Blacksmithing_Tutorial "Skyrim:Blacksmithing Tutorial")**: Learn the basics of [smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Smithing "Skyrim:Smithing"). ([radiant](/wiki/Skyrim:Radiant "Skyrim:Radiant"))
- **[Greatsword for a Great Man](/wiki/Skyrim:Greatsword_for_a_Great_Man "Skyrim:Greatsword for a Great Man")**: Adrianne asks you to deliver a sword to [Proventus Avenicci](/wiki/Skyrim:Proventus_Avenicci "Skyrim:Proventus Avenicci"), her father.

## Warmaiden's

During business hours, Adrianne is often smithing in the outside workshop while Ulfberth sells her products inside the store. Both Adrianne and Ulfberth are merchants (see [notes](#Notes)), though it is for Adrianne the store was named. Apparently interested in swords even as a child, she received "Warmaiden" as a nickname from her father.

Without the [displayed merchandise](#Displayed_Merchandise), the main trading room is completely empty, save for five food sacks scattered around the room. Ulfberth can be found here every day between 8am and 8pm, offering his merchant services. Behind the counter is a door leading to a small dining area with a burning fireplace and a [cooking pot](/wiki/Skyrim:Cooking_Pot "Skyrim:Cooking Pot"). There is also an alternative exit to Whiterun.

On the balcony of the second floor is a small table with some [sliced goat cheese](/wiki/Skyrim:Sliced_Goat_Cheese "Skyrim:Sliced Goat Cheese"), right next to the door leading to the bedroom. The bedroom contains a minor loot chest in the northwestern corner and, in the adjacent corner, a dresser with some [clothes](/wiki/Skyrim:Clothing#Clothes "Skyrim:Clothing"). On top of the dresser sits the store's [business ledger](/wiki/Skyrim:Business_Ledger "Skyrim:Business Ledger"), a locked [strongbox](/wiki/Skyrim:Strongbox "Skyrim:Strongbox"), and a plate with a [boiled creme treat](/wiki/Skyrim:Boiled_Creme_Treat "Skyrim:Boiled Creme Treat"). The double bed stands at the southern wall with an end table on either side, both with clutter. There is a rare copy of *[Killing - Before You're Killed](/wiki/Skyrim:Killing_-_Before_You%27re_Killed "Skyrim:Killing - Before You're Killed")* on top of one end table and a bottle of [ale](/wiki/Skyrim:Ale "Skyrim:Ale") and a loaf of [bread](/wiki/Skyrim:Bread "Skyrim:Bread") on the other.

The workshop outside includes a [workbench](/wiki/Skyrim:Workbench "Skyrim:Workbench"), a [tanning rack](/wiki/Skyrim:Tanning "Skyrim:Tanning"), a [grindstone](/wiki/Skyrim:Grindstone "Skyrim:Grindstone"), a [forge](/wiki/Skyrim:Forge "Skyrim:Forge") and a [smelter](/wiki/Skyrim:Smelting "Skyrim:Smelting").

## Displayed Merchandise

- The following items are sitting out in the store on counters, shelves, or tables. They can be purchased or taken for free if your [Disposition](/wiki/Skyrim:Disposition "Skyrim:Disposition") with Adrianne or Ulfberth is high enough. They [respawn](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawning "Skyrim:Respawning") after ten days.

|  |
| --- |
| - **Armor**: 1 [Iron Armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Armor "Skyrim:Iron Armor"), 3 [Iron Boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Boots "Skyrim:Iron Boots"), 1 [Iron Gauntlets](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Gauntlets "Skyrim:Iron Gauntlets"), 1 [Iron Helmet](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Helmet "Skyrim:Iron Helmet") - **Weapons**: 2 [Iron Battleaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Battleaxe "Skyrim:Iron Battleaxe"), 1 [Iron Dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Dagger "Skyrim:Iron Dagger"), 2 [Iron Greatsword](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Greatsword "Skyrim:Iron Greatsword"), 1 [Iron Warhammer](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Warhammer "Skyrim:Iron Warhammer"), 1 [Steel Dagger](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Dagger "Skyrim:Steel Dagger"), 1 [Steel Greatsword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Greatsword "Skyrim:Steel Greatsword"), 1 [Steel Sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Sword "Skyrim:Steel Sword") |

## Standard Merchandise

This is the merchandise found in the Warmaiden's [merchant chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Merchant_Chest "Skyrim:Merchant Chest"). These items are only available if purchased from the merchant; they cannot be pickpocketed or stolen. They are [restocked](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawning "Skyrim:Respawning") every two days.

[Ulfberth War-Bear](/wiki/Skyrim:Ulfberth_War-Bear "Skyrim:Ulfberth War-Bear") at the counter, with the doorway at the right leading to the back room

## Notes

- If Adrianne is inside, she and Ulfberth share the same [merchant chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Merchant_Chest "Skyrim:Merchant Chest") (essentially the same inventory and gold); however, when outside during business hours, she has a separate merchant chest with independent merchandise and gold. Therefore, you can do much more business at the store if you approach each of them individually while Adrianne is outside. Once you enter the shop, however, Adrianne will share Ulberth's chest until they reset.[*verification needed*]
- You can invest in Adrianne and Ulfberth separately if you catch Adrianne outside the shop.
- [Gunmar](/wiki/Skyrim:Gunmar "Skyrim:Gunmar")[DG](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard") may mention Warmaiden's when asked what he has for sale: *"It's no Warmaiden's, but I've got a decent supply."*

## Bugs

- After leaving the shop, it may become locked, even during business hours.
  - It will remain locked until you break in, or may unlock itself if you leave the area and come back. If someone notices you picking the lock, it is a crime. However, Ulfberth will act normally and won't accuse you of trespassing, assuming you broke in during the day.[*verification needed — see [talk page](https://dev.uesp.net/wiki/Skyrim_talk:Warmaiden%27s#Warmaidens_Permanently_Locked)*]
- Ulfberth may present dialogue that appears to allow for multiple investments, but only the first will count.
- Items sold to Ulfberth are removed from your inventory but gold may not be received.
- The tub of water that's part of the forge outside doesn't exist as a physical object (you can walk though it as if it weren't there) nor something to interact with to enter the smithing interface (there is no option to when scrolling over it) like all other forges.
- If you buy something from Adrianne then head inside and buy something from Ulfberth, then exit the store and ask Adrianne what she has for sale, her shop inventory will now mirror Ulfberth's, down to the gold available and what he had for sale, even though she is still outside and should have her own separate inventory. **?**

# Skyrim:Water Stone

## Overview

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | [SR-mapicon-Standing Stones.png](/wiki/File:SR-mapicon-Standing_Stones.png) | All-Maker Stone: Water Stone ([view on map](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=solstheim&centeron=Water+Stone)) | | |
| [Clearable](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons#Clearing "Skyrim:Dungeons") | Yes |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | 10 days or 30 days |
| [Level](/wiki/Skyrim:Encounter_Zone_Level "Skyrim:Encounter Zone Level") | Min: 10 |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| DLC2StoneWater, DLC2StoneWater02 | |
| Region | |
| [Solstheim](/wiki/Skyrim:Solstheim "Skyrim:Solstheim") | |
| Location | |
| North of [Raven Rock](/wiki/Skyrim:Raven_Rock "Skyrim:Raven Rock") West of [Fahlbtharz](/wiki/Skyrim:Fahlbtharz "Skyrim:Fahlbtharz") | |

The Water Stone

The **Water Stone** is an [All-Maker Stone](/wiki/Skyrim:All-Maker_Stones "Skyrim:All-Maker Stones") north of [Raven Rock](/wiki/Skyrim:Raven_Rock "Skyrim:Raven Rock").

Once [cleansed](/wiki/Skyrim:Cleansing_the_Stones "Skyrim:Cleansing the Stones"), this stone gives you the power to [heal](/wiki/Skyrim:Waters_of_Life "Skyrim:Waters of Life") everyone around you for 200 points. Once you use this power, it disappears from the Powers menu, and you must return to the Water Stone to obtain it again.

## Related Quests

- **[Cleansing the Stones](/wiki/Skyrim:Cleansing_the_Stones "Skyrim:Cleansing the Stones")**: Use the [Bend Will](/wiki/Skyrim:Bend_Will "Skyrim:Bend Will") [shout](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Shouts "Skyrim:Dragon Shouts") to cleanse each of the remaining [All-Maker Stones](/wiki/Skyrim:All-Maker_Stones "Skyrim:All-Maker Stones").

## Walkthrough

The cleansed Water Stone

The stone shows the usual signs of construction, with wooden scaffolding on the southeast side, and [pickaxes](/wiki/Skyrim:Pickaxe "Skyrim:Pickaxe"), [shovels](/wiki/Skyrim:Shovel "Skyrim:Shovel"), hammers, and carts in the vicinity. Several [nightshade](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightshade "Skyrim:Nightshade") plants and [blue mountain flowers](/wiki/Skyrim:Blue_Mountain_Flower "Skyrim:Blue Mountain Flower") grow around the stone. A path leads south to [Damphall Mine](/wiki/Skyrim:Damphall_Mine "Skyrim:Damphall Mine").

Before you cleanse the stone, a leveled [cultist](/wiki/Skyrim:Cultist "Skyrim:Cultist") will attack as you approach. [Benkum](/wiki/Skyrim:Benkum "Skyrim:Benkum"), [Hjalfar](/wiki/Skyrim:Hjalfar "Skyrim:Hjalfar"), [Liesl](/wiki/Skyrim:Liesl "Skyrim:Liesl"), and [Palevius Lex](/wiki/Skyrim:Palevius_Lex "Skyrim:Palevius Lex") will be working on and bowing to the stone. When you cleanse the stone, a leveled [lurker](/wiki/Skyrim:Lurker "Skyrim:Lurker") will rise from the water around the base of the stone and attack you and the workers. After the lurker is defeated, the sailors and their ship just offshore will be removed from the game once you leave the area.

## Notes

- Typically, a low-level [dragon](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon "Skyrim:Dragon") will also fly in from the west, although there are scattered reports of this not happening. If you haven't already triggered the rieklings hidden in barrels to the southeast, they'll also arrive shortly, which can make for a chaotic battle. Fortunately, you don't need to guarantee the safety of the sailors.
- The Water Stone is not included in the list of stones you can wake up at when [sleeping](/wiki/Skyrim:Sleeping "Skyrim:Sleeping") on Solstheim if [Cleansing the Stones](/wiki/Skyrim:Cleansing_the_Stones "Skyrim:Cleansing the Stones") isn't finished yet.
- The Water Stone also appears in *[Bloodmoon](/wiki/Bloodmoon:Water_Stone "Bloodmoon:Water Stone")*.

## Bugs

- The sailors are sometimes not removed along with their ship after the stone is cleansed, and are left stranded by the beach west of the stone.
  -  The [Unofficial Dragonborn Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Dragonborn_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Dragonborn Patch"), version 1.0.2, fixes this bug.

# Skyrim:Wave Breaker

## Overview

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [SR-qico-Miscellaneous.png](/wiki/File:SR-qico-Miscellaneous.png) | **[Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar") is threatened by a [giant crab](/wiki/Skyrim:Emperor_Crab_Guardian_Spirit "Skyrim:Emperor Crab Guardian Spirit")!** |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Quest Giver: | [Courier](/wiki/Skyrim:Courier "Skyrim:Courier") |
| Location(s): | Shack west of Dawnstar, [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar") |
| Reward: | 1,200 gold |
| ID: | ccBGSSSE001\_Crab\_MQ4 |
| Added by: | [Fishing](/wiki/Skyrim:Fishing "Skyrim:Fishing") |
| --- | |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | **← Previous** [Beneath Bronze Waters](/wiki/Skyrim:Beneath_Bronze_Waters "Skyrim:Beneath Bronze Waters") | **Next →** [End of the Line](/wiki/Skyrim:End_of_the_Line "Skyrim:End of the Line") | | |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | ← Previous | [Beneath Bronze Waters](/wiki/Skyrim:Beneath_Bronze_Waters "Skyrim:Beneath Bronze Waters") | | → Next | [End of the Line](/wiki/Skyrim:End_of_the_Line "Skyrim:End of the Line") | | |

A powerful ocean spirit emerges

## Quick Walkthrough

1. Wait for a [courier](/wiki/Skyrim:Courier "Skyrim:Courier") to deliver a [letter](/wiki/Skyrim:Viriya%27s_Hastily_Written_Letter "Skyrim:Viriya's Hastily Written Letter") from [Viriya](/wiki/Skyrim:Viriya "Skyrim:Viriya").
2. Travel to the shack west of [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar") and join the defense.
3. Kill several waves of [frenzied mudcrabs](/wiki/Skyrim:Frenzied_Mudcrab "Skyrim:Frenzied Mudcrab") and regroup with Viriya inside the shack.
4. Loot [flaming pots](/w/index.php?title=Skyrim:Flaming_Pot&action=edit&redlink=1 "Skyrim:Flaming Pot (page does not exist)") from the mudcrabs and use the catapults to launch them at the [Emperor Crab Guardian Spirit](/wiki/Skyrim:Emperor_Crab_Guardian_Spirit "Skyrim:Emperor Crab Guardian Spirit").
5. Speak with Viriya once the spirit is defeated. Visit the jarl of Dawnstar for your reward.

## Detailed Walkthrough

This quest will only be triggered after you have exhausted [Viriya](/wiki/Skyrim:Viriya "Skyrim:Viriya")'s fishing jobs. The final quest she will offer you is [Beneath Bronze Waters](/wiki/Skyrim:Beneath_Bronze_Waters "Skyrim:Beneath Bronze Waters"). Once this is completed, you will receive a [hastily written letter](/wiki/Skyrim:Viriya%27s_Hastily_Written_Letter "Skyrim:Viriya's Hastily Written Letter") from a [courier](/wiki/Skyrim:Courier "Skyrim:Courier").

Read the letter and you will learn that the [frenzied mudcrabs](/wiki/Skyrim:Frenzied_Mudcrab "Skyrim:Frenzied Mudcrab") have emerged again and that the guards sent to deal with them were attacked by a spirit. Head to the shack on the shore west of [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar") where you will have to defeat the frenzied mudcrabs alongside Viriya and the guards. After a short while, another wave of frenzied mudcrabs will attack, once defeated you will need to regroup with Viriya inside the shack.

All of a sudden a loud explosion goes off and the whole ground rumbles. Head outside to see the huge "Emperor Crab Guardian Spirit" that has risen from the sea! The explosion was from a wagon full of Flaming Pots. The Frenzied mudcrabs will begin grabbing these pots and scurrying away with them. Kill them and grab these pots while avoiding the guardian crab's huge pincer slams. Head back to the catapults and load them up with flaming pots one at a time. Activate the catapult and it flings the flaming pots at the giant crab ghost. Do this about six times and it will be reduced to the ether. Viriya gives her thanks. Head to the White Hall in Dawnstar to receive a gold reward from the Jarl. At this point Viriya goes missing but after a little while sends a letter to begin [End of the Line](/wiki/Skyrim:End_of_the_Line "Skyrim:End of the Line").

## Notes

- This quest can be initiated by heading straight to the fishing shack, even without getting the letter.
- The courier has been known to arrive, meet you in Dawnstar and start this quest even if [Beneath Bronze Waters](/wiki/Skyrim:Beneath_Bronze_Waters "Skyrim:Beneath Bronze Waters") is in progress but unfinished. Whether this is intentional or a bug is unknown.
- After this quest a fisherman respawns at the fishing shack.
- About 4-5 of the Flaming Pots can be collected and kept when the quest is over.
- It was originally planned for the quest to end with you fighting a living Emperor Crab rather than a ghost. Once the crab was defeated, you would have been able to hollow out the crab's body and use it as a player home, however this was [cut for time](/wiki/General:Cartogriffi%27s_Posts#2022 "General:Cartogriffi's Posts"). There is a placeholder cell still in the [Creation Kit](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Creation_Kit "Skyrim Mod:Creation Kit") (`ccPlaceholder18Interior01`), as well as an unfinished location record stating it was called the "Emperor's Homestead".

## Bugs

- Sometime Viriya will not join you in the shack. Save/quitting and reloading can cause her to trigger the regroup in shack but is not guaranteed. However, fast traveling to another location, saving and reloading than fast traveling back to Viriya will fix the bug in most cases.
- If Dawnstar is under Imperial control, the objective to report to the Jarl may still direct you to [Skald](/wiki/Skyrim:Skald "Skyrim:Skald"), who is likely to be in exile in [The Palace of the Kings](/wiki/Skyrim:Palace_of_the_Kings "Skyrim:Palace of the Kings") in [Windhelm](/wiki/Skyrim:Windhelm "Skyrim:Windhelm") (in this case, [Jarl Brina Merilis](/wiki/Skyrim:Brina_Merilis "Skyrim:Brina Merilis") will not have dialogue options to complete the objective). Additionally, the guards spawned to assist in the battle may still be [Dawnstar Guards](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar_Guard "Skyrim:Dawnstar Guard"), some of whom seem to be Essential or Protected -- once they make their way back to Dawnstar after the fight, they'll fight with (and kill) the resident Imperial soldiers. **?**

## Quest Stages

| Wave Breaker (ccBGSSSE001\_Crab\_MQ4) | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Stage | Finishes Quest | Journal Entry |
| 50 |  | I received a hastily written letter from Viriya. I should read what it has to say. *Objective 0:* Read Viriya's hastily written letter |
| 100 |  | Viriya received a letter from Jarl <Alias=Jarl> in Dawnstar. The frenzied mudcrabs have emerged once again. When the Dawnstar guard arrived, they were attacked by a spirit that caused them to flee in terror. Viriya is already on her way to Dawnstar and asked for my assistance. I should answer the call and return to the shore west of Dawnstar. *Objective 10:* Return to the shore west of Dawnstar |
| 200 |  | I have arrived once again at the shore west of Dawnstar. The signs of a recent battle scar the surrounding area where Viriya and a handful of guards have stood their ground against a mudcrab horde. As I approached, the frenzied mudcrabs began to emerge from the sea again. I should help Viriya and the guards defeat them. *Objective 15:* Kill the Frenzied Mudcrabs (100% Remaining) |
| 210 |  | I've helped Viriya and the guards defeat a wave of frenzied mudcrabs, but more are coming. I should take a moment to prepare for the next assault. *Objective 25:* Prepare for the next wave |
| 215 |  | Another wave of frenzied mudcrabs have emerged from the sea west of Dawnstar, this time more numerous than ever. We will need to fend them off once more. |
| 225 |  | After several hard-fought battles, the frenzied mudcrab attacks have stopped for now. We should regroup inside the shack nearby. *Objective 18:* Regroup with Viriya inside the shack |
| 235 |  | Now that we've regrouped after the battle, I should speak to Viriya inside the shack. *Objective 20:* Speak to Viriya |
| 300 |  | The guardian spirit of a massive emperor crab has materialized off the coast, along with a new wave of mudcrabs. The guardian spirit is too large to be harmed by normal means. However, I should be able to use the catapults the guards have put in position nearby. The mudcrabs seem to have collected the flaming pots from the wrecked wagon near the shack. I will have to recover them from the mudcrabs and use them to fire the catapults at the guardian spirit. *Objective 40:* Fire the catapults at the Emperor Crab Guardian Spirit (0/4)  *Objective 41:* Fire catapults at the Emperor Crab Guardian Spirit using flaming pots (0/4)  *Objective 42:* Obtain flaming pots from the frenzied mudcrabs |
| 500 |  | Using the catapults, I managed to vanquish the emperor crab guardian spirit. The swarms of frenzied mudcrabs have come to an end. I should speak to Viriya now that the battle is over. *Objective 50:* Speak to Viriya |
| 600 |  | In the aftermath of the battle, Viriya thanked me for helping defeat the guardian spirit of the emperor crab. The guards must tend to their wounds, and Viriya will make the long journey back to Riften. The emperor crab guardian spirit and its horde of frenzied mudcrabs should no longer threaten the people of Dawnstar. I should travel to Dawnstar and inform the Jarl that the battle was a success. *Objective 100:* Report to Jarl <Alias=Jarl> in Dawnstar |
| 2000 | ☑ | In the aftermath of the battle, Viriya thanked me for helping defeat the guardian spirit of the emperor crab. The guards must tend to their wounds, and Viriya will make the long journey back to Riften. The emperor crab guardian spirit and its horde of frenzied mudcrabs should no longer threaten the people of Dawnstar. I traveled to Dawnstar and informed the Jarl, who rewarded me for my efforts. |

- The following empty quest stages were omitted from the table: 0, 220, 230, 250, 275, 450.

Notes

- Any text displayed in angle brackets (e.g., `<Alias=LocationHold>`) is dynamically set by the Radiant Quest system, and will be filled in with the appropriate word(s) when seen in game.
- Not all Journal Entries may appear in your journal; which entries appear and which entries do not depends on the manner in which the quest is done.
- Stages are not always in order of progress. This is usually the case with quests that have multiple possible outcomes or quests where certain tasks may be done in any order. Some stages may therefore repeat objectives seen in other stages.
- If an entry is marked as "Finishes Quest" it means the quest disappears from the Active Quest list, but you may still receive new entries for that quest.
- On the PC, it is possible to use the [console](/wiki/Skyrim:Console "Skyrim:Console") to advance through the quest by entering `setstage ccBGSSSE001_Crab_MQ4 stage`, where `stage` is the number of the stage you wish to complete. It is not possible to un-complete (i.e. go back) quest stages, but it is possible to clear all stages of the quest using `resetquest ccBGSSSE001_Crab_MQ4`.

*This [Skyrim](/wiki/Skyrim:Skyrim "Skyrim:Skyrim")-related article is a [stub](/wiki/UESPWiki:Stub "UESPWiki:Stub"). You can help by [expanding it](https://en.uesp.net/w/index.php?title=Skyrim:Wave_Breaker&action=edit).*

# Skyrim:Wayward Pass

## Overview

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | [SR-mapicon-Pass.png](/wiki/File:SR-mapicon-Pass.png) | Pass: Wayward Pass ([view on map](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=Skyrim&centeron=Wayward+Pass)) | | |
| [Clearable](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons#Clearing "Skyrim:Dungeons") | No |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | 10 days |
| [Level](/wiki/Skyrim:Encounter_Zone_Level "Skyrim:Encounter Zone Level") | Min: 5 |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| WaywardPassExterior | |
| Region | |
| [Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:Winterhold_(region) "Skyrim:Winterhold (region)") | |
| Location | |
| The mountains southwest of [Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:Winterhold "Skyrim:Winterhold"), between [Alftand](/wiki/Skyrim:Alftand "Skyrim:Alftand") and [Nightgate Inn](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightgate_Inn "Skyrim:Nightgate Inn") | |

Shrine at Wayward Pass

**Wayward Pass** is a [pass](/wiki/Skyrim:Passes "Skyrim:Passes") through the mountains in the southwest of [Winterhold](/wiki/Skyrim:Winterhold_(region) "Skyrim:Winterhold (region)"). It is the resting place of an ancient traveler, adorned by a [shrine of Arkay](/wiki/Skyrim:Shrine_of_Arkay "Skyrim:Shrine of Arkay").

The pass is halfway between [Alftand](/wiki/Skyrim:Alftand "Skyrim:Alftand") and [Nightgate Inn](/wiki/Skyrim:Nightgate_Inn "Skyrim:Nightgate Inn"). The entrance and exit of [Ironbind Barrow](/wiki/Skyrim:Ironbind_Barrow "Skyrim:Ironbind Barrow") are on either side of the pass, but there are no paths to them.

At the crest of the hill, you will find an altar surrounded by five other graves, some with floral placements in front of them. Atop the altar you will find an [amulet of Arkay](/wiki/Skyrim:Amulet_of_Arkay "Skyrim:Amulet of Arkay"), an [iron helmet](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Helmet "Skyrim:Iron Helmet"), the [Ancient Traveler's Skull](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Traveler%27s_Skull "Skyrim:Ancient Traveler's Skull") (appears as a regular skull), a [steel sword](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Sword "Skyrim:Steel Sword"), and two [linen wraps](/wiki/Skyrim:Linen_Wrap "Skyrim:Linen Wrap"). In front of it, there are a [steel shield](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Shield "Skyrim:Steel Shield") and a pair of [iron boots](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Boots "Skyrim:Iron Boots").

## Gallery

- 

  The pass from a distance
- 

  Northern side of the pass

tags: ["Move Protection", "Semi Protection", "Skyrim-Bugs Fixed by the Official Skyrim Patch", "Skyrim-Bugs Fixed by the Official Skyrim Special Edition Patch", "Skyrim-Bugs Fixed by the Unofficial Skyrim Patch", "Skyrim-Items", "Skyrim-Weapons"]
---

# Skyrim:Weapons

## Overview

**Weapons** in Skyrim fall into three classes, organized around the three weapon skills:

- [One-handed](/wiki/Skyrim:One-handed "Skyrim:One-handed"): daggers, maces, swords, and war axes
- [Two-handed](/wiki/Skyrim:Two-handed "Skyrim:Two-handed"): battleaxes, greatswords, and war hammers
- [Archery](/wiki/Skyrim:Archery "Skyrim:Archery"): bows and arrows, crossbows and bolts[DG](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard")

## Weapon Categories

This page only lists the **base (unenchanted) weaponry** available in Skyrim. Base weapons are considered to be those which appear in standard leveled lists and those which can be made by the player at [Forges](/wiki/Skyrim:Forge "Skyrim:Forge"). There are several other pages that also provide information on weapons. If the weapon you are looking for is not listed on this page, you may want to check one of these other resources:

- **[Weapons (All)](/wiki/Skyrim:Weapons_(All) "Skyrim:Weapons (All)")**: All weapons on one page for easy comparison.
- **[Generic Magic Weapons](/wiki/Skyrim:Generic_Magic_Weapons "Skyrim:Generic Magic Weapons")**: Magic weapons that are found in random loot.
- **[Leveled Items](/wiki/Skyrim:Leveled_Items "Skyrim:Leveled Items")**: Magic weapons that vary depending upon your character's level (generally quest rewards).
- **[Artifacts](/wiki/Skyrim:Artifacts "Skyrim:Artifacts")**: Weapons with one-of-a-kind enchantments and unique appearances.
- **[Ammunition](/wiki/Skyrim:Ammunition "Skyrim:Ammunition")**: Arrows and bolts([DG](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard")), both normal and enchanted.
- **[Staves](/wiki/Skyrim:Staves "Skyrim:Staves")**: Staves are used for casting magical spells instead of physical attacks.
- **[Specialty Gear](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear "Skyrim:Specialty Gear")**: Weapons and armor only obtainable from specific factions.
- **[Skyrim-Weapons](/wiki/Category:Skyrim-Weapons "Category:Skyrim-Weapons")**: Category listing of all the weapons available in the game.
- **Search**: Type the name of the weapon into the search box on the left and click the magnifying glass icon to find the weapon.

## Overview

For all of the weapons listed on this site, the provided damage values are the weapons' **base damage**. The actual damage your character will do with a weapon is derived from the base damage according to your character's weapon skill and any relevant skill perks. The damage can also be increased by using [smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Smithing "Skyrim:Smithing") to sharpen a weapon.

Weapon damage formula:

```
displayed damage = Round[ (base damage + smithing increase) * (1 + skill/200) * (1 + perk effects) * (1 + item effects) * (1 + potion effect) * (1 + Seeker of Might bonus) ]

* Smithing Increase should use the unrounded value based on this formula.

* Perk Effects = .2 * Barbarian/Armsman/Overdraw rank level

* Blessing of Auriel is additive with item effects while Blessing of Boethiah is additive with potion effect

Damage =
 ((Right Hand displayed Damage * (1 + Power Attack Bonus) *  (1 + Power Attack Perk) * (1 + Dual Power Attack perk)) * Dual Power Attack Mod) + ((Left Hand displayed Damage * (1 + Power Attack Bonus) *  (1 + Power Attack Perk) * (1 + Dual Power Attack perk)) * Dual Power Attack Mod)

* Power Attack Bonus = 1 if power attacking

* Power Attack Perk = 0.25 if power attacking and you have Savage Strike or Devastating Blow

* Dual Power Attack Perk = 0.5 if power attacking with two weapons and you have Dual Savagery

* Dual Power Attack Mod is 1.5 for the right (1xattack) and 3 (2xattack) for the left hand if dual power attacking (else 1)
```

Note on Dual Savagery, you don't have to be power attacking with both weapons. Single power attacks get this bonus as long as there is a weapon in both hands.

## Weapon Materials

Weapons appear in several different materials and styles, which determine the quality of the weapon. Higher quality weapons do more damage (but are generally heavier). Which quality weapons you will find are generally determined based on your character's level.

The following table describes the standard level at which a weapon type begins to show up in randomly generated loot; lower level weapons will also randomly appear. Enchanted versions of weapons will begin to appear one level later; see [Generic Magic Weapons](/wiki/Skyrim:Generic_Magic_Weapons "Skyrim:Generic Magic Weapons") for details. See [Leveled Lists](/wiki/Skyrim:Leveled_Lists "Skyrim:Leveled Lists") for details on how these lists are used to determine the probabilities of individual items appearing. Level of "N/A" means that the item is not available from standard leveled lists. Although daedric weapons can be found in random loot once your level is high enough, the chances of finding daedric weapons is 20 times smaller than other weapon types.

Most weapons from the list below can be created at [Forges](/wiki/Skyrim:Forge "Skyrim:Forge"), by using the [Smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Smithing "Skyrim:Smithing") skill. The ability to make a weapon is not dependent upon your character's level, but is instead dependent upon which Smithing perks you have unlocked. All standard weapons can also be tempered at [Grindstones](/wiki/Skyrim:Grindstone "Skyrim:Grindstone"), if you have the necessary item. Tempering is twice as effective if you have unlocked the perk necessary to forge that item. Tempering an enchanted version of the weapon (whether custom-[enchanted](/wiki/Skyrim:Enchanting "Skyrim:Enchanting"), or [generic](/wiki/Skyrim:Generic_Magic_Weapons "Skyrim:Generic Magic Weapons")) is similar to tempering the base version – it requires the same item, and is twice as effective if you have unlocked the forging perk – but also requires the [Arcane Blacksmith](/wiki/Skyrim:Arcane_Blacksmith "Skyrim:Arcane Blacksmith") perk to be unlocked.

| Level | Material | Perk to [Forge](/wiki/Skyrim:Forge "Skyrim:Forge") | Item for [Tempering](/wiki/Skyrim:Grindstone "Skyrim:Grindstone") |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | [Iron](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Weapons "Skyrim:Iron Weapons") | No perk needed | [Iron Ingot](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Ingot "Skyrim:Iron Ingot") |
| 2 | [Steel](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Weapons "Skyrim:Steel Weapons") | [Steel Smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Smithing "Skyrim:Steel Smithing") | [Steel Ingot](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Ingot "Skyrim:Steel Ingot") |
| N/A | [Silver](/wiki/Skyrim:Specialty_Gear#Silver_Hand_Equipment "Skyrim:Specialty Gear")† | N/A | N/A |
| 6 | [Orcish](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Weapons "Skyrim:Orcish Weapons") | [Orcish Smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Orcish_Smithing "Skyrim:Orcish Smithing") | [Orichalcum Ingot](/wiki/Skyrim:Orichalcum_Ingot "Skyrim:Orichalcum Ingot") |
| 12 | [Dwarven](/wiki/Skyrim:Dwarven_Weapons "Skyrim:Dwarven Weapons") | [Dwarven Smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Dwarven_Smithing "Skyrim:Dwarven Smithing") | [Dwarven Metal Ingot](/wiki/Skyrim:Dwarven_Metal_Ingot "Skyrim:Dwarven Metal Ingot") |
| N/A | [Nord Hero](/wiki/Skyrim:Nord_Hero_(material) "Skyrim:Nord Hero (material)") | N/A†† | [Steel Ingot](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Ingot "Skyrim:Steel Ingot") |
| N/A | Skyforge Steel | N/A‡‡ | [Steel Ingot](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Ingot "Skyrim:Steel Ingot") |
| 18 | [Nordic](/wiki/Skyrim:Nordic_Weapons "Skyrim:Nordic Weapons")[DB](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonborn "Skyrim:Dragonborn") | [Advanced Armors](/wiki/Skyrim:Advanced_Armors "Skyrim:Advanced Armors") | [Quicksilver Ingot](/wiki/Skyrim:Quicksilver_Ingot "Skyrim:Quicksilver Ingot") |
| 19 | [Elven](/wiki/Skyrim:Elven_Weapons "Skyrim:Elven Weapons") | [Elven Smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Elven_Smithing "Skyrim:Elven Smithing") | [Refined Moonstone](/wiki/Skyrim:Refined_Moonstone "Skyrim:Refined Moonstone") |
| 27 | [Glass](/wiki/Skyrim:Glass_Weapons "Skyrim:Glass Weapons") | [Glass Smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Glass_Smithing "Skyrim:Glass Smithing") | [Refined Malachite](/wiki/Skyrim:Refined_Malachite "Skyrim:Refined Malachite") |
| 35 | [Stalhrim](/wiki/Skyrim:Stalhrim_Weapons "Skyrim:Stalhrim Weapons")[DB](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonborn "Skyrim:Dragonborn") | [Ebony Smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Ebony_Smithing "Skyrim:Ebony Smithing")††† | [Stalhrim](/wiki/Skyrim:Stalhrim "Skyrim:Stalhrim") |
| 36 | [Ebony](/wiki/Skyrim:Ebony_Weapons "Skyrim:Ebony Weapons") | [Ebony Smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Ebony_Smithing "Skyrim:Ebony Smithing") | [Ebony Ingot](/wiki/Skyrim:Ebony_Ingot "Skyrim:Ebony Ingot") |
| 46 | [Daedric](/wiki/Skyrim:Daedric_Weapons "Skyrim:Daedric Weapons") | [Daedric Smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Daedric_Smithing "Skyrim:Daedric Smithing")‡ | [Ebony Ingot](/wiki/Skyrim:Ebony_Ingot "Skyrim:Ebony Ingot") |
| N/A | [Dragonbone](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragonbone "Skyrim:Dragonbone")[DG](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard") | [Dragon Armor](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Armor "Skyrim:Dragon Armor") | [Dragon Bone](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Bone "Skyrim:Dragon Bone") |

† Silver weapons (regardless of type) gain 20 base damage when used against undead or werewolves. This amount is not improved by your weapon skill, but is increased by relevant enchantments and perks. This effect is not considered an enchantment, so silver weapons can be enchanted like any normal unenchanted weapon, though they will always display the text for this effect and not for any enchantment that you may place on it.

‡ All Daedric weapons can also be made at the [Atronach Forge](/wiki/Skyrim:Atronach_Forge "Skyrim:Atronach Forge"), in which case the Daedric Smithing perk is not needed, but instead the [Sigil Stone](/wiki/Skyrim:Sigil_Stone "Skyrim:Sigil Stone") must be obtained. Tempering weapons created at the Atronach Forge is still augmented by the Daedric Smithing perk.
†† Nord Hero weapons can only be created at the [Skyforge](/wiki/Skyrim:Skyforge "Skyrim:Skyforge"), and only after the quest [Glory of the Dead](/wiki/Skyrim:Glory_of_the_Dead "Skyrim:Glory of the Dead") has been completed.
‡‡ Although Skyforge Steel weapons cannot be forged, tempering them is still augmented by the Steel Smithing perk.
††† Forging Stalhrim weapons also requires completion of the quest [A New Source of Stalhrim](/wiki/Skyrim:A_New_Source_of_Stalhrim "Skyrim:A New Source of Stalhrim").

out of the standard `LItemWeaponBattleAxe` [leveled list](/wiki/Skyrim:Leveled_Lists "Skyrim:Leveled Lists"), although it is included in several less common battleaxe leveled lists. As a result, this axe is less likely to be found in random loot than other base weapons.  - PC Only The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 1.3.2, fixes this bug. |

### Bows

All traditional bows require [arrows](/wiki/Skyrim:Arrow "Skyrim:Arrow") as ammunition.

- **Stagger**: 0 (50% chance for 0.25 with [Power Shot](/wiki/Skyrim:Power_Shot "Skyrim:Power Shot"))

† The long bow cannot be created at [forges](/wiki/Skyrim:Forge "Skyrim:Forge"). It can be tempered using [firewood](/wiki/Skyrim:Firewood "Skyrim:Firewood"). Tempering is not augmented by any Smithing perks. While it possesses the fastest firing speed of all the standard bows, it has another drawback besides also having the lowest base damage; it has reduced range compared to all other bows, with arrows dipping in flight far sooner than when fired by the others. To compensate for longer shots, the long bow must be aimed higher to land long-range shots and achieve equivalent range.
‡ The hunting bow cannot be created at forges. It can be tempered using [leather strips](/wiki/Skyrim:Leather_Strips "Skyrim:Leather Strips"), and tempering is augmented if you have selected the [Steel Smithing](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Smithing "Skyrim:Steel Smithing") perk.

†† The Nord hero bow cannot be tempered. Its tempering entry (`TemperWeaponSkyforgeBow`) incorrectly links to the Nord hero battle axe instead of the bow.

- The [Official Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim:Patch "Skyrim:Patch"), version 1.9.26.0.8, fixes this bug.

### Crossbows DG

Crossbows are only available with the [Dawnguard add-on](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard "Skyrim:Dawnguard"). The Dwarven Crossbow can only be acquired by siding with the [Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnguard_(faction) "Skyrim:Dawnguard (faction)") (see [Ancient Technology](/wiki/Skyrim:Ancient_Technology "Skyrim:Ancient Technology") for details). The Elven and Ebony Crossbows require the completion of the quest [Night Hunter](/wiki/Skyrim:Night_Hunter "Skyrim:Night Hunter"), while other types of Crossbows can be purchased from the Fletcher in [Solitude](/wiki/Skyrim:Solitude "Skyrim:Solitude"). Crossbows all require [bolts](/wiki/Skyrim:Bolt "Skyrim:Bolt") as ammunition instead of arrows. While all other types of crossbows and all bolts can be manufactured at any forge, regular and Dwarven Crossbows can only be crafted at [Gunmar's forge](/wiki/Skyrim:Forge#Gunmar.27s_Forge "Skyrim:Forge") in [Fort Dawnguard](/wiki/Skyrim:Fort_Dawnguard "Skyrim:Fort Dawnguard") or at the All Forge in the [Myrwatch](/wiki/Skyrim:Myrwatch "Skyrim:Myrwatch") [Creation](/wiki/Skyrim:Creation_Club "Skyrim:Creation Club"). Forging Elven and Ebony Crossbows requires the completion of the quest Night Hunter, and other crossbows will need to be acquired from loot or merchants first before they can be crafted. Crafting Stalhrim Crossbows also requires the completion of [A New Source of Stalhrim](/wiki/Skyrim:A_New_Source_of_Stalhrim "Skyrim:A New Source of Stalhrim").

- **Stagger**: 0.75

[†](#note_Enhanced)The Enhanced versions have an additional property: *Attacks with this crossbow ignore 50% of armor.* This effect is not considered an enchantment, so enhanced crossbows can be enchanted like any normal unenchanted weapon, though they will only display the text for this effect and not for any enchantment that you may place on it.  The text display issue is fixed by the mod [Enchantable Special Item Fix](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Enchantable_Special_Item_Fix "Skyrim Mod:Enchantable Special Item Fix").

[††](#note_Speed)Despite saying they have the same speed as the long bow, all crossbows fire significantly slower than most of the normal bows. Unlike with normal bows, however, it is possible to circumvent this by means of an exploit: unequipping the bow then re-equipping it by means of hotkeys will cause the crossbow to be reloaded.
