| [Clearable](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons#Clearing "Skyrim:Dungeons") | No |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | 10 days |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| WhiterunWatchtowerExterior, WhiterunWatchtowerExterior02 | |
| Region | |
| [Whiterun Hold](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun_Hold "Skyrim:Whiterun Hold") | |
| Location | |
| West of [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun "Skyrim:Whiterun") North of [Secunda's Kiss](/wiki/Skyrim:Secunda%27s_Kiss "Skyrim:Secunda's Kiss") | |

The Western Watchtower

The **Western Watchtower** is a small [fort](/wiki/Skyrim:Military_Forts "Skyrim:Military Forts") west of [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun "Skyrim:Whiterun").

## Related Quests

- **[Dragon Rising](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Rising "Skyrim:Dragon Rising")**: Investigate a [dragon](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon "Skyrim:Dragon") sighting near [Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun "Skyrim:Whiterun").

## Walkthrough

The tower is along the main road heading west from Whiterun, allowing the [Whiterun guards](/wiki/Skyrim:Whiterun_Guard "Skyrim:Whiterun Guard") in the area to watch for any potential threats approaching from that direction. It can be entered via the crumbling wall that forms a ramp upwards. Outside the tower the area between the fallen walls contains a training dummy with some Iron Arrows shot into it. Inside the tower are two unowned bedrolls and a small table with a lantern. The second floor contains an archery target and a bale of straw, while the top floor is empty. There are two [Imperial Legion](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Legion "Skyrim:Imperial Legion") banners flying from the tower.

Four Whiterun guards will be stationed at the tower to keep watch for enemies (see [bugs](#Bugs)).

Once the quest [Dragon Rising](/wiki/Skyrim:Dragon_Rising "Skyrim:Dragon Rising") is complete, the area between the fallen walls contains two burnt corpses with loot and the charred remains of a wooden hut.

## Notes

- There are two [iron ore veins](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Ore_Vein "Skyrim:Iron Ore Vein") in the rocks to the west.
- The Western Watchtower will not be marked as a location on your map until the quest Dragon Rising begins.
- The Imperial Legion banners will be replaced by the [Stormcloak](/wiki/Skyrim:Stormcloaks "Skyrim:Stormcloaks") banners if the [Stormcloaks capture Whiterun](/wiki/Skyrim:Battle_for_Whiterun_(Stormcloaks) "Skyrim:Battle for Whiterun (Stormcloaks)") during the [Civil War](/wiki/Skyrim:Civil_War "Skyrim:Civil War") questline.
  - Version 1.3.3 of the [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch") replaces the default Imperial Legion banners with yellow Whiterun banners to better reflect that Whiterun Hold is originally neutral in the Civil War questline.
- Version 4.1.1 of the [Unofficial Skyrim Special Edition Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Special_Edition_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Special Edition Patch") replaces Western Watchtower's fort map icon with the one used for [Imperial towers](/wiki/Skyrim:Towers "Skyrim:Towers").

## Bugs

- Sometimes the guards may be missing, possibly if the Stormcloaks hold Whiterun.
  -  The [Unofficial Skyrim Patch](/wiki/Skyrim_Mod:Unofficial_Skyrim_Patch "Skyrim Mod:Unofficial Skyrim Patch"), version 2.0.1, fixes this bug.
- The guards stationed at the tower may be stuck inside the entrance, both before and after Dragon Rising. **?**

-

# Skyrim:Weynon Stones

## Overview

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| |  |  | | --- | --- | | [SR-mapicon-Landmark.png](/wiki/File:SR-mapicon-Landmark.png) | Landmark: Weynon Stones ([view on map](https://gamemap.uesp.net/sr/?world=Skyrim&centeron=Weynon+Stones)) | | |
| [Clearable](/wiki/Skyrim:Dungeons#Clearing "Skyrim:Dungeons") | Yes |
| [Respawn Time](/wiki/Skyrim:Respawn "Skyrim:Respawn") | 10 days or 30 days |
| [Level](/wiki/Skyrim:Encounter_Zone_Level "Skyrim:Encounter Zone Level") | Min: 18 |
| Occupants | |
| [Ice Wraiths](/wiki/Skyrim:Ice_Wraith "Skyrim:Ice Wraith") | |
| Console Location Code(s) | |
| WeynonStonesExterior01, WeynonStonesExterior02 | |
| Region | |
| [The Pale](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Pale "Skyrim:The Pale") | |
| Location | |
| South of [Dawnstar](/wiki/Skyrim:Dawnstar "Skyrim:Dawnstar") | |

The Weynon Stones

The **Weynon Stones** are a [landmark](/wiki/Skyrim:Landmarks "Skyrim:Landmarks") consisting of a circle of stones. It is located between [Korvanjund](/wiki/Skyrim:Korvanjund "Skyrim:Korvanjund") and [Fort Dunstad](/wiki/Skyrim:Fort_Dunstad "Skyrim:Fort Dunstad"), just south of the path.

It is home to a pair of [ice wraiths](/wiki/Skyrim:Ice_Wraith "Skyrim:Ice Wraith") who will attack as you approach. This can be a challenging battle at low levels or if you don't have any resistance to frost magic.

## Walkthrough

As you enter the circle of stones, the two ice wraiths will appear and attack. There is an empty altar up a flight of stone stairs with a large stone head above it. Beside the altar is a [burial urn](/wiki/Skyrim:Burial_Urn#Urn "Skyrim:Burial Urn"). Behind the large stone head is a [chest](/wiki/Skyrim:Chest "Skyrim:Chest") with a novice-level lock containing random [loot](/wiki/Skyrim:Leveled_Lists "Skyrim:Leveled Lists"). Opposite this is a [shrine of Talos](/wiki/Skyrim:Shrines#Shrine_of_Talos "Skyrim:Shrines"). On the shrine are two [steel ingots](/wiki/Skyrim:Steel_Ingot "Skyrim:Steel Ingot"), a pair of [iron gauntlets](/wiki/Skyrim:Iron_Gauntlets "Skyrim:Iron Gauntlets"), and two pieces of a [broken steel](/wiki/Skyrim:Broken_Steel_Battle_Axe_Head "Skyrim:Broken Steel Battle Axe Head") [battleaxe](/wiki/Skyrim:Broken_Steel_Battle_Axe_Handle "Skyrim:Broken Steel Battle Axe Handle"). The two pieces cannot be picked up. In the surrounding area there are several [snowberry](/wiki/Skyrim:Snowberries "Skyrim:Snowberries") bushes.

## Notes

- The location's name may be a reference to [Weynon Priory](/wiki/Oblivion:Weynon_Priory_(settlement) "Oblivion:Weynon Priory (settlement)"), as also suggested by the connection to [Talos worship](/wiki/Lore:Talos "Lore:Talos").

# Skyrim:When the Cat's Away

## Overview

When the Cat's Away

## Quick Walkthrough

1. Read [M'Sharra's Diary](/wiki/Skyrim:M%27Sharra%27s_Diary "Skyrim:M'Sharra's Diary")
2. Find and read [M'Sharra's Confession](/wiki/Skyrim:M%27Sharra%27s_Confession "Skyrim:M'Sharra's Confession") in Dragonsreach Dungeon
3. Travel to [Lund's Hut](/wiki/Skyrim:Lund%27s_Hut "Skyrim:Lund's Hut")
4. Loot the Silver Armor
5. Fight the East Empire Warden

## Detailed Walkthrough

M'Sharra's diary

To start this quest, head to the Bannered Mare in Whiterun. Find *[M'Sharra's Diary](/wiki/Skyrim:M%27Sharra%27s_Diary "Skyrim:M'Sharra's Diary")* on top of the table within the kitchen area. This will begin the adventure after reading it.

Next, visit the Dragonsreach Dungeon and look in the corner near the Prisoner's Evidence Chest to find *[M'Sharra's Confession](/wiki/Skyrim:M%27Sharra%27s_Confession "Skyrim:M'Sharra's Confession")*. This indicates that she left a full set of Silver Armor within a hut near Rorikstead.

This is actually [Lund's Hut](/wiki/Skyrim:Lund%27s_Hut "Skyrim:Lund's Hut"), the skeever-infested hut once owned by Lund, who lies deceased inside. Head there and inside find a chest that indeed contains a full set of Silver Armor along with a copy of *[The Crimson Dirks, v2](/wiki/Skyrim:The_Crimson_Dirks,_v2 "Skyrim:The Crimson Dirks, v2")*. Head outside and an [Imperial Captain](/wiki/Skyrim:Imperial_Captain "Skyrim:Imperial Captain") and two [East Empire Wardens](/wiki/Skyrim:East_Empire_Warden "Skyrim:East Empire Warden") will begin attacking. Fight them off and enjoy the new set of armor.

## Bugs

- The note "M'Sharra's Confession" may not spawn on the ground in the dungeon where it is supposed to, but under the floor.
  -  If the note seems to be missing, it can be reached by using the `tcl` console command to go under the floor.
  -  Alternatively, one can use the console to advance the quest to stage 30 and bypass the need for the note.
- It is possible to Clip through the wall with a wooden Plate, Pressed against the wall, and Sprint into it until you are inside the wall, Then you must walk along the Dirt that is loaded within the negative Space, By which you can grab the note before falling and being re-positioned in the Dungeon.

## Quest Stages

| When the Cat's Away (ccBGSSSE056\_Quest) | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Stage | Finishes Quest | Journal Entry |
| 10 |  | People from across Tamriel have migrated to Skyrim in the wake of the Great War. Perhaps if I poke around the tavern in Whiterun, I can learn some of their stories. *Objective 10:* Search for a note at the Bannered Mare |
| 20 |  | I found a note at the Bannered Mare written by a Khajiit named M'Sharra. She claims to have been a warrior in another life, and decided to start a fight with the guards so she could relive some of her past glory. I should visit the dungeon to see what became of her. *Objective 20:* Inspect Dragonsreach Dungeon for clues on M'Sharra |
| 30 |  | I found a note at the Bannered Mare written by a Khajiit named M'Sharra. She claims to have been a warrior in another life, and decided to start a fight with the guards so she could relive some of her past glory. While she was no longer present, I found a note next to the evidence chest that implies she left an armor set in an abandoned hut near Rorikstead. *Objective 30:* Retrieve the armor from the Hut |
| 1000 | ☑ | I found a note at the Bannered Mare written by a Khajiit named M'Sharra. She claims to have been a warrior in another life, and decided to start a fight with the guards so she could relive some of her past glory. While she was no longer present, I found a note next to the evidence chest that implies she left an armor set in an abandoned hut near Rorikstead. Inside the hut was a set of silver armor. |

- The following empty quest stages were omitted from the table: 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90, 100.

Notes

- Any text displayed in angle brackets (e.g., `<Alias=LocationHold>`) is dynamically set by the Radiant Quest system, and will be filled in with the appropriate word(s) when seen in game.
- Not all Journal Entries may appear in your journal; which entries appear and which entries do not depends on the manner in which the quest is done.
- Stages are not always in order of progress. This is usually the case with quests that have multiple possible outcomes or quests where certain tasks may be done in any order. Some stages may therefore repeat objectives seen in other stages.
- If an entry is marked as "Finishes Quest" it means the quest disappears from the Active Quest list, but you may still receive new entries for that quest.
- On the PC, it is possible to use the [console](/wiki/Skyrim:Console "Skyrim:Console") to advance through the quest by entering `setstage ccBGSSSE056_Quest stage`, where `stage` is the number of the stage you wish to complete. It is not possible to un-complete (i.e. go back) quest stages, but it is possible to clear all stages of the quest using `resetquest ccBGSSSE056_Quest`.
